Also I asked a guy that he was refilling the fridges about a wrap if it's spicy or not and he helped me choose between two options  that I had ,with his  answer.

Thank you for the experience.

I will definitely go back to Costa but hopefully not to the specific one because that means I will visit the hospital again for a health issue.

Not a great Costa experience.

-Staff are impolite and moody.
-Observed them changing bins, picking dropped items from the floor, and then serving food without washing hands.
-Make wrong drinks, and then insist that is what you ordered!
-Drinks not in keeping with Costa taste.

Not much food options, and long waiting times, and don’t accept Costa points, either.

Th public probably go here as it’s conveniently located in the hospital.

Shocked to discover that a Hospital Costa cab be better in all areas than flagship high street stores. This was my 2nd Costa Coffee ☕ shop visit after months boycotting Costa. After nearly 30years of Costa Coffee loyalty. I was forced to go in here. Glad I was. Highly recommended. Basic Costa with a tiny WHS. Can't fault it.

I went to this Costa branch on my way to Tesco to redeem my voucher to pick up a cup of tea.
Staff member Abdulrahman was polite and friendly and provided me with a nice cup of tea.

The staff member was quite rude. We were her first customer and she was just rude because she didn't want to be at work that day. Perhaps you should think about hiring those that will appreciate their role more than this woman here.
author_name: Namjin has V nice Suga Kookies for Jimin Jhopefully

review_text:
Very cosy and satisfying service.
Things to improve: volume of music is too loud, staff go for smoke breaks outside in downstairs floor, smell of cigarettes is unpleasant and they leave the door open so cold air enters for a good 10 mins which ruins the warm environment of the cafe.

Usually don't write reviews but here goes. Really rude staff here. Been in there a few times and have left feeling bad about ordering a drink. Was also present when the police came in to ask a few questions about an incident which had occurred earlier in the day and they were so sarcy to them. It was really unprofessional. The Hammersmith Costa is a much more friendly experience.
Pretty crowded and did an decent salted-caramel-latte.

The coffee bar next to the Hilton Hotel offers a variety of delicious sweet and savory snacks. My cappuccino was good but not perfect, unfortunately lacking in coffee art. Its location with direct access is convenient. Overall, it's not bad but has room for improvement.

This must be voted the worst Costa coffee shop there is… guy could barley speak English, no food on display terrible coffee, the only good thing they have it’s the convenience of location… next time I’ll walk an extra few minutes for a different experience!

I recently visited and unfortunately, my experience was disappointing. The place was unorganized and not clean, which made it uncomfortable to stay. On a positive note, the coffee was good; I ordered a flat white, and it was enjoyable. However, the overall atmosphere needs significant improvement.
The Hilton hotel refuses to give free coffees through the costa app, be aware, no toilets available either.
Nice convenient Costa, but there is no WiFi, only the Hilton's WiFi which you need a room number for.

Magda is truly been so helpful and understanding! Helped me find an alternative vegetarian option for the breakfast deal, and also made my drink with an alternative milk after my morning brain forgot to ask for nondairy milk! Thank you for being so kind and patient!!☕️

I recently visited the Costa Coffee on Melcombe Street and was disappointed to find there are no customer restrooms. This was inconvenient, as I had to leave the cafe and run to Marylebone Station to use their facilities. I believe it's important for businesses, especially those with 30 seats like this one, to inform customers about the lack of restrooms beforehand. Perhaps a sign at the entrance would be helpful.
Staff was blatantly rude, waving the cup sizes in our faces and not listening. This blonde-haired woman would not be polite to us. The drinks were good and so was the food. Very unimpressed

I had a very disappointing experience at this café. One of the staff members raised their voice at my son, which is completely unacceptable. The overall attitude and service were unprofessional and unfriendly. I do not recommend this place at all.

Had a tea and cake today at Costa (thanks to a little National Tea Day treat for members) ☕🍰 The girl at the counter was lovely, super friendly and service was quick ✨
Sat by the window looking out at the garden — such a chill vibe 🌿 Great spot to hang out with a book or just unwind for a bit 📖💆‍♀️

This cafe really isn’t trying. Almost all the seats are downstairs in a windowless basement. All ordering has to be on an impersonal screen and there are no customer toilets. Hard to see how this will survive surrounded by lovely chains such as Ole and Steen and Paul and a host of gorgeous independent cafes. If you want the level of  customer focus here you can get it for a fraction of the price at McDonalds which does very good coffee

Absolutely divine!
Smooth, rich, and perfectly balanced – this is hands down the best chocolate in Costa. Wonderful service too, making the whole experience a real treat! 🍫✨

We were craving for something sweet and appealed by the promotion sign of Costa Coffee on the street. The photo on the sign was the Ruby Chocolate & Light Dairy Swirl, which looked amazingly pretty in dreamy pink color.

We did get their Ruby Chocolate drink with less whipped cream to share with two of us as an afternoon dessert. It was really sweet but extremely satisfying. We could barely taste the flavor from ruby chocolate. We will suggest to ask the staff to give an extra straw since the paper straw was easily soaked and getting soft.

I stopped by this Costa Coffee for the first time today and had a really nice experience. From the moment I walked in, the two young women working there greeted me with genuine smiles and such friendly energy. Their positive attitude instantly made me feel comfortable. Really great team and great service I’ll definitely be back.
Really great Costa! Staff were super friendly and made my coffee just right. The place was clean, had a nice vibe, and plenty of seating. Perfect spot for a quick break — I’ll definitely be back!

Wonderful location of the Costa Coffee on Westbourne Grove in London. Friendly team and atmosphere, very clean, nice and tasty pastries and coffee selection. Busy with a lot of locals and connecting about local activities. My personal score is a 4.8.

The only gift I got on my birthday was this piece of cake that Costa gave me for free...🙏🏼🤍♥️🍰
Some gifts, even though they may seem small, can make you feel good for months.🥰🎂
Thank you very much Costa

I have been going to this Costa regularly and Kate makes the whole experience even better. She is such pleasant soul and a genuinely kind person - always recommending the best cakes!! I want to thank her for being her. She makes a difference

Came here on a whim, I don’t usually go to Costa, but wanted to try the Iced Spanish Latte which was amazing - but more so, I was served by Aneta who was an angel. Very patient, kind and considerate. You can tell she’s a people’s person. Costa is lucky you have her, very rare to encounter people like that.

Really bad toilet state. Some unknown liquid all aver floor in splotches. Broken hand dryer,

No proper drinks price list visible. What they have are 3 LCD screens that show adverts for most of the time and then randomly show 1 page of the drinks menu which can take forever to show the page you want to see making the whole thing pointless.

Tables were clean.

No WiFi , which is really odd for a Costa not to have WiFi and mobile signal is very poor here in the shop (EE at least).

Unlike the shambles of the toilet , front of house is much better presented with a decent amount of seating too.

Great Americano and tart from the Costa inside Paddington Station. Easy to find this café, find the main shop area, take the moving stairs to the 3rd level, it's tucked away in the corner (directly above Barburrito mexican eatery)... On a side note: Good luck finding a table on a busy morning, always jam packed with the early morning commuters.

They have great location on the top level of The Lawn here.

Decent sandwiches, nothing spectacular but served it’s purpose. They feel fresh.

Good and quick service, helpful.

Needed some food and coffee while waiting for my train. Was pleased to find Costa located up some escalators. Can’t complain about the quality. Price reasonable really. Could do with some more seating maybe but there are plenty of places to sit down in the station itself.

The Indian female staff member provided terrible service. She didn't explain how to use the card slot when it didn't work. She was chatting with other staff members, ignoring customers and providing poor service. She should be more attentive to customers,

This is a nice location at Paddington station.  Order at the counter and the employees here are great.  So nice, funny, and helpful.  I enjoyed watching them interact with each other.  I love Costa coffee so when I see a location I stop.  Fresh hot latte and a croissant … life doesn’t get better than this.

I visited Costa Coffee and even though it was quite busy when I arrived, I was greeted with a warm hello straight away. The staff were friendly as always, and everything ran smoothly with no issues.

I was served by Andreea, who was lovely; she asked how I was and made my coffee quickly. The coffee was very good, just as expected.

Every time I come here, the staff are consistently nice, polite and very friendly. Kushbu is a star, knowing her regulars and their drink , very impressive!!
I’ve never had a single issue, and it’s always a pleasure to return. Highly recommended!
My Costa barista Adam is phenomenal he’s knows me by name and gets my order perfect!!! Adam is your guy :)

I always have a great experience at Costa, but I have to give a special shoutout to Prashant. His customer service is outstanding every single time I visit. He’s incredibly friendly, attentive, and genuinely cares about making each customer feel welcome. Whether it’s remembering my usual order or just offering a warm smile, Prashant goes above and beyond to make every visit a positive one.
Costa is a leauge of its own no other coffee shops compare. Guy on shift to day is very polite and welcoming of customers coming in. Keep up the great work dude 😎

I come here often even though as a cab driver im all over London i sometimes drive far to buy a coffee at this branch. Main reason being friendly and welcoming staff. In no particular order, kushbu, Preshant, Bhavani, Nisha, the tall man (forgot his name new and one part timer asian ) and last but not least Andrea. Thanks to all 🙏

Its one of the newest coffee shops in Edgware road.
Amazing atmosphere with friendly staff.

I visited Costa Coffee on Edgware Road today and had a lovely experience. Two friendly ladies took my order with a warm smile and provided excellent service. The atmosphere was clean and peaceful, and I felt very comfortable. Thank you for the professional and kind service. I will definitely come back again! 🌟☕️

Average coffee and food - sandwich was a little too dry. Service was amazing - they were fast and friendly.

Shop is small with limited seating.
Nice and friendly and quick service

Shit service  and rude staff my wife eyesight is bad she went near the  till and I was reading the menu for her and the lady was very rude.totally unacceptable behaviour specially for disability person.and my phone was off we had train to catch I asked can I charge my phone for 2 min she said no .i mean I bought a coffee as well .it happened 24.12.2025

Like any other Costa Coffee, drink and food are the same.

Service is great, staff served with a smile and enjoy your drink.

Busy during morning time.

Friendly , good customer service!
Good and coffee not top level but acceptable

I went in for a coffee with a friend and a man came into the store and asked me for money. When I politely declined to give him any, he started spitting on me and racially abusing me. This went on for 45 mins while the staff did nothing. They even gave him a free coffee ! When I asked the shift supervisor for his name, he claimed that he didn't see  or hear anything. Women should be able to enjoy a coffee without this sort of abuse.

This place is my second home, i do my morning reading  there, i meet my bodies, sometimes meditate!😇, the staff are friends to everyone, they take alot from the community, it is not an easy spot to serve, they get robbed, violated and they had to hire a guard to protect the fridge for quite a while, i know them by name, they r so professional, clean and what they do to someone in need is remarkable!, never tried the food but coffee is my fav, it  is very busy there.
Got a simple order wrong. Clear communication with customers is the bare minimum.

Overall, the food was good and it was fairly affordable in a normal cafe setting.

The English breakfast was great. The normal was definitely enough. Sadly no drip/filter coffee, but we had Americanos and tea and they were both good. I was surprised by increased prices from what was on the past reviews, but it was still reasonably priced.

This place is a find!

There are so many cafes in the area but it can be hard to find a good quality veggie breakfast. This was the exception! It has really great options, all produced with care and of great quality.

The service and staff were really lovely as well - special shout out to MC Sina for the Sunday morning dance mix which set me up day!

**A Deeply Disappointing Experience – Unworthy of Hospitality**

Our recent visit to La Costa Cafe was, unfortunately, one of the most unsettling experiences we’ve had in any public setting. My friends and I entered seeking a peaceful time over coffee and light snacks. The ambiance appeared calm and welcoming at first; however, what followed was both unprofessional and deeply disheartening.

While we sat and conversed—as any group of friends would in a café—we were approached by a staff member, an older woman wearing glasses, who asked us to lower our voices, stating that she didn’t have her headache medicine. The request was delivered with a tone that felt more like a personal grievance than a courteous suggestion, leaving us shocked and confused.

What made this incident especially troubling was the clear sense of discrimination we felt. We could not ignore how her attitude shifted the moment she saw us—women of color wearing hijabs. Her approach lacked basic hospitality and respect, giving the impression that we were being singled out not for our behavior, but for our appearance.

A café should be a space of comfort, inclusion, and respect—not one where customers are made to feel like intruders. The management at La Costa Cafe needs to reevaluate the values they promote and the individuals they choose to represent them.

We left feeling humiliated, unwelcome, and hurt. This establishment failed us on every level.

It was an excellent breakfast we had in this restaurant, good quality of the food is excellent and so does with the service. Sitting on a terrace in the middle  of the city of Westminster on sunny morning was a blessing. Special thank you for Joseph for his wonderful service and professionalism, we definitely will recommend this restaurant and for sure we will come back when we visit London again.

Had breakfast here this morning on our last day in the area, I wish we had discovered this place earlier as the breakfast was the best we have had. All ingredients were extremely nice and tasty, the staff were very pleasant as well. We would 100% come back here in future
A nice find in the heart of the city for those on a tighter budget. The bakes look good and the hot chocolate is outstanding. The staff members are friendly and the toilet is clean.

Amazing toast. Was really tasty. The AC worked really well. When I was there it wasn't too crowded. The toilets are okay and the staff were really courteous.

Overall, unexpectedly a very pleasing experience since I didn't have high expectations.

I had a toasted sandwich, crisps, muffin and an Americano which was very enjoyable. The baristas were friendly and appeared to enjoy their work even though it was obvious that they were overworked and understaffed.

Update:  On my last two visits to Greenwich the coffee shop was closed down and there was no real alternative such as a Starbucks or Nero.
Good place to just pop in for a quick coffee.  Friendly staff.   Coffee was spot on….!   Clean and tidy.

Me and my dad went into Costa and I managed to find a seat downstairs as all of the tables upstairs were full. I struggle with trays and steps  and  My dad struggles with steep stairs at the best of times — especially with wet shoes — so he hovered at the top of the stairs holding the tray, trying to decide if it was safe to come down. In the end, he decided not to risk it.

What really disappointed me was that not one member of staff offered to help. There was a vulnerable man clearly struggling to get down the stairs with food and drinks, and not even a word of assistance or concern from anyone.

It really wouldn’t have taken much for someone to offer a hand or even just acknowledge the situation. A simple bit of kindness and awareness would have made all the difference.

Nice spot for a quick coffee break in London. The coffee was good and served hot, and the staff were friendly and efficient. There was a decent selection of pastries and snacks, and the place was clean and comfortable. A reliable choice for a coffee on the go or to sit and relax for a bit.

We visited Central London yesterday evening (Christmas Eve) to see the Christmas lights and enjoy the festive atmosphere along Regent Street and surrounding area. The crowds did become a bit overwhelming and after much walking in what was a cold Winters night we found this Costa near Piccadilly Circus, which was a haven of warmth and peace, with very polite and helpful staff, who served us a delicious toastie and coffee at a very reasonable price. There's no rush to finish our food and the place is kept very clean, with seating on ground and lower floor, where there's a customer lavatory. Our bus home stooped outside the Costa, so a perfect end to an evening out.
Very friendly stuff and beautiful coffee spot to just relax in the middle of London. At 12 pm it was pretty empty and coffee was delicious.

We had a great time here. Sometimes a coffee shop with seating in London isn't so easy to find so it was nice to be able to sit and enjoy a good breakfast. The staff was also nice and pleasant and they have a restroom.

Good coffee, food, and desserts.
Toilets for customers are available downstairs (with a code to open the door).

Nice place to stop for a quiet break. Service calm and efficient (possibly annoying if you are in a rush, but nice if you want a calm atmosphere). Food and drinks are reliably good, as are the hygiene systems. The washrooms need a code (so reserved for customers) but were not very clean when we visited (which I guess just depends on who has visited before you—I don’t think staff can be blamed).

The lady who served us didn’t seem very happy or helpful. When my friend asked where the toilet was she just pointed at the toilet. At which point my friend realised the door needed a code and went back to ask the lady who said it was on the receipt, which we weren’t even given the option of receiving after paying. The lady then begrudgingly printed of another code and this code didn’t even work. The atmosphere inside was great and the food was too, just a shame the staff didn’t seem interested at all.

This is not Costa standard. The coffee machine looks so dirty. 2 employees working in the whole shop. I ordered my drink for in. He gave me a take away cup, when I said I ordered in, he just poured my cappuccino in another glass in front of me. Taste is very bad.
Hidden away from main streets, it's a little less busy. Right opposite urban parking.  If you have a long drive probably 1st place to stop sit , relax and have a drink.
Your chai, oh my. Delicious. Quiet. Nice. Across from Rok It so you can sip and then run across the street and buy a vintage hat, then dart back to finish your drink. I did.

Having seen many Costa Coffees throughout the city of London I decided to give them a try. Of course I tried their Flat White, and it was pretty tasty. Unlike many of their London competitors it was a heartier flat white, by which I mean there was more volume to it instead of simply compacted into a small 4oz cup.

The location was fine, and the interior fairly clean. There is seating outside the shop which made for a nice spot to rest our legs after walking all morning. There's a lovely little park across the way.

I had been surprised with how fast each coffee shop in london had made my flat whites, bur this one took a bit longer. There was no line in front of me, and while it didn't take more than 5 minutes to get my drink it's worth noting that it wasn't as lightning fast as all the other spots.

This spot is well worth the visit to get your coffee fix.

cheers!!

Costa Coffee has always been my first choice wherever I go, and even though I only stop in for their hot chocolate, it never disappoints. Their hot chocolate is pure comfort in a cup—rich, creamy, and perfectly balanced in sweetness, making it an absolute treat every time.

What makes Costa stand out is the consistency. Whether I’m in a busy city branch or a cozy countryside café, I know I can count on their hot chocolate to warm me up and brighten my day. I love that they offer different variations, like adding whipped cream, marshmallows, or even seasonal flavors, which make it feel special no matter how many times I order it.

The atmosphere is another reason I keep coming back. The cozy seating, warm ambiance, and friendly staff make every visit enjoyable, even if it’s just for a quick stop. Whether I’m grabbing a cup on the go or taking a moment to relax, Costa always feels like the perfect choice.

Costa Coffee is always my go-to wherever I travel.

The shop is kind of small to stay in, not really a lot of tables. My friends and I decided to take our coffee to go.
We went here every single day of our trip for our breakfast.
Their chocolat and carmel iced coffee are super sweet and kinda taste like pastries.
Also, I tried the cinnamon roll that I highly recommend.
Smooth espresso and satisfying coffee. Delectable treats. Plenty of seats inside and out. Quick and friendly service.
A staff member decided it would be appropriate to sit down behind the counter and play TikTok reels out nice and loud for most of my entire visit. Some of the least professional behaviour I’ve seen.

Easy to order. Very nice staff. Lovely service.
Lots of table to enjoy your coffee and even work here.

Location and seating were… acceptable. But the real highlight was the customer service (if that’s what we’re calling it). I ordered one tea and was charged for two — apparently, a special two-for-one deal where you only get one. When I pointed it out, the barista decided to bless me with a quick math lesson: “2.99 + 2.99 = 5.98.” Clearly a man of many talents — barista by day, math tutor by the side.

Ignoring his charming attitude, I clarified that I ordered one tea, after which he took time  out to process a refund — no smile, no apology, of course. I sipped my hot water (they call it tea here) and when leaving, asked how long the refund might take since I don’t live in London. That’s when I received Lesson #2: “It will take 5-7 days, could be tomorrow could be after 5days.” It would have been good to stop there but of course there was more “The money will come to your card, not to me. You have the receipt.” Brilliant insight that I wouldn’t have known. God bless his kind soul, maths and banking all in one day. I think he smiled a tad bit here. Boy am I lucky!! 🥰

Maybe he was having a bad day. Or maybe this is his best day. Either way, if you enjoy condescending baristas, unwanted tutoring and the faint aroma of regret in their bathrooms, this is your spot. Otherwise, save yourself the trouble.

Date of visit: 2 Nov 2025, between 5:30–6:00 PM. Refund was processed on 4Nov.
I love this Costa! It's so cozy, and there's tons of space downstairs to chill.
Delicious drinks and coffee and fantastic pastries
Excellent place for lunch. I had a chicken burger and it was definitely 10/10. Fresh and very well cooked. Would definitely come back at some point. Thank you 🙏🏻

A busy Costa but it had plenty of seating freely available. It is only a short distance from a central line tube station. This is not really much different from every other Costa. Staff appeared friendly. Food was prepared well, and toasties served hot. Coffee tasted as expected. A good place to kill some time before a meeting.

Large coffee shop on the high street. Good location for a meeting over coffee. Although it's often very busy.
The coffee is acceptable, but not much better than that.
The place is still a good place to chat, the music is not to loud And the crowd is quiet.

Always a good coffee shop. Wifi on site. The tshirt uniforms are nice for the staff and an improvement on the uncomfortable looking brown buttoned up shirts. Usually a more urban atmosphere than other coffee chains.
Love the coconut milk options. Awesome when you can't I've dairy. Good amount of power ports if you want to work or study. Mostly down the back and near the window.
Just another Costa Coffe. The menu is usual. The day I was there the place was almost empty with lot of free seats and the staff was courteous, although not very fast and the espresso machine.

Bad service. Asked for Butter pecan latte for drinking at the cafe and this was made thrice with cream when mentioned not to use cream. For in cafe drinking they were pouring in take away cup, they had to be reminded that we have order sit in. The cup was changed three times as well. Also the cake I took was not complete. Do not recommend this costa.

Decent Costa Coffee branch.
Minute walk from Tottenham Court road.
Nicely decorated and spotless at 9am.
Self-serve machine added.
Not convinced it's a great idea for coffee shops. You really want to talk to barista about coffee choice. Too many clicks to say you want something extra hot without too much milk... Pass.

Nice balcony with extra seats.

People come to enjoy good coffee not to see unhappy disconnected staff who seem to be unbothered.
Coffee is ok just not that great and the staff make sure you don't like it because of the attitude.

Smiling is free.

Food:
Latte and cappuccino oats drinks were nice not out of the ordinary taste of coffee experience.
The vegan lemon muffin was good as gooey center was a nice surprise

Service:

We came close to closing hrs after a few min in announced 15 min to close the branch so we been served in TA cups to save on cleaning from staff end. And cups. Could've asked preference as we finished our drinks indoors .

Atmosphere:

Moderation of noise to quiet.

All in all
aside the cup choices issue.
Coffee very basic .
Came in time that not much food or snacks had to offer so will give it a second chance to see other food and coffee options

This Costa was lovely, and the drink I got too was delicious! At this location they had self order kiosks which I thought was cool, so you could customize your drink to however you like it, so this Costa was nice and I think made one of the best mochas that ive had yet, although homemade mochas are just as good!

Nice coffee and most importantly a toasted gluten free sandwich and vegan cake on sale. The friendly server brought our tray to the table when my toastie was ready. Thank you!
It is well positioned just off Oxford Street but is a bit clinical inside. And one of the two toilets was out of action which meant a long queue.

Very open feeling, clean and large. Not that busy when I came (6pm hour). Great range of bakery treats. Quick and friendly service from the Barista who served me. Drink was a bit too hot and needed to cool, and annoying that the part-enclosed area amongst the back was no longer open for customers some time in the 6pm hour (I just about finished, after prior notice). Toilet room should be cleaned better.

It's nice to be served with a smile, I greeted the young lady very nicely nothing back didn't even give me the receipt, and just said stand to the right your drink be ready. I can understand if it is busy but it wasn't, and secondly the coffee was bitter which means they do not clean their machine properly sorted out Costa Coffee your prices have gone up quite a bit and sadly the product has gone down, that also goes for your staff.
These sweet little messages and the great coffee really made my day

Today i visited this Costa coffee store which is not on my way to work but made my whole day.
Shout out to that constantly smiling  face that was so kind and patiently helped me with the app ,explained all those new options of drinks
It does definitely worth it visiting, you just gained a new loyal customer

A very small coffee shop and there’s only 1-2 people working at once so it takes very long for one order. One person taking the orders and one person making them. I don’t think this is the staff’s fault, but I’m seeing this more often. Please resolve this because it brings down the customer’s experience.

I happened to come across this place as the weather suddenly turned cold, and decided to stop by to warm myself.

To conclude, I was truly warmed—not only by the hot coffee and hearty meal, but also by the gracious hospitality of the lady who served me.

Thank you very much. I sincerely hope that your warm service will continue to bring joy to many more customers.
Great Costa, lots of seating and a great atmosphere. Staff were helpful and friendly and the shop was clear and tidy

Literally the worst Pain au Raisin I have ever experienced, much worse than the frozen and home bake variety. Someone in Costa thinks it's OK to offer something half way between a pastry and a sponge cake.  I dread to think what a French person would say. Just stodgy nonsense that doesn't match the label. Also, whoever decides how many raisins go into the recipe is a mean, soul-destroying accountant. Probably the same guy who took the nuts out of mixed nuts on British Airways and replaced them with pretzels made with toenail clippings to save half a penny per customer. I thought the pastry looked a bit sad behind the glass, and sadly now I know!! But this is sad, soul destroying Costa. You're better off somewhere else!
After a long day shopping in London ,glad to see a Costa away from the hustle and bustle. Tea. Pleasant staff. Seatings plenty. Quiet for London. Perfect. 4.5🌟

I believe Coca Cola should just cut their losses and sell Costa.  They are putting it on the same trajectory which is leading to Starbucks decline/oblivion - poorly designed locations - in terms of furniture and atmosphere.  Their stores are starting to look and feel like hospital cafeterias, and no one goes to those places for the atmosphere or coffee (usually another, more important reason).  Some finance person probably said it would stop people from taking a laptop and sitting in a cosy spot, which reduces store turnover.  BS.  Instead, what they're creating are cold, uninviting spots.  No fault to the staff at this location - their service was great.  What are you doing Costa?

I was there on Saturday 22nd Nov 2025 and I must say that guys who worked that day were extremely nice and friendly! Such a nice service.
I have lost my debit card there and I came back to get it after a few hours and the honest staff gave it back to me.  Very good honest employee's 👌
Great Costa. 2 servers busy shop super efficient and constantly tidying. Spotless shop. Well done

Costa coffee, Southampton branch has been really amazing in terms of providing services, food quality and overall management. I have been there multiple times and always gotten best service from them . So, I would highly suggest everyone to go there and experience best flavour of coffee with best service..
I don't find any inconvenience here like other reviewers. Still nice and clean. I LOVE COSTA🩶🩶🩶 hahah(oh just the wifi is way too slow)

Nice branch if Costa on Tottenham Court Road, branded slightly different to the usual ones which is nice as the old style is looking a bit dated now

Service was friendly, fast and efficient. Definitely a cut above a lot of other Costa stores on service and cleanliness

I popped in to get some soda and a I was served by a very polite gentleman, who checked for me if there is a cold one, but then I was offered a glass of ice for free! Thank you for the great service today!
Quirky & quaint little coffee shop. Nice staff. Interesting mock parquet flooring.

Until today after having walked into this branch of the Costa Coffee Franchise, I never new a Costa Fresco existed. Upon walking in you instantly notice the difference with this particular store rocking a single player/small group vibe. The abundance of electrical outlets and the generally young audience it attracts certainly gives the place a sense of fashion and character. Following a niche and young crowd, it does have its draw backs in terms of what the average individual between the ages of 18 and 30 would expect from an establishment of this style. The free WiFi, although provided by 02, failed to let me connect via my laptop. The authentication web page takes so long to load that providing your browser hasn't given up trying to connect, your hot beverage will by now be a cold. Other than the WiFi, the abundance of open plan seating and working desk space is a real point in making this place more attractive to the younger worker.

Moving on to the service and quality of the product Costa Fresco supplies; the service is good however the presentation of the product seems a little lack luster. On my visit, I ordered a single Grande Hot Chocolate for drinking in and it was presented to me with no plate. Following this the option of having the necessary marshmallows and cream was also not offered. The task of carrying my hot chocolate glass, serviette and elongated spoon wasn't an issue on this occasion, but I can imagine anyone with bags or otherwise hand-less, would have to make two trips.

The location is good being in eyesight from the exit of Goodge Street Station on the Northern Line. A single look to the left at the entrance of the station and the coffee shop will be sure to catch your eye with the iconic "COSTA" in the distance.

The interior design of the store has joined the group of the ever-populating "exposed ceiling and minimal lighting" scene. This presents and huge attraction value and certainly makes the place seem more friendly to its target audience.

In conclusion, the link in this chain of Costa Stores is an attractive addition and is certainly a role model for the "older style" counterparts to look up to. It's minimum service definitely needs work but if you're not a pompous, up-yourself character such as I, then I'm pretty sure you'll be able to get over it pretty swiftly.

A potential roll -out  'next generation' store concept, piloting new products, presentation and price points.
A food expansion program very much belabours the obvious + minor tweaks, twists and takes; all at reasonable prices.
Nothing here revolutionary but a 'leap forward' focus shift bound to stir-up the competition and other prat a mangers.

We ordered some drinks whilst killing some time to get our train back home.

The mango & passionfruit cooler was very nice and totally recommend.

We also had a mango bubble frappe which again was nice.

The costa shop was very clean and tidy.
A more wallet friendly coffee chain that is somewhat similar to Starbucks, still a good place to grab a quick breakfast if you have no other alternative.

Tasty coffee served in huge cups. Staff were super friendly. Outdoor seating area was clean and tidy.

The Shinano bun was tasty and reasonably priced. The staff are friendly and have a pleasant smile, which brightens my morning.

Good Costa Coffee, The staff is competent and friendly.

Beautiful seats place down stairs.

Tuna panini was americano.
Crunchy, malty and soft. Coffee was perfectly made. Sitting area is small
2nd time I have been here in two weeks and the second time they have not had tiramisu drinks but there was nothing shown that they were not available don't advertise it if you don't serve it

Fantastic little spot across from the awful hotel i was in brought a smile to my face when I walked in. Today I ordered a carrot cake with a flat white coffee. British carrot cake has a distinct flavor profile. It's usually light, moist and delicately flavored with cinnamon and ginger - and in this case, that's exactly what my cake was, thanks to Costa's bakery, which delivers fresh cakes to the cafe every day. The cake itself consists of grated carrots and finely chopped walnuts, which give it texture and a nutty character. Cream cheese frosting, often flavored with vanilla, provides a delightful contrast. Overall, it's the balance of sweetness and warm spices that make this a beloved classic. Furthermore, I would like to say that the Italian Costa Coffee was voted the best coffee shop in the UK and if you look at my photos, this must not surprise you.

In addition to the carrot cake, I also had a delicious Salted Caramel Brownie and Millionaire’s Shortbread at this cafe. I also had lunch there a few times last month, and ordered the Creamy Mac & Cheese, Wiltshire Ham & Mature Cheddar Toastie, Pole & Line Tuna Melt Panini and BLT Sandwich, which I also recommend to everyone.

If you would like to watch a video of me having carrot cake at the Costa coffee, please go to my YouTube channel:
https://youtu.be/3L__63mYM3s?feature=shared
