This is a very convenient Costa right at Whitechapel station. The staff are always friendly - service with a smile and a laugh - especially Mohammed. Come here often when in Whitechapel.
Really nice customer service, but I am not sure if this is the costa near underground station.
For Costa Fenchurch street and barking - Staff are always so friendly and personable. It’s nice that they remember my order - really a great job from all of them 😊
Really friendly and quick service from the staff. Only thing is they often run out of the veggie baps - it’d be great if you could make more of these so I could have your breakfast deal :)
Lovely man served us on a Saturday morning with a smile. Offered us a free bag for other items we were carrying from M&S. Double cupped our hot coffees. Costa is lucky to have him!

Very welcoming staff!
Prompt service
A pleasant experience indeed

It’s been a few times I come to this costa an it has caught my attention the lack of tidiness. They never use surface cleaner only wipe it out.
The caffe is alright in general.

This Costa Coffee is the perfect pit stop before catching a train! My order was ready before I even finished paying… talk about efficiency. The team worked together seamlessly, and I was genuinely gobsmacked by how quick and friendly they were. If you’re in a rush but still want a great coffee, this is the place to go.

Bad Review: Costa Coffee Experience

I visited Costa Coffee on 28 November, around 7:20 PM, and encountered a truly disappointing service experience.

The staff member serving was rude and unprofessional, chewing gum with her mouth open and clearly displaying an attitude.
When it was my turn at the till, she left the till to chat with another staff member without saying a word, leaving me waiting and confused.
I received my drink before completing the payment, which felt disorganized and inattentive.
After I paid, she completely disregarded me, offering no thanks or courtesy. I felt ignored and embarrassed.
It wasn't even busy, so the rudeness was perplexing and unnecessary.
I’ve been coming to Costa for many years and have always been greeted with kindness; this was a stark departure from my usual experiences..

As respectfully as possible, if you don’t know how to handle customers, you really shouldn’t be in customer service. I hope management takes note and trains staff to maintain basic courtesy and professionalism.

Food tastes good but it isn't filling. The tables always a mess and full of rubbish, lazy staff are busy chit chatting. The lady with the hijab never speaks clear enough. I come everyday. For the last couple of visits, the lazy staff have made me ruin my clothes- the cardboard wasn't properly closed and hot melted cheese runs down my leg- this happens when the guy does it (idk his name). I ordered a large hot chocolate with marshmallows and gingerbread syrup. The man told the hijab lady my order and she started getting a medium cup out. I reminded her that I ordered a large and she was going on about things that I can't understand. I repeated that I ordered a large and then it turned out she was making the people behind me drinks. Then I watched her make my drink and I honestly swear, I did not see the gingerbread syrup go in. I reminded her about my order and she was going on about random stuff that I was like, "Can you just put in the sryup?" "Blah blah blah you need to order it." I did order it but then I sniffed my drink, smelled normal. I tried it and there was hardly any gingerbread flavour. I find when I go to other branches, Its relaxing but ordering at this place is stressful. The man almost forgot about my overpriced tiffin and he needed a reminder.
Wonderful staff, always happy to answer any enquiries and meet customers' expectations.  😊

Been here after visiting tower Bridge, the views are amazing if you get the right seat.

Our order was as expected, it was priced appropriately given that is in London.

Will visit again when in London.

I went there for a coffee and they were not polite. They gave me two coffees with a different size and they were cleaning and they did not allow us to go upstairs. It was full. And we did not have any room to be comfortable. I don't reccomend this place. There are some other coffee shops close by.

I ordered a hot chocolate, but it was very weak. The chocolate flavor was barely noticeable; it tasted more like chocolate-flavored milk. However, the location was fantastic! It was right at the bottom of the Tower Bridge. Enjoying a hot chocolate while admiring the view was a real treat!!

It was quick to get in and out. I had finished exploring the Tower of London and was on my way to Tower Bridge. It was a rainy day, and I was looking for some coffee.

Ordered coffee and hot chocolate- both beverages were good (unlike here in the USA, where you add cocoa powder to hot water and name it hot chocolate)

There are a couple of high iron tables and an outside sitting arrangement. I did not check the first floor, but I saw a few customers going upstairs.
Good view, nice outside seating area. Sometimes can be a bit slow and staff seemed a bit down but maybe that's just because it was near the end of shift

The machine wouldn't scan my qr code to collect my free drink reward but the staff were quick to help me order at the till. Friendly team, nice atmosphere, quick to complete order & a tasty drink! Very nice start to my day!
Went in busy Tuesday morning, service was exceptional. Thank you to Maddy for making great coffee and being enthusiastic in the morning when other people are rushing to work and looking grumpy!

I love Costa Coffee and love to take a break in Costa especially here. They have wonderful staff and best manager I have ever seen. He makes best drinks and always welcome his customers with big smile.
Staffs are so sluggish working in such a fast-paced environment. I’ve missed my train twice just waiting to get a hot chocolate. Avoid if you can

Good enough but the staff were not been trained properly i had been served many time by store manager and he’s a nice guy but a short lady made my drink with only jce cubes , i’ve been charged 4.50£ for only ice cubes literally had only bit of syrup and 10x amount of water not nice at all.

Cila is a fantastic member of the barista team. I came in to have some food and drink after a long day, and was making some mistakes with my order. She was incredibly patient and helped me a lot to fix my order. Thank you for a great experience, Ill definitely be coming back to this place whenever I am going through the area!

Bruni and Corina are making really nice experience! Very rare to feel in chain like Costa such an amazing feeling that somebody take care of you - small details like asking for for water at the hand off, or double check that you are waiting for double espresso and starting conversation 🙃

Well-known café famous for its fast service and delicious coffee. making it a great spot for relaxing or working in a comfortable atmosphere. The friendly staff adds to the experience, ensuring that every visit leaves a lasting impression.

Great Costa coffee shop conveniently located in the pedestrianised Peninsula Square, all that you would expect from a Costa, seating inside and outside. Close to the O2, North Greenwich, Jubilee line station and the Thames cable car.

A really quiet place, where you can enjoy a nice cup of coffee, and have a nice conversation or do your work. I had some projects to finish, and the spot was amazing. Good WiFi connection and loads of outlets options to charge my laptop.
Staff friendly and professional
Nice small as having easy breakfast before day out as use gift card for amazing Christmas present.
The service in this Costa is always speedy; nice comfy seats and tables that turnover quickly. Good place to pop into for a quick coffee and relax.
On the lower ground floor within Cabot Square.  There are a few seats here; most of the business is take away. Service was efficient and polite and the premises are clean.  There are toilets nearby.
Pretty generic windowless Costa. The drink and food were fine but the staff on a Saturday afternoon/evening was pretty surly/miserable.
Busy but a nice place for a break, my favourites are the hot chocolate and mango/passion fruit cooler, it's normally difficult to get somewhere to sit

Ordered a white chocolate whipped coffee and was handed a cup of frothed milk with white chocolate sauce, there was no coffee whatsoever. Really disappointing as that day I had slept for less than 5 hours after a hectic Wembley concert and I was heading to my 8 hour shift. I would have complained in store if I hadn’t already tapped in for the tube - proves how exhausted and rushed I was for which I really needed some caffeine

I placed my order online from uber eats at 15:50 . (27 October )
Im going to leave photos so you see that I received only 1 muffin instead of 2
Very disappointed because I paid for delivery. And it was for me and my child . I think the stuff are going to eat the one muffin which I paid for.
Im not even able to call the  number provided, as they are not picking up . Not professional at all . Never gonna order again 😡😡😡😡
The customer service today was really above and beyond compared the the other Costa coffee shops around. I got the Blueberry muffin which was also really fresh and tasty!

Don’t think I’ve ever known such amazing customer service - Kasia greets everyone with the biggest smile and a lovely morning chat. The amount of regulars who barely need to open their mouth before she asks if they want their usual blew me away! How she remembers everything and brightens everyone’s day is so wonderful x

I don't need to talk about items in Costa Coffee, everybody will be well versed in what they provide.
I live on London city island aha whenever I need a hot drink or a pastry I come here.
This place is located in Canning town station so it's extremely convenient for commuters.
I have to give a shout-out to the staff, they are all so lovely, they always have a smile on their face and are so easy to talk to 😊

It is very rare for me not to give a five-star rating in a review, but unfortunately at this Costa coffee I experienced very poor service. When you walk up to the counter you at least expect a hello or what can I get you? Not just a blank face? The cafe has modern decor and a nice outdoor seating area. The coffee was of a good standard and so was my pastry.

Ordered on just eat. Order confirmed at 19.09 am and it now 12.19pm and they’re still ‘preparing the order’ so sorry, can’t say how the food was as apparently it will take another 25 minutes. Looked to cancel but advised I’ll be charged if I do as they are preparing it! Been preparing it for over 2 hours now but there you go. Hopefully it will arrive and the drinks will not be over 2 hours old. And I thought Costa at lambaste square was bad!
Cup of cappuccino was very large and tasted good! Good choice of sandwiches and cake. Lovely staff!

I had a very disappointing experience at this Costa Coffee. As a single parent with a young child, I expected at least basic courtesy but the staff were rude, unhelpful, and didn’t even offer a smile. My little one was crying, and instead of showing any understanding, they kept calling out my order without offering assistance or bringing it over.

The tables were dirty, and I struggled to manage both my child and the pram while trying to collect my order. I couldn’t leave the pram outside, so I had to carry everything myself. No one offered help, and the atmosphere felt completely unwelcoming.

To make matters worse, when I asked for a small juice at the end, the staff member she snapped, “Grab it yourself,” with an angry expression. It was unnecessarily rude and dismissive a simple act of kindness would have made a huge difference 3 rude girls are working there.

I hope Costa takes this seriously and addresses staff training empathy and cleanliness should be basic standards.

Ordered a large latte that was given to someone that ordered a large latte with extra chocolate. The staff member who made the coffee correctly told the other staff member that it’s a standard large latte but the other staff member didn’t care and presented it as large latte with extra chocolate and proceeded to throw away several orders as being server which led to me waiting another 10 minutes until I had to ask where is my coffee at which point the manager behind realised what had happened and asked staff to make my coffee. The coffee itself tested like pure milk, no coffee taste whatsoever

This Costa outlet is just inside the western entrance to the Excel Centre.  Despite there being 6 staff, a row of self service ordering machines, and three or four coffee machines, it seemed to take longer to be served than in other Costas without all these things (and it wasn't particularly busy at the time I visited).  You can order from the baristas or at one of the machines (I did not see anyone doing that) and you get a numbered receipt to pick up your order at the other side.  I hope that they don't roll out this workflow model across their network, though I appreciate that it might be necessary when it's very busy.

Despite being served by a positive upbeat member of staff, me and my wife were told 9 minutes before closing that the seating area was no longer open even though we were mid-coffee and not due to take much longer. Being ushered out pretty much ruined our experience at this Costa between gate N7 and N8. I’d suggest trying the other one first before going to this one. Could’ve easily have been a 4-5 star experience had we not been rushed away before closing time.
It was such a busy day in Excel London. We had our breakfast there, despite the crowd, the staff were so kind, quick and friendly, the food was fresh. Thank you!

Considering how many people they have to serve during conventions, it always amazes me at how efficient their service is.  Costa isn't my favourite coffee but it's alright and certainly hits the spot when you forgot to get a coffee when walking past the Starbucks on the outside.  It's handy that this is inside the convention centre.

Absolute terrible service. Ordered 2 hot drinks, 1 savory and 1 sweet item on tablet.
4 staff stood around talking. Place wasn't busy at all. Seemed to take a while to do drinks. Once my drinks were passed to me, staff continue to yap while I was waiting for my food items. I was totally ignored, I had to say where's my food. After checking my paper receipt slowly as you like, get my food. Pass it over with no apology and with what looked like major effort and seemed annoyed that I asked.
No Terry's choc orange piece on muffin I ordered. Or any whole milk in the Americano. Didn't bother going back to complain as It wasn't worth the hassle.
Out of all the Costas I visit (and it's quite a few) this here by far is the worst for staff attitude and laziness. One even stood there flapping her hands around inside her apron, leaning on the counter constantly talking! A big 0 of of 5 for Customer service. Interaction and Professionalism.
Won't be visiting again.

The place smells!

I was looking for a comfy place to study, cause I saw the photos that they have sofa but the atmosphere was so bad, noisy, smell like toilet everywhere.

Not recommended to seat, just grab your coffee and go.

Also, in front of the shop there are alway these people leaning into the window and smoking, and the smell get in the shop. just avoid seating in.

Called into here on Saturday morning for breakfast, if I am totally honest this was the first and will be the last visit to this particular Costa. We were greeted upon everything with a straight faced female barista, who said 'do you have cash' we thought it was a strange question upon wanting to order, she followed with 'the card machine is broken'. Not being locals to the area, we asked her if there was a cash machine nearby, she replied 'No'. We walked and managed to get cash, we returned giving the place the benefit of the doubt, and wish we hadn't bothered. We ordered a breakfast wrap and toast with butter and marmalade, this is the first ever Costa that I have been given cheap margarine with the toast (usually it's Lakeland butter), the toast arrived only toasted on one side, with cheap margarine and no marmalade, I asked for the marmalade I had paid for to another female barista working there and she replied 'she's gone, I don't know what you ordered' despite my receipt being there on the tray, however she proceeded to go and get the marmalade, which they didn't have so she offered me jam instead. My husband's wrap wasn't heated through thoroughly either. The coffee was usual Costa quality coffee, which indeed was nice. Overall the attitude is the staff, and the poor food, gives this the rating it has. Such a shame as Costa is generally one of my 'go to' establishments.

On Monday, October 27th, around 4:00 PM, there were two blonde women (possibly of Eastern European origin?) who were very unfriendly. I am a tourist, and they made fun of me because I don’t speak English perfectly. Very unprofessional behavior!
Enjoyed one  of their  seasonal autumnal  drink with maple syrup and roasted hazelnut in latte  served with whipped cream  with gingerbread loaf cake  absolutely  delicious and recommend
Brilliant atmosphere especially when you want  to have your coffee in peace before you start work . Very friendly staff and polite especially Lina and Carla

This is the  bigger branch of Costa in Willesden Green. There is plenty of seating and also free wifi and hand sanitizer. Baristas are friendly and coffee is good. Can’t complain, it’s a costa on the high street.

Drinks are now almost £5 but the quality is definitely missing I actually have only drank half the cup before throwing it down the sink

I will be trying the other cafes around

The girl  behind the counter called genali was rude . Like very rude I have my own restaurant and I know how important is customer service.
Not again
Clean and smart little Costa, plenty of seating inside. No complaints.
I love coming here. Its easy to get a coffee but it's popular so don't try to find a seat during peak times. My favourite barista Denisa is the best.

The location is great and good size shop, most of the time clean and quick service, depends on the person serving. But generally, most of people working there are kind and friendly. The key point the furniture especially the chairs in and out are very uncomfortable, they need to change them. I think same chairs for the last couple of years. Otherwise, this is a good Costa.

I visited the Costa Coffee branch in Wembley on a quiet Sunday morning before the outlet crowds arrived, and with no event taking place at the nearby stadium. The café was clean and peaceful, making it easy to find a table and enjoy our coffees.

The service was good, with friendly staff members who were attentive to our needs. The drinks and food offerings were consistent with what you'd expect from a typical Costa branch – if you're a fan of their menu, you won't be disappointed.

There is one toilet, which is likely an issue on busy days.

Overall, a decent Costa experience.

**Great Coffee Spot Near Wembley Stadium!**

I stopped by Costa Coffee on Olympic Way, and it was the perfect pick-me-up. The coffee was delicious—smooth and just the right strength. The staff were friendly and efficient, even with the matchday crowd.

The location is super convenient, right next to the stadium, and there’s plenty of seating inside. Clean, modern, and a great atmosphere. Highly recommend if you’re in the area for an event or just passing by!

4/5 – Will definitely be back!
Came in here 24th November after doing the Wembley stadium tour with my Grandpa, drink prices were meh but the hot chocolates are lovely!

Typical Costa in a high traffic area when events are on. Would normally be a 3 or 4 star. However, when staff place the rubbish bin at your table like it's a chair so everyone walks up to you and throws rubbish in the bin, it's not so great.

My visit to Costa Coffee was an excellent experience. The café had a warm and inviting atmosphere, perfect for both relaxing and working. The staff were professional, attentive, and made me feel genuinely welcome. The coffee was rich, smooth, and full of flavor — definitely a standout compared to many other coffee shops. The food was fresh, well-prepared, and complemented the drinks perfectly. Overall, Costa Coffee delivered a high-quality experience that I would highly recommend to anyone looking for great coffee and a comfortable environment

I had a pleasant experience at Costa Coffee. The atmosphere was cozy and comfortable, making it a nice spot to relax. The staff were friendly and helpful, and my order was served quickly. The coffee was smooth and flavorful, and the snacks were fresh. Overall, it was a good place to enjoy a coffee break

I love a Costa, but wow is the dark-haired girl who was working there Thursday morning (14th August) incapable of simple things to do with the job and will end up overcharging you.

I swiped my ‘beans’ from the Costa app in front of her..ALL I wanted was another shot of coffee as I was travelling far to Great Ormond Street Hospital that day…and she kept telling me to do it ‘again’ and ‘again’. From the unfriendly look on her face and manner I could tell she was in a mood..but I’m sorry we all work and have our stresses, handle yourself. Especially when you’re old enough (she looked about late 20s).

I ended up swapping 8 of my ‘beans’ as opposed to only 2 before I could get my free shot of coffee (a double was not only owed but needed after that ordeal) and she STILL tried charging me full price till I pointed out she hadn’t applied the discount! It really shouldn’t be that hard, if there weren’t 3 people waiting behind me I would have kicked up a bit of a fuss..but we all need our caffeine fix at the end of the day and it was only about £2 wasted, she really wasn’t worth it. What’s worse is the dirty look she gave me after I corrected her politely on the price (although my manners were wasted).

I’d rather wake up earlier and go to a Starbucks, worst customer service I’ve ever experienced in the 15+ years I’ve been going Costa. I’ve done a lot of customer faced roles and even when I was 18 I didn’t have such attitude and incapability.

Honestly I’d go elsewhere on a Thursday morning or you’ll end up being overcharged, I can’t speak for the rest of the staff as it was just her I had bad experience with.

My day always starts with a cappuccino from this store, and it’s always spot on! The ladies here do a fantastic job welcoming customers every morning with warmth and big smiles. It truly makes a difference and sets a positive tone for the day.

Well done to everyone—please keep up the great work and continue creating such a welcoming atmosphere!

i use this branch very often all staff nice and making good coffee and good customer service
well done wembley park team
Busy with not enough staff. Service and food OK, but not much space to sit, no Costa wi-fi available, toilet was locked and out of order, and beans did not get added to my Costa card.

⭐ 1 star – Service: I visited Costa Harlesden during heavy cold rain with my client and his mental health support dog, seeking shelter and a drink. Staff initially let us in, but the manager refused service, citing a “council policy” that does not exist and hygiene concerns, despite Costa’s website stating dogs are allowed. The dog is calm, gentle, and well-behaved. I felt humiliated and distressed being turned away in the rain. I am a loyal customer with a loyalty card, and this incident broke my heart. I later returned to buy a coffee for my client when the dog stayed outside. I now doubt if I would even think to come back again and have to question whether Costa as a brand lives up to the values I stand for - basic human dignity and caring for animals.

Re: Costa Coffee (Owner) — Thank you for your handling of this matter. I would like to accept your apology and have replied to your email with a few clarifications. I would be grateful if you could respond accordingly, after which I will revisit my comment and adjust the star rating as appropriate.

Good food, albeit a bit pricey. You
Simply must try the maple hazel latte.

However.
The the thing that made this experience deeply unpleasant is the bathroom situation.

One person took up the one and only
bathroom for ten minutes, and I have a bladder and bowel condition which I have a RADAR key for. When I knocked on the door the man said five more minutes.

Staff wouldn’t do anything and Im left feeling as if my bladder is about to give way and I do not wish to have an accident in public right before a big meeting.

The tables could also do with a deeper clean.

EDIT: I ended up leaving because I was seconds away from having an accident and dashed into the pub next door

Will not go here again.

Been in this Costa a few times as work takes me to area, always seem to have some one with metal health issues in here bothering customers. Food and drink very expensive, got a medium capppuchino yesterday andi charged £4.10
Absolutely disgusting customer service from two members. They will close the store one and half hours early than the usual time. Avoid this place!

Excellent Costa Coffee, very clean environment, the staff are very kind and welcoming. Mone the Barista is very friendly, always smiling, and competent.

Classic Costa cappuccino and a variety of croissants.

Soft music, located in Neasden where Bob Marley and The Wailers lived in 1972. The tribute mural, right in front of Costa, was painted by MR Cenz, an internationally renowned graffiti artist.

This Costa is situated between Brent Cross (where the M1 ends) and the Wembley Ikea.

Highly recommended.

Free Wi-Fi.

Very clean toilets, including one dedicated to people with disabilities and wheelchair users.

I have order on line and everything was mess,so soggy and 1/3 cup of mocha,they use a bakery bag instead Costa coffee standard bag,the drinks not been close properly and the panini been put on the pastry bag,is not a driver fault but is from the people's they not pay attention.

Pleasant experience! A nice and quiet place to have your coffee. Plenty of options to choose from, its a regular costa coffee shop. Feels like home and also you can collect points!

5* very nice!!

Unfortunately, my experience was very disappointing, especially with the staff member Sana. I ordered a drink, and when I came back to let them know it didn’t taste right at all, she responded with complete indifference, saying: "That’s the recipe we use," without even offering an apology or suggesting a replacement or any kind of solution.

The issue wasn’t just the drink itself but more about the attitude and the way I was treated. Costa is known for good service and caring about customer experience, but what I encountered was the complete opposite. I felt like I was dealing with someone who couldn’t care less about customer satisfaction or quality.

I hope the management takes note of this kind of behaviour (SANA) because what keeps customers coming back isn’t just the product — it’s also the respectful and professional service.

This Costa is an excellent place to work with your laptop. Great staff members, who are always polite and well trained at delivering your order on time. I would like to thank Mona, Sana, Elena, Mary, Suela and Carolina.

Costa Coffee at London Designer Outlet provides a familiar selection of drinks and snacks, making it a convenient stop for shoppers in need of a break. The atmosphere is comfortable, and the quality of beverages meets expectations.

However, customer service falls short, with noticeable delays and a lack of attentiveness from staff. Improving service efficiency and engagement would enhance the overall experience, ensuring that it matches the brand’s usual standards.

Absolutely loved my dine-in experience at Costa on Monday afternoon The shop was spotless and so well kept, which made the whole visit feel even more enjoyable. I was served by a lovely Asian lady with long dark hair with a tattoo on her wrist and neck ,she was honestly a breath of fresh air—so warm, friendly, and genuinely attentive. She really made me feel welcome. The coffee was top quality as always, but the amazing service and great atmosphere made this visit stand out. Highly recommend!

I would like to comment how the staff behave while at work. They are very noisy, not focus, their friends sat neat the counter, talking so loud like they are in their house. They focus on their friends, talking, laughing and worst giving unlimited drinks for free. No boundaries and some of their friends standing in front the counter takling to staff and laughing. This staff needs training for customer service. Poor Costa , if you are having this kind of staff , your company end up bankruptcy

The lighting is absolutely VILE. Go if you want to feel like you are sitting in a laboratory and have a totally agitating visit.
O and tables filthy, clearly not enough staff.
Costa need to up their game and make customers actually want to actually visit  their stores, completely uninviting.

I’d like to express my sincere appreciation to the team at Costa Coffee, especially during the morning hours. Every visit starts with a warm smile and professional service. The staff are friendly, attentive, and always maintain a high standard of hospitality.

The coffee is consistently fresh and delicious, and the atmosphere is perfect for starting the day right. A big thank you to the morning team for their great attitude and outstanding service you truly make a difference. Keep up the excellent work

For me to pay for a caramel latte with double portion  paying extra not even getting what I ordered as they gave me just a regular caramel latte and asking for a straw just to be told "we only offer straws for cold drink " just ridiculous . One straw! Terrible . Asking for a straw is just too much.

the staff are absolutely what makes me want to come here! I have been served by a staff member I did not catch his name but he had round glasses, he was so friendly and cute plus the drink was delicious! overall the staff in this costa are so nice and good coffee!

Two female staff member there were very rude. When my coffee order was done the one female barista with circular glasses moved my drink away from me when I went to retrieve it and laughed in my face. Overall very rude and inconsiderate

Please teach your staff how to make a proper latte 🙏. Its should not be a flat hot milk poured in to the cup with espresso. Training in customer service and dealing with complaints would also help. The girl who made my coffee today had no clue what she was doing and when I asked why my latte had no froth, she responded that this is what latte looks like and if I wanted more froth I should have asked her earlier🤦🏻‍♀️

Arash is the name of the barista that was very rude and couldn’t care less about customer service. I complained to the manager and he was worst. I asked for his name , he wound not give it to me. I know he is a Romanian guy, in fact they both are. It feels like it’s their private place and they chose who gets a coffee and service and who doesn’t. The manager took my coffee and started drinking it in front of me. I was left with no coffee. Costa Coffee you have stooped so low hiring people like Arash and his fellow countryman manager. I will take it to head office!!!

This is my favourite Costa Coffee in London! The vibe is fantastic, and the downstairs office-style space is perfect if you want to sit with your laptop and enjoy a great coffee in peace.

A big shout-out to the manager, Aggelos! He’s incredible. Always cheerful, genuinely funny, and he makes every visit a great experience.

His energy really sets this café apart.
I highly recommend this café to anyone looking for a welcoming place to work or relax with a quality Costa coffee ☕️

One of the cleanest cafe’ I’ve ever seen in London. They keep everything so so so clean and tidy.
Also outside tables were with nothing on top.
The store manager served us, Angelos (the name on the tshirt tag), super gentle and friendly. Also his staff; you can see there’s a nice atmosphere.
Espresso and macchiato are both good
Great service, convenient location next to West Hampstead overground and underground. Loads of seating inside and choices of snacks, sandwiches etc. lovely decor and great vegan hot chocolate!

This place is designed for a quick coffee takeaway and delivers on that fantastically. I have never had to queue up for long and the order is ready very quickly. There is an indoor seating area available that's split into 2 levels with plenty of seating. Also, I like the coffee served here compared to the next door shop :)
One thing they really need to change is the TV screen menu which keeps flipping so much that it takes literally minutes of staring at screens to get back to the desired view to figure out what's new in the menu. Please have some form of fixed menu to help the customers.

There are 2 floors in this Costa, the seatings indoor are planty. Everything seems to be newly refurbished so it's still very comfortable and new! Probably one of the most beautiful Costa I have ever been. I didn't get any bad customer experience from the staff but the only time I'm with them is when I ordered, so don't have much to say about it.

Great customer
Friendly environment
Welcoming atmosphere
Amazing staff
Highly recommended store
Thanks to Nayana

I was greeted and serviced by a very lovely and friendly lady!
She prepared the coffee well and the Chai Latte extra hot and we enjoyed it our beverages a lot!

Thank you very much

A random lady sitting across the room came and threw my coffee in my face and on my laptop. She was threatening me and trying to start a fight. When I asked the staff to call the police they didn't want to as she was a regular customer and they knew she had a habit of attacking people and didn't want to provoke her. Apparently that's the kind of establishment who don't really care to what happens to their customers and have no concept of security. The would rather keep things quiet than actually deal with this kind of issues. Pitiful and a shame. Maybe if the lady decides to attack the staff they will call the police then right?

Genuinely the kindest staff I’ve ever met, no matter how busy they get they still welcome with a smile and make amazing drinks and food. I come here every week or couple of weeks and I’m already remembered by them and it’s always a joy :)

AWFUL, lured me with temptation of sticky latte & whipped cream & powder & stars on the top served in a clear glass. The glasses as just for display. What I got was latte in a cheap paper cup as whipped cream is finished but told me only after they served. THIS IS THE LAST TIME A VISIT ANY COSTA BRANCH. Unfortunately the only way to get a good service in London is to go to an upscale cafe and pay more even though £4.50 for a HORRIBLE COFFEE is already expensive. To make things worse there are families with kids crying screaming the hell out of them and the area near the toilet is riddled with flies.

Bad attitude and slow / poor service.
They had plenty of staff and there was no queue buying coffee but somehow mine took 15 mins. When I did. Get it it wasn’t even very hot so I had
To Send it back.
The staff dynamiques seemed very off. Nobody seeemd happy.
But at least the toilets downstairs were clean
They make a great Flat White. A pleasant Cafe in grungy Camden.
Yummy and quick. Chai latte! Great Olivia Rodrigo music!!!!!!!

Me and my friend reached at 7:44 pm the store closing timing is 8:00 pm and crew members said it closed and they cleaned everything. After checking timing I went and asked for coffee after some discussion she maid coffee but it is just like water and very cold.

Highly professional response from Chalk Farm Costa store manager Deeph, always a pleasure to have a coffee when he's in store, polite and friendly manners always, and not to forget he also makes the best coffee ever
Thank you Deeph

I was deeply shocked by the state of the food served at this Costa Coffee branch. My niece bought a cinnamon roll here yesterday morning, and after taking a few bites we discovered it had visible mould on the outside and furry growth inside the pastry. The photos speak for themselves.

This is completely unacceptable and raises serious concerns about how food is stored and checked before being given to customers. I expect far better from a national chain.

I’ve reported the incident to the relevant food safety authorities and hope Costa takes immediate action to ensure this doesn’t happen to anyone else.

Avoid until the hygiene and food handling standards are properly addressed.

Unfortunately this Costa Coffee gets the basics wrong. The lack of milk alternatives (only soy milk available) for starters. No filtered coffee. Out of order bathroom and run down furniture. Hard working staff with limited communication and language skills. The only thing going for this location is the ample basement seating, the power plugs and the outdoor seating.

We went in Saturday for a late lunch, lots of seats upstairs and down which was a nice surprise as sometimes it can be hard to find somewhere to sit.

I ordered a tea and a bacon roll which the barista toasted for me. I can't eat butter because of the fat and was happy to see their rolls are not buttered so was perfect for me.

Staff were nice, place was clean and tidy. A nice visit overall to take the weight off my feet.

So i went downstairs. The shop was open until 18.00. It was 17.00. The lady came down, kicked us out saying they are closing in 5 minutes. There were a few people downstairs but no seats upstairs. Totally bad service.
Expensive coffee. But it was clean and girl that was serving was very nice and kind.

We do love this place. Very dog friendly. Servers - hit or miss - sometimes smiling and welcoming and sometimes blank face without any affect - we went there 4-5 times a week in summer. Guess depends how their day is going. Plenty of sitting spaces. Decent coffee
Terrace outside is not big but is good enough for enjoying the espresso and smoking.Elena always smile and very friendly

I got to say this is by far the best run costa in London the staff are amazing and the manager is a legend. Always polite always talkative and actually takes time to ensure your order is exactly how you want it. Got to say going to another Costa after the Finchley Road branch is always a bit of a let down tbh
A clean and spacious Costa outlet with efficient and friendly staff.

At 1 minute to3pm I ordered a coffee and Bakewell tart , I am a regular customer in this branch. I get serverd my tart and wait for my coffee to be made while waiting at 3pm the staff put a 50% label on the tarts I question this and she said as I ordered before 3 she could not do anything ,she could have said wait 1 min . Last time I spend any money there again on average you just lost a loyal customer who spends £20 a week with you.

I had a great experience at Costa Coffee thanks to Abir, the manager. He was friendly, professional, and made sure everything ran smoothly. The store was clean, the service was fast, and the staff were very polite. You can see that Abir really cares about his customers and team. I’ll definitely come back again. Great job, Abir!
Excellent. The store ambiance was too good, staffs were also so friendly. Specially the coffee was nice.

The Costa Coffee Shop has got big windows allowing us to enjoy our coffee watching passes by on the Regent's Canal.
We thoroughly enjoyed the atmosphere!

“I had a fantastic experience at Camden Costa. The staff were welcoming, attentive, and made me feel very comfortable throughout my visit.
Specially Abir the store manager he know what to do and how to manage the store well.

Polite and friendly staff, a bit slow 🐌  serving and toilet 🚻 needs more attention !!!
Nice view to regent's canal, outside tables ...
More seats are available in the  lower level or basement

The staff were smiling and serving well. As a regular customer at this store, I came to give a positive review. Unfortunately, some negative comments from others may give a misleading impression, but I hope my feedback reflects the true image of this store and supports the staff.

I’ve also noticed that staff are often trying to move dodge people out of the store. On occasion, well-mannered individuals have asked to use the toilet without ordering, only to end up shouting at staff for no apparent reason. It should be understood that the staff are simply following instructions given to them, and this process has been set by someone else.

Very disappointed with this Costa Coffee location. They refuse access to the toilets unless you’re a paying customer, which is incredibly unwelcoming — especially for a brand that promotes itself as friendly and community-oriented. To make matters worse, the staff were rude when I asked politely. Definitely not the kind of atmosphere you’d expect from a coffee shop. Avoid!

The barista here, called Sanchin, was so nice to me. I spilled my hot chocolate everywhere by accident and he made me another hot chocolate for free and cleaned it all up and was very nice about it. Thank you!

These men were absolutely horrible!  Customers had  asked for tissue, as there were none in the bathroom. The staff  had  caused a whole scene by being verbally abusive to the customers even after the customers had purchased an item in order to be granted the code for the toilets. Do something about these staff members as they were also racially profiling the BLACK customers.

So my sister started a time of the month and they wouldn’t let her in and it wasn’t even 730. It was 7:25 And the guy was just so rude and was pointing his finger Saying “no” the looks to me and my sister was so rude talk about customer service a load of bs honestly. I just know your parents would be ashamed with this behaviour that you would let a young girl go to the toilet in time of need.

The place looks absolutely peaceful and unique, but the staff were surprisingly ignorant and rude. I ordered a medium oat latte, but the lady made a small one instead. I politely explained that I had ordered a medium, thinking she might have been making the small for someone else. Instead of remaking it properly, she just poured the small drink into a medium cup and topped it up with oat milk. She then tried to tell me that the size doesn’t matter because the ingredients are the same which honestly shocked me. Very disappointing service for such a lovely-looking place.

Me and my friends will go there just if we want to chill or do something e.g school work or as artists draw and the coffee and food or desserts are lovely and it’s a overall chill and calm place, even better as dogs are allowed!

I'm glad they're aware of eco issues. I bought coffee with my reusable container and got 0.25 pound deduction on the price.
Great atmosphere and friendly staff although the free WiFi here doesn't work during my stay.

Headed to Costa in Cali for a quick coffee for me and my co-worker and they never disappoint! My friend & I had a caramel latte and it was superb 👌👌👌. Eva & Kritika the barista's were so friendly and helpful too! Much appreciated!
Nice Costa, but VERY RUDE Bangladeshi staff. They pretend not to hear you when ordering. Not professional or welcoming at all. Other staff are good. Really need to hire and train staff properly.

I've been a customer since they opened. I live in the same road and it's very convenient for me plus the coffee is very good specially the flat white. The staff has always a nice word for you and always welcome you, they keep the place  with a good atmosphere.
As a regular customer I would like to update my review: saying that it isn't  easy to please everyone
in every aspect possible (this would be perfection),
the staff in this shop has been  friendly and hardworking. I come here everyday for a nice and tasty coffee.

A good spot for morning coffee. The cafe is fairly quiet but with some chatter. Lots of seating upstairs and downstairs with ambient music playing in the background.
Service was really good. No queue and drink made very promptly. I would visit again if in the area.

Walked in and came to the counter, the kiosk assistant saw me and continued talking to her colleague. Eventually she did approach me and I clearly stated "oat chai latte" twice. It was made with dairy milk, i left had a sip immediately knowing it wasn't oat and came back stating that it wasn't oat. No apology at all. It was made again but with no "sorry"

Will not be coming again. It was also basically empty so it wasn't even like it was busy.

It’s clean & good place to enjoy your coffee. The staff are polite & they give you the order quickly. The best thing is that they are smiling, amazing customer service, good outdoor seating & they always say nice words to customers. The place is too close to Caledoniand Rd & Barnsbury station.

There food is amazing. I had ordered there before, and the Toastie tuna cheese is nice, hot and warm, and their slice of blooming chocolate cake is soft and fresh and tasty to eat. There caramel latte is amazing hot and warm, Just what you need in the morning when you ahead off to work. would recommend anyone.

There were several cafes in the area, but I chose Costa since I thought it would be safer to get a good coffee.  I am quite picky about my coffee.  I prefer them made at the right temperature, flavour, and colour.  I must say that was the worst coffee I've ever tasted at a huge coffee chain.  Rather disappointed.  It is no surprise that it was quieter and less crowded than other Costas.  I won't be going to another Costa.

I ordered my flat white, nobody told me that you have only one size of take away cup.
So they charged £4.20 for that and it looked like an XL latte.
I wanted to have my coffee inside but they didn’t bother themselves to asked me if I wanted another cup or something. So I threw my coffee and I went to Pret A Manger. I’m sorry because never I used to do this but I’m working in hospitality and I don’t understand how it’s possible this kind of service ans support for this brand.

I’ve been twice in the last month and the tables were very clean (I read some of the previous reviews but they seem to be older). The members of staff at the till and the barista were very sweet. I haven’t visited the restroom so I can’t comment on that. The sitting inside is a bit tight but there are seats outside as well (for when the weather is nice). If you’re on a wheelchair or have a buggy and you want to sit in, I’d recommend a nearby cafe or to get your coffee on the go.

It was the first “cafeteria” I visited in London. Workers are very nice (even if you do not speak English, they are very nice to help you understand) and it is a good place to have breakfast or to have something quickly. Its location is comfortable too.

Edit: I came back today to this place and oh boy,  the changes were amazing. The indoor and outdoor place clean and sanitized. Staff offered friendly service and overall a good place to have a coffe. I'm super happy to increase the stars from 1to4, and special thanks to the staff member that served me today.

I have visited this particular Costa on and off for the last month and let me tell you that I only have seen dirty and messy tables, literally sticky and the staff seemed unbothered in cleaning them. The toilet is a nightmare on its own right, looking unclean and smelly every time that I have visited. I am sorry to say this but this place is literally a ground zero for infections and if a health inspector would visit i am sure this place would be out of business. To be honest I won't think I will be back any time soon as I truly find this place hazardous.
A standard chain of cafe with a good range of food and drink in a decent area. There is a good amount of seating and a clean bathroom for customers.

Delightful manager who opened the store early to let a few bright eyed early doors customers in. Super friendly and welcoming and had me served along with the queue in just a few minutes. Clean and tidy with some good window seats (not many of them though) to see the world go by. Outside seating too.

I visited this cafe today and I’m terribly dissapointed with the service and the place. First thing the bartender today   Didn’t understand our order, the hot chocolates were cold, he placed the aircon when outside is freezing , we asked him politely to turn it off and he didn’t, then he opened the door and we were freezing ). To be honest people working in this environment should be hospitable, fully trained and more polite ). Not coming back again .

Wonderfully warm and efficient welcome on a very rainy yet sunny last Saturday before Christmas 2019. Would have gone independent but memories of the bacon in a lightly toasted sourdough roll and promise of loyalty points lured me in.

Not that I'd ever have the expectation that every employee always has to smile at customers, but to get a lovely service with a smile from both the cashier and the barista in the morning was a nice start to my work day (04/03/25). Coffee up to standard as always in Costa.

I visited this Costa Coffee in the morning at 6:30 am (it opens at 6 am). As expected, it was very quiet, and everything was very clean, including the restroom.
The staff were absolutely kind and welcomed me with a smile, which is quite rare in London at 6:30 in the morning.
The atmosphere was relaxing.
The cappuccino was very good (I am Italian and I can tell the difference between a well made cappuccino and a bad one).
Honestly, I was surprised by the overall quality excellent for workers heading to the Holloway Road area in London.
Perfect for meeting friends, studying, or business meetings.
You can park in front of Costa (on the opposite side of the street) with free parking until 7 am, or in Jackson Road (two minutes on foot) with free parking until 8:30 am.
Absolutely recommended (at the hours I visited) from 6:30 to 8:30 am.
I will write another review when I return at a different time.
Entrance and restroom are also accessible for people with disabilities and wheelchair users.
Good service some days and average to good on others. Depends who is working. Usually an ok spot,

I have been coming to this friendly place for a while now and the manager is welcoming, the staff are great however I want to mention one person, who makes the best hot or cold drink, very patient, professional, knows his role his name is ‘khalid’’ he makes the best cold drink my favourites, just ask he will make it perfect for you! Thank you costa Holloway team you are amazing,Ethiopian and Eritrean people love that place, it reminds you of home!
I stayed in London for a couple of months and went to this Costa almost every day. They make a great Cortado, and all the staff were very professional and courteous.

The WiFi is not working properly in this Costa Caffee. I reported this a month ago and still same issue, it takes ages to connect to the WiFi and then it is the slower connection I have never had. You are forced to use your mobile data if you want to do something online.

This is the worst service ever received from Costa!!. The first time i habe been there, i had to tell the baristas to order, as they were chatting between them, then when i asked the latte, the done the wrong order ( and i was the only customer there). The second time I visited, asked for a soya mocha, and they just done normal one, and being intolerant, i habe notice straight away.
The guy behind got upset and vwry rude denying what i ordered!! Despite that i wanted my drink remade. And when remade, the cream gun fell off from the barista s hands on the floor!! Simple ehat is done! He took it from the floor and poured cream in my drink! Dear Costa coffee people, before appoint somebody behind the counter, at least make sure what having that role means! Will never visit this place again for sure.

This place lp
Very
Customer service is very nice, the guy named, Abishek was amazing. The way he handled my order as I was in rush . Quite cooperative. Thanks

Tried the newly launched maple hazel frappe. Mr.REHAN did an outstanding job of making sure it was prepared perfectly just as ordered and yes it was impressive. Very relaxing , syrups were used in proportion and the final drink with cream on top tasted PERFECT. He also made sure I liked the drink and satisfied with it. Very welcoming very attentive staff.
Staff 10/10
Ambience 10/10
Cleanliness 10/10

Unbelievably amazing staff in this Costa. We left a wallet behind when we left and the staff picked it up and held onto it, and made sure we knew that when they found it there was no cash in it (luckily there was no cash in the first place so we didn’t lose anything). They keep up orders for high volumes of customers at peak hours, the tables and floors are clean, the service was incredibly polite even before the wallet incident happened. Thank you so so much to the crew who staffed this shop in the afternoon of 19/11/2022, you really saved our skins and gave a us a fantastic experience anyway. The other regulars who come into this Costa are all locals who are very polite and clearly come to socialise with friends and children. Can’t say enough good things.

Also had their festive Toblerone latte and was really surprised by how pleasantly spiced the chai latte is! The pigs and blankets panini was also very toasty and served piping hot, as it should.
I go in Costa most mornings to get my drink before taking my daughter to the park. Service is lovely and very friendly

nice, cozy and convenient place to stop for a coffee near hackney central station. their coffee is great, and you can get a variety of plant based milk alternatives like oat, soya and coconut! there is though, a lack of dairy free or vegan options for food apart from one quite plain panini (though this is the same for all costa branches!) there is quite a lot of seating inside, and a convenient toilet for customers. the service is friendly and efficient, even early in the morning :)

I have been visiting this branch of Costa for about one year and have found it to be clean, hygienic (including the toilets), and comfortable. I come in here to do my work and enjoy a coffee whilst doing so and find the atmosphere calm and conducive to being well productive. The staff are always very friendly and efficient. In particular there is one lady with blonde hair who always serves me with a greeting and a smile. This Costa is a real hub for the community and is a great spot to relax and recharge as well.

I’ve been a regular at Costa Coffee in our neighborhood, and I just have to say how impressed I am with the service every single time. The ladies working here are always so warm, friendly, and welcoming—it really makes my day better. They greet everyone with a smile, no matter how busy they are, and make sure you feel valued as a customer. Their positive attitude and dedication to providing great service make this place stand out. It’s more than just a coffee shop; it’s a place that feels like a community. Highly recommend to anyone looking for not just a great coffee, but an uplifting experience!
The barista instore did her best. She was a little sad to let me know the price has almost arrived a £5. For one hot drink.

first I got boiling coffee, I asked for lukewarm and I burned myself, the second one was completely cold, the third one was made with problems by the manager Denisa, who is unable to train staff and has 0 customer service skills. Reaction  on her complain was laughter on me. Avoid z

I had forgotten just how good Costa Coffee is :) !  Had not been for a while. Had ordered a medium Cafe Latte  & Cappuccino. Nice roast and creamy coffee. The Sunday staff  were real nice . The young lady that served us made our coffees really nice and hot, just as we like them. Thanks

The staff here are so welcoming, with permanent smiles often. Okay, maybe an off day was experienced by one recent reviewer, and that happens for sure.  Generally, though, the staff are efficient and always happy to engage with customers.

I have been going there for about a month for a few times a week. Very good service and very good wifi.
Special thanks to Simone, Othman and all the team.

I had a disappointing experience at this Costa Coffee. I was placing three separate orders, as I was ordering for myself and two friends. When ordering, I politely asked for two soya lattes and, by instinct, held up two fingers to indicate the number. This wasn’t meant to be patronising at all: I am a teacher by profession and naturally speak with my hands.

The server responded sarcastically, asking if I was sure I wanted two and not three, while holding up two fingers himself. I replied politely that yes, two was correct. He then continued the joke by saying that “this” (again holding up two fingers) was seven. While he may have thought this was humorous, it felt unnecessary and uncomfortable.

When I went to collect the drinks, one of the soya lattes had been correctly labelled decaf, as I’d requested. The server then pointed at the unlabelled latte and said it was the decaf one. At this point, I felt that he was becoming quite rude and his jokes aimed at me were degrading. I acknowledged his “joke” politely by nodding, but I didn’t say anything back to entertain it because I wanted the interaction to come to an end. He repeated himself several times before muttering under his breath that he’d said it three times and I “didn’t get it.” I did understand his joke, but quite frankly I didn’t find it humorous in the slightest because it wasn’t. It was just impertinent.

At no point was I rude or patronising - discreetly gesturing with my hands does not indicate incompetence, so I do not understand why he felt the need to belittle me. It felt as though the server misconstrued my natural way of communicating with my hands and decided to deflect his own insecurities onto me through mockery.

Overall, this was an incredibly disappointing experience and I won’t be returning to this branch. I have previously worked in customer service myself and I’d like to make it clear that sarcasm at the customer’s expense is not banter; it’s poor judgement and poor service.

Costa Café is a reliably solid choice for a coffee stop, with a familiar menu of hot drinks, iced beverages, pastries and light bites. The layout is typical of a high-street chain: straightforward, functional, with seating arranged to make good use of space. It doesn’t feel overly plush or boutique, but it’s comfortable enough for a quick meet-up or a stop while passing through.

During busier hours, Costa can become crowded, especially when people queue up for their morning caffeine fix or midday snack. However, staff tend to keep things moving reasonably well, balancing orders and clearing tables with decent efficiency. At times the wait can stretch, but the pace generally remains tolerable. The staff are usually polite and accommodating, though like all chains, the experience can vary slightly by branch.

The drink quality is dependable: your latte, cappuccino or flat white is unlikely to astonish, but rarely disappoints either. Flavours tend toward the safe and familiar rather than adventurous or artisanal. Pastries and food options are standard for a chain café—convenient, decent, but not gourmet. Cleanliness is acceptable for the most part, though in busier periods tables may linger un-cleared for a short while, and spills or crumbs sometimes linger in corners until staff circulate.

Overall, this Costa Café offers what you expect from a high-street chain: consistency, convenience, and a familiar menu. It’s not extraordinary, but it’s a dependable place to pause, get a decent coffee, and recharge.

My wife asked for takeaway cup because she have to go work to put coffee and she say no but after she gives no moor costa for me.

we went to this costa a couple hours before closing so was pretty quiet inside with only us and another customer however one of the staff was conversing with a customer so incredibly loudly at an almost shouting level that we had to move and they still continued and despite completely ruining the atmosphere to the point that we couldn’t hear our own conversation another staff member said she wasn’t doing anything offensive and they then laughed it off after.

I came in this morning just to have breakfast—coffee and toast—but then I changed my mind and decided to try a matcha latte, which I had never tried before. I received very good advice from Agne, who kindly explained the taste, helped me choose the right size for my first time, and recommended adding syrup for better flavour.
Today is my birthday, and Agne truly made my morning. She provided outstanding customer service and even asked me afterwards if I liked the taste, which I really appreciated. A big thank you to Agne for such a lovely experience and for making my first matcha latte a great one.

Staff always friendly, polite and helpful! I go in quite regularly and the staff always greet me with a smile and all know my order (frothy skinny hot chocolate even during the summer!!) and quickly get it and in my reusable cup if I have remembered it!!They provide a wonderful boost to my day, thank you!!

Fantastic staff! Always go in first thing and the staff on shift are always attentive, quick and just lovely! Dont even need to ask as they know my order so just go in, scan app, pay and then enjoy! Felt compelled to write this review after seeing some of the others! I personally cannot say anything negative! You guys are brill :-)

As others have mentioned, the customer service is shocking. The girl who served us (afternoon of september 27th) was helpful and polite. But the man on shift was so rude, we couldn't believe it.

He was frustrated at our order, rolled his eyes when he'd made the wrong thing, actively complained about our order to the other staff member.

There's plenty of cafes in the area. Next time I'll be choosing anywhere else.
Always great service here. Staff very warm and quick at making quality coffees. Good amount of seating and choice of quick bite foods. Bathrooms are usually in good order.

Awful latte - not at all creamy just foamy and bland, 😒 even after I asked them to do it again!!! Save your money and go to Tesco’s Costa in Gainsborough Road - Leytonstone!! They are experts there.  Please train your staff, as no value for money!! £16 for rubbish!!!! 😖😡

Costa Coffee is a great spot for a quick coffee break or a relaxed catch-up. The coffee is always good, and they have a nice selection of snacks and pastries. The atmosphere is cozy, and the staff is usually friendly. A reliable place for a quality coffee experience!

Best place for refreshments or short meetings.
I really enjoy their summer drink specially Lemonade and mint drink is really yummy. Cafe look really clean and tidy. Also service was really good.
Highly recommended friends and family

I was a regular customer since Costa open in Wanstead until now . All our family member go Costa but from today I am not going Costa anymore. Manager is very rude, no manners. All bakery is not fresh , left over  bakery so I stop buying bakery. We have a nice others coffee shops around us.

Friendly staff so this gets 5 stars just for that, however it is an average Costa with the same drinks and food. It has a very limited amount of outdoor seats with some couches inside that are usually taken due to being the best seats.
Superb service. Such a nice bar tender so proffessional!!! Real Angel and his name is Angel.

Ordered a drink, sat down. Staff started doing other things e.g collecting deliveries, sharing jokes, general chat. The cafe was very quiet so they weren't busy serving other customers. After 10 minutes my husband went to ask about my order. They then re took my order and started making it, no apology or acknowledgement for having forgotten it. The male staff member who was meant to take my order was on his phone, stepped on my husband's feet while pushing past him and offered no apology to me or my husband. Wouldn't bother coming here again :)

Rudest staff I have ever come across. Attitude for no reason. Young boy with the black hair/ beard rolled his eyes for absolutely no reason after I  kindly asked for a take away cup. 0/5* for service. He should go out and see the sunlight more often

I have eaten here twice this month and both times the food was barely edible, the service is poor.The gentleman serving is very attentive to young attractive women, which I'm not one of, and very dismissive of the rest of us. This week he forgot my toastie as he was very busy discussing ice and fruit with the young girl behind me.
I certainly won't be stopping here again, I will save my money and have my treat at home.

Super rude staff. One staff was listening to a conversation of two customers and the other staff turned their back to us to fiddle around with some boxes (not even unpacking them)
My friend is very soft spoken so when she ordered and the staff didn't understand her they gave a rude 'What?'
Overall just not inviting at all.

This Costa has the friendliest staff ever. There's always a lovely greeting awaiting you at this coffee stop! Of all the coffee shops I visit, this one is top for the great staff and atmosphere - the food and drinks are good too!

Brilliant service by Sonia, and all the other staff who all never stop working ,you can really tell when someone is going the extra mile and this is something Sonia and her staff did for every customer. This is the best Costa in south birmingham which is why i visit every morning after going to the gym. All thank you for always being so pleasant, keep up the great work see you Monday am Paolo

Only two service assistants on since 730 and yet to have a break ( now 3pm). Run ragged, bit still smiling and full of cheer. Helped them out a bit by collecting cups and plates left out on tables. They were ever so grateful. ☕

I visited this Costa and I decided to have the Black Forest hot chocolate and it was absolutely delicious. The staff are very friendly and efficient. It’s well stopping off for a break if you’re in the area. Recommended

the staff were really sweet, and really considerate. my mum likes her drink extra hot, and they made it the perfect temperature for her, and they even gave an extra babycinno to my little sister, who has disabilities. the coffee was great, as usual
Big Costa good food friendly staff and really clean with fast service was outstanding my chocolate Twist was so good I can recommend Mr. Wig. YouTube

I’ve been a couple of times recently and Chris has been really polite, professional and has stood out for his quickness. Always has a smile on his face and a great relationship with what seemed like regular customers.

Quite dirty.
Ordered strawberry frappe, after put in till was told there is no cream felt kind of put on the spot. Paid £5.25 ( full price) for small drink, cream missing. Usually when ingredients are missing the product is marked as 'out of stock'. In my opinion at least a discount should have been offered as one of the main ingredients is missing from the drink.

Very friendly and positive staff. I came to have break,shower and obviously coffee.Place was clean and very well looked after. Nice and polite staff who know how to treat customers. I wish everyone would have the same attitude. Good place to stop for a quick break. I will be gladly coming back when in the area. I can recommend it to fellow drivers.
Thank you very much.

Always pop in here on the way back from Devon/Cornwall😎. Never had any issues with staff, always super polite and very helpful😁, coffee & the cakes always spot on👊, it's a place to relax n unwind with a great view out over the hills🌄 or even sit outside☀️. You defo need this pit stop b4 you hit the M5/M6 jct🤦😭🚙🚗🚛🛻🚙🚚🚛🚗

I was drinking my latte and felt something strange in my mouth, had to spit it out whilst I was driving and discovered it was a fly. So disgusting ! It was at the bottom of my drink with the syrup. The lady who served me on the till was quite rude too, so overall awful experience. Will not be returning.

Every time I come here the staff are very grumpy & not very welcoming, not to mention painfully slow. Usually a 20 minute wait with only 3/4 people in front of us… also one time I came here, we were the only ones ordering drinks as it was quiet, I put my order in & the 2 girls working proceeded to stand against the coffee machine playing on their phones chatting - one made themselves a drink which I assumed was mine after 5 minutes of just standing around & when I asked is that mine she just bluntly said “oh…no.” THEN started making my drink. Quite shocking really when you pay £10 for a drink & a snack.

Black Forest hot chocolate, completely pointless, the gentleman did not stir in the syrup at the bottom, squirted the most pathetic amount of cream on top, put the lid on and gave it to me. I asked where the syrup on top is and he said they don’t have any. So basically what I had was a standard hot chocolate. Complete waste of calories

If you don't know how to make a flat white, don't know what you are actually doing here. Awful coffee, awful milk, awful everything. Maybe should also consider stripping off those 30 cm nails from your fingers...

Stopped here for a quick break during a long drive and was genuinely impressed. The Costa Coffee at Frankley Services (M5 Northbound) is clean, well-organised and has a welcoming atmosphere. Staff were polite, efficient, and served everything with a smile, even during a busy time. Coffee was perfectly made, and the pastries were fresh and tasty. Plenty of seating, and the service was quick without feeling rushed. A great little pit stop if you’re heading north, definitely one of the better service station cafés I’ve visited.

Taking the order was fine, hame and cheese, plus a sausage bap and a medium latte and brown sauce with both.
We sat in a corner away from the hustle and bustle and the guy handed over our tray and told me to get my dog off the seat, I asked why and he mumbled policy etc, etc and cleanliness, I showed him the dirty cloth seats everywhere, the one I was in and my dog, he said we could use the vinyl seats which we did in the middle of the shop. We had lots of compliments about how well behaved she is and lovely. Here is a quick video from a different costa

Never had a problem here before, but today the blonde, tall woman at the checkout was rude and snappy when I dared to ask for a tissue. There was no need for this especially because I framed my request politely.
I get the pre- Christmas time is stressful, but don't take it out on your customers, who leave their hard-earned money here.

Probably won't come back as there's other nice coffee shops in Kings Heath that I would rather give my service to.

I’ve never encountered such unprofessional and frustrating service from a staff member. Aisha was visibly struggling to manage her duties, repeatedly making drinks incorrectly—not just ours, but other customers’ as well—which caused significant delays and created a chaotic atmosphere. It was clear she couldn’t cope with the pressure.

When I politely asked about my child’s marshmallow preference—he specifically wanted a white one—she responded aggressively, dismissing the request and implying I was wasting her time.

To make matters worse, she failed to secure the lid on a hot drink, which spilled onto my son’s leg. This level of carelessness is unacceptable and potentially dangerous.

Such behaviour reflects poorly on Costa’s standards, and it’s disappointing to see this level of service. If staff are unable to manage pressure or communicate respectfully, they should not be placed in customer-facing roles.

As a regular visitor to this branch (kingsheath)
I always end up waiting a good while (for a pot of tea) even when not busy,
Staff really need training in customer service, no welcome smile nor manners just sad looking faces,
Don't understand if they dont enjoy the job just leave plain as that
Cozy, friendly and chilled time every time I am there. Lovely staff members, delicious drinks and food 👍

Silent drive in where I came to have a coffee. I checked the reviews which were bad at the time. However, I took a risk. But I loved it. The service was good and the store was clean and maintained so. The drink was made beautifully as well.
Rose was a lovely barista and gave me excellent customer service had a lovely experience at this Costa would recommend!! She made an awesome hot chocolate
The coffee tasted very bad compared to other Costa’s and worst customer service ever experienced. Staff needs more training about customer service and lady behind the counter is under pressure.
Worst experience Staff was so rude specially girl with glasses they were not bothered to take order and seems unprofessional. Don’t want to come back her and they can’t even make proper Chailatte

Came for a coffee & cake here with my family :friends & as soon as we entered the lady spoken with her a few times ( laila) at the counter had a lovely, welcome towards us. Drinks were made as requested and overall experience of drinks and customer service excellent

I work contracted for NHS over the road and have been going here for a year in the mornings. Not sure if management has changed but the service has gone down hill. Now refuse to serve anyone until 6am on the dot and refused to put cream on a white chocolate mocha, never come across a coffee shop that refuses cream toppers on their coffees.
Fantastic Costa coffee cafe always a nice atmosphere and nice service extremely helpful the staff where there

It maybe another Costa however this one excels by its location just off the main road, has adequate parking and sometimes busy drive though.
The decor and the layout is well designed with comfy seating all around, was greeted by the lovely friendly Amy who is prepared to engage with her customers, there is various choice of coffees, of different styles, there is a abundance of snacks, some wearing the M & S label.
The store is clean tidy and especially the toilets are fit for purpose

I am a regular morning drive-thru customer. Everything is smooth and sometimes quite quick but recently I have had 2 of my drinks mistaken: I ordered a chai latte but I was given a hot chocolate. Since this has happened twice I make sure to check myself if I have the correct drink before driving away. Service here recently has gone kind of downhill, some staff really don’t seem friendly. They laugh and joke between themselves and don’t speak a word to the customer not even a hi hello, they just shove the chip&pin device at you for payment.

I came into costa this morning with my friend. When we walked in, there were no tables due to people working on laptops which could have otherwise accomodated multiple paying customers. Granted there was work being done on the big windows which had slightly limited tables. I dont understand where this new influx of people using coffee shops/dining areas as their work space for pretty much the entire day, has come from. There was even one lady who was making it extremely obvious to every other customer that there was a charging plug available for her so was interrupting others in order to get one.  Id say it spoils the ambience of an otherwise pleasant experience, which is now turning into an office environment which i am impeding on.
Thanks to Costa in Shirley for letting me hold a Macmillan fundraiser on Sunday 24th Aug. With all donations including online we raised £1083.00 🎉🎉🎉🎉
I got put off food here due to seeing a bug on this pastry in the picture attached. Atmosphere here gets very loud and noisy. Not an enjoyable coffee shop anymore

I went here for breakfast and to wait while I’m having my car serviced. It was really a nice place to hang out. It has nice decors and conducive seating. The seating area was vast. It was not very busy as I was there just after 8am. The store and toilets were clean and tidy. Superb service kudos to the two lovely ladies (staff). Coffee was good as I want it to be and the bacon bap was not bad either (no picture of the bap -was too hungry so I ate it as soon as I sat down 🤣. Had a great experience staying here this morning. I did not even notice the time. ☺️

I love costa, always will. This branch has great customer service, altho the food selection is small and when it runs out that’s it no more it’s a great little place to come chill , work and eat/drink.

This is my first time sipping a Costa coffee at a cafe back in 2021. That time in Malaysia just has a Costa coffee machine at petrol pumps which is the taste was not very nice. But was different taste when I sip the coffee at Costa Coffee Cafe..

On Saturday I attended costa in next shirley with my daughter.  I ordered for us and proceeded to our table to check on my daughter and tell her what was coming.  As a typical teenager she wasn't happy with the cake I ordered so I quickly went and amended the order, the server was amazing and so helpful and made the the whole transaction smooth and was so polite and professional, the cake that my daughter didn't want obviously couldn't be put back and she said we could still have it, as I don't eat sweet things I offered it to the young ladies behind who were also grateful to the kind server . I didn't get her name but she had blonde hair in a pony tail . Thanks again

I am very disappointed with the hygiene at this costa, I feel very put off as it is not very clean and they have alot of flies that are always flying around your food.
