I feel so disappointed because it is a great location but I dont see any passion from the staff unfortunately. Ive seen and thats including myself, customers just walk out with out finishing our drinks and food. One customer walked out disappointedly nodding there head and saying "disgusting". I hope management will do something about the hygiene and the fly infestation. I dont think I or many other customers will be returning until this issue is resolved.

Took my Dad today and had such lovely service. The girls were so busy and despite this were so happy and helpful. It was a girl with tattoos in particular and I also saw her come away from the counter to help an elderly couple choose a sandwich from the fridge which I thought was so lovely

I'm not complaining about the food and drink or the staff just to flies. There were loads of them. We just had to keep swatting them away.  It was very disgusting.  It's autumn, October,  flies shouldn't be this active now.  It must be an infestation!! It shouldn't be allowed in a food and drink establishment.

Did not enjoy my experience here
The coffee was spilled over the machine rather than in the cup and the barista did not care about it, even the cup was dirty from the outside
Overall did not like it
Good coffee
The place is good, but there is a lot of noise inside the shop.

Visited today and my drink was made very poorly. The mango and passion cooler has lots of chunks of ice in it I know you’re busy but don’t let quality slip I’d rather wait for a properly blended drink.

Toilets an absolute disgrace.  So unhygienic especially in an eatery.  Water cold and only dribbling out of tap.  Not good.  I do hope staff have other facilities!!!???
Not a good visit unfortunately.

I'm sitting in Costa with my wife and whilst trying to enjoy my coffee one of the workers decides to sweep dirt and dust mop  the floors leaving them soaked and spray chemicals all over the tables. At the same time leaving spray bottles all over the place with kids about. No wet floor signs. The after using her hand to wipe debris off the seats goes and makes people's food and drinks without cleaning her hand. No coshh awareness no food hygiene no respect for customers that do not wish to have a serving of chemicals with their drink and food.  Leaving brooms mop and bucket lying around. What are they taught here. Very disappointed.
Very nice coffee shop in Solihull. Good service and good beverage selection plus everything you expect from a Costa. Happy to give five stars!

I am extremely disappointed as I have never faced such attitude whilst being in Touchwood before. I am a sixth form student who is paying for a service, a middle aged worker, Trina, was absolutely vile. She greeted me with attitude immediately, questioned my friends with hostility whilst i payed for their items and this has not been the only occasion where i have had issues with this worker specifically.

Poor customer service at the Costa Coffee opposite Krispy Kreme in Touchwood. The middle-aged female staff member wearing glasses was rude from the moment I ordered, huffing and puffing and clearly showing attitude. She then slid my drink across the counter while rolling her eyes. This is the second time I’ve had a similar experience with the same worker.

I spent my money on both drinks and food, only for them to be made very badly, which made the experience even more disappointing. Overall, a complete waste of money. I wouldn’t recommend this Costa at all — go somewhere else for your food and drinks 💯👎

I popped in here with my young daughter a few weeks ago. Coffee and soup were great, as was my bacon and brie sandwich. They also happily catered for my daughter with a half sandwich that wasn't on the menu. Whilst the service wasn't overly welcoming it was quick and polite. The place was busy - locals must know it's a good place for a spot of lunch with plenty of parking. ☺️
Very friendly staff excellent cup of coffee many thanks guys

Small cafe near a small group of specialist shops. Good coffee and cakes. Cooked lunches and sandwiches. Sells some locally made ice-cream. Need to order at the counter but will bring your order to the table.
One member of staff here was very rude before I had even sat down. My small hot chocolate took 10 minutes.

I am  loyal costumer for over 10 years.I never had bad experience until I visit Barnes hill costa.the older lady works there is very rude.She comes to me and said that I can’t sit there without consuming.I was waiting for my husband to finish shopping and come to me because I had disabled child which I can’t leave unsupervised at any time she is autistic non verbal.i feel very discriminate.i was going to purchase coffe one’s my husband can take care of my daughter.there was someone else sitting there without consuming and she never told him anything.

I would give all 5 stars, but it depends on what staff member is on, Sinead if iv spelt it right is a 5star member staff, having autism I feel welcomed, there are few other staff that are good too, but I don’t like going when the Manager is working as she is not just rude, but doesn’t put effort into making the drinks as they don’t taste the same, seems like she doesn’t want to be there.

Don't know if the lady was new but I ordered mint hot chocolate and it was bright green and when I asked her if it's too green and maybe she put too much in she said no when I tried it later on all I could taste was extreme mintiness ..had to throw it away
Don’t fill the cup up all the way and I expect it to be since I’m paying full price

Today 18th of January I ordered coffees the away, have to go to work. But the girl decided to make full tray of people who were behind me first. Looks like it was done deliberately. Teach your staff to follow the order. Because of you, I was late to work - hope you are happy

Very helpful staff and hardworking team, orders can be slow at times but that is more to do with the place being popular. Car park (and parking spaces) is very small which can be a negative, seems to be a lot of young people who just come in to either use the WiFi or sit and eat their own food - this appears to go unchallenged by staff.

What on earth is going on with this Costa? We ordered a latte and a Frappuccino and asked for an extra shot of coffee in the frap.

The latte was watery and has almost no coffee in it. Like they’re skimping on the espresso.

The frap had no coffee in it whatsoever.  I went back and asked for the frap to be remade.  (Still not a great job but whatever at least the coffee was added this time) she said something about Costa are now using instant coffee rather than espresso ermmm what?

The latte I just threw away.  What a colossal waste of £15.

We are huge fans of Costa but my god this was bad.

Worst tostie I’ve had paid 9  one side was burnt one side was white disgusting I’m so sorry no one could eat it I took bite spat it out  I’m use to eating at from Birmingham but the drink was fine so don’t know what happened

Love this Costa. Friendly staff. Great drinks and food. Dog friendly. Very chilled.
My mate Reggie really enjoyed his pup cup.

one star because the workers were very rude and disrespectful towards us minors. we were pressured to buy an item from this costa coffee, even though we had been wanting to quickly warm ourselves up. the woman who had served me was very disrespectful and did not give me any sort of acknowledgment at the til while i was standing there for a good 5 minutes. i should not have to be the person to announce my presence to have a drink at this place. it is horrible and i feel really disrespected that we have been treated as less because we are children (14-15). she also made my drink spill out on the top and proceeded to not give me a tissue.

Ordered a mocha to go and  I told the cashier that I was sitting down whilst they were making it. They completely forgot about the order and I ended up waiting an extra 10 minutes. Service was slow even though the place wasn’t busy.

Lovely branch of costa with really nice staff. At busy times service can go a bit off the rails but all intentions are good. The ladies keep an eye on customers and who is on the premises, as they have had homeless people trying to use the bathroom. However they deal with this very kindly. Great housekeeping, nice and clean but they deserve a refit.

Been here many times, it's location is fantastic and if you're lucky, you might find a parking space right outside. I must admit that some of the staff here are very helpful and nice. The atmosphere is very relaxed and I enjoy visiting this branch on Bearwood High Street.
The service was quick and friendly and my coffee came perfect. Shout out to Hiten who is a great member of staff there. Thank you

At first, the clean, spacious, maze-like layout of the place — with its well-designed signage, tasteful arrangement, and a kind, welcoming Indian lady at the front — creates a chic and safe atmosphere, especially on a freezing, heavily rainy afternoon.

However, once you sit down to relax for a few minutes and enjoy your coffee, the experience quickly changes. The excessively loud music that keeps cutting in and out, the harsh glare of the ceiling halogen lights sharply directed at every seat, and the oversized cameras positioned in the corners and seemingly focused on customers’ phones, all take away the pleasant first impression. Instead, they evoke the feeling of being in a temporary holding facility or a mental health observation unit.

Despite my initial enthusiasm, I honestly cannot give more than two.

A few problems on this one. Firstly I can't believe a small vanilla latte is £4.50. So , if your going to charge high prices I expect something decent. But that's not what I experienced,  what I experienced was utterly disgusting.
I have no idea what temperature you gave me today but certainly not a safe one. I knew it was hot when I pick it up. So I left it for 20 minutes! And after 20 minutes i tried it and I burnt my tongue....its not right and not acceptable.  your staff would know while making this and didnt care
Once it cooled down from temperatures of the sun it tasted awful, in fact it tasted like burnt coffee. No vanilla taste, just burnt coffee.

A cute Costa in a busy area. Size is deceiving and has 3 different seating areas. The prices will always be s downfall and continue to find it too pricey, although coffees are good. I get it.... however, the slice of a lemon slice being the same price as a whole cake continues to baffle me

This branch of Costa is just off 5 ways island in Birmingham city centre.
I enjoyed a smoked bacon bap with tomato sauce & and an Americano.
The food, service & atmosphere were perfect.

I had a really disappointing experience at Costa. The cup I was given looked like it had been used before — the lid was loose and didn’t feel sealed properly. The coffee was cold and filled way too much, making it really hard to handle. As soon as I picked it up, it spilled all over my car and clothes. Completely ruined my morning. Not what I expect from a brand like Costa.

The shop is nice, bright and airy. It has nice decors throughout. Staff were friendly and accommodating. I even got a free cookie as the first one she gave me is broken which is quite nice. I had a wet cappuccino and it was as I want my coffee to be. I love the atmosphere of the shop a bit busy but with the right amount of buzz going.

Just been serve and the service is not as good as the one in bearwood and in harborne, its 10:12am and the barista is already stressed and cant even give a good nice smile, i think being nice is going to be a once in a week thing and so as the listening, she forgot my flat white and not even a sorry is given. Not nice

Really Can not fault the service, the atmosphere, the staff are always cheerful 😃

Every time we are on route to work we grab a coffee here or have a meeting here.

The staff are so welcoming. You are always able to grab a vegetarian / vegan option hot or cold meal snack.

There is always something in  the fridge, we know we will get fed 👍🏻

Here there are only 2 members of staff working hard, good service amazing attitude. Constant customer flow this store needs recognition it’s always like this. Always happy to accommodate always.

29/02/2023 served at 1045 keep up the good work.
Dog friendly, very helpful and attentive staff. Quite a busy outlet for a Sunday

The place has had a makeover and as with other makeovers they have made it worse.

Gone are all comfy seats by the window. In has come very uncomfortable seating high marble oval tables hard school type seats.

High industrial ceilings, which seem to be the fashion these days, makes it difficult for people, like me, with hearing difficulties to hear anything in a conversation.

Staff fine coffee fine but clearly they have had enough of students spending all day here and made it hard to stay for more than a few minutes.

One good thing now open till 8pm.

Very disappointing experience. I ordered a Too Good To Go bag from this Costa Café, but when I arrived at the shop, I was told they had no stock at all and nothing could be given. There was no prior notice, no message in the app, and no warning before I travelled to the store.

If there is no offer available, that’s understandable — but customers should be informed in advance, not made to come all the way only to be told there is nothing to collect. Even worse, there was no apology from staff for the inconvenience.

This is poor customer service and a waste of customers’ time. I would not recommend ordering Too Good To Go from this location unless their communication improves.

We liked hot cheese sandwiches and latte plus the hot chocolate.

Remember if you want to use their toilet, get the bill receipts which have alphanumeric code to unlock the toilet door. They don't want to use their toilet unless you are buying anything for the cafe.

No dedicated parking and would recommend using parking paid options behind Sainsbury's and Iceland.

I tried the new Maple drinks, We tried the Hazel Latte and the Maple Hazel Hot Chocolate. The latte was fantastic, creamy, well-balanced, and overall my favourite. Our server was quick and attentive, which made the experience even better. The hot chocolate was much sweeter, so if you enjoy sweeter drinks, it’s a great choice. Personally, I preferred the latte since I’m not a fan of overly sweet drinks. The café offers really unique and delicious options, and the flavors are genuinely enjoyable. Our drinks were nit very hot, a little colder, next time will be asking them to make the drinks hotter.

I went here to do some work for a few hours last week and it was a really nice atmosphere. After a while, one of the staff came and asked what I was up to and then brought me a slice of carrot cake on the house! It was so kind.

It's always a great experience at harborne Costa, warm, friendly welcome with a smile, brilliant service from Andrew and Hamza. Always making everyone laugh, great duo, deserve a pay rise for all their hard work, everyone leaves smiling. Keep up the great work 👍

Wouldn't visit this Costa again. Panini came out cold, was told it looked 'pretty steaming' to them despite the cheese in the middle still being cold and hard/ unmelted. Was put in to toast again but no difference, ended up taking out the cold cheese

I give it one star because I can't give it less. The manners and education of the workers are terrible. On this afternoon shift, two men and a woman were working here. The man with glasses started cleaning the establishment 3 hours before closing. I tried to sit in the seats and he didn't allow me to do so. What they do is 3 hours before closing time, they close the way to some tables so that people can't get through and thus have fewer people. In the photo you can see how they close the way with the chairs. They go away and keep the space like that for hours.Pathetic.

I visited this Costa last Saturday with my mum and my baby twin nephews, following the snowstorm earlier that week. While seated, I noticed water leaking from the roof, and some of it had fallen onto one of my nephew’s forehead. There were no wet floor signs, and the seating area had not been closed off, which is concerning given the safety risk — especially with babies present.

I informed the staff member on the till (a light-brown-haired man with glasses, appearing to be in his late 20s), and his response was very rude and dismissive. He replied in an aggressive tone, simply saying “I know,” without any apology or attempt to resolve the issue.

Unfortunately, this is not the first time I’ve had a negative interaction with this staff member. I’ve previously experienced him huffing and showing irritation when I added an item to an order in the drive-through.

I expect better customer service and basic safety measures from Costa, particularly when leaks are present and families with young children are affected. I hope this feedback is taken seriously and addressed.

Very bad parking arrangement
The car park management keeps sending u penalty notice even though u have paid the parking ticket. A big hassle to write to them to appeal the case. Waste of time to do that. Don’t drive and park there!

One of the best Costa branches in Birmingham. It also offers drive-through facilities. Plus there is ample parking outside. The interior is nice and spacious with tables and chairs. It is well-lit, ventilated and heated, making It an ideal place to work or study with a hot beverage.

The staff are very helpful especially Alex, who is a very skilled barista who makes great-tasting hot drinks and lattes. He is an exceptional member of the Costa Team, who always tries to go above and beyond by being kind, courteous and considerate. I love coming to this branch and always will thanks to the love and great service I have received from the staff here.

We visited the Costa Coffee at Broadway Plaza today and ordered hot chocolates along with some food. Unfortunately, all items were served lukewarm. The hot chocolates in particular were completely unacceptable — while the glasses themselves felt slightly warm, the drinks inside were cold.

As regular Costa customers who are very familiar with how hot chocolate is normally served, we raised this politely with the server. The big Asian male server with glasses responded in a dismissive and rude manner, repeatedly stating that “that’s how they come,” despite our concerns.

Rather than preparing fresh drinks, the server poured the hot chocolate we had already drunk from into a jug, reheated it, and poured it back into the same cups. This was extremely unhygienic and not an acceptable way to handle a customer complaint. Fresh drinks should have been made.

The drinks were still not properly hot afterward, but we chose not to complain further. However, my young son, who had been looking forward to his drink all week, asked if his could be made warmer. When we returned to the counter, we were again met with a cold and unhelpful attitude. The server went so far as to tell us not to tell him how to do his job, repeating that this was “how they come.”

The overall attitude displayed was rude, arrogant, and completely lacking in basic customer service. I was horrified. This experience was extremely disappointing and has put us off returning to this branch. We will not be visiting this Costa again.

The person serving was kind and attentive, but the store definitely needs more staff and more trained people. As a barista, I can see the coffees were not made well and the teas we ordered tasted like dirty water and were just warm not boiling. The atmosphere was nice as we just came from the movies but wouldn’t be ordering from here again.
Nice young lady served me was very polite and friendly hot chocolate with cream was lovely and hot also sandwich tasted good.
Refurbished Costa in Odeon Luxe. Go here before a film. Also next door to a rock climbing place. They scan Costa card separately. The new look is nice better than the old look.

Called into Costa on Sunday to order hot drinks to go with our film, to get a double dose of inter-minority
microaggression. I waited several minutes, beside the till to be served, as both members of staff had their backs to me as they made the hot drinks for someone else. The female,  staff member then walked towards the till, just at the point that a woman arrived beside me to also wait in line. Instead of serving me, she began to serve this whiter, lighter woman. I told her excuse me I’ve  been waiting to be served (as though this needed some explanation for my waiting by the till) and this staff member rolled her eyes, as though I was an inconvenience for asserting my rights. To add to this, the other customer afterwards began glaring at me! It was Luke, the manager, arriving afterwards to work out, diligently if the hot chocolate powders were vegan, that has made this experience less bitter. I’m so tired of having to decide whether to raise a complaint and be seen as an angry, Black/Brown woman causing a fuss or just ‘compartmentalise it’ and go about my day. I chose the latter.  From this experience, I’ve decided to use Google reviews to call out racism wherever and in whatever form, I experience it.

shocking service , workers messing around , joking with friends ignoring customers having to wait long leaving a huge queue for customers. drinks served 10minutes later and toast was cold. Gave me my drink half empty , had to ask her to fill it. as you can see in the picture my drink was flooding and didn’t even add toppings as labelled in menu. Didn’t mix my drink properly

Honestly, the staff here are amazing...Anna, Aaron, Charlie & Ellie!

Every single person is friendly and takes a little time out, to chat with the customer while the order is being prepared.

My kids love coming here too, because of that. The food and drinks are always prepared with care and any problems...it's solved!

This Costa has recently been refurbished and so, it's even better!

My favourite Costa Coffee in Birmingham. Staff are always lovely and kind. Nice open space and great customerservice. It can get really crowded during the weekend but it's everywhere you go.
Since they have done the refurbishment it feels more uncomfortable. One thing I hate is the air-conditioning being on full blast all the time.

We often come here with my wife and my little daughter and its just lovely.
There is a barista called Ana  and she is on top with the service,the kindness she gives to my little daughter and everyone who goes there its very nice.
We love going there some afternoons.

We visited this coffee shop and thoroughly enjoyed the experience with yummy food and drinks - highly recommend Costa.  The atmosphere was nice but would have preferred a more secluded area.  The baristas are very friendly and Hazel was especially nice 👍

Usually I have a great experience at this Costa, but today I was greeted with the worst service I have experienced to date. An old white lady was not listening to my order and because she got so frustrated she ignored my many requests to have Oat milk in my latte. I also had a costa number to enter ad she acted as if she didn't have time, energy, or a care to type it in despite me being an exchange student. It wasn't until another barista was making the drink that she told me I have to pay extra. I was totally okay with that, but why did it take 6 different times for me to say my drink order with Oat milk, for her to finally understand. I am very disappointed with this experience.

Great to see a huge Costa just a few minutes walk from. University station.
All the seating is in the vast basement. Or outside. Quick service as most customers are buying takeouts. Always love the atmosphere on university campus so this is a lovely experience.
The Chinese guy's service is second to none. Absolutely incredible.

kind staff and quick service considering how busy it gets, however seating area downstairs can get a bit hectic. coffee is good, nothing exceptional-- just another costa. food selection is a bit limited, but that is just costa anyway.

Can't rate this outlet highly enough,it gets extremely busy during lunchtimes but staff are efficient and always in great spirits. Super friendly. I've been out of term time also and the service level is fab then too, took my young daughter and our server was so nice to her, add some sparkly sprinkles to her drink which made her really happy. It's just those little extras that make for a great experience!

place_name: Costa Sports Centre - University Of Birmingham
review_text: had the worst Latte ever yesterday.  I am wondering whether the barrista has replaced the coffee granules before he made my drink. The Latte was tasteless and can't taste any coffee at all, just frothy milk. Very disappointed.

place_name: Costa Sports Centre - University Of Birmingham
review_text: Very difficult to find a seat - lots of tables monopolised by people with no sign of any Costa food and drink consumption. People openly eating their own packed lunches. Had to ask someone to move their stuff off one of the four chairs they were ‘using’. Presumably the university subsidise it.

place_name: Costa Sports Centre - University Of Birmingham
review_text:
Very poor customer service;
Staff mixing up coffees, my cortado is almost the size of latte. Staff refusing to serve in glass saying that an hour before closing they only serve in paper cups to drink in. Point scanner didn’t work for the third time in a raw.

place_name: Costa Sports Centre - University Of Birmingham
review_text: Just the job after a punishing exercise regime if you are not on student loan. Putting back the calories is perhaps not a good idea.  Once in a while for a treat is fine.  Prices are not suited to full-time students and the choices of food is limited for me as most are non-Halal.

place_name: Costa Sports Centre - University Of Birmingham
review_text: The staff are lovely in this Costa, but the coffee is always cold. They froth the milk first and by the time they pour it in the froth has already gone cold. For £3.25 I just don't think I'll go back. I did mention it, and apparently they use a temperature gauge but in my opinion they should heat the milk last, not leave it to stand for 3 mins while they complete the rest of the transactions. I haven't had this occur with other Costa outlets.

Costa Coffee never disappoints! The coffee is always rich and flavorful, with a great selection of drinks to choose from.

The staff is friendly and efficient, creating a welcoming atmosphere. The pastries and snacks are also fresh and delicious.

Overall, it is a fantastic coffee shop that we always enjoy visiting. Highly recommended!

Staff were geat, food was ok but I'd say overpriced for a sandwich and cake. Hot chocolate was great though!
WiFi wasnt working.

Nice place to go for a drink. Only 4 stars because of lack of space for pushchair in the café. The staff are really friendly and were really helpful when my wife's drink got cold due to the little one having a nappy eruption.

The coffee is typical Costa coffee, so don't expect anything good. The white hot chocolate is not very nice at all and definitely doesn't taste like white chocolate and was barely warm. It also left a funny after taste which all 4 of us thought was strange. The hot chocolate is pretty bad too, not hot, not chocolately. It baffles me that Costa are so popular with how bad they are.

Half of the place was closed off too even though they didn't close for another hour and a half. This was a little frustrating as they closed off a big section by the tills so it was a tight squeeze to order with people walking past.

The staff were friendly though which is the reason for the 2 stars. Definitely won't be back though.
The coffee was really nice,but we ordered another coffee and didn’t give us it and we payed £10

I was quickly served by a lovely lady who was happy to answer all my questions. My drink was quickly made and presented beautifully, it was amazing. I had ordered the Terrys chocolate orange hot chocolate and highly recommend it to all chocolate orange lovers!

Always good quality food and drinks. Service can be slow but worth it. Nice relaxing area to chill, and good promotions on the app. Sadly not enough seating for the amount of people wanting drinks as it’s the main arrivals area and there is often hundreds of people in this area. Good news is that it is open 24 hours so when we arrived at 2am coffee was ready to go.

I ordered an almond croissant.
It seems they decided to give it to me instead of throwing it in the rubbish bin! It was embarrassingly stale and a bad croissant. I looked at the others they had, and they were just as pitiful. I guess Costa has confidence that people will eat whatever they serve since it’s a big brand, but it’s truly shameful. To serve customers the most expensive and the stale ones at the airport. I hope my voice reaches them!

Ps. I shared my opinion with the staff and they replaced the croissants two times to provide fresh ones to me but unfortunately, all the croissants were stale! It’s not the staff's mistake that I will increase my service stars but the rest of the stars will stay low!
Staff member was extremely rude when asked for advise for what to order. Need to Brush up on customer service skills.

This is the second location of costa which I have tried. This place has a nice ambience and is a good place to sit and spend your time. I had a flight early in the morning and had a layover of about 9:00 hours. Stayed couple of hours here and had good time. Their mint tea is nice and the snacks they got are great too.

I am a regular visitor at the Sheldon branch and always look forward to my coffee and conversations with the staff.
The staff are always so welcoming and warm , especially Kohinoor, Christina, Ven, Hitten and Muhammad.
Coffee is always made with care and I have met some very nice people there.

We use this branch every Friday. Always exceptional customer service from Venkatesh and the team. As we are regulars they always remember our drinks order, they are always very friendly, polite and welcoming, even when very busy and under pressure. The store and facilities are clean and tidy. The drinks are well made and cakes very tasty too. Very much recommended.
Lovely going to costa meal deal are worth it seating out side just opened a week  ago staff were so friendly and pleasant car park free for 30 minute

Kohinoor very very good customer service with smiles 😊
Thank you

I recently popped into Costa Coffee in Birmingham, hoping to get a proper taste of British culture, so I opted for a nice cuppa tea. I must say, I was expecting something a bit more authentic, considering it’s the UK and all. The tea itself was decent enough—nothing to write home about, mind you—but it lacked that proper brew you’d expect from a traditional British tearoom.

The staff were friendly, and the atmosphere was cosy enough, but if you’re looking for a quintessentially British tea experience, this might not quite hit the mark. It’s more of a quick stop for a caffeine fix than a place to savour the finer points of British tea culture. Still, not a bad spot for a casual catch-up or a quick break.

Good new store inside the department lounge located near the 1-20 gates. Nice and bright, open and quiet, worth walking from the main area here for a relaxing coffee. Love the big seating area and the staff are very helpful and friendly. Great offers on the app.

They don't have any idea how to make coffee. I ordered a latte and they steamed some milk, poured the espresso into a separate cup and then chucked it into the steamed milk. They then charged me 4.65 for the pleasure.

The service was incredibly slow despite it being very quiet in there.

Please teach your staff how to make coffee. I mean, it is supposed to be a coffee shop right?
Conveniently located Costa that allows you to sit and watch over the arrivals area whilst you wait for people. Offer’s the usual Costa offerings.
Located ‘airside’ in departures but near gates 1-15, not in the main departures area, so not as busy as some of the other outlets. Drinks exactly as you’d expect from Costa.

She got the first drink order wrong, no problem.  My coffee seemed a bit low in the cup and queried this. I accepted all was ok when the explanation was given, but then she made comments about me to the next customer. No need for that at all....

the staff were so sweet today. they were probably really tired by this point because we headed there a little less than an hour before closing time, but they didn’t show it at all, they were really pleasant. they gave my little sister a small hot chocolate instead of just plain milk, and the cappuccino today was flawless
Exceptional service every single time we go in. The staff make a chain coffee shop feel like a small, family-run one. A real credit to the staff.

Good hot coffee
Friendly staff
Clean toliets

Amazing experience from all the staff. My mom and I are both disable and we were treated with lots of respect and care.  All the staff are amazing, very helpful.  They advised me of offers that I wasn't aware of. It was very busy but still this Costa coffee was so clean. Thank you to you all.  This was my first visit and we will be back.

Brilliant customer service from Sam and Amy. Both so lovely, welcoming and friendly. I had an issue with something and they managed this exceptionally well, professional and courteous. Thank you Sam and Amy :)
Hot chocolate with whipped cream is sublime just what you need on a cold winters day. Very clean and tidy premises friendly staff which is always nice to see.
Love Costa coffee flat white, my only issue is I like my coffee to be hot I have to ask for extra hot when ordering. Good range of cakes but exspensive in my view.
I was actually at shell petrol station which was as expected, but the coffee was normal from the takeaway machine.
Very good coffee
Used the drive thru as its convenient for us instead of getting out of the vehicle. Great fast service and very pleasant staff

Regularly order via Deliveroo and drinks are always consistent, hot and packaged well! I absolutely love the messages on the cups also, always brings a smile to our faces…  today I received a lovely little gift also! Thank you so much ❤️

Pleasant pit stop today. Everything was clean and tidy.
Good choice of food and drink, but most importantly, a warm and friendly welcome from the staff—particularly Tom.
He was seen to be very helpful with customers and went above & beyond.
The thoughtful message on the cup was also a lovely touch.

Came in on Sunday 21st December at 2.30 we were told they were closing at 3.30 stopped serving food not even a toasted tea cake. Unable to use toilet as they had cleaned them and had closed them. Half the tables were cleaned and chairs put up. Considering they had another hour of trading it was a poor show .
Vistas yesterday 11/09 and there were 2 gents on the counter. Who were friendly and really friendly with my little boy. Really good service

The exceptional service and customer support provided by this Costa store deserve recognition. The store manager, Dan, exemplifies professionalism and warmth, ensuring a welcoming atmosphere for all customers. I express my gratitude to the entire team for their dedication to customer satisfaction.
Staff is not that welcoming 3/5, coffee and sandwich is okay to good, but location is an ideal stop for a quick coffee or a relaxed break after a walk.

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

I recently visited this Costa Coffee in Birmingham city centre while staying nearby for a meeting. It was a fantastic experience — the coffee was excellent and a perfect way to freshen up my morning. The atmosphere in the shop is warm and inviting, and I truly enjoyed my time there.

The location is right in the heart of town, surrounded by a lovely area that adds to the overall charm. The staff were incredibly friendly and welcoming, which made the experience even better.

If you’re in Birmingham, I highly recommend stopping by this Costa Coffee. It’s a great spot to relax, recharge, and enjoy a great cup of coffee.

Thank you to the team for such great service!

Absolutely must try the seasonal maple & hazel range. I had the hot chocolate one and it was DELICIOUS!!! The staff were a bit busy talking amongst themselves and felt slightly put off but still polite and courteous. The area was amazing too and appreciated inside and outside options. Overall wonderful time had waiting for others in the SeaLife centre.

I was served today by Tom and he was so lovely. Genuinely helpful and had a jolly persona. I feel it’s important to praise these qualities, because actually, they are becoming more and more rare to see
friendly and professional Staff, nice place where definitely you want to drink your coffee or have breakfast/lunch, Liya was looking after ourselves and everything was warm and delicious

I ordered the same thing I get every time, but this time I was given a medium drink in a glass, which it normally never comes in. I switched it to a plastic cup because I hate drinking from glasses, and then realised it was only half full. Two other people ordered the same drink while dining in and theirs came in plastic straight away. I understood it was busy, so I waited, but the drink ended up being watery. Overall, a bad experience.

I had the unfortunate experience of this Costa today. I went to the drive through, at 16:02, to order an Oat Hot Chocolate, which I thought would be simple enough, but when I arrived at the window I was greeted by a rude customer service member who didn’t acknowledge me, didn’t tell me my order just stated “£4.00” whilst waving a card machine in my face. I said excuse me, and I was met with the same expression, I asked if it was the correct order being an Oat Hot Chocolate, and I was responded to by being asked whether I ordered a regular or large, I responded regular and proceeded to pay.

Now my order for an Oat Hot Chocolate, isn’t due to a preference, it’s due to an allergy. I paid for my drink and carried on with my day. When I arrived at home, my partner had noticed that there should’ve been a label on top of the drink to state the drink has “Oat” milk to signify that there is alternative milk within the drink instead of dairy milk. Being that I am allergic to dairy, I started to panic as the itchiness I was experiencing was from nothing other than the hot chocolate I had been sipping.

As a result of this I was violently sick and had to take allergy medicine to prevent any further sickness.

I thought about returning to the store to complain, I tried to call the store but to my surprise Costa Coffee on Holyhead Road has no number to complain to just directions to their restaurant.

I’m not a regular to the business but I certainly won’t be returning, especially not to this rude restaurant that cant comprehend a simple order or even acknowledge their customers.

Lovely experience, I came in with my boyfriend to try the new Christmas range and I was beyond impressed with all the stuff, especially one girl named Jam and Sam/samantha

My boyfriend has a nut allergy and they was so informed, so considerate and so informative about the traces and things of that matter

(Jam even wrote this on my cup) xx

Amazing team, this is coming from someone who used to work at costa for 2 years

Jai’s customer service was terrible. He was talking into the till. No eye contact and no conversation whatsoever. Food was cold and so was the drinks. We paid almost £30.00 took my sister after a long hospital stint.

He honestly needs retraining. He isn’t bothered and he clearly doesn’t want to work here.

I’ll think long and hard before I come back here.

Rashid and Martyna were amazing in handling the situation. They were brilliant as always :)

Customer service was ace. A reward on my app was not working and they sorted it out straight away. Food was hot fresh and tasted great. Tables were being cleared and wiped quickly. Atmosphere was great. Very clean coffee shop. Be back soon .

Friendly efficient staff, order prepared as per my requirements, the food and drink was perfect for my requirements at the time. However, most of the tables were waiting to be cleared and cleaned and the toilet could have been cleaner. I have dust mite allergies and was sneezing a lot in there - usually an indication they're not dusting enough! Looked up and the rafters all looked quite dusty - perhaps in need of a clean.

Disappointing visit to this Costa.
Tables piled with dirty dishes.
Toilets are shocking.
Hot chocolate tasted of nothing, so I left it.
Best thing was the vegan billionaire slice.
The Lady, who appeared to be the manager, was the nicest person there.
Needs a good wipe down everywhere.

The staff are incredibly rude, I witnessed one member carelessly slamming a customers cup & food onto the tray and another talking badly about other customers. The drinks and food are always good, but staff ruin the experience when they act as if it’s offensive you’ve walked in the building.

Arrived at location at 5.15pm but chairs were already up on tables and floor being mopped. Asked the manager (who served me) politely if it was still open because it said it closes at 6pm. After being glared at through her glasses she said it closes in about… (pause) 30 mins. So preferred coffee and went to the toilet but it was locked off. Asked if I could use it and was told it had been ‘sanitised’ and can’t be used now. I then took a seat to drink the coffee and the manager went outside to get the big bin and bring it inside! 😂 Great place. Maybe a senior manager needs to speak to the manager and give some advice about customer service. Please.

November 16th at 2:31pm approx.
Extremely rude Barista in training.
Don’t know her name, but she seemed like an Asian girl with glasses.
She seemed like she didn’t want to be there,
She was getting frustrated with my wife and showed signs of impatience.
This is extremely off putting and unsure what kind of customer service is being endorsed here.

Lovely café — cozy, yet quite spacious. The team is very friendly. I stopped by a few times while Momina was on shift, and she helped me sort out some problems with the app. She was calm, patient, and genuinely friendly 😊. Plus, her drinks are always excellent!

p.s. bring your reusable cups, they can make drinks in them!

Stopped here for tea, coffee and cakes on a wet Monday morning. Service was polite and efficient. The 'raspberry and almond bake' was delicious - but rather small for the £2.85 price tag. And 25p dearer than at my local Costa!!
Jacob always makes the best coffee with the best service. My order of a flat white with coconut milk is always perfect, with the milk never burnt and always the perfect temp.

The ambiance and drink are up to the mark as all Costa branches but this one at Frederick street, I’m not coming here for the first time , the staff were super friendly and sensible ladies. But this particular one with glasses with blond highlights a was rude one. I never experienced a rude service like this and that is the reason I am writing this review. Like in other costa, for customers use they keep extra chocolate sprinkles and caramel sprinkles like that at the counter or somewhere, so I saw that here and I was just looking and reading the label like, so this girl very rudely said, “ excuse me! Could you please leave that alone”! and instantly apologised to her saying I thought it’s for customers because it’s kept right at the place at the counter and she said no! And later I went to ask for some extra chocolate sprinkles and she literally put like approximately two spoons, it was a lot a common person with common sense won’t do that, and I said ohh that too much and laughed! Her audacity to say ohh sorry u didn’t say when to stop. That was deliberately done, u could say . And I went back to my seat without saying anything more to her and she said do u want me to make a new 🆕 one. I said that ok , thanks 🙏. So this made my dad very bad in the morning very disappointed for giving such unworthy people this kind a job.

Visited this costa for a drink with friends. The service was very quick and staff were polite. Plenty of choice of different seating areas, however the air-con was quite strong on a fairly cool day, meaning the seating areas around this were uncomfortably cold, so we had to move to another table.

I’ve had the pleasure of using this Costa a fair bit over the past few months. It does exactly what you’d expect to find in a good Costa.
The staff are welcoming and friendly. There’s great consistency in the drinks and food.
Furthermore on the one occasion my drink wasn’t what I’d expected, they were very happy me to make a fresh brew for me. ( the issue was skimmed milk as opposed to my usual semi). It’s funny how sometime the way a complaint is dealt with gives you a better perspective of the people you’re dealing with.
This branch us in a wonderful location being just off the old junction of the canal between Regency Basin, Cambrian Basin and Sherbourne Wharf. It’s a lovely place to spend time.

We Stopped for a coffee at Costa Utillia Birmingham Brindley Place on a quite Sunday afternoon. The shop was quite with only a handful of customers. What was astonishing was the number of tables that had not been cleared. It looks dirty and unwelcoming hence we left. There were two staff behind the counter chatting and laughing. Very disappointing will think again before giving them my custom..

I have been to this costa a few times in the past and service/quality always seems to change. However, i thought i would write a review on my experience today.

The staff were fabulous today. They were a patient and a kind attitude. Unfortunately they didn’t have any assistance because the other members of staff were in the back. However, on their own (and still training) they made the most fabulous drinks.

If you are going to be sitting in, there is a large sitting area for all. However, keep up the work!

Was 9am on a Sunday morning. Stopped for a takeaway coffee with my dog to be told they don’t allow them.

I thought Costa did. Anyway just won’t get my money nor a good review.

Costa coffee are so mean they only have one member of staff on in the early morning.  This means customers have to wait for what seems an eternity to be served.  The lady behind the counter is under pressure and doing her best. Another member of staff for the first hour should be employed. At the extortionate prices costa charge this would equate to three cups of coffee. Costa put customer experience first and profits second.
Very convenient location whether you’re a patient or visiting. The staff are always friendly 😊
Excellent staff so welcoming like a prop coffee shop very pleased with Taz,Priya,Sofia,Raymond doing fantastic job 😀 👏
Nice

Visited this coffee shop on 19/1/26 evening time and was disappointed with the customer service. One staff member serving was very rude and dismissive, which made the interaction uncomfortable and unwelcoming.
The hot chocolate and cheese sandwich itself was fine, but good service is just as important. I hope the management addresses this, as it affects the overall experience.

Conveniently located near Snow Hill train station for what I imagined to be a good place to stop to do a call before getting on the train.  The place itself is nice - feels like there is plenty of room, more tables downstairs etc - but - no WiFi!  Perhaps it is making a statement about getting people to mindfully enjoy their drinks and pastries, to look up from their devices and talk to people around them, to realise that the joy of humanity is not in a laptop or a phone...well if so, good for them, but if you want to do a call before getting a train, don't stop at this one...

Fantastic staff and service, quick and professional with a friendly demeanor!
However due to the surrounding environment the place would be better with a security guard for both the staff and customers, people come in and out stealing once a while, the surrounding stores have security guards and Costa should too.
Otherwise would be a 5 star
My beautiful dog and I make this costa one of our regular places, staff are so friendly and helpful even when busy

Me and my boyfriend come in to Costa evening time on Saturday night and we was greeted by the lovely Djalo and Cadi who were beautiful, bubbly women. Their service was impeccable and it was so nice to see the incredible energy that they both radiate. The most friendly, nicest workers!!

I went to this Costa last month and I had a coupon so I just wanted to redeem that, but the staff said they are not accepting any kind of coupons or discounts, however the one of their staff members who was nice gave me 10% discounts from his side. The ambience was very quiet too. Overall my experience was not as good as I was expecting to be.

The coffee is nothing short of perfection – rich, smooth, and full of flavor. Each sip feels like it’s been crafted with care and expertise. The atmosphere is warm and inviting, with a perfect blend of cozy and vibrant energy. Whether you’re here to work, catch up with a friend, or simply enjoy some quiet time, the space feels like it was made for you.

The barista deserves a special mention – their passion and friendliness shine through in every interaction. They’re not only skilled but also go above and beyond to make you feel welcome, adding a personal touch that truly elevates the experience. Can’t wait to come back!

I activated my voucher (timed voucher to be used within the hour) by the time I reached the till I had been waiting for almost 15mins. I wanted a Spanish latte to be told they had sold out, which is fair enough as it’s a new drink, quite popular so I understand the sell out but a notice to say that had sold out would have been better, I waited all that time to not get what I wanted. I then went for a small caramel latte, I went to pay using my £1 voucher for this to be rejected and was told the voucher could only be used in participating stores, agreed not the cashiers fault but this didn’t help with the long waiting times and not being able to get what originally wanted which left a bitter taste in my mouth. I visited the store on a busy Friday lunch time, one would have expected more people to be serving especially being that the store is on the train station. Today, I visited this Costa with three friends. Two of my friends ordered drinks from Costa, while I had already purchased a Chaiiwala tea from outside. We intended to sit in Costa, as we regularly do at many other Costa branches and have never had an issue before.

A male staff member (I am unsure of his name) approached us on three separate occasions, repeatedly telling me that I was not allowed to drink my tea because it was not purchased from Costa. After initially ignoring the comments, he returned again and publicly pointed at other tables, saying, “Look at these tables, no one else has food or drinks from another place, so you cannot do the same.”

This was extremely humiliating, especially as other customers were watching. As a result, my friends and I felt uncomfortable and embarrassed, so we got up and left.

I understand policies regarding outside food and drink if no purchase is made. However, we had ordered drinks from Costa, and the situation could have been handled discreetly and respectfully. Instead, it was addressed in a way that made us feel singled out and embarrassed.

Due to this experience, I will not be returning to this branch and would not recommend it to others.

I’m writing this review after two separate visits to the same Costa Coffee branch, both of which were negatively impacted by the same staff member.

On my first visit, I had an extremely unpleasant experience. From the moment I interacted with this staff member, his body language and tone were rude and unwelcoming. When I requested a caramel frappe with soya milk, he questioned me repeatedly: “Is it allergy-related?” I explained it was a preference, yet he continued to speak over me in a patronising tone, almost arguing about my choice. He seemed irritated and dismissive throughout the entire interaction. Despite the poor customer service and the drink tasting off, I decided to let it go—assuming he may have been having a bad day.

Unfortunately, during my second visit, I encountered the same staff member again. His behaviour was just as unpleasant—if not worse. He was not at the till when I entered, and when I waited patiently, he stared at me from the dessert section with an arrogant expression before eventually coming over. I placed the same order again: caramel frappe with soya milk and extra caramel. Yet again, he asked, “Is it an allergy or a preference?” I answered, “No,” but he stared at me blankly and asked again as if I hadn’t spoken. His attitude was grumpy, dismissive, and completely unprofessional.

This staff member seems deeply unhappy in his role. His attitude is not only uninviting but creates an uncomfortable environment for customers. The drink quality was again poor—nowhere near the usual Costa standard. His behaviour suggests either a lack of interest in the job or possible bias, which is deeply concerning.

He knows who he is He was a tall man with dark hair, of Filipino or Asian descent. I am not writing this to target anyone unfairly, but to raise a valid concern about repeated poor service from the same employee. If this behaviour continues unchecked, it will damage the reputation of the branch and drive loyal customers away. I sincerely hope management addresses this issue and takes it seriously. No customer should feel unwelcome or spoken down to over something as simple as their choice of milk.

I recently visited Costa Coffee in Wylde Green, Birmingham to celebrate a birthday, and it was a fantastic experience.

The atmosphere was cosy and welcoming, perfect for a relaxed celebration. There’s plenty of space inside, with comfortable chairs that made us feel at ease while we enjoyed our drinks and treats.

Parking was hassle-free with up to 2 hours free, which is always a bonus. The café is wheelchair accessible, including accessible toilets, making it a great spot for everyone to enjoy.

We were served quickly, and the staff members were polite and attentive, ensuring we had everything we needed.

While Costa’s prices can be on the higher side, it’s not too noticeable unless you’re a daily visitor. Overall, for a special occasion like a birthday, it was absolutely worth it!

Highly recommend Costa Wylde Green for a relaxing and pleasant experience.
