Avoid at all costs. I went last Sunday with my partner and had a Brie and cranberry toasty, which wasn’t worth the price tag anyway but he ate most of it. I then had a Black Forest hot chocolate, regular and this is what gave me food poisoning. It didn’t taste off but I hadn’t had anything else that day a within 10 mins of consuming it I had a bad stomach ache start. 30 mins later I got in bed in so much pain in my stomach I was crying. 15 mins later I was on the bathroom floor feeling sick and ill and sweating. After an hour I was sick for the first time and it was so violent and mostly the hot chocolate. This continued all of Sunday evening and for 3 days I couldn’t eat much from stomach cramps. The service was also poor and I’d describe it as ungrateful: not looking at me and being really slow with the order. I’m not going to Costa again and I’ve seen lots of similar cases online about Costa with cream causing food poisoning and high traces of salmonella on their surfaces and coffee machines. I just want to warn people so they don’t have to go through what I did right before Christmas.

Popped by just to grab a snack and a drink while in the area.
It didn't seem too busy but everything seemed delayed. Yet this may have been due to the drive thru orders. The staff were nice to deal with. Though the cafe was clean, it felt cramped, the space between tables was tight and they weren't even full. The price caught me on the hop, but talking to one of the other customers they recommended getting the app for a lot of promotional discounts.
Loved the fact there was a random tap to top up your water bottle for free.
Lots of parking outside.
Enjoy.
Visited twice and both times it was a great experience. Clean coffee place with nice staff and well made coffees!

Service was slow despite there being three staff on (none of which seemed to be in a particular hurry to do anything).

Not been into any Costa for quite some time. Didn't look at the prices before ordering (otherwise would have not bothered). Having already requested two Cappuccinos and being asked for £8.20 was too polite (or stupid/embarrassed!) to decline.

Couldn't fault the coffee once I had it but (due to the price) it will be a very long time before I go into any Costa again and this particular branch (due to price and slow service) not again at all.
Spent hours in here studying.  Great coffee and nice friendly staff too.

I recently visited Costa Coffee and had an amazing experience. The atmosphere was warm and welcoming, and the staff were friendly and attentive. My drink was made perfectly—rich, smooth, and full of flavor. It was the ideal place to relax and enjoy a great cup of coffee. Highly recommend!

First time here and really like this costa. One of the very best I've ever been to. It is very big with lots of seating and as it's new the place is very clean and tidy. Hope they keep this up. Drinks were good and the food was nice. Highly recommend a visit here.

Costa fortune for awful and ignorant service. Female staff more interested in writing inappropriate messages on male customers cups  and flirting while ignoring other customers.......shocking and appalling. Also i was not advised for 20p more i could have had a medium coffee. Coffee was cold and rest assured i will never return........

Aimee served me at the drive through today and what a polite and friendly lady!!

She is what you call service with a smile! And thanks for the marshmellows.
Always drop in when I’m around the area, Basia does the best flat white out of any other costas I’ve been in, she’s a great asset to the store

Updating my review of this from a 4 star to 5 star review. I previously reviewed at 4 stars due to lack of seating space.

Since the re-fit there are more seats available but the staff here are some of the best I have experienced at a Costa. As other reviews have said, they are beyond what I've experienced in other coffees shops. Such welcoming smiles and treat every customer as an individual, no matter how busy they are they make time to smile and take your order in such a polite way.

If any head office staff read this review, I have been a customer here for over 5 years, the staff here deserve an award for their consistent level of exceptional customer service 🫶
Great little place for a quick breakfast while waiting for the huge charity shop across the road to open :-) served promptly and with smiles (a rarity at this hour lol) xxx
Popped in for a cuppa, nice place friendly staff

Complaint Regarding Hygiene at Costa Coffee
Date: July 22, 2025
Approximate Time of Incident: 9:20 AM - 9:35 AM
Subject: Serious Concern Regarding Staff Hygiene Practices
To Whom It May Concern,
I am a regular customer of Costa Coffee, and unfortunately, today, July 22, 2025, between approximately 9:20 AM and 9:35 AM, I witnessed an incident that has raised serious concerns about your branch's hygiene standards.
One of your staff members, while on duty, petted a customer's dog and immediately after, without thoroughly and properly washing their hands, continued with their tasks (which involved preparing or serving beverages/food).
This action is highly unsanitary and has undermined my trust in the cleanliness and hygiene of your establishment. As someone who values hygiene greatly, and considering the presence of individuals with allergies and sensitivities in the community, this incident is unacceptable to me.
I kindly request that you take this matter seriously and implement the necessary measures to re-train your staff on strict adherence to hygiene protocols, especially handwashing after contact with animals, to prevent such incidents from recurring.
Thank you for your attention to this matter.

We always come here for a coffee and have a great time staff are lovely and polite! We had a slight issue (not the stores or staffs fault) which was resolved so well by Roberto who was very understanding and helpful. Thank you.

We only stopped here because we couldn’t find a decent independent coffee shop. Costa Coffee is good not great but what it has going for it is the seating area. I find that the cakes and food are very bland compared with other good independent shops, Costa needs to do better.

Excellent coffee shop perfect location in Boldmere high street.

Very busy and the atmosphere is great.

Hot toasties are available different types of seating from sofa to chairs and service was fast and good.

Rather expensive for a regular specialty coffee. I considered the vitamin B12 coffee (see photos), but at £3.95 for a regular size, I declined.
I had a Flat White for £2.80
I won't return until the restaurant has a selection of value for money coffees. This may happen when the pubs are allowed to trade.
You can pay with cash.
Good selection of sweets treats
The service was excellent served and seated very quickly by the barista i was served by jack who was very efficient at making our drink so me and my wife could be seated and enjoying the coffee.

Very bad experience at Costa Coffee. I visited on Monday morning with a valid app voucher, but the manager refused to honour it. She was rude, unprofessional, and didn’t even try to help or explain. It felt like she just didn’t care. This kind of attitude is unacceptable, especially from a well-known brand. False promises, poor service, and a complete lack of respect for customers. I won’t be coming back, and I’d strongly suggest others think twice before visiting this branch.

We came in today for an iced latte and were served by Luke—he was so kind, friendly, and welcoming. However, we noticed another staff member who came across as quite bossy and unfriendly toward the team, which could be handled in a more positive way. Huge thanks to Luke—you’re amazing!

Wasn’t keen. If you want a quiet coffee then you have got no chance here. Staff are extremely loud with the cutlery and saucers/plates/mugs. Is it competition for who’s the loudest?
Quickly drank my drink and went. I’ll stick to greggs.

Nice hot and cold coffee and tea,  it's my favourite store and going regularly there by myself or others.
Also, fair prices for drinks are quite expensive for food and snacks.
But friendly and professional service.
Highly recommended 👌 👍
Bacon Roll for a quid with any medium hot drink purchased before 11:00am. Couldn't resist the offer. Have to say it was rather better than I was expecting.

Cash only today although I am of an age to remember how to use it.
Bit of a wait to be served with a polite apology although my request to have my waiting time knocked off the bill fell on deaf ears.
Sadly no toilet is available and of course any ensuing inconvience is apologised for(tell my bladder that) however the public facilities are located round the corner in the shopping centre so it saves any unnecessary cleaning on Costa's part
I go there on weekends with my family. Tables always dirty, they sometimes give drinks takeaway on weekends and I don't understand why if we drink in. These photos were taken over the weekend.

4 members of staff I thought great Costa isn’t treating them like slaves, but why is the service soo slow? Looking at other comments there seems to be a similar theme. Costa’s Coffee is good not great but what it has going for it is the seating area. I find that the cakes and food are very bland compared with other good independent shops, Costa needs to do better.

The staff at this Costa are some of the rudest I’ve ever encountered. In particular the woman with the lower lip piercings. She simply doesn’t want to be here.
There is no welcome, please or thank you. They make you feel incredibly unwelcome and god forbid you ask a question to the wrong “station” - “that’s not my job I don’t do food”. Seriously need some customer service training in this busy location.

Excellent service. Arianna was such a lovely girl and makes excellent coffee!
Ps is she single

Yesterday, me and my friends went inside for coffee as it was raining outside. We ordered mocha. I was very excited to taste costa coffee as i heard they do nice coffee but what a disappointment. The worst mocha i ever had.

Paul the manager was so n his own at 8:30 in a morning, and was looking after everyone with 1000% energy and effort ! This is the type of person you need on your team ! Especially at 8:30am on a Sunday morning
I love the Altrincham Costa Coffee branch! It is always so clean and well maintained and all the staff members work so hard. They are always so friendly too. Drinks always made perfectly :)
Food came relatively quick and the barista didn’t blend my boba this time! (A different Costa blended my boba so I had 1) The food was great, would recommend.
Once again here in Altrincham store ordered a Cappuccino and got a Latte in a Cappuccino cup. Why is it so had to for coffee shops in Altrincham to make a proper Cappuccino?
No idea the man who served me (in a Santa hat) but one of the nicest and most friendliest people I've ever seen. Made my day in 5 seconds

I went to Costa this morning in the drive thru with my colleagues. We had made a big order and got all drinks with oat milk. 2 gingerbread oat lattes and 1 oat white hot chocolate.

Turns out the white hot chocolate was cows milk. It’s a good job I’m just lactose intolerant and not deathly allerigic so the only thing that was dead today was the toilet.

Just a heads up to listen clearly when taking orders.
author_name: Luigi “Big man Luigi big dog” Martello

review_text: We placed an order on uber eats over an hour ago. Paid over £20 and every driver kept cancelling. We managed to speak to a driver who told us ‘the man at the till is very dismissive’ and is saying the order doesn’t exist? There is absolutely no way of contacting the Costa shop so we’ve been sat waiting for them to finally cancel the order (which proves it did exist) for over an hour.

Entered the store today to receive my 1 pound three network offer I was unfortunately served by a women with black hair and European accent the minute she saw I had the offer she looked at me in disgust and judged me instantly when she made the drink I don’t know what she gave me but I ordered a strawberry and cream frappe and she gave me a small kids looking drink with no strawberry sauce and didn’t even taste like strawberry she obvs just palmed me off with a cheap milkshake when that’s not what I ordered the offer says you can have any size and she gave the smallest one without asking I came to this same store last week and got the same offer ordered the same drink and was given the right one and I was served by someone else this women clearly had an issue with me having an discount I would recommend re training on her manners with  the employee involved I’ve now left the shop angry when all I wanted was a drink trust me there was nothing luxury about this ‘milkshake I was served that was meant to be a frapee
I visit this costa more often than any other, I love it, the staff are really friendly and always get my order right, they make the hot chocolates consistently strong, which I love.

They forgot to put our food in the microwave. (They did do it after we asked where our food was.)

And whoever made my tropical mango bubble blended my boba - I had very few boba (1) it was still nice, but the boba is the reason the drink is so pricey.

Costa is my favourite shop, but this one was the worst I have been to.

I visit most weekends and the staff are so welcoming. They really make you feel a valued customer and are interested in how your days been. I visited today Friday and the young man I have been served by on a weekend has really come out with more confidence, after a hard week at work he made me feel listened to whilst waiting for my coffee as normally in my job I am the one who has to listen. Thank you ☺️
We had a breakfast in their service is top very nice place 10/10 to the staff

Was recently in Costa Timperley. The staff are always really lovely & helpful, but on this particular day, there was a lady working there who was going above and beyond for an elderly gentleman who had lost his wife. She was absolutely amazing and so kind, even walking his dog for him while he sat and had a cup of coffee. The staff are what make this store and you should be very proud!
Ben was super friendly and professional. Great customer service and a genuinely positive attitude. He made the whole Costa Coffee experience much better. Thanks!

Very nice service the lady was very helpful I bought bacon sándwiches by mistake because I’m vegetarian I can’t eat it was my mistake but she changed for me I really happy this service definitely it will be my place
Thank you for all staff ❤️😘

I visit the Costa Drive Thru at Citygate, Sale, every morning before my commute to work.  Chantelle and Craig are amazing with their welcoming smiles and are the funniest double act ever! They both brighten my day and should be recognised for the fantastic customer service they offer :)

Costa Coffee really has lost its sparkle in recent years,but the Costa Coffee here has to be one of the worst!. The place just feels empty with zero character or friendliness. Young staff just going through the motions doing as little customer service as they can. No welcome, never mind warm welcome!. There are used cups and plates spread around,with staff not interested in tidying up, food under tables, mediocre selection of food on offer, obviously because there not keeping on top of ordering, the list goes on. I've worked and still do in the customer service industry for the last 35 years and all the staff here need to be retrained as they are doing the bare minimum,not interested in makeing customers welcome or happy just going through the motions waiting until there shift has finished. Shockingly bad!!!

Don’t even want to leave a review because as many people will rate this place, people will start to go there and the cozy atmosphere will be lost 😅
Very tidy cafe, great service, quiet environment- everything you need for a nice coffee time. Love it! 🥰
Great service from staff every time I visit but Stretford, Sale or Urmston  desperately need a 24hr coffee shop 🙏🏻🙏🏻🙏🏻

A Convenient Coffee Break

Located on the A56 Washday Road, this Costa Coffee Drive-Thru is a convenient pit stop for those traveling on the M60. We opted to dine in, taking advantage of the two-hour free parking.

Our order was promptly taken and fulfilled accurately. The drinks were piping hot, and the food was satisfactory. We appreciated the warm and friendly service provided by the staff.
posted 2/11/24

Ordered hot chocolate twice times here… yuck taste bland and not taste like hot chocolate at all!!!some more

the price is expensive than the other stores!! Avoid this costa if you want to get a nice drinks.

Went in to use laptop to work… got given the wrong order (oat milk not coconut) but the guy serving was so rude and miserable I couldn’t be bothered going back. Nice lady server though. Place was clean and tidy

Come in here regularly. Great service and fab coffee. I have reduced mobility and everyone always happy to bring coffee over. It's really popular with good music. A big thanks to everyone especially the Manager Suleman and Chris
Nice café with high quality beans.  Costa is the BEST!!!
The best café with a kind and friendly staff — definitely give it a try!”

Went in today and asked for 2 pizzas for me and my friend after my university lecture, i asked for this to be in a box as I would be travelling with it to which the staff replied back with no respect and huffed while getting it for us. We then asked if that box can be put in a bag due to travel and don’t have space in my own handbag to which he rudely said “but it is in a box” the staff need to be trained correctly with good customer service skills as this was unacceptable.

This has to be the worst greggs. 4 times now, when a bacon roll is ordered, they send something that looks like scraps, they put butter on there when specified not to due to allergies.

The staff are not suitable to handle bacon due to religion which is clear when you visit the store. This is not a racist comment, but a fact. NO MUSLIM will TOUCH PORK!

I will be filing a complaint regarding this, if they dont want to touch pork, dont work there. And before being accused of racism, I am a muslim man ordering for my friend, and even I find this treatment disgusting...

Greggs fix this!!!

Everything was alright except the staff the guy was so rude when i said him can u add some caramel for my drink and he started shouting at me that why you don’t say it before? May I know what is wrong with you? Am I looking like I can’t afford that 60p for that shot of caramel? I am on the way to my work and it just ruined my whole day

Thanks for the extra shot of your angerness instead of caramel mate 👍
The hygiene is poor, the staff is disrespectful, lacking common sense and manners towards customers. This is the worst Greggs location I have ever been to.
Whoever is recruiting the staff keep on doing it. The staff are wonderful so kind and professional perfect for the job. l look forward to my visit and I am never disappointed.

As a regular customer at Greggs, I recently noticed a new staff member who truly impressed me. She organized everything in the shop efficiently and maintained a clean, welcoming environment. Her customer service was outstanding, and her polite, friendly attitude made a big difference. She's a great addition!

I wanted to say thank you to the young woman who served me and my son earlier today. It was my son’s birthday and he was telling the young woman about it with such enthusiasm it can be quite distracting haha. She offered him a free doughnut which made him so happy, it’s just a small gesture but it goes a long way to building goodwill and returning customers in the community.
Thought I'd leave a review as it was the first greggs I've been too which wasn't able to warm up the cold baguettes, not sure if I'll visit again

This place is horrible. The workers are clearly unhappy with their jobs and take it out on the customers. would not be surprised if they spat in the food. you have to pay to literally breathe in this place. I had grabbed a snack from Sainsbury's and my friend got food from Greggs and paid extra to eat outside on their benches. I was not allowed to eat my snack. EVEN THOUGH I was outside the shop. depressing

One star to the sweet hardworking lady (no name badge so don’t know her name), and the pies (once reheated at home they taste great).

Then no stars to Brenda who is miserable, sarcastic and rude.

Pies are served cold every time I’ve been there - how is that possible when it’s taken out of a warmer?

I swing past here on Saturdays as I am in the area. I have gotten a sausage roll each week for the past month. Twice I’ve gotten the regular sausage roll, which was hot and tasty. Twice I’ve gotten the vegan sausage roll, which was cold. The flavor is good, but a cold pastry just doesn’t satisfy in the same way a hot one does.

The service here in the mornings is very frustrating. The two older ladies working behind the counter spend far too much time chatting with regular customers — not just now and then, but every single day during the morning rush. This causes long queues, and it feels like they don’t realise people are in a hurry to get to work, especially when trying to catch their train. I’ve had to walk out 2 times this week alone due to the delays and being in a rush to catch my train.

They really need to be separated on shifts, as I can see the other staff members getting annoyed at being left to do all the work while they stand chatting. It’s fine to be friendly when it’s quiet, but at peak times it comes across as unprofessional and inconsiderate. A mystery shopper should visit during rush hour, as less chatting and more focus on serving would make a big difference.
Visit this branch every week for a large cup of tea and am always pleased with the service as well as the product.

What a lovely shop, you always feel welcomed by Jill and Ellie they always have the biggest smile on their face and give my son the biggest wave.
My son has SEN needs and they cater for his needs without judging its very friendly shop.
The food is lovely and fresh and it’s very clean shop you can see the shop is well looked after.
Thank you everyone in the shop for making my son feel welcomed it means so much to us.
Piping hot and fresh sausage roll. Used to be better, guess inflation took a toll.

Small counter opened inside the station and it's usually good with service but today a couple of staff members were discussing something amongst themselves and the lady serving me got the order wrong - asked for a chicken bake she thought I asked for two of them. Corrected her and then got back to work to see that she gave me a sausage roll instead of what I had asked for. Charged me for the chicken bake though so paid extra for something I don't want at all. On top of that the push to get the app is a bit annoying. Will try the bigger store outside the station next time.

asked worker how long my sandwich will take and got the response of 2 minutes. I was in a rush and imediatly said yes and paid. It took 6 minutes as the workers showed no effort moving slowly despite me obviously being in a rush. Causing me to miss my train be late for school. Never going back again, sandwhich was mid anyway
Nayomi did excellently in understanding my needs as a customer and was prompt with their service! 5 stars!

I’m a huge fan of greggs as a place to get my food on the go.  I never come to this branch for different issues that will explain, but as I was passing by and was hungry decided just to get a steak bake. I told the lady behind the counter TWO times Steak bake and I paid and left. I forgot to scan my app but to be fair she didn’t even asked and in my local greggs they always do. When i went to give a bite it was VEGETABLE bake, where’s the meat??? no steak… very disappointed. This is probably the last time I will come to this branch. They were not busy at all to say it was a simple mistake… What would happen if I had allergies? Cross contamination? It’s always a disappointment to come by. I love greggs but I can never buy cookies packs or a sandwich because they never have? I was already skeptical as the store always seem quite dirty but always something wrong? You missed a customer here. I wonder where’s their manager? Is this a standard in greggs? The one next my house is always great, even on busy times…
Food is always cold, and 3 quid for a cold pizza is a bit of a joke. Staff are friendly.

I have visited several Greggs stores and have always had positive experiences but on this occasion the manager was extremely rude He did not take the time to listen to my complaint and spoke in a very unprofessional and disrespectful manner
Worst experience with the management especially with manager paul because of his ride behavior and the worst food quality with cheap products
such a lovely place and amazing breakfast

No hygiene whatsoever, staff handling cash and handling food without washing hands or using gloves.

Due to COVID 19 staff are supposed to wear a mask but they have it handing over the chin instead of covering the mouth and nose.

Seems to only have one staff member serving most the time and during busy periods there are no additional staff members that use their common sense to help out, they would rather talk in the back and keep customers waiting huddled in the small area in the front.

Poor management and hygiene should have a visit from trading standards.

Absolutely shocking. Had you waiting in aye for 20 minutes then to tell you card machine is working. No communication. Was told to go get cash out of the cash machine and que back up!!! Wouldn’t go back there if I was having a diabetic hypo. Absolutely useless and waste of my times.

Greggs Tooting is really easy to get to, and it’s consistently a great place to stop for lunch or a snack. The staff—including Mercy, Amir, Ali, Vanessa, Surya and the whole team—are always incredibly welcoming, friendly and helpful. They really do go out of their way to make sure every visit is pleasant.

The food is reliably good, fresh and varied. I’m a big fan of the vegan roll (surprisingly tasty!), and their regular menu options always hit the spot. The Too Good To Go bags from this branch are especially good value—always generous, well packed, and a smart way to grab a variety of bakery treats while helping cut down on waste.

Greggs Tooting is a solid, affordable place to grab a quick meal or snack, and the team here definitely make it even better. Highly recommended if you’re in the area!
While this branch isn't as spacious as others, the staff's friendliness and efficiency make up for it. I was in and out in under 10 minutes.

I am delighted to share my positive experience with the store; it truly deserves recognition. The service provided was outstanding, and the team's attentiveness made the visit very enjoyable. I must commend Surya, whose exceptional service and friendly demeanor enhanced the overall experience. Furthermore, the quality of the food was excellent, adding another layer of satisfaction to the experience. Overall, it was a wonderful visit, and I look forward to returning soon.

I highly recommend trying the Fajita Chicken Bake; it is a personal favorite.

The pizza was exceptionally satisfying, and I can't tell if my hunger heightened the experience. Nevertheless, I was thoroughly impressed. It featured a delightful crunch on the outside, complemented by a pleasing texture as the cheese melted in my mouth.
Cold pasteries and confusing communication by staff. They placed one pastry by the till but not the other half of my order.

I’ve given Greggs 2 stars just because the girl on training today was so good! She was lovely and smiley, which is unusual for this branch of Greggs, and she alone would have gotten 5 stars. I originally gave it 1 star because of the fact I didn’t receive from the app what I was promised. I’ve reposted the original review I left and the response from Greggs in the images. They never got back to me. Of course they didn’t. But anyway the trainee today gets top marks!

This is by far the worst Greggs in London! The staff are ting and childish. They stay round the back talking and you have to call them to be served. They have zero customer service and there is never any food after 1pm. Close it down and open an M&S instead!

Was told by management that I'm not allowed to receive ketchup with the purchase of sausage rolls, I told them that the staff do it every single day as I am a regular and have never been told I'm not allowed ketchup. I said I'm more than happy to pay if that's the case and required. however, the manager would not even take my money for ketchup. he said the only way I could get ketchup is if I purchased hot food, I said I bought two sausage rolls and that is hot. so what is your point?, he said no, we don't count those as the hot food items. so he said I would have to buy one of their hot food line items to get ketchup, I called customer support and they offered me a free hot food voucher in order for me to get ketchup, I personally find sausage rolls inedible without ketchup and I find it very hard to believe a place whose main dish is sausage rolls don't offer free ketchup or even the ability to purchase ketchup. and I think the manager of the store needs a reality check and a lesson in pleasing customers.

Visited around 1pm on a Monday and I was shocked by the level of cleanliness in a food establishment. It wasn't busy at all and their were 2 members of staff behind the counter. yet the tables were all dirty, bin overflowing and dont get me started on the state of the toilets. Only positive is, the service received was quick and efficient.

Their fresh bakes are excellent, moderately priced, comes to you piping hot! Located just next to a large supermarket. Huge carpark. Very busy during lunchtimes. If no indoor seating is available, you can organise a picnic box from their range of pastries and baked goods and sit on the many benches on the terrace. Also try their pizza bites. Occasionally I order their bacon omelette breakfast rolls – excellent! Staff can be stressed sometimes – thats what you’d see when the shop is full of hungry people. but they usually keep their cool and are always respectful – it was the hottest day this year!

Place was filthy. Floor hadn’t been swept in hours, maybe all day. Tables hadn’t been wiped down either.
Only one staff member behind servers and one stacking shelves.
First time at a Greggs. Never again.

What’s with the famous Greggs sausage rolls? Tasteless that’s what.

I was about to give the spicy chicken bits two stars because I didn't like them. The flavour was just not to my taste, but I did receive soup cheese nibbles along with a butter bun.

It was so delicious that I have to give it a big thumbs up. The service was also really helpful in helping me pick some bread that went really well with the soup

I am a regular customer of this store, because I pass this Greggs on my way to work everyday. The staff is familiar with me because I visit everyday, and they are super friendly and the service is amazing. I don't need to comment on the food because I am a huggeeee Greggie. I wish the store every success. Special thanks to Syed who always goes above and beyond to provide exceptional customer service. However, if you could please clean your tables as soon as customers leave, that would be greatly appreciated, hence 4* on the atmosphere aspect. Thank you Greggs!

Even though there are closer Greggs locations to me, this branch is always my go-to when ordering through Deliveroo. They consistently have the full selection of pizzas available, including the BBQ chicken one which is often missing elsewhere. The food always arrives fresh, piping hot, and perfectly made. It’s reliable, fast, and definitely worth choosing over the others. Never had a bad experience with them.
Zach is one of the employees that work there and he was extremely fast and understanding.

Greggs is usually a banker and great value. Got stung this morning with a very sloppy sausage breakfast roll, where even while watching, the server poured brown sauce all over the bap. Very mucky to eat and just a bit lazy.

I stop in at this Greggs whenever I’m back in London seeing friends, and the staff there are so great! Super friendly, always happy to have a chat, excellent customer service makes the difference with chains, and they’re truly excellent.
Kojo’s service was fantastic - he was really friendly and welcoming. And called the guy back in front when he forgot his change. Really brilliant customer service from him - great experience today.

The worst branch ever!!!! I’m shocked at the way the package the food and don’t follow order instructions? It was simple to follow the order and yet they still fail to provide the items ordered and paid for?! Staff need better training because I’m absolutely appalled

Kojo the gentleman that served me gave a service I've never seen before in any greggs. From taking extra initiative to put my Tottenham cakes in a box to his service with a amazing smile and a all over friendly encounter. His customer service skills are 10 out of 10.

Shit Marketing

The offer looks like it applies to all sausages , but it’s vegan-only when you try to order.
Disgusting way to lie to customers 🤮

There appears to be a lack of organization and cleanliness in the establishment. The tables are often messy, and the store is generally untidy. The staff does not seem to be attentive to cleaning and maintaining the tables. Additionally, the staff members frequently appear to be in a negative mood.

It is unclear whether the customers are the cause of this situation. Furthermore, there is a leak in the ceiling, which I believe is caused by the air conditioning unit. The leak is not minor; it is significant enough that a large bucket has been placed in the middle of the floor to catch the dripping water. The water drips onto the floor and customers as they pass the bucket to reach the counter. It may be advisable to consider temporarily closing the establishment until the issue is resolved.

I use them from time to time for 2good2go orders, usually get good amount of food, some sweets when I'm lucky.

Can't complain about service like others but I agree about general untidiness of this establishment and visible wear and tear. Don't think it's a nice place to stay for longer, but collecting food - yes please.

Food is good too, cheap sausage rolls, decent sandwiches but don't expect you will be attacked by rush of flavours either.
Fantastic service by young lady called Telisha her service and communication is amazing. I hope I have spelt her name correctly. She was on duty 3/11/2025

I was at greggs this morning, I wanted to buy only 1 steak bake and go mind my business, the guy who served me made me change my mind to buy 2 and even bought a drink. the guy which I don’t know his name gave me a friendly customer service, very professional. And also with his  smile which. Why don’t you provide your staff with name badges it’s just common Sense Greggs.

It's Greggs, so don't expect getting your tastebuds blow but it's decent food. I was surprised by pizza piece, it's very old-school and reminds of pizza I could eat back in old good country in 90's. Crunchy but hard base, quite filling and good to kill smaller hunger.
Chicken sandwich was very meh though, it has lots of ingredients but barely any flavour, I didn't feel I'm eating chicken, could use some spices.

Service could be a bit rough.

Based it Tesco so don't expect much. It's decent.
Being served my beloved black Americano during my work break is the highlight of my shift. Staff members are efficient and professional bringing smile to positive energy. Great job!

Ordered sausage rolls, was informed they went very hot. Said that’s ok. Once served they were stone cold.politely said to the girl serving these are stone cold, she then said I told u they weren’t very warm. I politely replied there  is a big difference between not very warm and stone cold. She then argued we’re not a  hot counter.annoyed that she had been called out when she  clearly had over cooked for a slow day as shelves full
Of cold food. And not many buying. Last time we attended we were offered hot ones from the oven, rude member of staff  who needs to be retrained in customer service without being rude to customers. Will see if use again but probably won’t.
Great service, very friendly and approachable staff. Had a stunning meal, nicely packed, fresh and tasty by a brown young gentleman. Definitely recommend getting the pepperoni pizza.
Excessive waiting time for delivery all the time.

I’m writing to raise an issue regarding an overcharge I experienced during a recent visit to your store. I requested a coffee with an extra shot, which—as per the signage in-store—should have incurred an additional charge of 60p. However, I was instead charged for both a mocha and an espresso, which significantly exceeds the advertised price for a simple extra shot.

When I queried this, I was informed that the till did not have an option to charge for an extra shot, and that the machine wouldn’t process it otherwise. However, I regularly order the same drink—an extra shot in a coffee—from other Greggs locations without any issue. In those cases, the correct 60p surcharge is always applied without confusion.

I’d appreciate clarification on this discrepancy, as well as a refund of the overcharged amount. I understand that technical limitations may vary by location, but charging more than the stated price, especially when a clear sign indicates otherwise, is misleading and unfair to customers.
Small shop but excellent service from staff on click and collect. Had it all ready nicely packaged. Efficient and good customer service

I went to Greggs on Mother’s Day to treat myself to a cheeky vegan steak bake and the service given by Jedai and his colleague was exemplary. I have to say now a days good service counts for everything! The only reason I gave a four star for food is that the steak bake was cold. However definitely will be going back as I loved the service. Some 5star restaurants could learn a lot from you lot ⭐️

Standard Greggs food. Can't complain for the price.

Staff however need people training. No level of customer service. A good morning is returned with a grunt by the staff. They do the bare minimum to serve. Almost a threatening atmosphere.

It doesn't cost a penny to smile or say good morning?

A reasonable greggs - however not always full stocked, due to its side and location

Staff are friendly

I'm not writing this to be a Karen or because I think my two pounds that I couldn’t spend will bankrupt Greggs. I get it — I love to sleep in too, and maybe they’re short-staffed and/or overworked. Just keep in mind that the shop doesn’t actually open at 6 a.m. as stated. Have a plan B for your breakfast.
Half of my order didnt show up and all the cookies were burnt

Opening on time seems to be an issue at so many Gregg's branches . 5 or 10 minutes isn't a massive problem but constantly 30 minutes + isn't good practice but the norm for Gregg's . As there are branches being closed in various areas ,staff can't be wondering why .  Unreliability maybe   !!!!!!!
Ordered a delivery half od order was missing cookies were burnt ham and cheese toastie was the worst ive ever had the misfortune of tasting.

Sundays this place should open at  8am.
Still not open at 08.25.
Always late opening.
Explicitly asked for two vegan sausage rolls, get home to see he gave me regular. Money wasted, food in the bin. Thanks.

Greggs, within the Esso garage, is a hidden gem. A great variety of meal deal items, including a drink.
I downloaded the 'Too Good to Go' app, which helps to stop food from going to waste! All food is fresh. And at a discounted price. Please install the app to take advantage of offers on a daily basis! It's surprisingly good!!!! (All 'Surprise' bags vary)
Always a pleasure, never a chore! Their stodge can break even the stubbornest of hangovers.

My fave stop off every morning! Coffee for my last section of commute and the staff are fantastic. Friendly and always a smile even at 6am!  A daily am and Pm coffee slot for me and I don't even need to ask for my coffee . They seem me coming.  Good food , good variety but it's the staff that make the visit.
The women had very bad attitude on her face and with customer service

I was collecting 'reserved' food for my freezer to eat at a later date. I recommend downloading the app 'Too Good to Go'. Save food from going to waste.

Greggs (136) was very busy! They were almost out of everything when I collected my 'surprise bag' at 4 pm. Another Greggs on Lewisham (High Street) cancelled my order for 'reserve' as all their surplus stock was sold out completely!

I am writing to formally raise a concern regarding an upsetting experience I had at your store on 19th April, between 16:00 and 16:05. I am a regular Greggs customer and often use the Greggs app to place my orders. I’ve always appreciated the service across various branches—until this visit.

As I entered the store, there was no staff present at the counter to greet or assist me. A team member walked past me in the aisle, heading outside, without acknowledging me at all. After a brief wait, I politely said, “Excuse me, can you please…”, to which she abruptly responded, “Can't you wait!” (first words from her mouth" while walking away and proceeded to the back of the store.

When she returned to the counter, I simply requested a slice of pizza and a mocha, pointing to the item. Her first response, in a flat and condescending tone, was: “Learn some manners, I’m in the middle of something here.” There was no greeting, no apology for the wait, no professionalism whatsoever.

Stunned, I asked, “I’m sorry, are you talking to me?” to which she replied, “Yeah, I’m talking to you—the rude guy.” I was genuinely speechless and deeply disappointed. I pride myself on being polite and respectful in every interaction, and I felt unfairly targeted and insulted without provocation.

To make matters worse, the pizza I received was cold, and there was no apology or acknowledgment of the poor service from any staff member.

I strongly urge management to review the CCTV footage and, if possible, audio recordings of this interaction. This level of customer service is unacceptable and damaging to the Greggs brand. Having worked in fast food myself for over a year, I understand the demands of the job—but there is never an excuse for rudeness or lack of basic courtesy.

I hope this matter is taken seriously and appropriate steps are taken to ensure no other customer has to endure such treatment in the future.

I visited this Greggs today for lunch and picked up a box labelled: ‘Southern Fried Potato Wedges’. Upon leaving the store and opening the box, I discovered that there were chicken goujons inside. I am vegetarian so this was very disappointing.

Update: After contacting Greggs customer care, they gave me £8 store credit for the inconvenience 👍🏾
Freezing cold pasty 110% convinced its been there since last night...drinks in the fridge also room temperature 2 hours after opening🤣🤣🤣swerve at all costs
The food is always tasty especially the sweet treats lol but prices have gone up :/ but still tasty bakes and great delicious treats, my kids love the Yum yums, biscuits and doughnuts!

Review Title: Disappointing Service and Pre-Prepped Coffee

The staff here seem to have an attitude problem. Many people in this area come from different backgrounds, and English isn’t the primary language for a lot of us. If a customer asks you to repeat something, it’s important to remain patient rather than show irritation.

Additionally, I noticed my coffee being served is pre-prepped, sitting in cup before the order is placed. While I understand mornings are busy, this practice raises concerns about freshness and hygiene. Please ensure coffee is made fresh for each customer to maintain quality and avoid contamination.

Literally never had a problem here. The people are always nice and polite and the food tastes as it always does at Gregg's. Just wanted to balance out the bad reviews. Don't avoid it - it's just Gregg's mate!

I’ve bought katsu chicken bake today - inside it was completely cold. I’ve asked politely to change it to the hot one. The lady who pretended to be a manager refused to do it. She accused me that is my fault because I didn’t check if it’s warm before taking a first bite and she won’t be able to re-sell it!!!!! Have you ever had such a nonsense???? They tried to food poison me with the cold bake and it was my fault!!!!
Please avoid this store as it’s happening constantly.
0 hygiene standards!! Just got a dry and cold pizza 🤧🤢🤢🤮
Overcharged on breakfast pastry deal . Challenged the price was told this is it !!

My first time at this Greggs location. I have to say, in all my years of frequenting Greggs, today was by far and away the best Greggs experience!!  The staff were cheerful, polite and very helpful.  The store was very clean and well presented.  My kids loved our little sit down break. Greggs shops can at times be very hectic and not very pleasant.  However today at this location everything was very nice. This is due largely to the staff, there was genuine care and attention to myself and kids as well as everyone else who came in while we were there.  I will make a point to come to this location more often as it is close to where we live.
I’ve been a few times today food and hot choc where stone cold! It’s very hot and Miss but I won’t be going back
Food was fresh, and had more variety than others. There are only 2 tables though - it would be nice if there were more but that’s just because of the size of the store.

I've been here 3 times and the food is always cold hardly anything was available to buy to eat fancied a steak bake had to settle for a chicken bake as thats the only thing I could get due to not eating pork when I asked if any stake bakes are coming out the answer was no I have to put them in the oven. I felt like saying PUT THEM IN THEN WHAT YOU WAITING FOR! But I didn't as I didn't want to be rude. Got some potato wedges for the kids which were half cooked, packet doughnuts were soggy upon opening luckily I was on my way home so I was able to heat up the chicken bake in the oven before eating it. I've just spent £18 and I feel like I have been robbed. This place needs better management someone that cares about serving people food. Wont be going back.
Inconsistent. Been a number of times the store opens very late or not at all. Food is either cold or out of stock. It’s really just a hit or miss

poor attitude from the lady staff Maija on 30/1/26 at 7.55am. i asked for mint hot choc and she made hot choc. instead of apologising she said i did not say mint. firstly what kind of customer service is this? secondly how are you sure that it could be you did not hear properly? next time make sure to train your staff to repeat the order and have proper customer service

I ordered 4 slices of Margherita and was really disappointed. The food came in a bag, poorly packaged, “supposedly delivered in a box”and the slices were completely dry, as if they had been sitting around for hours. When an order says “preparing,” it should mean the food is being freshly baked — not something made earlier in the morning. What I received was cold, dry, and far from what I expected. This kind of service feels careless and lazy. If they want to keep customers, they really need to focus on quality and delivering fresh food every time

Let's try something else before you end up here.

My wife is a big fan of Gregg's, probably because it's nostalgic for her.  Unfortunately, for me, this was a horrible morning snack experience.

I ordered what my wife recommended which was the sausage roll and seemed to be one of the only things I thought I would enjoy from their menu.  Unfortunately, I was less than pleased with the sausage roll.  Not only was it excessively greasy, but when I bit into it, I felt that the entire thing was undercooked.  The roll felt much too doughy and the sausage was cool to the touch and definitely not cooked long enough.  It was an extremely unpleasant snack and I don't think I'll ever go again.

11am the store was close with it shell full. Even in the afternoon it close randomly for good full an hour. Looks like the staff are avoiding long queues. Hope some manager will come across this message and have a look what's going on.

I had an amazing experience with customer service, thanks to Israa! From the moment I reached out, she was professional, friendly, and incredibly helpful. She responded quickly, listened carefully to my concerns,  went above and beyond to resolve my issue.

What stood out the most was Israa’s attention to detail and genuine willingness to help. She didn’t just provide a generic response—she personalized her approach, making me feel truly valued as a customer. She was knowledgeable, patient, and took the time to ensure all my questions were answered thoroughly about my concerns

It’s rare to come across such exceptional customer service, and I truly appreciate the effort Israa put into making sure I had a positive experience. Thank you, Israa, for your outstanding support!

10 STARS 🤩 The staff are brilliant and so nice to hear to be happy 😊

FESTIVE BAKES ARE IN! yummy yummy yummy ALWAYS a firm favourite at Christmas. 2025 Greggs in Chiswick is a large and busy Greggs with nice views of the church green.

As it Winter the falling leaves and the Christmas special menu items make an atmosphere way better than any shopping centre Greggs.

The large windows but cosy interior lighting in winter make this both a bustling hub and relaxing atmosphere at the same time.

In fact we stayed for a second sausage roll and bake 😊 it was a rainy day and the staff cheered us up😊

All the staff, everyone are brill, welcoming and happy. Fast at Keeping the tables clean as well which is tough with all the pastries 🙂

ALL MY REVIEWS ARE UNBIASED AND NOT INFLUENCED DIRECTLY OR INDIRECTLY also I don't know anyone who works for Greggs or anything and paid for my food with my own money.

So messy! The print from the wet packaging stuck all over my fingers before I even got to the food. Taste is just okay, nothing special, but the way it’s made is a complete disaster. Whoever is preparing this needs to actually spread the filling properly across the baguette, not dump it all in one half and leave the rest empty. It’s impossible to eat like this and honestly just frustrating.

What we love about the new Chiswick Greggs is the lovely gentleman who works there called Paul.  Paul is always smiling, so helpful and makes us smile with his fabulous hats and different accessories he wears.  We go in there almost every day for a coffee and a sandwich.

It’s always pretty popular and great that they’ve extended the shop to now include tables and chairs. Very reasonable prices as well and even better value with the Greggs app and the meal deals. Great value for money.

Greggs management - Paul is an absolute asset to your company!!
What a wonderful addition to our high street. Place is clean, well stocked and at helm is a wonderful manager Rachel. Thank you for looking after us and sorting out my large cappuccino.
The staff here just made my day, they're brilliant! Plus always great value at Greggs ☺️✨

Hammersmith Greggs in the shopping centre.
I noticed some of the workers there, they keep rubbing there noses with there bare hands, but never go to wash their hands afterwards. I noticed also today that someone was scratching and rubbing there ears, as if they had a bad itch, but never did this gregg staff go to wash her hands, I had no choice but to leave the store without buying. The staff that are rubbing  their noses with their bare hands need to consider that they touch bags which the customer may use to eat of from or even touch. The staff need to think about health and safety when handling customers food. I imagine it is a lot worser problem behind where no cannot see anything.

I should have left this review a long time ago. The staff here are very friendly, human, helpful. That, and the location, is far too appealing to the point they remember my order. When it's busy, multitasking, teamwork and efficency is far better than other branches and stores. Great service!
I am daily hot chocolate consumer since I drank their double cream hot chocolate. Happy place to start your mornings. ✨

I wanted to balance out some of the bad reviews about this particular Greggs. People are quick to complain but not so quick to compliment. I often get a roll & coffee from this branch on my way to work. The staff are always friendly, work efficiently, and the roll filling is plentiful. My colleagues at work often ask if I went to 'the one in the station' as they also agree it's one of the better Greggs because of these positive traits. Another reviewer mentioned a staff member, TP, who I have to agree is part of the reason I returned to this branch in the first place; such pleasant interaction/service. Turns out that, after several visits, all the staff there are similar in their approach to customer service.
Recommended!

I have never experienced customer service quite as poor as I did today at Greggs and unfortunately it was down to just one staff member. A short Asian lady I think her name might begin with “In” or “I” based on her badge was incredibly rude and dismissive. She rushed me as if she were serving a queue of a million people, creating unnecessary pressure and making the entire interaction really unpleasant.

I completely understand that working in a busy environment like Greggs isn't easy, especially during peak hours. But there's a basic level of patience and courtesy that customers expect and, to be fair, the rest of the team seemed polite and professional. It’s a shame one person’s attitude can impact the whole experience.

Everyone has tough days, but taking it out on customers isn’t acceptable. I hope she can receive the right support or training to improve, because no one should leave feeling worse than when they walked in.

This could be a decent branch of Greggs if they actually *opened* when they were supposed to. They are constantly sold out of stock and the staff are not consistent.
Small changes will make for a more profitable establishment!

Very kind and patience staff as I do take time to think what I would like. They were very nice and friendly every time I visited the store. The manager was so kind with his offer and service. My mobile wallet failed to work, and I didn't bring my actual bank card on my visit. He had definitely made my day. Thank you! May the kindness return to you and multiple each and every day!
I purchased their Too Good To Go bags occasionally, and they had been very generous with it.
It's a small, friendly neighbourhood Greggs store.
Very good pricing. Ve dry good service. Just wish there was a seating option.

always really, really bad service. staff take way too long to prepare an order and almost every pastry i’ve ever gotten is stone cold. don’t even bother with the breakfast baguettes, half of the filling is outside of the sandwich and the sauce is carelessly slapped on.wont be going back again

This was a really good Greggs but lately the food is always stone cold, no food at all, went there and there was croissants nothing else and staff are very happy to say wait 10/15 minutes there needs to be consistency with these things.

This is an absolute disgrace of a Greggs. The attitude of the staff as you walk in and get served through the way they talk to you when you ask for a certain food item that is not on display.

We ordered The Festive Bake and I found it ironic that the customer service was at the other end of the spectrum, showing zero festive spirit. This company has fallen along way since the early days of the branches opening in London. Prices are going up and the quality is in free fall! Is there no quality control with the items they sell or for that matter a branch manager who looks at these reviews and addresses both the issue and the review left. The festive bake looked full and filled with festive cheer, but one bite in and the whole bake deflated like a lead balloon.  I would highly recommend going to Iceland in future and purchasing the frozen versions to bake in the oven at home. Better yet when in Iceland go to the self service checkout and you are guaranteed to experience a highly level of individual customer care and service from the AI in the machine then the human in the Gregg's that served us nothing but attitude and arrogance. 0/10!

Went yesterday around 2pm and I can definitely confirmed that the lady at the counter has no courtesy and respect. No hi, no hello : straight to why Would you like with rolling eyes 🙄
For the manager : please hire people who really want to work. It just spoiled my appetite.

I’ve been here twice, and neither time did they have any pork sausage rolls - only the vegan ones that look like cardboard. So I left empty-handed. I’m beginning to wonder whether there are religious/cultural reasons for the non-availability of pork - in which case it should be clearly flagged as a halal branch at the entrance. Meanwhile I’m getting my sausage rolls from the much bigger, nicer Wandsworth branch.

If you buy the morning breakfast roll /sandwich £3 offer, of the 3 options 1) bacon  2) sausage 3) omelette please avoid the sausage. The sausages I had were either overcooked or old ones which were rock hard and I could not eat them! But the Greggs burger bun is a great one and I enjoyed the softness inside and crusty skin. It reminds me of a French pastry bread.

I visited Greggs in Earl’s Court on New Year’s Day and received the best service I’ve ever had at any Greggs. Although there was no hot-to-go food available at the time, the staff were happy to prepare some fresh items for us when we asked. The place was very clean. Will definitely come back.

Best chicken bake I've had at Greggs in a while. Freshly baked, extremely hot, and excellent all around. My review is biased given it was fresh, and sausage roll was just lukewarm, but the freshly baked chicken bake was excellent.

This morning, my colleague and I went to get some food. The woman serving at the till, a white lady, was extremely rude to us. She claimed she had called us four times, but we genuinely didn’t hear her. Even after we apologized, she continued to be rude. We ordered two sausage rolls, but she only gave us one. When I told her we had ordered two, she said we should speak louder—even though we had. Her attitude was very arrogant, and I have never experienced such poor customer service. This happened at 9:45 a.m. on December 5th. I believe management should take action, as this is not the level of service expected from Greggs. I am a regular customer, and her behavior has made me feel uncomfortable about returning.
Lovely place for my breakfast in London. Good meal deals. Nice staff and quick service. Rise and shine deal is good. Location is perfect. Clean place. Cozy atmosphere
The food is nice and mostly cheap but I genuinely do not remember the last time I got an item that wasn't cold no matter how early or late I come.

I stop by this Greggs almost every day, and it’s always a pleasant experience. The staff are genuinely friendly and often greet you with a smile, which makes a big difference during a busy day. Service is quick and efficient, even when the shop gets busy.

The food is always fresh and just what you'd expect — reliable and satisfying.
The shop is clean and well-organized, and there’s usually paid street parking nearby, which is convenient. Also a great spot for a quick bite with kids.

Overall, a well-run location that delivers exactly what you want from a Greggs — quick, tasty, and friendly.

It had been a really wonderful experience visiting Greggs here for the first time and I must say, their staff are really well behaved, amicable and fast with their deliveries. Food tastes better when it is served at such a relaxed ambience. I'd recommend this place anytime for take outs. Thank you for the amazing service!
It's a small little place but well maintained, they sell a lovely variety of delicious foods and great for a quick lunch break!!!

I have been coming here every day. I love the team they are working so hard to please everyone. They greeting you from the door, smiling at all times. Big well done to Chloe to run this place to perfection. Thanks guys for making my day- every day !!!! KK

I have been here about 10 times. There are very rarely any sandwiches present no matter the time of day (with the exception of Tuna, there is always a stack of Tuna). The staff are very hit and miss, sometimes they’re friendly, other times they look beyond depressed. I asked one when the sandwiches would be restocked and they might as well have responded with “dunno no idea”. I don’t know who manages this branch but this is really disappointing.

Hygiene is horrible here, not wearing gloves when handling rolls, baguette, etc. when asked to put gloves before handling my baguette, the staff said his hands are clean, although he was touching various appliances etc. before. Terrible terrible terrible
There are certain brands and Greggs is one, where you know what to expect. That's a good thing.

Hi todays day 12/10/2022.   I experience one of the most arrogant and dirty cases.   I was ready to order when the old woman on counter aproached and was touching her noise with her hand , she was ready to pick up a bag to put my food in , before that i asked ger if please can you wash your hands or put some glove ... straight away she refused and triying to attend the next client .  That is teally disgusting and unproffesional she haven't got any hygiene at all . Later on she was disrespectfull because i complaint about it making me get out of a store .  I would not recomend this place to anybody as i cannot blame compnay the workers are themself. I will never come back .

Never write reviews but felt compelled to on this occasion.

Genuinely awful experience today (07/03/2025 - 12:10pm). Long queue, absolutely nothing available in terms of food, one member of staff who was frankly one of the worst shop front staff I’ve ever seen in any shop, ever. Totally appreciate working at Greggs isn’t the pinnacle by any means, but the attitude was shocking, mumbling, didn’t really have a clue what was going on.

Anyway, waiting in line, one by one everyone was leaving their food on the counter and walking off rather disgruntled. Turns out, the card reader wasn’t working. Perhaps inform the queue that’s the case rather than constantly face a barrage of customers who are ultimately going to be annoyed when they wait for 10 mins to find out they can’t actually get anything. Ended up taking me 20 minutes to wait and then be told I couldn’t purchase anything unless it was cash.

Being totally honest, the other members of staff I’ve faced in this particular shop are always friendly, fast and personable. Just this one individual, she clearly doesn’t want to be there, probably a good thing for everyone’s sake.

Greggs is as fast of food as you can get but its great!.  It's fast, it's filling, and its cheap.... and it taste good. The pizza is a bit greasy but filling, the steak bake is great and it's ready fast.

Waiting time was around 1 minute, as always really nice service, pleasant employees.
Their coffee is coming from coffee machine instead of being made by a barista, and I usually can't stand lattes from the machine, but in the case with Gregg's, their latte tastes very nice and is reasonably priced.

I love grabbing coffee to go here.
Good location near the bus station. The food is good and they have good offers. The staff are friendly and they helped quite quickly , in time for me to catch my bus.

Great place to stop by for a quick bite especially on Sundays when many places open later. They have kid friendly warm sandwiches including warm ham & cheese toasties. They also have a deal where you can get any sweet treat and a regular hot drink for 2 pounds.

Like half of the working class of the UK I actively go to Greggs to get a tasty sausage roll or a good valued latte. Unfortunately the staff at this particular Greggs are rude, unfriendly and half of the baked range appears to be over burnt. I will not be coming here again.

Food was pretty good, although as this was my first Gregg's experience, I have no baseline. Service was decent but the store doesn't follow the opening time! They only finally opened the doors around 10-15 mins after the posted opening time that was displayed on the store itself, causing a moderately long queue outside for breakfast and a rush when it did open.

Staff incredibly slow and in no hurry, massive queue with one person running the tills. Ended up waiting awhile before having to leave for work. Other staff stood around with no interest in trying to help clear the queue. Incredibly poorly run branch.
Staff have 0 listening skills. You might as well do yourself - it would be far quicker! All of them are just plain rude - time for a performance review of your staff!

Food cold, like it's been there for hours. Staff clearly don't want to be there - and I'm not surprised, given the constant theft by the homeless, which puts people off. Prices are very inflated compared to other locations and it's definitely not worth it unless you're desperate for a quick grab when your coach is due.

Decent ranges of pastries, baguettes and drink. Very good value and great for a quick snack or lunch. Though, if you’re going for lunch you need to get there for 12 or earlier as everything gets sold out. We wanted to get some soup but the veggie option was sold out and if we wanted it it’d be an extra 30mins wait. This Greggs is inside the Victoria Coach Station so can be easily missed or hard to find.

A young man who took our order and started preparing it and a young lady was loading the trays with pizza and pepperoni for baking. Behold Greg’s, Clapham—a culinary colossus squatting nobly among the mortals of South London, where sausage rolls are not merely eaten but experienced.
From the moment I crossed the threshold, I knew I was in the presence of greatness. The automatic doors sighed open like the gates of Valhalla, welcoming me into a cathedral of beige pastry and fluorescent glory. The air—perfumed with notes of baked dough and existential comfort—embraced me like an old friend who owes me £5 and never paid it back.
I approached the counter with the reverence of a pilgrim. The staff, swift and inscrutable, moved with the practiced precision of a Formula One pit crew who have seen things. I ordered, my voice trembling, and moments later was handed a sausage roll so perfectly warm it could have been cradled by angels. The first bite? Thunder. The second? Revelation. By the third, I was reconsidering my life choices and forgiving everyone who’s ever wronged me.
And let us speak of value. In an era of £14 sandwiches with a backstory, Greg’s stands firm, a socialist utopia where a few coins can still buy joy, calories, and a fleeting sense of purpose. This is not fast food; this is fast destiny.
In conclusion, Greg’s Clapham is not just a bakery. It is a lighthouse in the storm, a warm pasty in a cold, indifferent world. I arrived hungry. I left changed.
Five stars. Six, if the universe were just.

The new store design has also led to longer queues, which means I often walk past without going in. I’m not sure if this change is working as well as intended.

Review for [insert specific branch name here]

I felt compelled to leave feedback specifically about the manager on duty during my recent visit.

When I approached the counter with a genuine concern about an error in my order, the manager’s response was incredibly unprofessional. They were dismissive of my issue, spoke to me in a sharp and rude tone, and made no attempt to resolve the problem or even acknowledge my concerns. It was clear they prioritized moving through the queue over treating customers with basic respect.

I’ve shopped at this location before and generally found the team okay, but the manager’s attitude left me feeling frustrated and unwelcome. A manager sets the tone for the entire store, and in this case, that tone was poor customer care. I hope higher management will address this and provide training on how to handle customer queries appropriately.

I felt I had to share my experience from my recent visit. As I was queuing to buy my usual items, a delivery driver arrived at the store and approached the counter to discuss a delivery matter.

The manager on duty responded in a shockingly rude way – raising their voice, speaking sharply, and making the driver seem like an inconvenience. It was clear the driver was trying to resolve an issue professionally, but the manager’s aggressive tone and dismissive attitude was difficult to watch.

I was really uncomfortable standing there while this happened. No one should be spoken to like that, especially someone doing their job to get orders to customers. A manager should be leading by example, showing respect to everyone they come into contact with – staff, customers and delivery partners alike.

I’ve always appreciated Greggs as a reliable spot, but this incident has made me think twice about returning to this particular branch. I hope the management team at a higher level will address this behaviour and make sure all interactions are handled with professionalism and kindness.

Stunning store, very spacious and clean. Excellent customer service, the staff are very friendly & helpful and the food taste great too at an affordable price. I come here every week, this is one of my favourite stores as they are fully stocked all the time and their sausage rolls and bakes are delicious as they are not overcooked and dry like other stores. I see some people complain about cold food, the bakes are freshly baked then left to cool, they are not cooked/baked to order, so don’t expect hot food all the time, it depends if you popped in store at the right time to get freshly baked food, i go there every morning and i always manage to get hot food. Also don’t expect your food to be hot when you order for delivery as the driver alway accept several orders so your food could be delayed and get cold, as i’ve ordered pizza before from another branch and the driver dropped 2 other orders before he came to me and i got cold pizza, so it was actually the drivers fault unless you pay extra for priotity.

First time visiting this store and I was genuinely amazed by Yusef’s customer service. It’s not very common to be served by someone at Greggs who is so polite and friendly, so this was a lovely surprise. I recently had a bad experience at another Greggs, but being served by Yusef completely changed that. He gave great recommendations and made the whole experience enjoyable. Keep being a star — you’ll go far!

My colleagues and I have been coming here almost every morning since it opened as we work in Westfield. But not anymore. The wait time is ridiculous. There are 3 open tills to pay, and whilst the queue is stretched outside the store, we can see someone on their phone in the back giggling away. I don’t hold anything against pulling your phone out every so often but there’s a right time and a wrong time. They’re fridges never have any water, the food that’s supposed to be hot is cold and when we ask staff if they can do anything they just say “sorry no” and do not give a toss. They’re agonizingly slow and honestly have 0 customer service. Managers been spoken to previously but nothing changed. I’m disappointed and I wish Westfield had picked a different bakery instead. The whole Westfield working community feels the same way. Won’t be going anymore. Get a new team and some cleaners!
I always have a coffee here or a slice of pizza, but I want to make a special mention of Hannah, always kind with her million-dollar smile, a wonderful person, thank you so much ❤️

Ygor provided top top service. Had a big order for me and family. Was quick, professional and super helpful. Nothing was too much for him. Appreciate you brother you work hard it doesn’t go unnoticed. You’ll go far.

Really happy with this Greggs. The food is always hot and tastes great, and the customer service is excellent. The staff are friendly, helpful, and make the experience enjoyable every time. Highly recommended!
Not a 24h greggs. There is 24h personnel to tell you there’s no food. Not a chance you’re getting a bake after 1 am. Big sad.

I have consistently observed that food items, including baked goods, sandwiches, and sweet pastries, are often served cold and marked as stale without being discarded.

Additionally, the bakes are not being cooked frequently enough, and it seems that the staff are not following the two-hour rule for food safety. I have also noticed that hot holds, which should be rotated out, were still available on the shelf well past their expiration time. For instance, items that should have been removed by 1:00 PM were still being sold at 12:30 AM.

I am sharing this information as I am aware of Greggs' policies regarding food safety and customer care. I believe addressing these issues is crucial for the health and satisfaction of your customers.

Thank you for your attention to this matter.
Got a to good to go bag when I arrived they only gave me 2 items what a scam
Staff are useless and have no hygeine awareness at all, food is always cold

Greggs is usually a reliable place for a quick bite, but my experience this time wasn’t the best. The food felt a bit less fresh than usual — the pastry was slightly cold, and the flavour wasn’t as good as expected. The service was also slower than normal, even though it wasn’t very crowded.

The staff weren’t rude, but they didn’t seem very attentive or interested. The shop could have been cleaner as well, especially around the tables and the counter area.

Overall, Greggs can be good on most days, but this visit was disappointing. I hope they improve the freshness and service next time.

Vegan sausage roll. Need I say more? In a land of the ever popular sausage roll- Greggs comes out landing with a banging plant based vegan version! Not going to lie- had one everyday for breakfast as this was right next to our hotel / Paddington station. Super cheap & quick bite to eat. Greggs Vegan sausage roll for the win!

This review is for Greggs staff  at 10 Praed Street. Particularly the early morning shift staff managment on weekdays.
I am a regular customer who enjoys  Greggs' meals.I go there almost every morning; my order is similar every day. Due to it being the same staff that serves me every day, they by now know me and what I am going to order. It's FRUSTRATING that the staff start typing my order as soon I get to the till WITHOUT asking me what I want to order. Even before I open my mouth to say my order, the staff would start typing my order and ask me to pay, without asking me what I want to order or saying good morning. I found this behaviour totally unacceptable and annoying.

Early shift Staff at this Greggs, PLEASE, even though you know the customer is regular and going to order the same thing they ordered yesterday, ASK the customer what they want on that particular day. Let the customer say their order on that particular day. It's totally rude and unacceptable to start typing the order   without asking the costomor what they need. What if the customer wants to order something different on that day? Or what if the customer WANTS to be asked what they want to order even though they are going to order the same thing they ordered yesterday? You go and rush start typing preparing the order without even asking the customer what they need, assuming the customer is going to order the same thing is UNACCEPTABLE AND RUDE behaviour. Let the customer say what they want to order  even though you know they are going to order the same thing!!

This review is for the early shift staff at 10 Praed Street.

Had a extra large click and collect order for my office. Staff were lovely and very helpful as some items were out of stock, but this was communicated very quickly. Food was freshly baked for the order. I've been here before a couple of times before and whole team are very friendly, even in peak times when the queues are long the service is still great and as efficient as possible.
First time at a Gregg's. Cheap, fast, warm pastries. Glad to try it.

There's a chap who works here in the evenings (inc yesterday:15 Dec) who is the most polite, friendly and cheerful Greggs staff member I've seen. I'm usually in a rush to catch a train so never get the chance to tell him how nice he is!

Who ever is that lady working on 8/12/025 afternoon shift, i have not seen anyone as horrible in customer service as that lady.
Gregs offers community service but that definitely was not welcoming.
This ruined my trust in Gregs.
Sorry it may be blunt but that lady seriously needs to have a talk with the manager or anyone superior.
I went to grab a quick slice of pizza and was very disappointed. Not only did they not heat it up, but they also didn't seem to care, despite charging the usual price.

There was a black guy with a beard who served me, and he, genuinely, offered some of the quickest service I've recieved in my life thus far.

Exactly what's needed when people are running late to their trains.

Good opening times and nicely located branch of Greggs. I purchased some food and a latte at nearly 19:00 and the food was acceptable.  The mature lady I spoke to had a good sense of humour. I would recommend this branch to others.

The lad that served us today around midday was an absolute pleasure! Friendly and helpful. He made good recommendations and took our order perfectly.
A friendly face to make the day!

Thanks.
Absolutely wonderful little Greggs! Staff are super nice and the location is beautiful!!!
The doughnut was delicious and the coffee reasonably priced; however, the public seating area didn’t appear to be well maintained.

Nothing left on the shelves and what I had was cold
14:30 in the afternoon is near closing time either

It was a lovely little surprise seeing a new shop opened in the square, it was a pleasant experience with very helpful staff that accommodated my needs with speed and efficiency especially when I requested oat milk for my coffee and they said it was empty to then replace it very quickly. All whilst attending to the other customers around me to avoid any delay, I was seriously impressed when I found out that they only opened recently. I’ll definitely be revisiting

930am today. Virtually no hot food, all the display at front was cold stuff but they got me a steak bake from oven at rear after I complained. The front door doesn't close so cold wind coming in along with smoke from punter outside. Bins full, shelves practically empty, leftover packets on the table which I had to clear myself and staff doing nothing about it.
To run salt in, some guy walked in, too some food and walked out. Staff aware but did nothing.
Greggs, you can do much better than this.
We have used this place a few times while waiting for trains. Staff work hard and the long queues soon disappear, food and drinks have always been hot.
