Not open at the scheduled time.

Once again, this Greggs delivers the authentic ice-cold sausage roll experience. Purchased four this morning, all stone cold, and they went straight in the bin. Consistency is impressive, just unfortunately not in a good way.
Good

REOPENED TODAY 13/06 Great Portland Street is finally back to normality the new location is spacious and has indoor seating opposite BBC nice vibrant colours and decor within.

Friendly and efficient service when placing my order took less than a minute and my order was ready!

Highly recommend those local to the area the fix we’ve all been waiting for has finally returned!!!

Yes it's nice inside as it looks quite a new store. But that's the only plus. They don't sell Mac n Cheese! The worker said 'maybe we'll do it in the future'. Hardly a difficult item to stock.

And a warning to everyone ...I ordered ahead on their app and walked like 30 mins to get there as few Greggs close by. Got there and was told their screens aren't working so they don't know what I ordered! I told them what I ordered and then was told they don't have potatoes wedges in stock. Why allow me to order on the app only to tell me after getting there they don't have it?! What a complete waste of my time. I ended up demanding a refund. So many issues!

I do not recommend this Greggs!

I've never been disappointed by Greggs! Wonderful vegan sausage rolls and very nice coffee. Staff have been universally helpful and friendly -- even though they don't always have the customers they deserve! Utterly reliable!!
Useless. A box of x4 Pizza's and they sent me x1. Read the damn order properly. İncompetent.
Pretty decent. Go between 12-1pm and when they first open to get hot foods. Do a good vegan selection and the service is quick. Is what it is really.

Everything is great and the food is delicious, but one of the cashiers is just terrible. They constantly forget things — sometimes give a roll without the sausage, or simply miss parts of the order.
Today I had to remind them about everything: two coffees, a chicken bake, donuts — nothing was added until I asked several times.
As for things like forks or ketchup, you have to mention it multiple times.

They really need to train or re-evaluate their staff, because such carelessness completely kills the desire to come back.

Awful establishment of Gregg's with long q, not enough staff, dirty.  I went in for breakfast with a voucher but the guy didn't put it through so £4.75 for a pretty rubbish bacon baguette.  Now Gregg's core reason for working, it's USP, is it is suppose to be quick, cheap comfort food which is great value.  Now when your paying £4.75 for a rubbish baguette which for similar price can get a much better version for example in "Coco de mama" or "M&S" then Gregg's has no reason to exist. The coffee is atrocious, the pizza slices are now nearly as much as a dominoes personal pizza.  Lunch meal deal is ok but Sainsbury's, co-op, boots, Tesco do better cheaper ones.  The leadership at Gregg's are terrible and just like most CEO are just doing anything to keep share price high

So the food was hot, that's the only reason this place has got 2 stars. The service i received was atrocious. Staff don't smile or seem like they want to be there. I ordered a bacon and sausage roll and a sausage and bacon roll with extra sausage. When the lady (who was extremely rude to me) gave me my food she handed me a sausage and omelette roll and the baguette only had 2 sausage in it (which is what you get in a normal one) I told her about the roll and there was supposed to be an extra sausage to which she said that's what I ordered. Told her to check again. She muttered something under her breath and redone the roll but AGAIN done omelette and bacon. Had to tell her again and she snatched it out my hand and redone it. If you like being shorted on the food youve paid for and like rude staff then this is the place for you.
Won't be coming back to Greggs. Three times i been here and I asked if there any vegan sausage rolls I can get a rude response from staff. I don't understand why you guy's never have it.
Popped in on the off chance today. What a nice store the seating area looked clean even a ramp for wheelchair users & parents with buggies. Can pay with cash and card

place_name: greggs Villiers street unit 2 and 3, embankment station
review_text:
Hey, it's Greggs, what do you expect?

Shout out to older guy from their crew, he was checking my TGTG  meticulously  and for some reason that left a great impression. Somebody who still cares, and working in Greggs.

Food was ok, pretty good Mac&Cheese with breadcrumbs on top, very salty thou.

Chicken was passable, same with chips.

Clean and tidy shop although could use some better system for people waiting. Yes, I'm aware there is not much space there.

place_name: greggs Villiers street unit 2 and 3, embankment station
review_text: I was served by Bhagya not too long ago and she provided excellent customer service - not at all what I was expecting from a quick dine and dash. She was genuinely so lovely despite being hard at work. Just that small interaction really brightened my day!

place_name: greggs Villiers street unit 2 and 3, embankment station
review_text: Came on a Saturday morning - the staff were so kind and personable. A Greggs is a Greggs ya know so not much to say on the food or drinks (although notably there were no dairy alternatives for coffee) but the staff here were lovely

place_name: greggs Villiers street unit 2 and 3, embankment station
review_text: I just got a sausage roll at 8:40 and it's stone cold...probably a day old. Worse is the person serving me neither told me that it's not fresh nor heated it up. When I requested it to be heated, he refused. There was no rush either to excuse the incompetent service. Just to add I have greggs almost once a week so I know this is not their level of service.

place_name: greggs Villiers street unit 2 and 3, embankment station
review_text: I was served by a worker there called Sophia and she was amazing as she was patient and took her time with me. I’ll definitely come back. The food was delicious.

Its very busy as the whole area there are full of people so you should expect some que befir you got your order.
But good to pick up your drink from there and walk around to discover the places around.

It is quite a good option if you are around and don't want to sit in a restaurant or just want to eat sitting in an open air spot. Gregg´s offers some basic meals like pizza slices at quite good price, as well as coffee and some sweet treats.
I was there with my family and we had to wait for some 20 minutes to get the pizza slices ready, but we were compensated with a free coffee.

Greggs offers relatively cheap food in the heart of London's Leicester Square. Unfortunately, with the low price also comes low quality and the food is simply junk.
Great location by the park, where you can sit if you manage to find a bench, as the whole square is crowded with people.

A handy place to eat with a good location, but long wait times and customer service could be better.

I visited the Greggs store and the queue was very long and almost out the door, it took a while to get served. It was hard to distinguish where to actually wait and there were no markings and the central reservation with products on confused me as to where I should stand and wait.

The store had a security guard and there were several staff but by the time I got served the experience was average and the customer service wasn’t bad but it was by no means good either. The food selection was plentiful and there is also a refrigerator with cold drinks.

The location also advertises that it’s part of the Refill London water bottle refill scheme but I haven’t used it yet.

I ordered a sausage roll and it was hot, crispy and fresh. I assume due to the high turnover of customers this allows the food to be fresher.

Overall experience was average and I would go back again, the food was good which is the most important part but the atmosphere and service could be improved. The location and opening hours are very convenient.

What can I say about this Greggs. As always it's located in a convenient and easily accessible place. The menu is the same and they have started adding new items which is great to see and try. The staff are friendly and prompt. The food is always consistent and if the item you want is not hot they will let you know the wait time to make an informed decision.
It's a feel good place to snack that just hits the spot, or breakfast lunch or evening snack.
Great service, location and food. Well worth a visit for your pastry and sandwich needs.

Being nice even giving 2 stars and it’s only because SOME of the staff are friendly offers the help needed and because there is a lot of seating . Others though rush you , are no help at all there’s no point doing click and collect either as it’s no quicker. You still have to talk to someone and wait for it to be made . Drink was warm at best even then that’s me being nice and well I have seen cleaner toilets ! Not to mention at least 3/4 of there stock they never have . Food was ok had a problem the first time the greggs tried to fix it by giving me free stuff .

But it still wasn’t even worth the price!!! Not experienced this from any other greggs please don’t waste your time.

Nice to have an option to visit primark and then sit down with a nice hot drink and food. Criticism though is the lady who served me didnt say hello or good bye even though I said both and I also said thank you. The gentleman serving next to her said have a good afternoon to his customer while she was silent. Also three of the staff members were then stood doing nothing. The tables were dirty and needed cleaning so they could have spend a few minutes cleaning them. I had to wipe down a table myself to be able to sit and eat

*Just to add* a family had to ask for "more blue roll" to clean a table themselves

I never write reviews unless the service is either absolutely brilliant or truly abysmal — unfortunately, this experience was the latter.

I was served by a short lady wearing a head covering on Wednesday, 8th October at around 7:30 PM, and she either dislikes her job, has zero customer service skills, or both. From the very start, her attitude was unfriendly and unwelcoming.

I ordered some cookies, a slice of pizza, and a drink. The Margherita tray was empty, so I asked if there were any more. She glanced toward the kitchen, then back at me, and said “no.” I therefore chose the pepperoni option instead.

After sitting down, I found that the pizza was completely cold. I went back to the counter and explained this, asking for a warm slice. The lady, clearly annoyed as though I was inconveniencing her, said they couldn’t reheat it and that a fresh one would take about 10 minutes. I told her I was happy to wait. Her colleague then asked whether I’d ordered Margherita or Pepperoni — despite her having just told me there were no Margheritas left.

While I was waiting for the fresh pizza, the lady apparently went on her break without telling anyone I was waiting for a replacement. After 20 minutes, I went back to ask how much longer it would be, and the staff had no idea what I was referring to. Once I explained, a young man at the counter checked and found that my fresh pizza had been ready for a while. He was very polite, helpful, and apologetic — even though the situation wasn’t his fault. I want to make it clear that my complaint is not about him at all.

When I finally received my new pepperoni slice, I also noticed there were now several fresh Margherita slices available — the same pizza I’d originally asked for and was told didn’t exist.

I genuinely don’t understand why:
a) the lady didn’t explain that the pizzas available were cold and that I could order a fresh one if I was willing to wait,
b) she lied about there being no Margherita slices left, and
c) she made no effort whatsoever to correct the situation after serving me cold food.

Her attitude was truly unacceptable — rude, dismissive, and completely lacking in professionalism. It reflects very poorly on your brand and this branch in particular. The only redeeming part of my visit was the young man who eventually helped me — he deserves credit for his polite and helpful manner.

I can totally understand that it's not easy to work in a busy shop and multicultural team, but what I totally hate is bullying coworkers loudly, in front of customers.
To the lady who aspires to be a manager - I'm not sure what you were trying to achieve saying  "use your brain" to your colleague who was busy cleaning, but you surely got attention of customers sitting around. This type of behaviour is far below the ground.
I hope that the official complaint I sent will help the company to find a more professional staff.

Food was average, drinks were nice and cold, the lady who was serving us also nice and helpful.

I will not under any circumstances go here again.
I usually don’t write reviews unless it is an exceptionally outstanding or terrible. This Greggs has possibly the worst staff and seating atmosphere. They charge %20 on any purchased produce on top of the item’s price if you’re having it in. That is absurd, to top it off, the old lady who I assume is aiming for management is one mean rude lady. She is very hostile towards costumers and would follow you around to tell the costumers where they can and can not sit. People come here to have fun and relax from everyday life stress, however, staff like her ruin that for people and cause businesses to shut.

I had a good experience in Greggs Victoria today.
Shop was very clean.
They had a nice selection of food.
My coffee was good and served in a cute cup with a Christmas decor.
They also have a great selection or snack and treats.
They have security due to many homeless people around the area, this is very good.
The staff wished me a nice day.
I recommend.

I had this server her name was Shekinat and she was genuinely so amazing and helpful, very nice to talk to, she was on her own at some point and you could tell just how much effort and dedication she was putting into her work, I really respect that and I will definitely be returning!!

I went to Greggs Victoria in the morning to taste their mouths watering breakfast rolls.

I had the breakfast roll filled with an omelette. It was a delightful blend of flavours that had me coming back for more. The brown sauce added an extra layer of flavour that made it irresistible.

But what truly stood out was the staff member Charlie. His warm smile and attentive service made the experience even more enjoyable. His dedication to customer satisfaction is unmatched, and I highly recommend Greggs Victoria for their delectable breakfast rolls and outstanding service.

Purpose of this review is just to thanks Greggs, it’s been 3 months I’m frequently being to Greggs with colleagues during my lunch break just to have my favourite Potatoes wedges and mozzarella cheddar bites 😋 with Cappuccino.

Nowadays I’m really impressed with their service specially one of the staff Muhammad, he is so professional the way he greets and welcomes the customers not only us but to each and every customer with same enthusiasm & same energy, whenever visited I have noticed that.

Believing that will continue the journey with Greggs for longgggggg.

Thanks Greggs 😊
Great food at great prices. If you have the app, you get good deals and free food and drink after collecting enough points.

This place is fantastic. The staff are friendly always smiling and the service is brilliant.

The interior is really nice and the store is always clean.

I've had lunch here a few times. The Tuna rolls are good and have a good amount of tuna in them. Vegan sausage rolls and cheese and onion bake are a must and delicious!

I highly recommend this place.

Heard about the rite of passage or cult following of people on the internet talking about Greggs. Had to make a final pit stop here before leaving the great country of the United Kingdom. Quite straight forward, ordered a Sausage Roll and a Steak Bake. We enjoyed the Steak Bake more than the Sausage Roll. Really solid value for the money, employees were chill and got to enjoy our snacks inside the restaurant.

I’ve been served by Talia today at lower marsh and she was so friendly and polite I usually don’t get nice customer service at Greggs but today it was very nice seen someone actually does talk and smile at Greggs thank you Talia for the best customer service

Service very slow 10min wait to be served ? And eventually when ordering a bacon cob 4min wait on bacon Absolutely ridiculous ? People behind leaving ? One trainee serving ? Also sit in available with no toilet facilities ? Correct me if I’m wrong but is this legal ?  If you charge a fee to eat in then don’t you have to supply facilities ?

Was very impressed with Mary this morning. There was a regular customer behind me and after she had taken my order and started it she started the order for the customer behind. Knowing his regular drink and cutting down both his and the other customer wait times in the process. Great customer service on a busy morning.

Arrived before a family to order a drink. When I want to order, the cashier with the cap told me to go back and queue behind the family. When I explained, he just ignored me. I fed up and did not order from this store even though it was cold outside and I need something to drink. I went to Black Sheep cafe just across the road. Better service and better coffee.
I have never reviewed a Greggs before but Vandhana was so nice to us and others that I feel I have to.  People like this is what makes a business special and us want to come back.

Closed off their seating area more than an hour before closing time, but didn't bother making this clear until after ordering. Judging by other reviews here, it appears cutting corners is the culture with the staff here!
Quick bite, in a rush but annoying that popular items are often sold out… Not the healthiest option but convenient.
They recognize the hierarchy of priority there... I stand in line for 5 minutes but suddenly a friend comes and is served without queuing and there is outrage that I noticed, what a joke.

Took my Girlfriend to London for a romantic getaway, we were absolutely starving. I'm quite a romantic guy and when we were walking down Embankment and I heard she was hungry, I knew just where to go. I walked her right past Elizabeth Tower and into the Underground and walked right up to the Greggs. Where we had a wonderful 2x Sausage Roll, Steak Beak, Raspberry Lemonade, & Apple Juice. She wasn't too pleased cause she wanted Chinese, but without a doubt I thought it was bloody lovely.

Used to be my favourite Greggs in London as it was on my way to work. But now most of the time I get a steak bake it's just room temperature! Now I avoid because I don't enjoy eating cold bakes! Not sure if they are trying to cut on heating bills?

Rude employee! I called them and tried to ask if they had a item but one lady I’m not sure what her name is, but she was very rude and started getting mad and kept trying to insist it was a prank call even though I was simply asking for a Coca Cola with those names on it. I really don’t understand why she this rude.

My son called them asking if they had coca cola whilst I was next to him, but the lady that worked there was being extremely disrespectful and rude towards my son. I had to close the phone call as it was escalating. I was very unhappy about this and I felt that the lady had not taken my son seriously and even said that it was a prank call and refused service. Once again, I’m very upset about this and will not ask for delivery or come here again.
Service and food are great. Usually pick up breakfast here after midnight or early morning walks around Westminster, or dinner on my way home after a day trip.
Went here on Christmas Eve just before they closed. The drinks and food were good, and the service and cleanliness were excellent. Thank you.
The toilets have never been cleaned. Absolutely disgusting for a place selling food!
How hard is it to keep food hot? All pastries are cold if you go there lunch time!
Brand new. Big, clean - large seating area. Big upgrade from the old site
Tried to order a coffee 15 mins before closing and they had taken them offline

This is one of the worst customer service experiences I’ve ever had. There is no queueing system at all, so customers are just standing around confused with no idea who is next. There is also no proper order system, which makes the whole place chaotic.

The staff are very slow, disorganised, and completely confused about how to help customers. Nobody seems trained or willing to take responsibility, and simple requests take forever.

I wasted so much time here and left frustrated. I will definitely not be coming back and do not recommend this place to anyone.

Opening times: from 6am, but they are not open at this time and impossible to come in and make any order; staff is not friendly from the morning and not answer questions about correct opening time, says nothing at all and makes big eyes; manager told: 20 mins more maybe. Just lost time during waiting and left the this place hungry without any meal. Can’t recommend to visit!

Perfect place for cheap but filling food on the go. There is loads of seating and it is located on the second floor.  You can buy tea and coffee at the counter. There are vegan potato chips, vegan vegetable bake, vegan sausage rolls and vegan doughnuts. The place is clean and the queue moves very quickly. Staff are efficient.

We were able to grab a table outside of Greggs on the upper balcony in King's Cross whilst waiting on our train home ( no mean feat as it was always mobbed, you just have to hope someone leaves as you walk by). Great place to sit, people watch and keep an eye on the arrivals and departure board. There was always a queue of people waiting to be served in the shop but this did go down quite quickly as the assistants were serving fast. I did notice that a lot of the shelves were empty most of the time, so they maybe could of been restocked a bit faster so as to give customers more choice. The servers were polite, well presented and served customers fast.

Despite the 10:30 queues, the staff dealt with each person, swiftly and efficiently.
The seating area was clean, well kept , food fresh. The morning deal of bacon baguette band a hit drink, wonderful.
Their baguettes are fresh, bacon succulent , Perfect ratio of ketchup to bacon. Tasty.  Great cook.
Well done to all

Ordered an iced Carmel biscuit latte and was a got coffee. Ordered 2 hash browns which were overcooked in the warmer where they keep them for self service. Very cardboard like and also got a sausage omelette baguette. It was all bread, 2 small pieces of sausage and the small “omelette” I’ve ever had. Absolute joke for the price.

Shubham was SO accommodating, had to ask for his name and thank his manager and staff for their kindness. I ordered a 4-slice Margherita Pizza and one of the slices didn't make it and they offered me a complimentary donut for my troubles and to my surprise, brought me the missing slice! We are visiting for a week here in London and Gregg's was a great start to the trip. For the Americans reading this, Gregg's is akin to a Dunkin Donuts with more variety in the food selection. Thank you guys again!

I bought a chicken bake and a steak bake. Steak bake was all busted, the filling had spilled and all dried up on the outside. I was in a rush so didn't had time to check and wasn't bothered to go back. The chicken bake was stone cold as well and had to reheat it, this is the second time this has happened. There's another Greggs at the station on the upper level by the East Midlands platform, you are better off going there. If I had enough time, I'd have gone there and probably should have as well.

Everything was great except the door was on a spring which kept the door open at all times. The wind was so cold even with the air curtain on. The staff tried to close it but the door kept opening on the spring.
What a waste of electricity to let the heat out of an open door in mid winter.

Not the best. The server misaligned the coffee cup, then I calmly watched as most of the coffee missed the paper mug. In the end I had half a cup of warm milk, very little coffee - most went down the drain. Coffee tasted faintly like ham.

Always receive excellent service here. Was really grateful today as I wanted a hot slice of pizza today so waited on the fresh one from the oven. Minar went above and beyond to put it in a box instead of the regular bags so that it didn't get squished. I like hot 🔥 food.

This Greggs branch is bigger than the usual ones. You can have vegan sausage rolls and vegan doughnuts. There is a place to stand up and eat your food. They offer free condiments and napkins etc. Staff are very organised and because of it's location it does get busy but it is worth the wait. I got the vegan sausage roll and the vegan doughnut which was soft and delicious.
Amazing service from the team and the person serving was great. Really appreciated his help with my requests.

Generally I like Greggs, the food is good value and generally the service is nice. Theres a couple of great staff in this branch who always take that extra 5 seconds to look you in the eye, say thanks and have a good day. But there’s one girl there who, I don’t know what I did to her, but it’s as if I’ve deeply deeply offended her by my existence! She seems to go out of her way to make an effort to let me know that. Hardly talks. No smile. No eye contact. Just this look of deep distaste. She may just be young and not give a crap but still, it must take up more energy to be this way than to just relax and give a quick smile. Anyway it reminded me today not to bother spending money on food on the way to work and just prep better myself.

Called in at 10.45 for bacon roll and they had run out and not serving anymore breakfast rolls ? Considering they advertised until 11am 👎 customer service was not good ? No wonder the place was empty ? If you advertise a service provided it ???

McDonald’s next door was still serving so money spent there  only £5.38 for twice the breakfast 👍

Food never available queue always out of shop with 1 or max 2 ppl serving. They have no sense of display availability at all. This has been taken today at my second visit pizza was not available in the first visit as in the second visit . Same situation. They run out quick of it and they cook probably 5 slices a time which probably takes 15 or 20 Min to cook. Every single day I start to queue every single day last slice left is bought by someone in front of me by the time I reach my tourn.

Despite its unassuming appearance, it is low-key (HIGH-KEY) one of the best Greggs in existence 🟦🟨 Friendly, competent staff (unlike the other St Pancras Greggs), and they don't run out of breakfast items an hour early (again, looking at you, other St Pancras Greggs). Also, last time I went they only had veggie pizzas at the counter, but offered me others in the oven, and gave me a shout when they were ready 💙💛

I'm done

I'm sure most people would expect a bake to at least be warm but this is now my second stone cold serving in as many visits. When you ask the staff if they could heat up your order you're told, "we're not allowed"

I'm done

I stopped at the Greggs in St Pancras (lower level) today and had a really disappointing experience. Normally I like Greggs because they’re quick, friendly, and reliable, but this particular branch really let the side down.

The woman serving at the counter, Karen, unfortunately had no sense of customer service. She was very slow, seemed easily confused, and even though she read our order back to us twice, it still came out wrong. When you’re in a busy station environment, speed and accuracy are essential – people are usually rushing to catch trains – but instead we were left waiting longer than we should have been for an order that wasn’t even correct.

On top of that, there was no friendliness or engagement. A smile or a simple “thank you” goes a long way, especially when you’re dealing with customers all day, but that was completely missing. It felt like she didn’t want to be there, and it showed in the way she interacted.

The food itself was standard Greggs – you know what you’re getting – but the service was so poor that it overshadowed everything else. Honestly, Karen may be better suited to a role in the back rather than dealing with customers face-to-face.

Overall, this branch does not reflect the usual Greggs experience I’ve had elsewhere in the country. Greggs in the north in particular always seem friendlier, faster, and more professional. St Pancras is one of the busiest stations in London, so I’d expect staff here to be sharp and efficient, but sadly that wasn’t the case today

I’m a regular Greggs customer and usually stop by in the mornings before work. The branches in Surrey have always been clean, safe, and served fresh food. Unfortunately, my experience at this particular branch was the complete opposite.

While I was trying to buy a Cheese and Onion bake, a man came very close to me and pressured me into buying him a coffee. He didn’t seem mentally well, and I felt genuinely unsafe. There was no security, and none of the Greggs staff stepped in. I ended up buying the coffee out of fear, better to lose £3 than risk being hurt. I really expected the team to handle this kind of situation better, even if it meant offering him a free drink just to keep customers safe.

On top of that, the Cheese and Onion bake I got was slimy and slightly sour, definitely not the usual fresh and tasty quality I’m used to.

Only positive thing was the girl behind the counter who was kind enough to offer me some mozzarella bites and tried to help, so shoutout to her.

Really disappointed with the experience overall. I’m still shocked.

I’ve been a Greggs fan for many years, but to serve all of your baked goods now cold with no option to reheat them in the store for you is unforgivable.
I asked the server if the sausage rolls were hot. I also asked if the Chicken bake is hot. The answer was “no we don’t serve hot food”. I spotted some commercial microwave ovens behind her so asked if she could heat my items up. The answer “ We don’t reheat any food here”. In other words, we don’t care if the food is cold and good luck if you get food poisoning from food left to cool down out of the oven and be served at less than 60 degrees C.
That’s a food hygiene violation.
The fact that the bag (in my picture says) Caution: Freshly baked products (especially the fillings) may still be very hot. What a joke!
Greggs, you’ve lost a customer for life with your stupid policy change.

Such lovely staff, especially Shamim and Hamisha, very welcoming and friendly for someone who has never been to Greggs! Fantastic breakfast, fast and fresh & inexpensive pastries. The classic British sausage rolls was the best

This is a really fresh modern store, has a good selection of food at a good price.
The staff were very friendly and made our day. There's some limited inside seating which is handy.  The tables could have been kept a bit cleaner and rubbish clearing away quicker.

Appalled with the service, specifically a staff member-I assume South Asian woman, I didn’t catch her name but was working at around 1pm on Tuesday 27th Jan.
Immediately a hostile environment with the noise pollution created by her, screaming next customer which created more disruption than beneficial. As I approached closer her tone got increasingly louder and aggressive. Misunderstanding with what I had named the pizza, I termed it as a “vegan pizza”, rather than a “vegetable pizza”, in which she told me to fix my language and doubted whether I knew what item I was talking about. Patronised me and made me feel incapable, waited to talk to me till I had “confirmed what item I had”. Rude and aggressive, honestly made it a very overstimulating environment by her deafening voice. Felt uncomfortable and targeted.
Please do not work in such conditions if you cannot handle it, would like a reply and confirmation from management that something has done.
Once again. 6am opening time is flouted. Staff just stocking shelves and not caring about people waiting. No apology or acknowledgement. They should get up early.  Head Office should be made aware

I came here thinking it was a mini Greggs. I didn't realise that with the smaller size comes smaller prices! It has been explained to me that Greggs Outlet sell food left over from the main stores but everything I had was fresh at a very reasonable price. Would recommend if you are in the area. Lower selection but lower cost is a fair trade off.
I didn’t know 🤷🏻‍♂️ about this - Greggs Outlet. All These I’ve Spending So Much Money on Those Other Expensive Greggs Stores 🏬 and They Opened These Greggs Outlet Which is Much Cheaper & Better 👍🏼
Amazing bargains will be using this place lots as everything is heavy discounted and parking is ok round here great idea thanks greggs
Discounted Gregg's items, stock varies but a decent selection of products.
Alright, I realise that Greggs is a massive multi national, but these Greggs Outlets support local communities as a charitable foundation. What a great thing for the business to be doing.

At 6 am the cafe was not yet open however it was opened when I knocked on the window by Sam. My complaint is about Grace, how I was treated by the young black woman. Her body language and the way she looked at me and just slammed my receipt on the counter when I politely asked for it was wrong. The manager Sam I later found out her name was very professional & polite.

Had a sausage roll & chips, a coffee & a cake. £10! Greggs is no longer cheap. It’s now expensive AND unhealthy. But worse still is the hygiene. It wasn’t a busy time, late afternoon on a Tuesday, yet the toilets were filthy, the bins were overflowing and the tables and floor covered in old food. Flys (even in late November due to the filthy conditions) thrive here. It’s such a shame, because a year ago when this branch first opened, it was very nice and clean, now it’s filthy. I won’t be returning. Next time I’ll spend my 10 quid across the road in Wetherspoons where at least the tables are usually clean.

Bad attitude from the manager/supervisor. Food was cold and I politely asked if they could warm it up. I was told they are not allowed to do this. If they can’t heat the food up then they should make sure the food under the heaters isn’t cold, and drop the attitude.

I love the food and options here for a cheap eat. One thing that is consistently happening however with only one lady who always is on the tills, which I try and avoid. Doesn't allow me to scan the Greggs apps. I tell I have it and tells me to scan the app when she has already pushed the transaction to the card machine. So it doesn't register on the app. I've given up arguing the point. Please raise this and fix this. I get attitude when I try and bring this up. I fight the point when I have a free one. Because I know I'm not meant to be getting charged.

The staff and food are great. Only issue was the toilet, I was very shocked at how dirty it was ! It really needs a deep clean , there’s lots of caked on dust everywhere and a lot of flies when I went inside

I go here most days for my lunch break around 3pm and most days without fail the cleanliness is poor. Tables covered in food, food all over the floor, bins full and condiments empty. Such a shame as it’s a newly built Greggs.

Sort this out please.

Very friendly staff. I went in at around 11:30 and the shelves were empty. I was hoping for a festive baguette, which had either sold out, or hadn’t been put out for lunch yet. The staff were quick, polite and friendly when paying.

Had a sausage roll & chips, a coffee & a cake. £10! Greggs is no longer cheap. It’s now expensive AND unhealthy. But worse still is the hygiene. It wasn’t a busy time, late afternoon on a Tuesday, yet the toilets were filthy, the bins were overflowing and the tables and floor covered in old food. Flys (even in late November due to the filthy conditions) thrive here. It’s such a shame, because a year ago when this branch first opened, it was very nice and clean, now it’s filthy. I won’t be returning. Next time I’ll spend my 10 quid across the road in Wetherspoons where at least the tables are usually clean.

Lovely jolly ladies working the shop at 8am Monday 24-11-25. Brightened everyone’s mood!! Such caring service, like visiting a family members home for dinner. Thank you ladies

Food is bog standard Greggs, nothing special

Been a Greggs fanatic for years. This store has stopped me going there permanently. Staff cannot manage the rush’s and have no sense of urgency.

Also a safety risk - have served multiple people incorrect food.

Avoid, go to one of the many sandwich shops nearby instead.
Products were there ready and waiting, got what I wanted and with helpful staff I was on my way.
Happy with a latte and a bacon and omelette roll for £3.80. You can't even get a coffee alone in some of the bigger coffee shops for that price alone

You can not sell cold cheese bakes or any other hot food cold.  It's disgraceful.  I just left a review on trip advisor but I won't be going in to any more of your food oulets.   Staff were great at least they follow the policy of not selling hot food cold.
Great service and delicious food.

1) why is there only 1 person serving at peak times? The queue is often outside the door!
2) why has the leather lane store been closed? Why hasn't there been any signage on the shutters indicating when/if they will reopen.

Greggs? Gregorys? We are in Westminster after all!

Standard Greggs offering to be found here. Cheap prices, tasty and filling pastries, sandwiches and assorted crisps and soft drinks.

Service was quick, friendly and efficient, the establishment was clean and tidy, no issues.

The young lad working the morning shift needs some customer relations and food handling training. My food has been manhandled repeatedly by this young chap for the last time. The ‘I hate my job and being here’ attitude is also unacceptable. I will no longer be supporting this store just because of that. Being friendly to people and respectful of the food you’re handling is not a lot to ask. The fact that no one is wearing gloves is also quite sickening. Come on Greggs. Sort it out.
Lovely staff in here. I was served a bad latte from Victoria Street but didn't have time to go back for a replacement. They very kindly replaced it for me. Great team Anthony
Had the worst cheese and onion bake this morning . Its not warm at all , the cheese is like a weird cream that had gone bad..

Go here quite often the workers are fine except for one miserable old woman with glasses behind the counter, always got an attitude, never smiles, always looks like she’d rather be anywhere else and overall just a very rude. I only keep coming to this Greggs is because it’s the closest one to work. Every time I go in I hope it’s one of the other workers serving me because she really is the only individual in there who acts this way

Great customer service today by Abdul! Helped me use the Greggs App and claim my points and any rewards I had. He was very helpful and provided great customer service with a positive attitude. Keep it up!

The staff here are lovely but unfortunately Greggs aren't just based on providing a all round pleasant customer service experience.
The issue at this Greggs seems to be consistent as I have visited various time and it has come to my attention that the majority of the savouries fresh bakes I've purchase from this Greggs are stone cold/distasteful.

"As bakers we believe that baking our savouries fresh each day gives customers the best quality savoury. If the sausage rolls and pasties were kept hot after they had been baked, then they would be subject to VAT and the customer would have to be charged a higher price, in the same way that we charge VAT on our hot sandwiches which are kept in a heated cabinet and are subject to VAT." - Greggs F&Qs

If that is the case, I shall be asking the baker before purchasing how long have they been left on the shelf? or no longer purchase from this particular Greggs. Quite frankly, no one wants to eat cold pastries.

Great Greggs Branch! Customer Service is excellent and I am always provided with a quick service despite the never ending lunch time queues! Stock Levels are okay for a small Greggs and I am offered a variety of products! The staff members are very kind and friendly and are always cheerful! The branch is always clean and well presented and with the smell of vegan sausage rolls flying around inside your automatically welcome! Prices are good and fairly cheaper than competitors but please Greggs stop raising the prices! I highly suggest that you try the Southern Fried Potato Wedges for £1 you get a huge portion!
Awful. I got food poisoning after bought in this Greggs 2 slices of Margherita pizza. 3 days of constant stomach ache, diharrea and vomits.

Excellent food establishment. Very kind helpful and funny staff. The nice short lady with black hair who served me had great customer service skills while under pressure from what looks to be short staff and the other worker the nice young man with a Hackney accent who works there has also great skills. The best Greg's I've ever been too. From one business to another thank you for your great service.

I do like a Greggs Sausage Roll but I love a Greggs Sausage, Bean & Cheese Melt more. I miss the long discontinued chicken mayo salad thick white bloomer sandwiches but there's still lots of comfort foods available. Doughnuts and cookies are lovely sweet treats and it's nice when they bring out seasonal products too, but the bakes are the best if you're going to a Greggs

Who doesn’t serve any veggie milk (at least soy milk) in 2025?
Absolutely ridiculous
And the staff is very rude and not helpful
Jaspreet Kaur is so so rude. Should not be in customer service. This branch only just opened as well! So disappointing

I often go to Greggs locally as it’s convenient and I love their pizzas. I’m used to our local Greggs having occasional wait times and the odd wet floor in the toilets—after all, it’s not a five-star restaurant.

However, this Greggs near Moorgate was by far the worst I’ve experienced. There’s a pylon right at the entrance, which means the queue from the counter blocks one side of the store—very poorly thought out. We had young children who needed to use the bathroom, and it was easily the worst public restroom experience I’ve had in the UK. The toilet clearly hasn’t been cleaned in a long time, the floor was messy, and the tables were sticky. I couldn’t help but wonder: if the bathroom looks like this, what’s the kitchen like?

On top of that, we were disappointed to learn that pizzas are only sold after 11am, which I’ve never encountered at a Greggs before. Overall, a very disappointing visit.

The Greggs @ Old Street, is the Most Horrible place to have a Snack,
I ordered a Steak Bake and was offered a Frozen piece, which I had to Bin after 1 Bite, Prior to which I requested if it can be warmed In a Microwave, but the Staff refused to do so and were making a joke of out of..... @ Greggs Old Street today 14th November @ 4.00 pm.

I again requested to warm it up and they refused & very abruptly refused even a refund for a Snack which i threw in the bin....

HORRIBLE GREGGS ..... Making Millions .... NO CUSTOMER SERVICE

Must also add, people who had paid money for the snacks, had just left it on the Table without eating .....

I Normally go to Wenzels ( any Branch) & they always offer warm snacks....... with a smile

Dear All Please DO NOT buy any snacks from Greggs.

Kind Regards
Anil Christie

A delivery that took less than 10 mins to accept and arrive yet my bacon and sausage baguette, bacon roll and sausage roll, sausage bean cheese bake were all freezing cold. How are you happy serving customers cold pastries. If you’re expecting any good food quality I wouldn’t bother ordering from here if I was you.

Couldn't find a minus but just wanted to know how these people get jobs in here. Who's supposed to be cleaning the tables? Surely there dosn't need to be two serving when the place is empty. One could definitely be wiping the tables.

This bakery is mainly for commuters. People getting on the train etc people walking pass .as it's a red route and no way of parking .
Not there fault ,but I drove 2 miles out my way to go there ,not knowing the area

A place that serves people and food should present itself in a more respectable and welcoming way. Unfortunately, both the dining area and restrooms appeared poorly maintained and uninviting.

The staff member on duty was sitting on their phone and did not acknowledge guests, which did not create a trustworthy atmosphere. Due to serious hygiene concerns, I didn’t feel comfortable ordering anything to eat.

I sincerely hope improvements will be made in both cleanliness and customer service

Just a quick eat on the way to the Sunday market. Not expecting anything gourmet here.. but found the sausage roll tasting more like luncheon meat than sausage. The pastry on the sausage role ain't super too.. but that was kind of expected.. but we simply have to experience this slice of the London life.

Little strange to see that the shop always keep the door open all day taking in consideration the weather. Staff on the counter seems like they don't understand very well english. The rest all good nice choice of sandwiches, chocolate muffin is delicious with a lovely chocolate cream in the middle.
Tables should be also kept cleaner.

Really huge shout out for team who served me a nice warm food with good options of sauces and huge smile on face. Whereas, Another great place of Greggs!  Menu keeps evolving to meet every budget and the range of products means there is something for everyone at any time of day!

I personally love the great value coffee (gives pricier merchants a run for their money any time!) and veggies, which I somehow manage to add to all my orders!

The points scheme is also fabulous and those points quickly add up to genuine freebies from the menu

Ordered omelette baguette and it was a complete letdown. The omelette was burnt to a crispy, dry, overcooked and completely lacking the soft, cheesy texture it should have. I wasted time going back for a replacement, only to get another subpar version.
As a note to the staff: an omelette should not be cooked until it’s dark brown. You’re ruining what should be a simple thing.
On top of that, the customer service was painfully slow. Not worth the hassle, won’t be returning to this branch ever.

Bought this apple juice 10 minutes ago and got on the train. It’s MOULDY! Absolutely vile please avoid. I should’ve known when I tried to feel the drinks in the fridge and all of them were room temperature. This is disgusting please fix it. Disappointed that I wasted my money and scared for people who didn’t check before drinking.

Probably the worst Greggs. They give you cold bacon rolls , charge you incorrectly (e.g. don’t apply the breakfast deal etc including coffee), don’t make sure your loyalty card has scanned, staff are complaining and chatting loudly. Every time I have visited the experience is of chaos and disorganisation.
I liked my buy 3 get one free sausage rolls here this weekend. Cheers.

Stopped for coffee approx 11pm Wednesday evening to warm me up - wish I didn't!

Seriously that bad. Luke warm is overstatement! Because had train to get to didn't complain but you can be sure I'll be putting my two pennies in - not even because I want money back but deep purple thinking they might get a hot drink for way home on a cold night.

Great option for quick breakfast!
Great selection of goodies with many vegetarian options available.
Sweets selection is good too.
Reasonable prices.
Make sure you use your app!

I love the tuna crunch baguette and sweet potato bhaji & rice It was very tasty I had fruit as well but no spoon was offered. The counter service person was good and also the sugar and sauce table was very dirty there was  no underneath bin. I hope it will help you to improve your store .

Breakfast rolls are not hot at all, just about warm.

Staff are friendly.

I ordered the Bacon & Omelette Roll, and with the first bite, I honestly thought the staff had forgotten to add the omelette! There was barely any omelette in it, and I was shocked. I’ve never had such a disappointing roll at Gregg’s before, especially considering this branch is £0.50 more expensive than others. Totally unacceptable!

Good little Greggs but there is a queue most times so bare that in mind if you need to catch a train. They have good vegan sausage rolls. Staff are fast and efficient. Loads of pastries, sweet treats, drinks available to takeaway.

Really below par on what to expect from a Greggs. The only reason I ordered from there was because Walworth Rd and Tower Bridge had no hash browns. Placed the order at 10:15am. The courier was there at 10:35am and he had to wait around til 11:15am for the order to be prepared and I eventually got the food at 11:30am. Half of it was cold and it was missing an item also. I've complained but I've only got back a quarter of what was spent. Wish I hadn't bothered in the first place.

I didn't even know that a gregg's was based in a tesco store! Fridges & the hot counter looked quite full for how busy it was. The bakes were limited! There are 2 tables to sit on. Can pay cash or card

Poor customer service. Rude and unwelcomed. Manager refuses to acknowledge the staff behaviour. Dan/daniel or whatever the fat person name is (btw his another store manager ) should not be working as he failed to deliver customer service. Comes in between a conversation I had with the manager and says ‘I will do what I want’! What a rude disrespectful man from a store manager infront of another manager! Unacceptable! Never going back there again! Rude and disrespectful! No wonder it’s 2.5 star. I will not be returning to this store ever again.

I have been going there since it opened and haven't had issues with any of the products or staff

Staff seem like they all go to school 😂 i mean that in a good way

Shop is open.. but no food to eat.. only coffe and sausage rolls..
Where is all the rest of the things? All the greggs i use has at least one or two of each in every morning.. this one is always with issues.
And they stating that the baguettes will be ready only in two hours... seriously?

Everytime I come in morning at 8 am or around 8:10 am no sandwich ready. Vey disappointing because it says they open at 6:30 am so normally as I’m aware it takes an 1 hour to prepare cook savouries. Bag up the doughnuts etc…. So surely by 8 am there should be a few sandwich ready on shelf but there hardly isn’t any on shelf completely empty. Maybe I should escalate it further and inform the area manager.

Mohd should not be dealing with customers. No eye contact, no acknowledgement. I ordered one item politely, including words such as “please” and “thank you” and yet he threw the bag on the counter without saying a word. We all have hard and stressful days. I work at Guy’s Hospital and we deal with patients every hour of every day but I  am always courteous and kind to them, even when I am having a bad day.
Mohd has a lot to learn in terms of people skills.

Staff look and interact with you like they wish they could be anywhere else but behind the Greg's counter. When there's a line that goes out the shop, I'm not sure talking with your co-worker over the unbaked sausage roles, next to the open oven, is the best approach, but you do you brother.

I don't often leave reviews, but feel I had to this time for excellent customer service. I had ordered ahead on the Greggs app, but it turned out I had ordered to the wrong Greggs! However, even during the busy morning rush, the staff members were happy to provide me with my order at this venue, and we're extremely polite and fast. It doesn't sound like a big deal, but this gave me an excellent start to my day.

I GOT FOOD POISONING!
I was debating whether to post this review or not, but here it is.
Back in January 28th, I got a vegan sausage roll, which gave me food poisoning. I know this because it was the only thing I ate all day! I ate it at 10:30am and felt fine, but an hour later I started to feel nauseous and decided to leave my planned exhibition tour quite early, I decided to go home and I made it to Whittards in London Bridge Station, where I was shaking, very pale, feeling faint and had shortness of breath, to my humiliatian, I ended up throwing up in the shop, staff called the station medics at roughly 12:30pm, where I had to get my blood pressure checked, temperature checked and also blood sugar levels checked. When I raised this concern with Greggs via email, I was told it couldn't have been food poisoning as it takes 48-72 hours for symptoms! (Absolute nonsense!) They done an 'investigation', found that everything was within the correct temperatures (which can easily be faked), everything was within the best before date and there was no cross contamination.
They gave me a £10 voucher as a gesture of goodwill despite me losing out on £35 for an exhibition I couldn't see properly and couldnt attend again, and the humiliatian of throwing up in an incredibly busy and public space, not to mention throwing up on myself as well and having to spend an hour going home whilst feeling incredibly sick and rough and smelling of vomit!
I gave my brother the voucher to use, but I will never eat a vegan sausage roll from them again, and I certainly won't be buying anything else from Greggs!
I urge you to avoid this Greggs as cleanliness is clearly not up to standard! Whether it is equipment, staff cleanliness or just not properly baking the food.
Risking eating some food from there is not worth your health and there are much better cafes and resturants in the area

Poor service. I order a bacon and egg baguette then the store worker went to get the baguette, meanwhile the other store worker sold two bacon rolls. I was then told there was no bacon left. Don’t mess with a man who’s had a long morning of graft!

visited Greggs near Whitechapel and while the location is very convenient, my experience was unfortunately marred by cleanliness issues. The coffee itself was decent, but I was quite concerned about the hygiene standards.
The bakery tray was noticeably unclean, which made me question the overall sanitary practices of the establishment. Additionally, I observed staff handling food without gloves, raising further concerns about food safety.
I hope the management takes steps to improve hygiene
They make a good tea and have a great app where you can order and pick up straight away
Service is incredibly slow. I try to go here before work but queue can be up to 15 mins and they usually only have one person on at a time. Usually end up defecting to Costa.

This company can spend millions on Nigella but not on making the brand better with training
Yet most branches have queues, never ready stock and staff members blatantly sitting on tables talking on phones. Do better!

Rafia served me and made it an amazing day for me today. Thank your for your patience, you helped me by being patient when I couldn’t make a decision, you were calm, and helped me with my confidence.
Thank you    🙏🏽

Greggs is a place that I go to everytimes I visit my favorite city in the world!! This time I get to explore Whitechapel and the architecture surrounding it. Of course Greggs is a must go - and the staff at this Greggs are the nicest ever. I wanted some pasties and the associates asked me to wait a minute and he brought out freshly baked pasties just for me. So nice! I always buy Greggs reusable cup so happened that they have a nice one - and the lady (olivia I think her name) and another lady associates was kind to wash the reusable cup for me before filling it with my beverages of my choice! How thoughtful. Definitely job well done! Will be back again. Such awesome people! Thanks!

Quiet a nice greggs branch; I'm sure this branch has had a revamp to allow the option of takeaway or eat in. You can pay with cash or card; their is even a toilet & plenty of room for buggies & wheelchair users

I'm usually really happy with this place when I stop by for breakfast and the staff are lovely.

Went by today (7th June) There is rubbish on every table. It didn't seem like the staff wanted to clear up. Who knows how long it has been there for....

Music was really loud and kept pausing/stopping.

Had the 6quid meal deal. Hot southern fried chicken bagged.  With wedges and coke. Had to pay extra on the sugar tax for the coke. Must off been good timing as the wedges where nice and fresh. Good flavour.  Enjoyed the chicken baguette. I have noticed the baguettes are getting slightly shorter. Will eat there again

the cheese and onion bake was excellent but i am a cheese lover so i might be a bit biased. i was very excited to try the infamous sausage roll because i have seen it for years online. it was very good too, delicate and buttery. the prices are extremely fair and the pastries are served still very hot! i will definitely be back on my birthday!

Popped in as was in the area; found the staff to be very polite & friendly.
Very limited bakes and cakes; but sandwiches were well stocked. The drinks section definitely needed to be restocked. I was quite surprised as their was enough staff on site to keep the store stocked for customers
The shop is small, and the customer service crumble under pressure when its busy, but when the food comes its really delicious.

Don't try this branch (112 Roman Rd, Globe Town, London E2 0RN).

I was served cold cheese 🧀 and onion pastry. I asked them to warm it up, they said its policy they cannot warm, so you get served cold food. I'd rather eat from a dogs bowl 🥣.

Rubbish service and food in bin. Worst branch in London.
customer services was good. nice location. they open very early and 7 days a week.
Good food good service but needs a security guard as homeless people just going in and helping themselves to food
Bacon Baguette with extra Bacon ordered online.  Arrived stone cold and Bacon was so hard and looked like plastic, so old and overcooked was inedible, even the dog didn’t want it.  Very disappointed
They served me a cold sausage roll and told me they could not heat it up. Staff was unfriendly and seemed bothered.

How long you gonna keep broken receipt printer every time I ask because I needed for my business ,printer doesn't work. Come on!!!!!!!!
I will stop buying it. Good luck and fix it.......
Staff will accept orders, have you wait 30 minutes and then cancel the order. Unprofessional or incompetent.

This place is a joke never open when it say it should be open always closed early and open late in the morning and the workers just look at you though the window don’t come and say sorry we’re opening late today
Just look at you thought the window
They lost so much money today 14 people left because they wasn’t open walked up knocked on the door and the man just looks at them thought the window and don’t say anything this store needs new workers that can open up on time or change the times

I stopped by Greggs for a quick lunch and honestly, the sausage rolls are absolutely fantastic – definitely worth trying if you're visiting the UK. The prices are really reasonable, which is refreshing compared to some other spots around here. Inside there are few seatings, so you can actually sit down and enjoy your food rather than rushing off. They have a solid selection of both savoury options and sweet treats, plus a good variety of drinks to choose from.
My only criticism would be that the staff didn't seem particularly enthusiastic or friendly when I visited. They weren't rude exactly, just a bit flat and indifferent. That said, it didn't really take away from the overall experience – the food quality and value more than made up for it. If you're looking for an affordable place to grab something tasty without breaking the bank, Greggs is a solid choice
Nice one. You have some seatings inside. The taste is not bad. I had chicken BBQ snack and the salt was bit less, spicy leaves is okay.

One of the very few sandwich shops open on the weekend. Prices are standard, so greggs is always a good shout for affordable food.  Fresh baguettes and pastry, this time I went for a lunch meal deal. Wedges side were crispy, baguette was toasted well and were great portion sizes. Staff were upbeat and attentive. Recommend this branch.

Greggs Fish Finger Sandwich.
Finally its available in the City of London at the Finsbury circus branch near moorgate.
Made to order but unfortunately the one I got, the fish had been done for ages and probably under a heat lamp hence the hard and chewy texture.
The fish was longer silky but coarse like rope.
The lettuce shown on the picture was salad mix.
Overall, it would have been nice judging by the would have been flaky fish but I went at the wrong time.
According to staff, the fish fingers are thrown away after 2 hours of cooking so I reckon my fish fingers were in the heat lamp for just less than 2 hours but just under because they were not thrown away yet.
Got here at 06:07 and it was closed!! Such an inconvenience to my morning. There was clearly staff in there working but just not open and it’s Tuesday so it should be open at 06:00. Not impressed.
Very friendly staff and tasty food
Thank you to Saya and the rest of the team. I love you. Amazing service biggest review I wish to give. Thank you.

Staff are forever stocking up shelf's and take too long to serve. Very lackadaisical service in my opinion.
Went to the store today at 530 and it was closed, the two staff members were stocking up for next day. Sign says 630am to 6pm. Rude man aggressively shouted we are closed through the door.
Not acceptable really.

Sandwich was missing half the ingredients.

The staff were lifeless. You can get more of an emotional response in a wax museum.

Poor service. How are the same staff still working there?!

This has happened more than once.
A very small shop but lovely workers.  I take my coffee there in the morning.  Very nice one.

Stuffs can’t explain what the app says on itself. Very rude to the customer while they are busy. Paid for a mocha and a cheese and onion. There is no chocolate on my mocha and the cheese n onion is cold. Very unprofessional and poor customer service.
Good service here for me, they serve customer proactively. The food is the Greggs food, but it’s good vibe.

I think the reviews completely justify the place. Specially i would not give even half a star. All thanks to Muna who first not only gave me the wrong order but also blankly told me i was wrong in placing the order. Rather than offering a refund for no oat milk, she said just have whatever coffee they have.
Worst place to have a coffee ever.

Great spot to come and sit down and have lunch. They also have charging ports which is very useful for those doing work. Not a big fan of their coffee or drink selections but ive always been a very big fan of their food options. Cheap prices and fantastic loyalty app (Greggs app) Where you can redeem food after collecting all the stamps. Fun experience and worth the time
A good place to grab a quick bite. Lots of seating. The vegan sausage had more salt than necessary. Also noticed this at the other Greggs at Canary Wharf station. Someone should look into this!

It is located at the main exit/entrance of Canary Wharf jubilee line station. It is very convenient for us to grab some food on the go. Small shopfront but got a good selection of food and drinks with reasonable prices.

The staff is always friendly and quick. Sometimes the bakes are better than some other days. I like them when they are piping hot.

Always a fantastic experience at this Greggs branch. The staff here are genuinely friendly and super quick, even when the station is packed! They handle the rush with a smile and are always polite. Great food, but the exceptional customer service is what really makes this specific shop stand out. Highly recommend!
Thank you Izabella for such a warm service and vegan sausage rolls. You are such a gem. Waiting for it, observed how you were treating everyone sooooo kindly and equally. Bless

The only milk they have is cow milk. Thought Greggs was more inclusive tbh. These days it's not that hard to get a carton or 2 of oat milk. Although I don't think that would be enough because there's plenty of demand for it, but for whatever reason Greggs decided to not include it in the menu...

A well place store for those who use the TFL services from Canary Wharf. It’s not the best Greggs I’ve been to but normally has a good selection of breakfast items and bakes.

Service is often quick but lacks the sparkle you normally get from Greggs.

I regularly visit the Greggs store on Chrisp Street during my lunch break. It’s a popular spot, not only for myself but also for many local residents. The location is very convenient for the community.
The staff are always friendly and welcoming, which makes the experience pleasant. However, the store tends to be quite busy, especially in the mornings and during lunchtime.

While the sandwiches generally seem healthy, their quality can be inconsistent at times. One major concern is the limited availability of halal options. Given that Chrisp Street is home to a diverse community, it would be great if Greggs could offer more halal chicken sandwiches or halal baguettes. This would better serve the needs of many local customers.

Additionally, the prices could be a little more affordable. They seem slightly high for the area, and a small price adjustment could make a big difference for regular customers.

The staff are good nothing to say there. The food and the way greggs is run now days is horrible. No hot hold for pastry to save on tax lowered the quality of food while raising the prices. Everything from there is discusting and bland. Sausage rolls taste like veg oil fat and salt

Greggs itself is great, just like any other Greggs you’d pop into around the country. The staff are quick, the food is exactly what you expect, and it’s perfect when you need something warm in between classes. The area around it isn’t the nicest, but honestly the shop is a bit of a lifesaver for college kids nearby. Whether it’s a cheap breakfast on the way in or a quick bite between lectures, it does the job and does it well.
Bacon and cheese wrap apparently, some of the staff in this Greggs need to leave immediately

The GREGGS in  crisp street market is just. Dirty. Very dirty.  To be honest I am  disappointed that is kept open it should close down because of cleanness hazards.   Just a honest opinion.  You can do better or hire a cleaner. This is a brand  company please  check the presentation of your shops

Melek the cashier was disgustingly rude. No greetings, no smile and just proceeds to ask me “What did you want?” then tossed my order on the counter with no tissues. Then gets upset cause I matched her energy as I snatched the food off the counter. If you want to be treated with respect then give respect to your customers. Most of the people working there have the same awful attitude but Melek is something else! Do not recomment. Disgusting service

I’d like to share some feedback about my recent visit. I came in to order some fries but was told they were unavailable. I then asked for just a Margherita pizza. However, the staff member who served it to me did so with a very unfriendly facial expression — as if I had come into their home and asked them for a personal favour.
I believe management should pay more attention to how staff communicate with customers. A friendly attitude and basic respect should be the minimum standard for customer service.

I had the worst experience at this branch!!! The team behind the till is worst i think they should close this branch because the team has no manners to serve customers with respect! The guy who served me i am also attaching the image was so unpleasant! I will never come to this branch again

Ordered a ham and cheese baguette today and the ham looked grey and smelled off. I mentioned it to the staff, but they seemed surprised and insisted it was fresh. They offered to waive the cost, but I wasn’t trying to get a freebie—just wanted to point out the issue. Ended up getting another baguette that was fine, but this wasn’t the first time I’ve had a strange experience here. Not sure I’ll be coming back. The same employee also made a snarky comment implying I had no money insinuating that to heat the baguette is more expensive.

Having now seen the reviews myself it seems fitting I will avoid this place forever . The Greggs in my high street is 100x times better

I’ve been a regular customer at Greggs, buying coffee almost every day, but I’ve never had a good experience at this store. Yesterday my friend told me that downloading the Greggs app would give me a free drink, so I tried it. At first, the staff told me it was only for a pastry; I explained that I thought it could be used for a drink and waited. He eventually gave me the coffee, but the rude attitude and the way he made me feel unwelcome completely ruined the experience. It’s not about the freebie—I happily pay for my coffee daily—it’s about the lack of respect and terrible customer service. I also have a slight hearing difficulty, and instead of being patient, the staff made the situation worse. Looking at other reviews, I can see many people have faced the same issue at this store. Yesterday was the last time I’ll ever get a Greggs coffee, and I wouldn’t recommend this branch to anyone.

Greggs!! my first choice for my quick breakfast .
The bacon rolls were freshly made, comes with  drinks for just 2.85.. So far the cheapest place that i can ate my breakfast at Barking after Mc'D..
Great concept and the staffs are amazing too..

Only cons was too much of homeless people roaming around the shop looking spare coins.. 🙃

So glad I found this place, all the local bakeries have closed down in this area. great value for money, they are trying not to waste food they supply food that hasn’t been sold in other branches at a discounted price.
You can ask them politely if they have what you’re after, as they don’t always stock the shelves. Who can resist donuts eh ? Taste so nice the soft sugary dough. Had to grab some pastries and vegan sausage rolls to go aswell.
Monika and Ruba were extremely kind helpful and sweet! We need more people like them, so welcoming and made my day! Thank you ladies :)
Amazing service from Wasim and zareen, they were kind, super efficient and made my experience 10/10!
In my opinion, Ruba and Diana are what make this service truly memorable. Their dedication and warmth create an experience that keeps me coming back every single day. Protect at all cost!

A classic fast food chain with a good selection of food and drink at a really reasonable price. There is a good amount of seating and service is very efficient. A good spot for something to eat before attending an event at the stadium or arena.

The most disgusting thing I've ever bought in my life!!!

Would you call this a chicken burger??? This chicken looks like it was made last week!

Nice warm food on a freezing day. Great friendly staffs.
Some WiFi indoor would be great.
By the way, half of your food displaying got snatched at 14:15 on 20th November. Why am I paying more to cover such loss?

Highly recomend, better then the other places in area. So much cheeper. As a student I've been numerous times for the breakfast and they never fail to disappoint. Today they didn't have omelets due to delivery delay but had a nice cup of tea with hashbrowns and a quorn sausage bap for under £5

Terrible service 0 manners not a simple hello good afternoon, not a thank you and when asking to heat a stone cold pizza for my 4 year old child I was refused with no reason apart from we are not aloud to at Greggs. Not aloud to heat something but aloud to serve what’s supposed to be hot food cold even though they heat things through out the day, diabolical and ridiculous. Won’t be returning

You know exactly what you’re getting with Greggs, and this branch delivers just that. The sausage rolls were fresh and hot, and the pastries were flaky and satisfying. Staff were friendly despite it being busy, and everything moved along quickly. It’s not gourmet dining, but for a cheap, filling snack or breakfast on the go, it’s hard to beat.
You know what you're getting with Gregg's but there's one main difference between each branch. Is the pastry still warm after 10am? I can confirm my steak bake was still hot at 12pm 🫡

I recently visited the local Greggs shop and had a great experience. The store was clean, and the selection of fresh pastries and sandwiches was as delicious as ever. I was particularly impressed by the friendly service from the cashier, a very nice European gentleman, who was polite and made my visit even more enjoyable. Highly recommend this location .

My mum gets these delicious cream/'Bavarian' slices for me. These along with the 'cream iced finger' are fresh, full of cream and moreish. Tastes like ice cream cake, perfect for enjoying in the sun! Cheaper, fresher & better quality than the supermarkets & online shops. Unfortunately, they're not available to purchase via online delivery apps otherwise I would be eating them more often.

I don’t usually leave reviews, but I felt I had to this time because of the amazing service I received. I came to the Kilburn Greggs today and was served by Maryam. From the moment I walked in, she was polite, welcoming, and really helpful. She gave me great recommendations and made sure I had exactly what I wanted.

It’s rare these days to find someone who genuinely cares about giving good customer service, but Maryam really stood out. She turned what could have just been a quick stop for food into a really positive experience. Because of the way she treated me, I’ll definitely be coming back to this branch.

Customer service like this makes such a big difference, and I just wanted to make sure it gets recognised. Well done Maryam – you’re a credit to Greggs!!!

Greggs is the best establishment to have graced our earth. The culinary skill, the notoriously amazing customer service and my oh my do not get me started on that margherita pizza, if I talk about that i’ll be here for ages. Anyways, all you should know is that you can never and i mean never go wrong with greggs. Alhumdullilah for greggs.
Arrived here around 11AM on a weekday, just right before a line starting forming. Ordered the steak bake and it was great. Good value.

Went in the afternoon for some sausage rolls but majority of the trays were empty including bakes, i was told that i had to wait 18 minutes if i wanted sausage rolls, i couldn’t wait that long so i went with the vegan sausage rolls which was below warm. Service was ok, bit disappointed that there were hardly anything apart from vegan sausage rolls, probably it was late afternoon when school kids finish school so everything was sold out.
The staff here are utterly useless and dont know what they are doing, how to operate tills or take orders.

Was there recently, bought a chicken mayo baguette and a chicken bake, the baguette looked a bit strange, the chicken did not look like what it usually looks like. I threw up about 8 times after that. There was definitely something wrong with the chicken, AVOID THIS PLACE.
I mean, I love a Gregg’s, who doesn’t, but this branch is a shambles. They gave my drink to the girl after me in the queue and don’t keep it properly stocked. Very poorly managed.

We were ‘served’ by a lady called Mel. Mel lacks customer service and does not give eye contact at all. She came across as rude and her tone of voice, when we asked a question was unnecessary. Kindness/politeness really does not cost.
Mel was great, she was lovely and really made my day today!
Anyone who doesn't give Greggs 5 stars is a Pret a Manger wannabe snob.

This greggs is the best.... Welcoming and fresh food.

Three cheers to Amaar for the service he provides and convos we have while ordering 🙌🙌

The only time youre disappointed in Greggs is when they're closed.

The staff is always super friendly and eager to solve issues should they occur.

🌱 The vegan festive lattice is surprisingly good! The texture of the vegan qourn and sage gravy together with the classical and infallible pastry of any greggs makes for a delicious lattice.

🌱 The vegan sausage rolls, however, are just the best. The ultimate snack or quick lunch (or maybe breakfast...)

The minced pies are nice, but too sweet. Nevertheless, bonus points for being vegan!

I had a really unpleasant experience at Greggs today. I went to collect a Too Good To Go evening bag that I was genuinely looking forward to. When I arrived, the food hadn’t been prepared yet, so I politely asked the staff to ensure the items were vegan, as I can’t eat meat for religious reasons.

The supervisor on duty of black skin tone immediately refused. I thought she might be joking at first, but instead she aggressively put a chicken baguette and other non-halal items into the bag. I felt uncomfortable but went ahead and swiped for the collection because she was waiting for me to do so.

After leaving the shop, I realised that since I had paid for the bag, I should receive items that I can actually eat. I returned to the store and calmly asked if the items could be swapped. She then told me they don’t give out hot food, which isn’t true—the Too Good To Go description clearly states that the bag can include bakes, sandwiches, and desserts. Despite this, I was only given cold items.

When I explained again that I couldn’t eat the chicken, she laughed and said it was a “mystery bag.” Her attitude was rude and dismissive, and she accused me of making her look like a bad person when I was simply asking for edible alternatives. She then told me to cancel the order if I didn’t want it and aggressively took the bag from my hand. I was instructed to cancel online, but since I had already swiped for the collection, I’m now unsure whether I’ll even receive a refund.

Overall, the service was shocking and unprofessional. The staff behaved as though they were above customers. I would strongly advise others to avoid this Greggs location.

I arrived at 8.30am on a weekday only to be told they had no sausage rolls ready and would have to wait 10 minutes for the next batch to cook. Obviously I wasn’t going to stand there 10 minutes so left to get breakfast elsewhere. I don’t understand how 3 people working in such a small branch and not 1 of them realising that items were getting low and that they should get a new batch ready so people didn’t have to wait. I would understand if it was a couple of minutes wait but ten minutes! Why would they wait for an item to completely finish before then starting to cook a new batch, it’s basic intuition.
Always have a great experience here! I frequently go in on my way to work and the staff are friendly, service is quick even during morning rushes.
