Warm vegan sausage roll at a great price and served very quickly. Staff were friendly and attentive and it made me smile so you get the full 5 stars! Small store but packs a lot in

Ordered from this greggs. They sent me a 2 month out of date orange juice. Stupidly I never checked the date before drinking it. Which then led to me being unwell for 2 days. Greggs couldn't care less about health and safety. There investigation concluded with no issues found. Well this is clearly an issue. AVOID. Health inspectors need to look into there stock.

I just went into greggs on highbury  I bought two sausage rolls and a pink Jamie that were both put into VEGAN bags otherwise great service but there was a tall Indian gentleman behind the counter his hair was tied back but no hairnet not good practice around food

I came in here at 9:15 on the 11th of February 2025. I ordered a roll breakfast set which is a breakfast roll and a drink the lady behind the counter made me my breakfast roll. I then asked for a latte as my drink, but she said that I hadn’t mentioned that I wanted a coffee I replied that I had asked for a sept which is a drink and also a breakfast roll. She said I should’ve said what kind of drink I wanted. She angrily gave me the drink and said just take it.. terrible customer service and tried to make me feel bad for her error.

On the plus side, the food was nice

I’ve been going to Greggs Highbury with my service dog. I couldn’t be happier. The service is friendly and polite and they are super nice to my little dog which I really appreciate as public access can sometimes be a nightmare. It’s a pleasure to experience friendly, calm and normal and also to be welcomed and appreciated as a customer. Kingsland Road on the other hand needs a total staff overhaul I’ve never experienced anything more rude and discriminating than I have at Kingsland Rd but the stellar customer service experience at Highbury makes it all better.
The woman working behind the till, Julie, was so friendly and helpful - great customer service!

This Greggs branch used to be really good with former staff… now it is poor! The shop is never properly ready when open and I see customers arrive and leave with nothing as there is no baked goods ready to buy! I only buy a coffee there so it doesn’t usually affect me. Shame for other customers.
The team here are legends, 06:30am on a Sunday and they're working hard to feed customers that order, you guys are amazing, keep it up 💪🏻

This must be one of the worst managed Greggs in London. I have had staff complaining about how busy they are, they talk more than they work and there was only one person serving again this morning making us wait almost 20 minutes for a coffee!!

Just got home with my lunch, and there was ANTS in all the bags , I nearly threw up .
I phoned up gregs, and the guy said, " we are aware of this and reported it this morning."
BUT THEY STILL SERVED FOOD ........
If a manager reads this, I would appreciate an apology.
Worst Gregs, is always empty no sandwiches, no hot food  only coffee not the best too.
Lovely staff - particularly the lady who served me at around 4pm. Welcoming, friendly, and efficient.
Didn't catch the workers name but she was friendly and smiling and sorted my order out fast :) this morning

Staff serving were unfriendly. After being called up to the front like a school child in detention, the service I experienced was disappointing.

They said they didn’t know whether any of the pastries were warm/cold and were unbothered to even consider answering.

Considering the job is to sell pastries, this does seem essential.

All in all, not good. I didn’t buy anything, of course.
This is completely unexpectable. If you are out of an item just cancel my order. Now I have paid 1.99 delivery for this coke bottle which I am not getting back ! What a terrible service.

When I was at Greggs in Dalston Kingsland high road yesterday, I got a free birthday cake donut from the app, and a bottle of water, I paid for the bottle of water and said that I have a free birthday cake donut from the app, I’ve scanned the rewards code on the Greggs app, and the lady that works in Greggs wished me a lovely day, I then waited patiently to one side of the Greggs shop for around 5 minutes but no response then the Greggs lady came to me and asked me what was I waiting for and I said that I’ve asked for the free Greggs birthday cake donut on the app and so she asked me to scan it again, so I did that and then everything was good afterwards

Visited for the first time recently. They offer very basic food in here. It’s fairly inexpensive. I ordered a cheese pizza ‘slice’ - it’s not quite a slice though, more a single cooked rectangle pizza-style food. It’s not proper pizza dough, more a simple pale bready substance. The sauce isn’t very flavourful, vague tomato, sweetness. The cheese is also quite basic, edible but nothing to get excited about. But for £1.65 I won’t really complain (though I will critique); the pizza was just about what I expected. The service was friendly. I’d come back here for another cheap meal/snack.

Slow service even if pre-ordering and have paid a premium for a flavoured latte and been given a normal latte; twice now. Seems understaffed sadly, the workers always seem flustered and overwhelmed as nice as they are.

Some dumb workers in there. Ordered some things and some was missing, got a refund then I ordered again to get the things that was missing and still had missing stuff. Ridiculous. And the sausage rolls was way over done and have been very greasy.
This Gregg's is really run very badly. Head office needs to take some action. Shelf's are always empty. And druggies always helping themselves.Woth the right staff it can be a very good Greggs

£3.85 for a lunch menu and it's only just a sandwich and a drink not even a sandwich drink and a snack you can get all 3 of those at co-op for £3.50 honestly Gregg's not cool at all no wonder I left and didn't buy anything from you

Egg Mayo Sandwich is absolutely disgusting, bland and hard to finish. Their Potato Wedges are lovely, warm and indulgent. Their Tuna Melt Baguette is delicious too, but I do wish there was more tuna in it. Service is efficient even when there is a considerably long queue. Good value for money as £5 for a meal deal is pretty good, just don't get the Egg Mayo Sandwich.

Going to Greggs in Stratford Westfield to have a breakfast with my wife but we regretted as going there ruined our morning with a rude attitude of the manager Ann and the person who served us who doesn’t know what they have and what they don’t have to serve . No omelette at 9:30 in the morning after chose some thing else and refunding us because they also didn’t have it and the manager is not happy telling the person who serving us are you still with the same customer ? When I explained to her what is happening her answer is only offering a refund.
If you want to have a good morning, DO NOT go there

Only one picture of an eaten sausage roll as they are too good to let go cold while faffing with a phone!

THIS GREGGS GETS BIIIIZAAAAY! If you are lucky to bag a seat you can have a decent priced coffee,drink,sarnie, pastry and watch the hustle and bustle of Westfield.

During peak times the food always seems to be hot as I am sure the ovens are on constantly!

Also, what happened to the pizza? I'm sure it never used to be this good. The staff always seem to be lovely but very busy so don't expect a conversation about the theory of relativity you might have at a local Greggs lol.

Employee kept rushing us and forcing us to order quick. She would not allow us to use a code we had for a free drink. She told us “to hurry up because the place was packed” when we were just asking what we can get. She made us feel overstimulated and uncomfortable and felt judgemental.

Really huge shout out for Nita she is amazing lady served me a nice warm food with good options of sauces and huge smile on face. Whereas, Another great place of Greggs!  Menu keeps evolving to meet every budget and the range of products means there is something for everyone at any time of day!

I personally love the great value coffee (gives pricier merchants a run for their money any time!) and veggies, which I somehow manage to add to all my orders! Just need to get long chairs to seat whereas it prefer more and more customers while having seating to have food.

The points scheme is also fabulous and those points quickly add up to genuine freebies from the menu - but please add the option to buy a larger meal or subs with points 🙏

This store consistently experiences significant stock shortages, regardless of the time of day. Inquiries directed to staff regarding the persistent emptiness of the shelves have been met with dismissive and unhelpful responses.

GREGGS OUTLET IN PLAISTOW!!

A real Greggs lover for many years, heard about this outlet and so glad it’s finally here!

Opened as from Tuesday 17th October 2023

Monday - Friday 8am to 5pm
(Weekend hours vary)

We used to have a Percy ingles but got shut down and replaced with an African takeaway so residents of Newham had limited choices unless making your way to Stratford Centre.

Clean and friendly atmosphere, very friendly and helpful staff who go above and beyond with picking your order from delivery not to mention the great selection at more than half the price of your normal Greggs store!

Great variety and selection especially during the cost of living prices. Could not have been placed in a better location.

Purchased 2 packs of 4 sausage rolls, 3 packs of 2 Steak beak, 2 packs of 2 Chicken bake, 2 sandwiches, 1 baguette, a single Steak beak and a pack of yum Yums for a grand total of £15! (Would cost roughly £30 in normal store)

Bargain ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️
I really liked this bakery! I will definitely come back here again.

A good local Gregg's Outlet, mostly you'll get early morning workers and school children going in for their daily breakfast/ lunch.

Very good price for quick snacks.

The downfall is the display of food products is slow display,  as they are mostly empty or run out early morning, what the shop assistant would say. That is not the case as you could overlook the products behind the till on crates and ask for them to be given. Needs to be more prompt.

Lovely lady behind counter served me with a smile in the morning. I asked about the best before dates, to which she informed me that they should be eaten same day which made a lot of sense due to the very cheap prices of everything. Most stuff I bought was buy one get one free and was just as fresh as a normal greggs store. I also got a cappuccino and the server had a smile and was very nice in general, coupled with the affordable prices created a nice atmosphere in a not always nice area. I left happy with my purchase, knowing I can back for food and snacks if I’m ever in the area with no problem. Only thing I would say is that the self service milk jug was empty, but I can’t fault them for that as they probably genuinely didn’t know, and most drinks don’t require to add milk necessarily so not a big deal in any case. Regardless I was happy to know this store is in the area. 5 stars❤️

I go to Gregg's every morning on my way to work and I have to say I have a lovely experience every morning. ALL the staff are so welcoming and kind and always put a smile on my face before I get to work. It's not an easy location to work in I can only imagine, but the customer service here is just impeccable!!

This is one of the most prolific bakery’s in the united kingdom but in my opinion not the greatest by far. I tend to go here if in a rush and hungry wanting to get a quick snack on a budget but for the sake of an extra couple of £’s you will find better variety and quality food elsewhere. Their opening times is at 7am however once arriving early morning at 7:30am I still received a stone cold vegan sausage roll and was told it will take 20 mins for a warm batch. So in my case not so fresh at all. I also purchased two yum yum for £1.50 which tasted not to bad but overall the atmosphere is very rushed and there was little  communication prior to taking my money if my purchase would be cold and if I would prefer a hot fresh one.

Two muffins missing in my order, I called them just to see how to solve it, the woman who picked up the phone was really rude, and say there’s nothing they can do, not even a sorry, and she said she packed up so it was definitely correct, so where are the muffins? Homeless dogs ate them? Or some mysterious alien?
Definitely not ‘fresh’ or made ‘today’. Bread was stale and rock hard. Chucked half of it in the bin… no time to go back and complain as back at my desk at work. Not a happy lunch experience. ☹️

I gave the guy £10 and he gave me change of £5.25! So I said you've given me the wrong change. I was expecting £5.65. he took the money off me then gave me back £4.35!  He walked away. I am hesitant to share this review, but I feel it is necessary to bring certain issues to the attention of the management.

I have noticed a recurring problem with the store's opening hours. Despite arriving just before the scheduled opening time on numerous occasions, I have found that the store consistently opens late. I have attached a few photographs to illustrate this issue.

Additionally, there seems to be a persistent problem with equipment breakdowns in this branch. Last week, one of the ovens was out of order, and this week, it is the coffee machine. It is disappointing that customers are unable to enjoy a cup of coffee, especially during the morning rush.

On a positive note, I must commend the morning staff for their exceptional friendliness and helpfulness. They have consistently demonstrated courtesy and support, which is greatly appreciated.

I sincerely hope that the management will consider this feedback with due attention and take appropriate action.

On a cold and rainy day, I stumbled upon a true gem at Greggs – an exceptional ambassador b the name of Sumith who went above and beyond to brighten my day. Their enthusiasm and warmth were as comforting as the aroma of freshly baked goods wafting through the air.

As I entered the store, shivering from the chill outside, I was greeted with a genuine smile that instantly warmed my soul. This ambassador wasted no time in informing me about the Greggs app, highlighting the irresistible offer of a free coffee upon download. Their enthusiasm was infectious, and I found myself eagerly reaching for my phone to take advantage of this generous offer.

Not only did they assist me in downloading the app, but they also took the time to walk me through its features, ensuring that I could make the most of my future visits to Greggs. Their passion for the brand was evident in every word they spoke, and it was truly refreshing to encounter someone who took such pride in their work.

Thanks to this ambassador's kindness and dedication, my rainy day turned into a ray of sunshine. Their exemplary service left a lasting impression on me, and I will undoubtedly be returning to Greggs, not only for their delicious treats but also for the exceptional customer experience that this ambassador represents. Greggs is lucky to have such a shining star in their midst, and I wholeheartedly recommend seeking out their guidance for a truly memorable visit.

Staff focus on getting just eat orders first before serving customers that are actually waiting to be served.
Seems to be short staffed as long queues and slow service are frequent, and only one staff member serving.
been going here for 20+ years now. i don’t pop in often but it’s always been good. never had any issues. friendly staff. could do with some new items on the menu. but all in all a good shop

Polite & friendly staff. There was an altercation this morning and the staff were lovely towards the victim and kept composed throughout it. This isn’t just a Greggs, the staff make it much more than that.
Needed a coffee, bought a coffee. The staff were very pleasant and efficient. I will drop in if I am over this way again. Does what it says on the tin.
always Yummy food, staff are so polite, always fixes things when they go wrong, lovely to have a good chat with them. Wish i could rate higher.
Very fast efficient service. Clean and clutter free. Food was fresh and readily available.

I usually enjoy Greggs for a quick bite, but today’s visit was really disappointing. The staff were unnecessarily rude and dismissive when I asked a simple question about my order. Rather than being helpful, I was met with an attitude that made me feel unwelcome and uncomfortable. Customer service is just as important as the food, and unfortunately, this experience left a sour taste.

I hope management takes this feedback seriously, because Greggs is usually known for friendly service — but this location fell far short.

I am seething. I went to this branch, and the sour-faced Karen had the nerve to snigger under her breath when I didn’t read the tiny sign they had on their door. I missed it because I have severe insomnia, and I was trying to get from point A to point B as quickly as possible. This high street already overstimulates me on top of the insomnia, and they usually don’t even have a sign up, so I just acted accordingly.

I’m guessing she’s another person who assumes my life is perfect just because I’m young, and assumed I was wilfully ignoring what the sign said. This isn’t the first time that sour-faced Karen has been rude to me, and it will be the last time I go into that branch. She was lucky I didn’t confront her at the time, but I wasn’t going to let her make me behave unladylike—just because squaring up to her is probably the only way she’d respect someone.
Very quick and polite service. Only £3.29 for a sausage Baguette,  can't be beaten.  This shop also has internal seating.
Excellent, I cannot compare Greggs, it's superbly fresh and the management services is great, kings heath branch visited

What is it with this Greggs and running out of the basics? Went in, and they’d run out of sausage rolls and steak bakes. Again. Told that they’d be out in 8 mins, so say with the 4 other people waiting. When the 6 steak bakes finally came out, they were immediately snapped up by the others waiting and 2 other customers waiting in the queue. So I was told I would have to wait another 15 mins.
It only seems to be this greggs that waits till they’ve run out of an item before cooking another batch. Common sense says that that you ALWAYS have a batch of your biggest sellers in the ovens at your busiest time, but instead, this branch is always playing catchup with the basics. Even sausage rolls.
Edit 22/12/25
They ran out of sausage rolls completely by 1300

Very poor and slow service, ever since the refurb it seems like they have applied sloths.. unbelievable slow with the manager to busy showing content on her phone the workers stopping the que and service... bring back the old staff before refurbishment service used to be amazing its such a shame
Bought a ham and cheese baguette got it home and it was stale!! Shop looks dirty and unkept..Maypole Greggs I don’t rate, your lucky you’ve got one star.
Just bought a margarita pizza it was freezing cold and as stale as can be. This place is selling food that’s way past the eatable stage. Disgusting
Always enjoy the food from Greg's. My only criticism is that none of the shops keep the 'hot' food hot.
The food was co cold I had to hold a block of ice to warm my hands up afterwards

Shop empty, very slow service and even though we only ordered 2 breakfast rolls and 2 coffees the whole order was wrong..won't be returning..a similar view taken by everyone we know who has used it .It was an easy order shouldn't be wrong .

Great Place to spend time and have some quality food.
Would recommend everyone to take a visit 😊😊👍🏻
Cold steak bake and festive bake. Was told we cant warm them back up. Staff not really that bothered. Definitely the first and last time here.

I thought I'd try this new greggs & purchased 4 hot sausage rolls this morning for 4 of us only to be Very disappointed  as they were barely warm.i was expecting nice hot sausage rolls.if i wanted them cold i would  have asked for cold.i will not be returning to buy something that wasn't what I wanted.very poor

Food was freezing and stale
Sandwich fridge is broken
Flithy tables when its not busy

Visited on a Saturday afternoon. Store wasn’t busy but not a lot of choice for bakes. Approached the counter and started asking if they had any corned beef bakes, before I had even said “do you have” the older member of staff was shaking her head vigorously. I chose another item which was slammed on the counter. Member of staff (presume supervisor) who was serving me started barking orders at the 2 younger staff (who were both doing something and not just standing around) to do a task telling them she was busy serving all the customers (there was me and one other lady in the shop). No professionalism from the older member of staff  and left feeling awkward and sorry for the two younger staff members.

I only go there because the salesgirl is too beautiful. Otherwise, it’s a shame that there are no fries after 2pm, and the same goes for the pizzas… Service is so-so, but one of the salesgirls makes the experience worthwhile

Easily one of the worst Greggs I’ve ever visited. Older staff behind the counter on their phone at the til leaving me & other customers to stand and wait for her to be done, then she looked up at me & said "what you having?”
I mean seriously? Zero professionalism or self respect, I get my sausage roll in a bag slammed onto the counter which also had ketchup stains everywhere (but I’m sure whatever she was doing on her phone was more important) zero manners when serving me.

Not usually a complainer unless it’s really bad but man, this Greggs needs shaking up because it’s embarrassing in there.

Went here last week around 3pm. Was waiting in the queue. Young fair skinned girl with headscarf on, greeted the customer in front of me next them serve them. when my turn just stares at me very rude look I wasn't even sure if she finished serving them as someone else was putting there food in anyway I ordered 3 items saying please to her. She put 2 items in bags handing them over to me by the till, and took my money,  so I reminded her about the other item (these girls where too busy talking all of the time) i had no verbal response from her so I reminded her again because i was not sure if she heard me, and had a rude reply i am getting it!  and then slams my food down on the glass counter no eye contact just pure attitude. I left thinking about my experience i don't normally complain only when it's really needed. I hope this is sorted out , and then I could take down this neg. Review down.
This place lacks good management by looking at other reviews its a shame because normally i would say its pretty good ...

Hardly any baggettes and sandwich available early afternoon, they dont refill,
Staff here are rude, worst customer experience!! Especially the short hijab!!
This hall green greggs needs a new management team!!
First time I ordered from the app. Securely packed. Roast Chicken Bacon Club Baguette  tasted nice, well made and filling and Crispy Chicken Bites was more than to go with baguette.
Nice little Greggs with a good variety of baked goods. Efficient service and tasty food ideal if you’re passing by or grabbing a quick bite

If we could give zero stars, we would, but unfortunately, that’s not an option!

Me and my sisters have been regular customers at this Greggs branch, but today’s experience left us deeply disappointed and upset. My sister and I went to grab a drink, as we always do, and noticed a Student Beans deal stating that you get a treat with a drink. When we showed the app to the salesperson, he explained that the offer applied only to meal deals, which was fair enough.

However, a lady who turned out to be the manager made an unnecessary and disrespectful comment, claiming in a rude yet “professional” tone that we “always come and show the same thing” to get a treat with our drink. This comment was completely uncalled for and felt like an attack on us as regular customers.

When we asked for her name to file a complaint, she didn’t provide it, instead questioning why we wanted it. She then began lecturing us to read the terms and conditions of the offer, rather than simply explaining it politely in the first place. Her condescending tone and lack of professionalism were unacceptable.

Not everyone has the same ability to read the fine print on an offer—for example, someone could face language or literacy barriers—but instead of approaching the situation with understanding, she chose to humiliate and disrespect us. To make matters worse, she wasn’t even wearing a name tag, further avoiding accountability.

This experience has left a sour taste in our mouths, and I doubt we will return to this branch anytime soon. Customers deserve respect and professionalism, and today’s encounter was far from that. I hope the management takes this feedback seriously to ensure others aren’t treated this way.
It's a great Greggs to eat at great value meal deals

I’ve never been ignored in such a rude manner ever in my life. I came in for a quick something to eat and was not only Ignored but then ignored again because she wanted to serve you each driver. No I'm sorry i will be with you shortly. Just looked though me like I wasn’t even there. I’m a manager in the pub and restaurants field and if one of my staff behaviour like this I would like to know and that must be losing you a Fortune.

It’s a cheese an onion pasty, can’t really say more than they always taste good and it sorts the kids out when you are shopping. I wish the coffee was better but for the prices I’ll just stick to the pasty next time.
THE PLACE to go for a hot chocolate. I know it’s from a machine but it tastes good every time and is very affordable. In Greggs hot chocolate we trust 🙏

Samanta at Greggs is a total rockstar! I asked for a sugar-free syrup drink, and she went off-menu, whipping up the most awesome concoction just for me. It tasted fantastic, and she made me feel like a VIP. Big shoutout to Samanta for the creative vibes and making my day! 🌟🥤✨

Place was flithy. Tables not clean. Cups, crumbs, tea bags, you name it, all on the floor. Even the wind breakers/ divider's all on the ground. Coffee was way too bitter. Oh, and the pizza was stale. No cleaning attention to the public floor space!

This Greggs in Solihull outside is usually a lovely place the customers are very nice and polite also the staff are so nice in this store now they have some rude Asian girls Pakistani with highlighted hair very slim at the till who think they are gods gift making people feel uncomfortable and laughing at them to lower there confidence because they are insecure. They are disrespectful this place needs to change these horrible staff for friendly staff. I have never felt uncomfortable at Gregg's like how I did today however this is a clean store good food drink but the staff are disrespectful I also spoke to the manger she seemed very respectful and also listened very well thank you for that manger :)

One of the worst customer service experiences I’ve ever had. The branch manager was openly hostile, arrogant, and treated customers with clear contempt. Someone with this attitude should not be in a customer-facing role. This behaviour seriously damages the Greggs brand. I will not be returning.
Coffee was nice the hash browns were tough as boots . Big queue and the one lady is just sorting paper bags out the back,  you would think she would come to the front and bring the queue down.

I am extremely disappointed with the service at Greggs over the past three days. Each day, I have encountered the same frustrating issue: long wait times due to a severe lack of staff. There has consistently been only one person working at the front and one person in the back, which is simply not sufficient to handle the customer volume.

The lines are unbearably long, and the wait times have been unacceptable. It's clear that this isn't an isolated incident but a recurring problem that has not been addressed. The few staff members present are doing their best, but it's obvious they are overwhelmed and unable to keep up with demand.

This level of service is not what I expect from Greggs, and it is causing significant inconvenience. I hope management takes immediate action to resolve these staffing issues, as it is negatively impacting the customer experience. Until then, I will be reconsidering my choice to visit this location.

Absolutely appalling staff working on Thursday, there is 1 girl there that actually opens and makes food correctly, but other than that, they open late (20-30 minutes later) they make food incorrectly and the older woman gets an attitude when you mention it. If it was a one time thing, thats fine, but its happens every single week, and i hope that one young girl that does get it right gets the acknowledgement that she is doing her job correctly and very well. I understand being understaffed, but if that branch is consistently opening late and if obviously struggling, the company needs to have a look at themselves.

Great addition to the community, speedy service (or reasonable waits for fresh food from ovens) and nice staff.
Recommend the white chocolate cookies!

Popped into the Greggs in Bearwood and had a really pleasant experience. The staff were lovely  greeted me with a smile and served me quickly, which makes all the difference. The shelves were well stocked and everything I wanted was available, which isn’t always the case in some branches, so that was a nice surprise.

I grabbed a coffee with my food. To be fair, the coffee isn’t anything special  you can taste the difference compared to a coffee shop  but for the price you really can’t complain. It’s perfectly fine if you just need a quick caffeine boost to go with your sausage roll or sandwich.

The place itself was clean and well looked after, which is always reassuring. They’ve got a seating area too, so you can sit in if you prefer, although just bear in mind they do charge a little extra for that. Not the biggest deal, but worth knowing.

All in all, a solid Greggs friendly staff, good availability, decent value for money, and a clean environment. Would definitely pop in again if I’m in Bearwood.

Pleasant service and hot pasty. Steak bake and a latte for £3.35.
It seems that this will be my new Monday one now that the one at Cape Hill has closed.

This must be the most miserable Greggs in the UK! A female member of staff that works in there most mornings (short, Glasses, face like thunder ⛈ ) not so much as a hello or smile! Click and Collect means I order it then I collect it, it does not mean I collect it and immediately leave the store!! Management please put a request in to retrain this lady as I will not be visiting anytime soon. What a jobsworth!
A standard Greggs. Their new Spicy Veg Festive pasty tasted really bad.
I was order mozarrale circle and ice tea whis was 5 pound And i give her 20 pound notes she give me  5 pound back

Went there excited at 7:30 pm after work to use my free birthday treat voucher. The only customers were me and a couple in front of me (we all went into the store at the same time). The couple wanted two hot drinks and some doughnuts. The cashier’s till broke down so we were told the wait would be 5 minutes. While the till was supposedly rebooting the Greggs cashier made the drinks and doughnuts ready so once the till is fixed the couple can take their stuff and go. The till wasn’t fixed and I heard the cashier say the doughnuts are on her as compensation for the wait time… but I was also waiting too for the same time. As the till wasn’t getting fixed, the cashier told me that they’re closed now and so I left after waiting for 20 mins, and while the couple left with the free drinks and doughnuts. I think it was unfair as I was also waiting but was told they are not serving anymore at 7:50 pm on a Friday.

Had my daughter poorly in hospital and she wanted a pie so when I went lots of pies cooked but not one was warm what a waist of food. Whan asked if anything at all was coming out got told no so ended up leaving without anything at all

I visited Greggs today in a panic I needed a bottle of water for my partner to take medication in a hurry the lady severing could see I was upset she was very kind and let me jump the queue much appreciated I don’t know her name to say thank you
Very helpful, happy snd friendly staff, especially Claire and Nicola…was having issues with the app and was sorted out nicely, excellent customer service…even got a charity donation out of me 😂
This place is dirty food on the floor tables dirty  there was food moulded into the floor I was there for half an hour and no staff cleaned the tables I won’t be returning

Just wanted to say that a guy with long hair &glasses who works here is very friendly,always greet customers with a  smile and his customer service is just 10/10 people like him is a huge benefit to Greggs company. Should get promotion. Always feel welcome to come here.

Having frequented this branch of Greggs 2-3x per week over the last few years for lunch due to working nearby, it's a shame that I will not be continuing and finding somewhere else to go for my lunch going forward.

Every single time in recent months there is a queue literally going out of the door, usually this wouldn't be an issue, however there is always only one person serving on the till.

I only see two staff members on shift at a time and the other one is stuck in the back putting new food in the ovens/replenishing the food. This leaves the person on the tills to deal with a queue out the door, making all of the hot drinks which is time consuming and also dealing with the delivery drivers from the food apps.

It's a complete joke to have to wait over 10 mins to be served in a Greggs. I do not blame the staff at all, they are great, but upper management or head office or whoever is penny pinching by not putting enough staff on shifts needs to sort it out.

I came into this store while Christmas shopping was looking to see what could i eat, when a member of staff called Madeleine was there coughing which was quite off putting for a potential customer to see, especially as it was all over the food and she never bothered to put her hand over her mouth!! Needless to say i got out of there quick! If you cant even put your hand over your mouth and cough over the food should not be in a food establishment i cant really judge the food but she was enough to put me off anything.

The best Greggs shop I’ve visited so far.
Lots of tables to sit outside, which is very nice on a sunny day.
Very friendly staff.
Lots of variety of yummy food.
I love a Greggs. Easy tasty breakfast and nice coffee. Great prices. Staff are generally great! And they get things done quickly. It's great to see, I hope they are paid properly for their hard work.

Just been in to get my usual from Greggs. THE SHIFT MANAGER NORA. So much attitude for no reason at all. I understand no one wants to work on a Sunday but I do not appreciate you putting two pasties in one bag and when asked to put them in separate bags her service towards me was appalling. I asked what my total bill was and she did not even offer me a receipt. FROM A SHIFT MANAGER WHO SHOULD BE SETTING AN EXAMPLE TO EVERYONE ELSE ON HER TEAM! AVOID

I looooove this greggs! the coffee was amazing, taste more like a coffee than a water, everything is freshly made when I came and it tasted amaziiiiing! this branch is beyond expectations!

I’m impressed by one of the staff there and her name is Esha, she’s such amazing person with big smile and helpful gesture!🫶🏻

Absolutely terrible experience, waited over 20 minutes in a queue because there was one miserable woman named gent serving, multiple people left before even getting served because of how slow the service was and when I got to the counter to order I was told I would have to wait an additional 10 minutes for a sausage bap even tho it quarter to 11 whilst smirking and rude. She also left to go out the back for a few minutes while there was no one else in the front and about 8 people behind me so I just left
Avoid this Greggs

Big company like greggs and they can’t provide them sliding glass cases like lesser known bakeries and chip shops?! Flies crawl over the food and all the spittle from talking and dust etc. its grim and a huge public health risk. I went in this morning to find one of them bring out a bundle of bread rolls with her bare hands, then the one that served me prepared my omelette bap with his bare hands too?? Vile

A brand new Greggs on a lovely estate, absolutely ruined with poor hygiene and terrible, untrained, miserable staff. (Neem in particular) Bins overflowing, not a single table cleaned and the toilets seemingly are always out of order. I suppose it’s so they don’t have to clean them, like the rest of the shop. My pizza was fresh though, but that’s probably because they’d come out of the oven after having waiting 12 minutes in the queue.
We went there earlier for a quick pre lunch. We were served by a lovely lady. Chicken bakes were fresh and Lattes were just about right temperature. Plenty of seating available inside. Love Greggs.

This is a great Greggs. Nice and spacious with plenty of light. Nice to see shelves stocked so there is plenty of choice.
Great service from the team. Definitely recommend try this place.
Parking out side for 90mins if your passing. 5 star

We are regular customers of this branch and a recent addition to the team who really are excellent is a member of staff called Talisha. She was incredibly professional,  helpful and efficient.  She has joined an already great team of workers. Thank you for the great customer experience.

Children having a good old moan about being HUNGRY minutes after leaving the house. Children eh? 😆 🤣.  Big branch but blighted but 3 (SPECIAL OFFER) resident homeless beggers right outside the shop. Ordered 3 fresh pizzas, 2 vegan chicken bakes, 2 portions of southern fried wedges. Delicious and nothing to moan about. Would recommend but removal of homeless people would be nice.
Always have good vegan options here and the food is generally warm. The prices are reasonable as well. The customer service is always positive and friendly here. Recommend for a nice easy lunch!

We were looking for breakfast at a French cafe in Moseley Village after a long walk. Sadly the kitchen was closed so we popped into a nearby Greggs. The atmosphere is bright spacious and welcoming and the service good. The price of five items was just over £6 which was a pleasant surprise. My vegan sausage bap was excellent and the sausages herby. There are desk tables with dockets and varied seating and good lighting if you are working. I was surprised how much I liked this branch of Greggs as it’s not my first choice for breakfast when I’m in the go.

Greggs in Moseley Village has become my go to spot for quick, delicious meals.

From their iconic Sausage Rolls & vegan alternatives and fresh salads, this Greggs nails it every time. I’m obsessed with their pizza slices especially the post-4pm deal where you can pair one with a drink for just £2.85! Their breakfast combos (available until 11am) are perfect for busy mornings, and the Fairtrade coffee is a highlight.

The branch features sleek indoor seating, making it a great place to sit and enjoy your meal. The bright, clean space feels inviting, whether you’re grabbing a coffee or sharing a meal with friends.
The team here is always cheerful & helpful. Manager Scottie Hughes & crew make you feel welcome, whether you’re popping in for a quick takeaway or trying their Click + Collect service. Their energy adds to the positive vibe of the shop.
Whether you’re craving a classic bake, a healthy salad, or a sweet treat, this Greggs delivers quality with a smile. It’s become a staple in Moseley Village, blending convenience with a touch of modern charm. Don’t miss out—give it a try!

I would like to say that the following colleagues, Saaid, Danial, and Andie, are absolutely brilliant. They are always so pleasant, friendly, and welcoming. Every time I have interacted ywith them, they had genuine smiles on their faces and go out of their way to be helpful, making the service great and very efficient. Their positive attitude and willingness to assist really stood out and made us feel valued as a customer.

Thank you to the Greggs Moseley Branch, Birmingham for such outstanding service and always putting a smile on my face
This truly has to be the best beach ever.

I walked in, waited in the queue, and noticed a member of staff behind the counter, wearing no uniform with no head covering or hairnet, preparing their own drinks while the display was completely empty and the ovens were empty. When I asked for a cheese pasty, there was none. The waiting time for a fresh pasty was 25 minutes said by the woman behind the counter. This Greggs is a joke!

Ive used this greggs a few times on my way to work,but the last time there was a random man walking arpund touching everyrhing, then pulled back one of the glass plates in front of the cakes by the till and stsrted rumaging around underneath with his dirty outdoor coat rubbing over all the food. The time before that some random lady was in there with her pet dog, wasnt a guide dog either. Dont think il be returning.

At Greggs in St Andrews Shopping Park, Coventry Road, I had an exceptional experience. The staff demonstrated friendliness, promptness & efficiency, even during peak hours. The food was fresh & delectable, particularly the sausage rolls and chicken bake, which were served hot and crispy. The coffee was also of exceptional quality. The Greggs was clean and well-maintained , providing ample seating for customers to enjoy their meals. Its convenient location and easy parking further enhance its appeal.
It stands as one of the superior Greggs branches in the vicinity.

This was my first visit to the Greggs in Small Heath, and I only stopped in for a coffee but I have to say, I was impressed. The staff were friendly and quick with service, even though there was a steady stream of customers. My coffee came out hot, smooth, and just the right strength. For the price, it’s excellent value compared to a lot of other places.

The shop itself was clean and organised, and the queue moved faster than I expected. Even though I didn’t try the food this time, everything on display looked fresh and well stocked.

As a first impression, it was a really positive one. Sometimes you just want a straightforward, no-fuss coffee that tastes good, and that’s exactly what I got here. I’ll definitely be popping back in to try some of the pastries next time.
The shop is very good, but it's noisy.

Good service in general but more expensive than other Greggs located on high streets despite it is not, Toilet is always unavailable for customers under silly and false reasons which is very disappointing and stupid, items are not always fresh or tasty as other Greggs.🤢🥴
Could be much better, cleaner and cheaper.
I wanna update my review about this rubbish branch is the difference in portions and meal sizes specially Widges in comparison to other Greggs while they have same prices!!🤯🤬

Came in today at 10:30am place was an absolute dump. I couldn’t purchase anything from there from seeing the hygiene levels.

There was a water leak with no signage next to it.

This place hasn’t been open for long and it’s already giving the Greggs brand a bad name.

It’s an outlet, so the prices are really affordable. We got a couple of things for lunch: chipotle chicken flatbread (spicy chicken, chipotle chilli sauce, fresh salad leaves, mixed peppers and spicy mayonnaise in a soft folded flatbread) and cheese and caramelised onion toastie (caramelised onion chutney, sliced mature Cheddar cheese and red onion sandwiched between white bread made with sourdough, topped with béchamel sauce and grated mixed cheese). Both of them were pretty good, specially for the price. It’s a great option if you don’t have much time and don’t want to spend too much money.

Great staff very helpful and patient. Just obviously always busy in there hence 4 stars for atmosphere and 4 stars for food as there is a wide selection of choice but it changes and you cannot guarantee what you want will be there.
The south asia staff is rude, impolite and Uncouth. They mixed up the orders but instead blame their customers.
Great prices they need of these stores around West Midlands. Sausage rolls where nice and honestly sprinkle doughnuts are 10/10

Very good food and very good customer service. The only things I don't like is you have to pay extra for eating in and the fact the shop is so small it makes it hard to do certain things such as put the items you bought ( especially if there's more than one ) and managing change while doing all that is a mission. All they need to do is add a table of some sort next to the counter to make it easier.

Fantastic food the cheese and onion slice was filled with hot gooey cheese absolutely beautiful!!!
My grandson made a mess with the crumbs of the sausage roll and MAX the shift manager came and cleared the floor and table for us was so polite and friendly - great customer service is not easy to find any more and MAX deserves to be told how amazing she is
Thank you from the 4 of us 🙂🙂🙂
Served by a lovely lady named max, very polite helpful and happy to talk to customer's. Great customer service max thank you
Ordered two cheese and ham toasties and they arrived not toasted…

Scrum diddily umptuous. Want a coffee and a treat. This is the place that can't be beat. It's one of a kind. I want to eat here all the time.

Mocha Coffee, Hazelnut Hot Chocolate, and All pastries
author_name: If You Can Be Anything Bee A Good Person

review_text: Didn't check to allow me to scan my app, low/out on nearly all bakery and counter food items, bins overflowing and no free tables inside, along with a dirty floor.

All I tasted was tomato sauce in the Chicken fajita pasty; it was not pleasent nor I also didn’t taste any chicken inside of it, had puke when I bitten through…

I’ll advise that pizzas are needed to be cooked more longer to avoid cheese being undercooked as well as the pizza has being too saucy or not cooked throughly evenly at all.

I have seemed to received 4 Chicken Goujans instead of 5 from the original selection but overall the chicken was scrumptious, edible, and enjoyable to eat, very cooked through just how I like my chicken meat to be.

Sausage rolls are indeed yes ghastly, filled also overly with some kind of mystery processed meat, needs to be replaced with a luxury sausage then a regular depressed processing meat. I didn’t find it appetising to finish it all off at all.

For the potato wedges, needs to also be cooked more, just slightly so it’s not too soft or mushy. Overall it was quite edible to consume.

However, I understand the excuses for cutting costs for the tuna baguette has been a huge drastic downgrade, it’s upsetting, if you’re unable to keep the original recipes, it’s either not downgrade or remove the item at all, this is putting a huge loss either way. The sliced onions which is now considered are very insulting, especially for its original price too should be decreased.

I thinks it’s time for Greggs to improve more with the timers, and less on the laziness with ticking boxes. Timers should be adjusted 4-5 minutes accordingly for heat and consistency.

This should be taken seriously if you want to service highly with customer satisfaction. Your team needs to be adjusted strictly with up-to-date.
Good service but the lack of room inside let's it down. For the size why have seating. If a queue forms you feel like intruding on people's conversations as the space is tight.
Can't pick a simple order, ordered 2 baguettes and only received 1, then I get a poxy £1.94 refund, another nice touch, I get sent a full fat coke but I'd ordered a zero.

Went in for a bacon and cheese wrap.  Its only a breakfast item so they said although I can get them all day from other outlets.  No pizza, really low on bakes.  I ended up walking out of they cant supply things id expect from a greggs.
When i want here today (Sunday) at 1.29pm the member of staff who served me slammed the recipe on the glass and was just moody. Can any moody staff not take it out on customers.

Had a takeaway today from Greggs for the first time and received excellent service from the manager Jawad. He recommended me the sandwich and mentioned that the margherita pizza is good as I don’t eat pork and won’t be able to get the pepperoni pizza. Will definitely visit again!

Their cookies are so different than all the other greggs i shop at, for some reason the cookies here are always either burnt or they're hard as rock, i usually only buy cookies from them and this is always the case.
Food was very fresh and staff was friendly but there is no place to sit and eat. Enjoyed my experience

Nice place.
Staff are friendly.
Food tastes great.

Fantastic service very polite guys but....
My chicken pie was stone cold!...
I did use to warm food from Greg's but this one .... unacceptable!
The staff at this Greggs store have very good customer service skills. I was served by Dylan today who is very polite. Well done to the managment team.

07/03/25

Queuing out the door most morning after a 10min wait this morning gave up and went elsewhere,

Service has declined in my opinion due to online apps  ?

Great staff at this store  👍

But unfortunately online targets matter more to the Greggs company than face to face customers queuing out the door  ? made worse when short staffed ?

So when you have 10 customers queuing out the doorway and a just eat order ?  the online order takes priority ?

And when short staffed you’re just expected to wait ? shame the manager doesn’t have the authority to switch the app off when short staffed and concentrate on the customer in the shop  ?

In summary great staff 👍 shame the company expect so much from their staff  ?

Ordered bacon sausage baguette ketchup cold no ketchup .Bacon roll no sauce stone cold .3 hadh browns stine cold and were not fresh at all.2 pan devcjocolate gready and swuadhed to a pupl both if them.Absolutly disgusting will never use them again.
Pastries can be hit an miss, sometimes burnt sometimes undercooked slightly, also doesnt have a cold drinks machine which is advertised on the bus stop opposite, staff are nice though.

As per usual, Gregg's staff meet the expectations that the name demands.  Plenty of choice at 2pm in the afternoon. The cake I had was nice and fresh and the soft drink lovely and chilled. Some parking outside, and also locally.

Not left a negative review before but felt this was needed. Awful staff, rude, terrible people skills. Bare in mind foreign nationals coming into Birmingham will use this Greggs and could well be there first Greggs food they have eaten and sure it would be there last.
Talking to other staff about what there doing later while taking my order.
Food was stone stone cold as in it's been sat there for hours, maybe cook smaller batches to give people hot food guys just a thought.
Will continue to use Greggs as usual but will never use this Greggs again that's for sure before or after a flight.

Food was like ICE! went to get my first english greggs expecting great things and was madly disappointed by the ice cold steak bakes I got. Staff also was rude as flip as when I went to order she said “hello what would you like?” then halfway through me speaking she turned to her coworker and asked her if she had ever tried a fajita before and made me repeat myself. Wouldn’t advise you to go.
They need to watch the temperature for hot food. It's danger zone. I bought pizza before, also steak bake and today as well always cold.

Very bad experience. A white old lady was there and she was behaving so rude. Chill, lady. We are paying, you are not serving for free. Literally, age is just a number — some people never learn manners in their entire life.

Food was cold. Asked if any of the options were still hot. The pizza. It wasn’t.

Find a way to keep the food hot!

Many times i have had to wait 5-10 minutes for someone to come from the back and serve me, seen many people walk off due to the of the lack of service. The older gentleman who looks like the manager cant remember to put sauce on anything, whether you have paid or not or what you asked for in the first place.

Got told do not normally get customers around this time so food was all cold?

How is this possible? Really?

the manage was so rude he gave me cold food and cold code and when i said this is cold coffee and he said me i set temperature i have only this one i can not do anything for you he is very disappointed me and disrespectful . now i never use this site thank you
No welcome no smile and attitude is top of the range. Asked him food is cold replied yea no sorry or anything. Sorry won’t go again..

I won't give too many stars because it opens at 6:30 am and there's practically nothing to eat, no sandwiches, bakes are often almost cold, one of the employees is very slow at his job. The only thing I can praise is that the service is nice but what's the point if there's not much to eat
Popped in on the way to the park, both of the ladies were really lovely and smiley. Very rare these days! A credit to the branch x
Shaz on Greggs is the worst person who works in there full of attitude and very argumentative and very arrogant

Absolutely lovely staff! Very friendly, and get to know you if you're a frequent visitor. Breakfast items are often well cooked. Staff are always replenishing pasties and pizzas etc.. but for some reason when people ask if they are warm, they never are. They are either pipping hot and fresh from the oven or stone cold. Good sandwich selection.

Popped in here for a morning coffee. I was here just before 9am. A short queue, but that's to be expected given it's location. A small bit of parking outside or parking is available on the road. Coffee was nice and strong, really hot. Just what I needed.
Excellent ,we always recieve top service food is top-drawer and the bacon baps well stocked my daughter has two meals and drinks every week
Popped in for a bite today, food was great and customer service even better. The lovely girl pink hair is an absolute asset to your company. Polite, helpful and pleasant!
Thank you Shannon for preparing my lunch with love and adding a lovely note, a complete asset to the Greg’s team 🥰❤️

All fine, had to ask for napkins for my baguette to prevent a mess when eating as the supply was out, however the store was busy and the lady appeared to be alone.
Baguette and corned beef pasty were nice.

Went in for breakfast, good food but really unprofessional woman with red hair behind the counter. She was shouting and harassing the employee on the counter in front of the customers. She told him he was better off quiting. If i was the lad I wouldn't have handled it as well as he did. Straight up rudeness.
Good as always, no need to say more.

The usual no-nonsense short wait with value for money food.
Best of all is the bargains! Tuna baguette 1.25£ and doughnuts a £ for a pack of four.
Not sure if there's any hot food?
Satnav may take you to the back.
Lovely little shop bess prices. Very welcomeing and great service

Absolutely incredible. 4 sausage rolls for 1.55 which is absurdly good value. Despite being clearance they're still really nice. I heavily recommend putting them in the oven for 10 minutes to bring them back to life, it's almost like you went to greggs and got a fresh one
Best place for a good humble bargain food cakes , sausage roll pastry or sandwich 👌 place for greggs chef kiss
Amazingly Cheap and Friendly Service Even Near Closing Time

Keep it up Greggs, once again you have delivered good quality food and service. Clean and well maintained facilities are always available. There was a queue of people when I entered but it wasn’t long before I was served. I approve👍🏾

Always had great service and food, HOWEVER, the cleanliness of the toilets is an issue. Every time we have been, the toilet and floor has been visibly dirty and the bin full and sometimes overflowing. it is not that difficult to keep on top of basic maintenance and sanitation for customers.

Extremely dirty shop, seating, tables, floor and toilets absolutely filthy. Hot food in glass cabinets was cold and they refused to heat it up. Last few cream cakes, or the cakes that were broken were only offered to elderly white folk as complimentary (although they weren’t interested and declined)  not a very pleasant place at all

Visited with my Mum today. Received excellent customer service from Vish. Welcoming, friendly and professional. Thank you Vish.

A special thank you to Phil who is always so professional, polite and accommodating. Thank you Phil.

Average queue time of 15 minutes. 1 staff member working the till and 1 doing table service???
Rarely any pizza slices, the only thing that's kept stocked are the burnt sausage rolls

My favourite Greggs to visit. The staff are super friendly, one sometimes singing away to a tune or two at the back and puts a smile on my face. Always a great vibe when I go in 👌🏻
As for stock I’d say once every two weeks or so I walk out with something missing off my list which I find is pretty good considering the amount of customers they have.
I like this Greggs store, always very friendly staff, and the food is never bad.

Good customer service, the usual Greggs selection available, clean tables and seating.

Parking is on street and free for an hour but can be difficult to find during office hours.
Cannot give more than one star for atmosphere because we took food away.

Really busy branch of Greggs, lots of deliveries and queues are often out of the door.
Staff do the best they can, considering how busy they get.
Food is at expected standards.

I have been using this Greggs for over 10 years, I have seen staff come and go, they have never fell short of exceptional service! This location is extremely busy and they deal with a huge amount of diversity and pressure daily. I have nothing but respect and gratitude for the team! Always quick service, great availability & management of orders! Thank you to each individual team member 💙

Standard Greggs experience. Only let down is you are 90% guaranteed to be approached by someone asking for money outside, and as someone who has been homeless himself, I wish those poor folk who do ask would understand that, just like I realised, simply being there is enough - people can see you and they will help you if they either can or want to...asking just makes it harder.
Anyway, personal opinion aside, standard Gregg's experience - no notes.
Also: help a homeless person if you see one, please!

I ordered the pizza box off of uber eats and I am honestly disappointed. Pizzas were dark, cold and burnt. They think it’s acceptable to send food off like this in a delivery. Will not be ordering from here ever again.

I ordered food to eat in which went without a hitch. I asked to have some tissue and if I could use the cleaner to wipe clean a table to eat at. I was told it was against H&S to clean my table on my own but she didn't clean it herself. In the end I located cleaner cloth and spray in the waste bin cupboard and cleaned the table myself despite it being a risk to my H& S. I was more likely to be at risk of germs off the table as I would have been with cleaning spray.Go figure. H&S gone mad by people with no idea what it means.
Please have courtesy. Please learn manners. It doesn't take a lot to say 'hello' irrespective of your customers skin color and race.

The staff was friendly and helpful food was ok but seriously sort your toilet out the germs in there must be spreading to the food area the sink and toilet looks like it's not been cleaned since it was fitted  mess on the floor and water as you walk in the door ....never know a toilet to have two sinks in it

My experience at the Gregg's next to WBA stadium was fantastic service was the best one person in particular ALFIE always smiles very respectful customer service is the 10/10 very well spoken listens and even when the order could be a bit complicated he helps never gets annoyed very patient how it should be very happy with the visit I come regularly and will do thanks Alfie and Greggs for a lovely welcoming experience keep it up. 5*

This Greggs is a refreshing change from the typical setup. Housed in a big, white, old fashioned townhouse, it stands out as a unique establishment. Detached from other buildings, it has its own large car park, making it convenient for those driving in.

Inside, you'll find tables and chairs, providing a comfortable space to sit and enjoy your meal. Unlike many other branches, this Greggs also features a decent sized toilet room, adding to the convenience and overall positive experience.

As expected, Greggs serves its usual array of delicious baked goods, sandwiches, and beverages. The quality is consistent with what you would anticipate from this beloved chain. The service is commendable, with staff being friendly and efficient.

Overall, this Greggs offers a unique and pleasant experience, combining the charm of its distinctive building with the reliable quality of food and service the chain is known for.

Plenty of parking and quick service.
My coffee was good, but the chicken bake wasn't as good as I expected. The bake was a little doughy, suggesting it wasn't cooked properly. No problem with the filling, though.
Good business hours here.

I've had to do this review again as been taken off 2024 had a football competition near wba football ground. I was the coach driver for leaf school Bournemouth who came first. it was a long drive so when I'd parked up went to greggs opposite wba stadium. had 2 chicken bakes 2 pizza and a very big coffee. was hot food which surprised me
Great people working in this new Greggs

Great cheap prices.Being an outlet store, all items are half price or less compared to normal Greggs stores. Also, customer service by all staff members is amazing. Very friendly.

There is also free parking which is very good.

The best shop in Lozells, has a small range of sandwiches baguettes cakes pasties and few more.
Great cheap prices. (Being an outlet all items are half price compared to normal Greggs stores)
Lastly outstanding customer service by all staff members.

I am so glad that we have a Greggs outlet in lozells and the staff are so friendly and so helpful. I go there now every day for lunch because the price is just right thanks to all the staff. I recommend everyone should go there for lunch because the price is very good.

A beautiful shop between villa Road and heath field Road. Parking spaces are available in front of shop. Staff is very helpful and friendly. Prices are very reasonable. Just don't be late as they run out fast.

I was really disappointed with my visit to this Greggs. The service was slow, and the customer experience wasn’t great. Hannah was the one serving me, but unfortunately the process took much longer than it should have and felt disorganised. Definitely not the standard I expect from Greggs.

Went to the Greggs in Primark / City Centre. Been here before and they’re ok. They have 2 prices / for sit in and takeaway yet you’re not guarantees a seat at bust times.
Once we paid for our food- we had to wait a bit and even then most of the tables had crumbs and rubbish left by the previous users .
We went for vegetarian options as they don’t have any halal options for the chicken or meat. But their margherita pizza and cheese onion bake are always spot on and the wedges fine.

Looking for value and taste Gregg’s has it.
Sausage roll, a Belgian bun and coffee were great!
They actually have great meal deals … I’m a “tourist” so paying equivalent to $10 aud for a coffee seems a bit expensive… but I was pleasantly surprised when I had a Gregg’s coffee, only wish I found them sooner!

I had a disappointing experience at Gregg’s. The staff made my order wrong, and the price didn’t seem right, so I asked about it. They insisted the price was correct, so I ended up paying anyway. On top of that, the hot chocolate tasted really bad. Overall, the service and quality were not good.

The most slowest service I have ever seen in my life this morning,

One lady was just walking around like she didn’t know what to do and the queue was long and people in the morning are rushing to work and she comes on the till with the most bothered face ever starts talking with her colleagues and then while serving stops and looks at the other till and what the other customer is saying instead of serving her own.

This was utterly unprofessional behaviour.
First Greggs I've been to that hasn't been up to scratch. Staff hardly took any time to serve you (everything seemed rushed). Food was also cold. Can't recommend 😐

I ordered a pepperoni pizza and it was cold. They wanted to sell me a cold one. Greggs has been one of the best, but now they've disappointed me. I think the person who served me didn't want to help me.

Fast service and hot pasties. Brilliant!
Festive bakes were well stocked

Ideal location if working in or around snow hill / cathedral area of the city. Great for breakfast if working early in the office. Shelves stocked first thing in morning. Disappointed to see no omelette rolls this morning.  Otherwise nice clean location. Hand sanitiser by the entrance. Not allowed to sit indoors for the time being. Prices reasonable and the tea was delicious. Don't forget the gift card for that special person at Christmas / Birthday 😉

Decided to get something from this Greggs which is located in a good spot. Wanted to try something different so went with a Vegan mexican chicken-free bake. The one on display was barely warm so asked for a fresh one which took approximately 15 minutes. A long wait l know but it was nice. Normally l feel nothing but chicken will be chicken and although chicken might be better and as a self confessed carnivore l have to concede that this was lovely. The texture and the filling was extremely nice. The kick of moderate spice was just on the button. I would have it again. Also again a short review has ended up......not so short. Nice branch and helpful staff. Would recommend to all patrons of Greggs.

This is now the third time experiencing this sort of service at this particular Greggs and its when the tall, large , red headed male supervisor seems to be on - he is far more interested in chitchatting to team member than working, serving , replenishing the food or keeping that place flowing in any way. Today he took it one step further by trying to sweep myself and my college out the shop whilst waiting for our drink before resuming to talk about his mom with Brianna. All the while we waited for our drinks for ten minutes - only for the person who served us to look at us with a fat grin on his face - almost laughing at the situation and says "oh sorry i forgot" All we order were drinks!!! how could you forget!!! because sorry we interrupted you mother meeting! -  There was a que the whole time we were there varying from 2-5 people at a time and nobody received good customer service.

This visit feat - importance of laughing and joking at lunch time rush.
no sense of urgency or customer service
no leadership - in fact lazy leadership - leading the team astray!
Brianna shouting -"why you keeping your mom from us"
Being forgot about and literally swept out the shop
