Whenever I come across great services I have to leave a review: went to the drive through to grab a quick bite today and the young lad servicing us had a great attitude, he efficiently completed our order and was very friendly, which really brightened our day. Sometimes it isn't easy to work in food servicing jobs espcially dealing with customers, people with excellent working ethics deserve to be praised and appreciated more often!
On another note, food and coffee were good as  always :)

Absolutely without a doubt the worst Greggs I used.  The staff were useless and omg the food was not cold but freezing.  I went back with my 80 year old disabled dad and had to reorder asking what was warm.  Terrible avoid at all costs.  Even the second one when I asked what was hot was cold.  Lol the sausage rolls I asked them to heat up and narda and they were subzero. Two receipts to prove it. Dump I will actually sell my shares after this experience if they have no quality control for franchise they won't last long.

Ordered online platform and just sent the wrong food. Most likely as they ran out of breakfast. Don’t have it available to sell if you don’t have it. Waste of money and not getting a refund. Will never order from here again.
A fantastic team, always a pleasure coming into this Greggs

Wouldn't except a Scottish £20 absolutel joke
It's lethale tender, the excuse to many fakes it's a rubbish excuse
No problem paying for my fuel with one
This place is a joke. Been three times now and been over charged for items that they didn’t have in stock. Slow service, no one has a clue what they are doing. Charging £4.85 for this!!!!
A brilliant store, excellent availability and wonderful staff
nice little place for quick snacks . it is inside BP petrol station

This is a great Greggs cafe.
It is very clean, there are plenty of tables and chairs and the cafe is conveniently located next to one of the entrances to the Trafford Centre shopping mall.
There is a great selection. I opted for a breakfast barm which was soft and delicious. I also got a cappuccino and a strawberry jam iced donut. Both lovely.
If you arrive after breakfast time there is a great selection of sandwiches and pastries as well as the famous Greggs sausage roll.
It is worth getting the Greggs app if you visit Greggs a lot, you can get some offers and loyalty points. It’s also useful if your Greggs is busy because you can pre-order in the app.
I was lucky to be here on a Tuesday morning though and there was no queue.
The store is next to M&S if you’re doing some shopping and there are plenty of bus and tram routes to the Trafford Centre if you’re not driving.
There are many parking spaces around the Trafford Centre too if you are driving (as well as some very busy Tesla chargers) but as I always say, the Trafford Centre does get very very busy at weekends.
I would definitely recommend this super Greggs Cafe.
Thanks for reading

Shocking service!!! Staff are too busy talking and not giving out correct orders. Asked for a vegan sausage roll and they gave us a normal sausage roll, which I don’t eat for religious reasons. Pathetic excuse for staff, as they were not apologetic at all.
served by a literal angel, staff go above and beyond ❤️ best greggs i’ve been to!!!! will edit the review if i manage to catch her name, treasure these staff!!!
Food is always good and fresh at this branch, assuming because it’s so busy. Not the friendliest of staff but not rude either. Could probably work on their customer service.

We went to Trafford Centre today which is Saturday. I feel this is an amazing place for every age. After a bit of shopping we decided to do some snaking. So, we went to Gregg's. I had cheese pizza slice and wife had Vegan Steak Pasty with coffee. I managed to take some quick pics. This Gregg's is close to Debenhams.
Efficient and friendly staff, good stock of the range of products Greggs do.

This branch of Greggs offers nothing outstanding compared to other locations; you get what you expect. However, the staff here really make the difference. They were friendly and efficient during my visit, which left me with no complaints.

Some staff rude and dismissive. Don't seem to be aware of click and collect, claim that they "never send texts" to advise that your order is ready for collection. The texts eventually arrived two hours after the food was collected. A disappointing experience. The food was good though.

Good Greggs, plenty of space in the shop and the usual. Breakfast / pastry / desert selection as well as sandwiches and hot food. Good selection of cold drinks and all the Gregg's favourite hot drinks too. Remember to download the app and scan so you can collect stamps and get some free scran when you have collected / purchased 10 items under any one of the categories! ..........
.

I asked for an extra BBQ sauce with my chicken dippers as 1 isn’t enough in my view, the manager completely refused even though I offered to pay, she was very rude and the staff looked fear to deaf of her, and on leaving the shop she was shouting sarcastically by, she doesn’t know the 1st thing on customer service or management in that case,
I go in Conle Greens every day and the sauce is on the side for every to take, I went to Stockport Greggs today and they were free also
Friendly staff and the most important they have toilet, something that at usual in a Greggs is really strange to see.

Good food, staff mainly friendly. Great selection of sandwiches, hot snacks and sweet treats. Breakfast rolls are good and decent coffee. The sandwich and drink deal is good value. Ive tried the hot snacks like the chicken dippers and wedges and they're a bit hit and miss, tasty but sometimes they've been sat in the heater too long and have dried out a lot but other times they've been good. We place large orders for work and sometimes the staff seem annoyed about it but other times they're great, I guess it depends which staff member you get.

Lovely Greggs but there was a big queue only 1 serving whilst 2 other staff of those 2 staff 1 was replenishing but the other decided to empty a bin and he did notice the queue but still took rubbish out and yes I know it can be at times non stop serving but he didn't muck in so service for me had to knock stars off and I go out of my way to visit this Greggs hopefully he learns to be a team member
Worst Greggs I’ve ever been! Service was terrible stupidly slow. 20 people waiting to be served and only one person serving

Opens earlier now which is good. The usual female server is excellent who gets your order right everytime. However there's a young male who has served me twice and got the order wrong. Both times he's had to give me the order he messed up and the one i wanted. He must be costing Greggs a fortune!!

Yesterday my other half brought me home 2 sausage rolls, took one bite and tasted undercooked, the pastry on the bottom was almost raw and soggy. Binned them. Thought it was a one off so tried again today....exactly the same.....in the bin!!!
Great mince pies

Very disappointing in this Greg's. Every time I have a valid voucher on the Greg's app their app is mysteriously down.Even when I've seen other people scan their apps while I've been queueing up. This isn't just isolated to me having discussed this in my workplace I have had several colleagues said that this tends to happen to them too. Have made several complaints to Greg's themselves. Even though they are a franchise they are still obliged to follow the Greg's products and terms. I don't know whether this is just a management thing in that particular place or whether it's down to the station. However I do feel that people need to be aware of this especially if they've had the same experiences. They shouldn't be able to get away with this.

Is it me or are Greggs just not very good at hot chocolate.?
I purchased a large hot chocolate, expecting the ‘works’ cream and sprinkles. No, just flat not very chocolatey water. Do I have to ask for extras? Oh and a cold croissant too. Disappointed.
As a hgv driver I seldom get a chance to park for a Greggs , in the few times I’ve been here I always get a nice welcome and my breakfast banquette is always lovely. Well done

I love the bacon and cheese wrap, but it’s incredibly frustrating how often they’re out of stock. I’m there before 7:45 almost every morning, and 90% of the time, they’ve already run out. It feels like a missed opportunity, especially for a quick breakfast stop.

I’ve also noticed that the prices have gone up recently, which makes Greggs less competitive, especially when affordability used to be one of their strongest points.

Lastly, it’s really annoying that this branch isn’t on the app, meaning I can’t pre-order for pickup, which would make my mornings much easier. I hope these issues get sorted because I do love the food, but the experience has become a bit disappointing.

Over priced. Nasty, slow workers with no manners. Over 10 minutes wait for a bacon sandwich. Que every single morning. No wonder this isn't registered on the Greggs website! Subway next door is way better BIG AVOID

Stone cold food and rubbish customer service from the staff, asked for a bag before paying. Picked all the pasties up to put in the bag and the lady serving decided to place the folded flat bag on the counter and watch me put the 5 items in my hands down open the paper bag and pick the 5 items up again to put in the bag, no common sense at this place, no mention of the food being cold and it’s about 25% more expensive than other Greggs stores
Had a really poor service yesterday, I ordered one item and they got it wrong. No apology and barely any communication at all, nothing like my local greggs were the staff are polite and friendly

Pasties got 4 all cold they didn't say 😟
I know it's a franchise but come on its a greggs  shocking price I have been to other franchise what do food but don't see it inflated in price like this place does. And it was cold also.
I won't be returning

Bought a steak bake which was freezing, prices were extortionate and staff barley speak English. Lights were stupidly bright as well, I would rather sit on the wall outside near the busy road if I had to wait for a car to charge.
Fantastic to find upto 16 EV charging points all in one place. Brilliant. Don't need to drive around Manchester looking for a charging point and when you get there it's being used. Need more.

Always so helpful patient and happy, great food and prices but made all the better by being greeted with a smile
Thanks Steven and naz x
Woman wiping nose with her hand and serving customers failing to wash hands.  To be fair she looked like a scruff too.  Ill go elsewhere
Very professional staff,always happy to attend and deal customers no matter how busy it gets, food served very fresh and claean packing.Love it
Typical Greg's good food even better when you get it straight out the oven sausage rolls the best food in there followed by a steak bake yum 😋
A good neighborhood choice. Prices are fair and the food is reliable. The steak was flavorful, though a bit too rare for my liking. Still enjoyable.

Incredibly enjoyable experience. The vegan sausage rolls have a delightful taste. The staff are very kind and polite and the food is just fantastic. Greggs is one of my favourite bakery and they definitely know how to make Vegan sausage rolls. In conclusion if you happen to be visiting the Manchester museum you should definitely nip into greggs

I ordered a ham and cheese baguette and the baguette has arrived with grey meat ? It looks absolutely disgusting and completely different from the picture. Called the store and they said “it looked alright when it got sent out” how about you tell me ?

Seems ok. But check for flies in the packaging. My pack of 2 doughnuts had flies trapped inside and didn't notice until hours later so god knows what germs are now all over them (if it was noticed straight away I wouodnt mind but it was a good 4 hours of flies hoping all over my food).. And this was at night so greggs long closed now
Very good food and easy to get especially for uni students moving from classes. I always go here.

Cold bacon and sausage on breakfast bap and then to add that poor quality cold hash browns!
If I wasn't busy I would have returned the food, but glad I didn't.. customer always wrong.
Disappointing. Wasn't busy and looked well staffed..

Accessible with parking outside. Really clean, small seating area. Well stocked and lots of choice. It's always really busy with locals, passers by and workmen but the staff manage it brilliantly and efficiently so you dont wait long. Staff so helpful and friendly.
Staff are amazing! I did try the their new soup sorry very poor like water , i swapped it for a tomato soup which is fantastic & filling ! The other products amazing as per usual!

I ordered  20 sandwiches and coffee in the morning around 20 minutes before my arrival, when i arrived to the shop - Everything was ready! Fantastic service, great atmosphere and the staff are welcoming and chatty! My go to Greggs from now on 👍

I just want to say that team here is super nice , kind and helpful. They welcoming you with big smile and politeness. Food is fresh and hot. Cleaning is spotless. One of my favourite greggs in Manchester
The young lad who served me was super polite and super friendly, very impressed by this young lad. He needs a pay rise. So rare to find someone so polite

Just another Greggs outlet, I thought, until the Canadian guy behind the counter started serving and making conversation. One of the nicest, humane and pleasant natures humans that I've had the pleasure to speak to.

If only the "ideal" of customer service was given to him, and people like him, to define. We'd all be a bit more pleasant, content and hospitable.

Thank you
Good to have on a hospital site, whilst waiting
The woman behind the till was so lovely!
Nice place friendly staff bit expensive but most places are in hospitals
Breakfast was decent

Came in last Thursday around midday. Staff really rude. Did not acknowledge me, ignored the question I asked and then gave me the food without saying a word or making eye contact with me. Probably worth observing your employees with customers to check they actually know what customer service is. I was gobsmacked.
The staff have always been very helpful and pleasant. A special shout out to Eillis( sorry if I have spelt it  wrong) was very nice and helpful ... the biscuit was yummy thanks.

Once again we are missing items from our order. How hard is it to get 1 employee to check what's on the receipt and make sure its all in the bag. Its an absolute joke. The manager thats on now at this time, 2pm on the 22nd,  get a grip of your staff they're making you look like a wolly!
Food is good at reasonable prices. At times there are long queues. Staff are less to serve.

I WAS NOT SERVED.

I waited in line for 15 minutes but was not served. There were two queues, and I was in the second one. When it was finally my turn, the staff member serving our queue went to the back to prepare orders, and it seemed like the queue was effectively closed. At that point, the staff member from the first queue should have served me, but he ignored me entirely.

Fantastic customer service from Jake, lovely welcome into store,  very helpful and knowledgeable about the hot food, made some tasty sounding suggestions for my next visit. Thank you for the fast and exceptional service.
Lovely location with good tasty food. They even offer vegan options now. Nice and friendly staff with a cozy eating area.
Always great coffee from here which is needed on these cold winter days.
Nice staff and a decent donut

This morning I had the pleasure of being served by Jack [ Stockport Road in Manchester].  He showed outstanding kind manners and he was friendly and polite above my expectations. It was my first time in Greggs and I have really appreciated that Jack spent a considerable amount of time to go through the best options on the menu available.
I have particularly appreciated his politeness and the way he communicates with his customers. Thank you Jack for your great work!
I am honestly so impressed.

A very popular Greg's serving the local community well.  The guy in front made a special request for his breakfast barm, which she gladly obliged as the queues had not started to build this early hour, although there was regular traffic managed by a dedicated team of shop front and behind the scenes staff. Cheers guys.
Great service, food as expected nice hot and fresh,  always a good offer on!

The coffee machine has been broken for a week. The warm food is rarely ever actually warm. You’re not informed when choosing pastries that they’re cold, and the staff can’t seem to tell the difference between a sausage & bean pasty and a chicken one. The staff are also quite miserable.
They never have any food that’s hot in the evening and the staff are really rude , all tables always dirty but they want to charge you extra to sit in other people’s filth
Lovely clean store food always hit staff very helpful
A small one , but still great place for an early breakfast or lunch 👍🏻

I get it's a small shop but several times I've been in at 10am to be told no breakfasts today or no baps,sausage etc.Also very rare their isn't a staff member on their break during the busiest period. When it's available the food is great.

"Needed a Greggs pizza" called in today before 9am and was greeted by Sam who was very welcoming and warming, they don't usually make the pizza's until 11am but Sam went above and beyond and made me one early and was diligent towards my customer needs. I've been to Greggs all over the country and staff can just fob you off because they CBA with the individual needs and customers request, and can be miserable but this Greggs the staff are welcoming and warming whom look after the customers and their needs. Big thank you to Sam whom is a real asset to Greggs and a great customer services rep, and thank you to Caroline whom is equally as caring and another true asset to the company stating "We will alway look after our customers".

Thank you "Defo the best Greggs in the UK"
Leading British brand renowned for its breakfast offerings.
Clean and quiet with a well presented large seating area,  Staff are very pleasant. A hidden gem away from all the work men.

I was here on Tuesday 2nd Dec around 145/2pm. 2 ladies worked their socks off! A constant queue of 5/6 people all served curtiously and no sign of complaint despite being under a lot of pressure. They are a credit to Greggs and worth their weight in gold!
I saw staff refusing a disabled person to use their toilet. This was on Monday at 14:00hrs. They clearly had disabled toilet but these young boys (staff) refused. Greggs need to look into this.

As everybody knows, Greg's comes with a standard quality of products. The service given, however, differs store to store. The gentlemen at the store could not be more helpful, and gave suggestions. Even breaking into actual conversations with guests.

Highly recommend the Mexican chicken baguette.
Ordered off Just Eat.  Bag was sealed, so I know it wasn't the driver, but food has arrived with clearly a bite taken out of it.   Disgusting!

What can you say about Greggs that people dont already know.
The upgrade from the old place is a big difference, always clean and well stocked with fresh food
Ordered a bacon and sausage roll. It was so tasty, that I let my mum take a bite. I also brought a sugar strand donut and a capri sun.

Everytime I've gone in for Breakfast there are no Hash Browns, I normally go 10am ish but nothing on hot plate at all, Breakfast doesn't finish till 11am so they should be available, They offered to put some in but takes 20 minutes, I like to have my Breakfast Roll with my Hash Browns not 20 minutes apart, don't understand it, staff are nice and Foods nice but its happend a few times now.
I don’t know why I bother. 10 mins still waiting in queue. I think Greggs are more interested in online orders rather than footfall

Ewwwww didn’t even eat here walked out as did 5 other customers waiting in the queue. Waiting to be served there’s a queue of 9 people. All 3 worked arguing about who’s making this specifc brew. next minute some worker (big woman) comes out cradling 8 baguettes unpacked against her top, the baguettes touched her top arms and face couldn’t believe what I was seeing so walked out. Not a chance am I going to eat somewhere people don’t know basic hygiene absolutely disgusting why would you walk through holding unpackaged baguettes against your chest god knows if she had showered but rather not risk that
Ive visited here many times on my way to the hospital and I'm always greated by Debbie,lovely big smile,very happy and also chatty..Such a lovely lady.shop also well stocked and clean.

Called in this morning. I was 2nd in the queue but the queue was outside the door. I counted 5 members of staff & only 1 was serving. The others were avoiding eye contact with everyone in the queue & no one came to help. I asked for 2 chicken bakes & they were stone cold.
Also, just as a side, it was only just breakfast time, there was a full range of sweet stuff but barely any breakfast/slices/sausage rolls, maybe a couple of each. I work in a successful bakery & maybe the manager needs a little retraining.
A great store situated on the main road with customer parking. Full range of Greggs produce to eat in or take away. Conveniently located near to Salford Royal Hospital.

Customer service was brilliant a girl called ruby was so helpful and polite. She helped me choose a vegan friendly option. The shop is super clean and you can tell it’s well looked after. I would 100% go back!!!

Went today as the kids are off school and its not being used as the local youth club/doss house that don't buy anything. Greggs need to get a grip of this and ban them as they take up all the seats and leave bags everywhere. I was also appalled today by a male member of staff who slowly wanders around, doing the bare minimum and getting in colleagues way, if he went any slower, we would stop. Honestly greggs, get a grip of your shops and see what's going on. Yesterday the queue was out the door with little footfall coming out so I walked straight past and went to the coop.

I have been in here a few times for a breakfast roll and a coffee.
It’s great value and if you use the Greggs app you will get points.
Greggs is next door to the local Co-Op store so very handy for a quick bite to eat after shopping.
There is seating available inside if you’re in need of a rest while you eat too.
Greggs sell a wide range of sandwiches, cakes and snacks as well as pastries too.
There is a paying car park and barbershop nearby too.
Great value for money

Great staff! I always come here to get my free sausage roll from my priority app!

*edit* I come here every Friday or so and the staff gave Nala for Free 2 small sausages! She loved it 😍

Convenient location, although hidden away from the main areas of MediaCity, full range of Greggs usual offerings available, almost giving you too much choice. I ordered a pepperoni pizza slice and a hot chocolate, the pizza still tasted good after going cold, and the hot chocolate was perfect temperature, not too hot or too cold. Prices cannot be beaten for the quality.

I dont think theyre cleaning the milk section of their coffee machines properly. Had stomach issues both times when ordering coffee, a shame cause the coffee is cheap and decent but this recent bout of stomach issues is definitely looking like food poisoning.
Blonde guy working the till in the afternoon on 22/11/25 is awesome

Reid!! Never left a bad review for Greggs because it’s great food but the staff in particular Reid that was on the counter just seemed like he couldn’t be arsed at all seemed like he was 2 sausage rolls away from falling asleep tbh
Prefer Greggs coffees and sausage rolls to any other, every time I pass one will always pop in

The food and coffee ☕️ are not over expensive. The sit-in Greggs always play. Smoth music 🎶 in the background, giving it a carm atmosphere. Nicely to drink a coffee. And there's one in every town. If I feel like a coffee ☕️ I always use greggs if i see-estone available. As for the staff, they work hard cause it is bizy.
Food isn't the hottest... most times I've been it's been cold or not got anything I wanted pasty wise

I recently visited Greggs in Salford and tried their hot and creamy pizza slice, and I must say, it was quite a delightful experience. The slice was piping hot, with a generous portion of creamy cheese that melted in my mouth. The crust had a nice crunch to it, and the overall flavor was satisfying. While it was a tasty treat, I wouldn't rate it a full 5 stars, as I've had even more exceptional pizza slices elsewhere. Nonetheless, Greggs delivered a solid and affordable option for a quick and tasty snack.
Nice, fresh snacks all of the time. Their people are very warm, helpful  & quite friendly.

visited on my way to work, I got a sausage roll and a beef veg pasty. The sausage roll was pretty standard, but the pasty was an undercooked and cold mess. Raw pastry, and undercooked cubes of crunchy vegetables. Served ice cold without even being told. Extremely dissapointed considering how much smaller they're getting, and how much more expensive they're getting also. A real shame.
Received fast service and the staff were polite.

I didn't see any misshapen pasties but there was a load of Vegan Festive Slices!  Definitely so last season. They can't still be accidentally making them so I guess they're from frozen stocks 🤷 Anyway, they were 75p for a pack of 3 and tasted just as fresh and festive as they did the day they were put in the freezer.
There was a jovial queue going out the door, mostly made up office workers from the surrounding businesses. They seemed to be going for the baguettes and sandwiches brought in unsold the previous day from regional stores and now sold 2 for 1!
There was a real social excitement about the place, buzzing with that mystery bargain feel that we all love. I also suspect quite a few people return daily hoping to see if that intriguing someone who always gets the twin pack of Vegetable Slices is there again.
Definitely worth a visit.
Great prices, great products. Every time I've been there, I've been served by the same lady who really friendly and helpful

Greg's Outlet has good bakery products on sale at great prices. Best time to shop is in the morning, or definitely before 14:00. After that time school children "invade" and most of the goods are sold.

Atmosphere and staff very friendly. Been twice, both times spent less than £10 and came out with a lot of food. Spent £8.50 yesterday, I didn’t realise that there was a buy one get one free on sandwiches, sausage rolls and pasties. So ended up with 4 sandwiches, 8 sausage rolls and 8 pasties. (Most of which have gone in freezer)…
The downside? two times I have visited and eaten from here and the day after (now)… suffering from dysentery.. so is it really only yesterdays unsold food? That’s questionable.
“4 sausage rolls for £1 and a side of upset stomach!”

Cheap and cheerful, prefect for getting workplace treats at a reasonable price. I think the prices vary throughout the week and throughout the day. When I visited 2 packs of donuts and Yum yums were 3 packs for £1
So I gota few boxes to take into work to share out. Will definitely be visiting again
Sometimes online it says the wrong opening times but I really recommend this place as it has cheap food
Very friendly staff, and all the food is very cheap! Always enjoy coming in after shopping at Aldi!
Having an outlet for Greggs is a great idea :)
Nice place

Lovely staff esp when they didn’t have the iced finger I ordered on click and collect and said I could have anything else. I was happy enough to take the order slip to the shop down the road. I got my iced finger in the end :)

Today, on 15/12/25 Monday, I visited the store at around 4:15 p.m for some hot chocolate, but the staff member girl behaved so rude with me I felt really bad. She almost threw the hot cup on the counter top after making it and didn't say anything. It was such a rude behaviour which I didn't expect. She didn't prepare the hot chocolate properly either. It was overall a bad experience.

“
POOR SERVICE”
The asian guy was served me the wrong drinks which i ordered 2 hot chocolate’s and instead of that he gave me the coffee, that taste also not good.
It’s not fair and you guys are wasting us money
Why do you maintain these irresponsible persons to serve customers.
Such a bad morning to me I don’t recommend this

Add an 80p extra for a breakfast baguette ~ so delicious,not the same as a muffin but soft bread & buttered,the baguettes are the same as the sandwiches you can buy but totally fresh especially as they only serve these up to  11am & the sandwiches are allowed on sale for I’m guessing till sold.
Yummy! Merry Christmas🎄add a tea = £3.35.

i was first turned on to Gregg's by my good friend The Baron - I have heard all of my life about their world famous hot dogs, so when I finally made my way down to Gregory's I knew I was in for a treat.  I ordered their finest of hot dogs, and one juicy bite catapulted my into a tunnel of flavor and emotions I had never yet felt before.  I can't recommend Gregg's enough for their incredible hot dogs, I've heard from The Baron that their sausage rolls are also quite delicious but it would take a lot to drag me away from once again choosing those addicting world class hot dogs.
The branch was nice and there was only person inside, the only thing I want them to improve is to use gloves while handling food inside. I know your hands are clean but its still unhygienic.
Just out for a morning walk and passing by Greggs mean veggie Sausage roll. Busy time so slow service which is understandable. Good location especially for the commuters on their way towards station.
Ideally placed on route to Piccadilly station and bus routes,have plenty of products available,is always busy so be prepared for a wait,staff are friendly.
Steak bake or sausage roll? Good value and the staff are nice, but the service is slow. Ok for a quick snack.

Went to buy a breakfast deal at this branch after work this morning and a sausage roll (23/08/25) at just after 9am. I was served by this rude man who gave me a stern look throughout my custom and behaved like he didn't want to serve me. My order included a hot drink and I didn't see the drink. When he started process my payment, I asked him about the hot drink twice and he muttered about the machine isn't that quick! I literally had to repeat myself as he was muttering and I couldn't catch what he was saying. I wasn't happy about his customer service, so didn't say a thank you like I would have normally done. He then shouted out that I should have manners next time as my back was turned in front of the other customers. I replied by saying that he should have manners. Why is this man working behind the counters when he doesn't have the simplest common manners?
Very fast service even if there is a long cue. Would recommend the tuna crunch sandwich and the tandoori pizza. So filling and delicious. Go there all the time

In this economy, Greggs is undoubtedly the best place for food. The worker, Jax, was the most delightful and helpful worker I have had the pleasure of being served by and I will be going back more frequently because of them

I bought a cheese and onion bake but had to throw it away because the taste was unbearable. It felt like I received one that had been leftover and unsold from previous days. I was highly disappointed and ended up tossing it in the bin. 🙁

This is my favourite Greggs. I live across the street and the staff are always welcoming and friendly. Thanks to Monzo I get a weekly free sausage roll and I am always excited to pick it up (the little things in life…) and the staff are just delightful when you’re happy too. The shop is spotless and there’s rarely a long queue as they’re very organized. I look forward to popping in after a long day and picking up something for my husband just to brighten his day. So glad this Greggs is nearby.

I had the worst experience in the greggs today with customer service.I'm usually served by nice and warm people whenever I visit but today I was served by team member I don't wanna mention her name here .The shop was literally empty and I bought a drink, the way the drink was served was not at all pleasent, the staff was behaving like I was getting the drink for free.May be you should train your team members a bit on customer service.

I recently passed through Manchester twice and made three visits to this Greggs.

2 sausage  rolls + 1 sausage, bean & cheese melt = £4.70 (take away).

1 sausage roll + 1 americano coffee = £2.85 (eat in).

1 sausage roll + 1 vegetable bake = £3.40 (take away).

The food was hot & delicious, the staff were great.

If you want to sit down and enjoy your food there are a decent amount of tables & seats.  I did so on one of the three occasions.

My hands were numbed so a cup of hot water would warm me up. When I politely asked “ is it possible to have a cup of hot water?” when I picked up my order on the counter, she said “ No, we do not provide any hot water service !” Well, she was right and I did not pay for it anyway. From business point of view nothing wrong with her refusing to give a cup of hot water to go with our meal. From the society and good community point of view, it was a very bad influence and I was so socked. Since when our community became so cold and unfriendly? What would cost her to give me a cup of hot water in such freezing weather? Unfortunately , this whole process was witnessed by my 8 year old son, I packed up the food and left the shop. It is 10 minutes walk from my house. This unsympathetic and unhelpful services made me feel colder. Should we allow this cold service span in our society? We paid a very high council tax to keep our beautiful common and we share our house  keys with our neighbours, we enjoy our South Park garden with all other friendly and helpful people, so what happens to this beautiful India lady - the supervisor of Macdonald on Wimbledon Broadway? I asked another staff there and was told her name is Maria but not sure her surname.

The team lead Mary provided excellent table service, a rarity in fast-food establishments, making the experience feel more like a casual cafe. The staff were all incredibly friendly and efficient, ensuring a quick and seamless service.
Ordering through the app was a breeze, and my coffee, a decent brew for a mere £1.29, was promptly delivered to my table. The seating area was clean and well-maintained, creating a comfortable environment. Notably, the toilets were also spotless, a testament to the restaurant's commitment to hygiene.
Overall, McDonald's Penge offers a pleasant and efficient fast-food experience, elevated by the friendly staff and the team lead's commendable table service. For a quick and affordable meal or coffee, it's a solid choice.

McDonald's: Where dreams of a satisfying meal go to die. From the moment you unwrap your suspiciously warm burger, you're greeted with a symphony of disappointment. The patty, a gray, vaguely meat-like substance, squishes between your fingers, oozing a disconcerting orange grease. The lettuce is limp, the tomato a pale imitation of its vibrant brethren, and the bun – oh, the bun  a sugary, spongy mass that clings to your teeth like a desperate lover. And let's not forget the fries, those sad, anemic sticks of potato, drowning in a sea of salt, begging for a merciful end. Eating at McDonald's is not just a meal; it's an existential crisis served in a cardboard box. Avoid at all costs, unless you have a penchant for culinary despair.

This McDonald's exists in a state of perpetual occupation. Not busy as an occasional condition, but busy as an ontological fact. One does not arrive to find it crowded; one arrives to discover that it has always been crowded, and briefly makes room for you out of professional courtesy.

The doors open onto a scene of continuous, low-level urgency. Bodies circulate in tight orbits. Queues coil and uncoil like living things. There is the faint sense that if everyone stopped moving at once, the place would collapse in on itself. Students, hospital staff, commuters, teenagers — all pressed together in a temporary republic of hunger and impatience.

The interior is less a dining room than a holding pen for modern life. Seating exists, technically, but feels aspirational. Tables are occupied, vacated, reoccupied in a rhythm so brisk it borders on athletic. Anyone seated for more than ten minutes begins to feel morally suspect, as though overstaying a visa.

The ordering screens glow with bureaucratic indifference, absorbing decisions at scale. Staff operate with the calm efficiency of people who know that slowing down is not an option. Food appears rapidly, correctly, and without romance. This is nourishment delivered under pressure, not hospitality.

Cleanliness is heroic, given the circumstances. Trays pile up and vanish. Bins swell and are emptied. The floor bears the marks of constant traffic, but not neglect. The overall impression is not disorder, but ceaseless recovery — a space permanently one step behind, yet never falling over.

Noise accumulates rather than echoes: overlapping conversations, shouted numbers, the hiss and beep of machinery doing its utmost. It is not unpleasant so much as dense. Silence would feel unnatural here, even threatening.

How could it be better? Fewer people, perhaps. Or more space. Or time slowing by half a notch. But these are metaphysical complaints. This McDonald’s is operating exactly as the city demands of it: fast, crowded, mildly exhausting, and oddly reliable.

You do not come here to linger, reflect, or be surprised. You come to be processed, fed, and released back onto Denmark Hill with your immediate needs met and your expectations entirely intact. In that narrow but important sense, it succeeds relentlessly.
