Just had the single rudest staff member serve me at this drive through. "Hang on". "Next window". "Yeah, so what?" "I dont have a name". Surly, rude and offensive. Complained to the manager, Nadia, who was excellent, but this is a very bad reflection on Mc Donald's. How hard is it to say please or thank you rather than a addressing customers with a dreadful attitude?

One of quietest McDonald’s ever I have visited. Service is quite fast and smooth. Restaurant is quite clean and tidy. Unfortunately staffs are not as friendly as other McDonald’s. They are robotic. But that’s not a problem me as long as I get my food and drinks quickly.

I would like to share my feedback about Antonella. She is extremely polite, friendly, and very responsible in her work. Antonella communicates with delivery drivers exceptionally well and always keeps everything organized. Her speed and the high quality of her work are truly impressive. She consistently performs her duties efficiently and professionally. Thank you for having such a great team member!

This McDonald's near Cutty Sark station is by far the worst I've been to. I waited 20 minutes for my food, which is ridiculous for a fast-food restaurant. The place was unclean—tables were covered in crumbs, the floor was sticky, and the trash bins were overflowing. The staff didn’t seem to care, and service was slow. Avoid this place if you can!
The fries are stale and barely any salt or flavour. Burgers are so underwhelming just couple of drops ketchup or any other sauce and below average patty. Plus the options are very limited.

Depending on the time of day there may or may not be a queue. You can order on the machines on the side and use the app to collect points or just use it as a guest. Keep an eye on the screen for your order number. Usually there's a manager in store running the orders.

There's always kids of all ages in here and security guard on the door.

There's a toilet in here and more seating on the first floor, but sometimes they close the upper floor.

You know what to expect - nuggets, burgers, fries, mcflurries and apple pies.

Follow here and on IG for more travel and food and drink tips, ideas and reviews @one.more.step.along.the.world

Extremally busy branch in rush hours, I wouldn't recommend visiting that time. Long waiting time and lots of people around.

Food was ok, cheeseburger a little below from what I'd expect from Mc.

Service was rushing and props for that lady on the first line handing so many orders.

Not bad in terms of cleanness considering how big footprint they have there.

Please retrain your staff or hire new people. Most of the time my burger is built like this or missing a bun. Plus your staff lack customer service skills, I dont blame them only the managers who train them. PLEASE DO BETTER.

I went to the opening of Mc Donald’s in King Street Hammersmith We were a group Of Kids from a Children’s Hone apparently invited for the opening for many years after I used to love the Food but in the past 10 years not the case
To me it does not Taste Good Anymore the burgers are dry dry dry when I could afford the fillet of fish in the 1.99 meal deal
which is now What 5.00 That’s  a massive rise also I was not happy when they took away the free coffee after collecting the stickers on the side of the cup you brought a Double Expresso has gone sky High in price it used to be 99p now yesterday it was £1.69 now free coffee anymore if you pay cash

I hate using those screens after watching Channel 4 programme re the germs on them Disgusting I don’t see them wipe the down that often !!!There was also a Video on threads about there fries there is an ingredient added  that is more harmful than smoking Cigarettes

Now I love a Chocolate Milkshake it used to be £2.39 When the machine was working often stand in the que only to find this out then mc broken app came through which was great nothing more annoying than parking your car get to the store machine out of order I lost count how many times I had to phone customer service but this summer for the first summer I can remember every time the machines were working
But yesterday I paid £3.19 for a large chocolate shake see photos then the Monopoly sticker would not open photo included there was no sticker there as you can see what a rip off the milkshakes have gone up from £2.39 to £3.19 & Ian sure The Mc Donald’s inside Westfield charge more why should we get charged more. Because they are inside Westfield yes rent is higher but last time I went in there it was super busy so they defo take the money there

I know we have a cost of Living Crisis but I think these price rises across the board are far too high and most of the prices went up at a faster rate after the pandemic mean come on I bet they made millions in profit too !!!

I will take my cup back to get sticker but what if the sticker that was meant to be there was one if the big prizes ….,
I or We Will Never Know

You know I said about the free coffee you see when you are broke broke u could hunt around find stickers in the bin and at least get a Hot drink
You know that is probably what a lot of Homeless people like me used to do at the time so I think that is another wrong thing to do taking away this little perk I am sure it saved Mc Donald’s a lot of money
So there it is prices too High for my like so won’t be visiting as much if at all anymore sorry
I am a regular customer. What I really like is the cleanliness of the toilets. The environment is also very good, and the service has been very fast. That's why I give it five stars.

We didn’t actually eat here as we didn’t have time before our uber arrived but we did get talking to one of the security guards I believe his name was Mawhoo he was working a late shift on Friday night around 1am.
He was so lovely and friendly and really made us feel safe and comfortable while we waited for our uber. Just wanted to say what a lovely person he is and he really went above and beyond for his job - as 3 women out late at night it makes such a difference. Thank you so much :)

I think this is the first McDonald's I visited that stood out due to it's unique colour theme and interior design - definitely different from the usual layout. The restaurant was very clean and well-maintained. There are two floors with plenty of seating inside, as well as some outdoor seating available.

Service was quick, and the food tasted just like any other McDonald’s—reliable and familiar. Although there’s a decent number of parking spots, they can fill up quickly during busy hours.

Overall, a pleasant experience in a slightly different setting from the typical McDonald’s.

Last two times i have ordered from here for delivery my fries in two different meals were both missing. How do you consistently forget FOUR fries as part of a meal each time???? Foods always cold too and wrong dips given. Two stars is generous. Disgrace should be disenfranchised

The food is consistently cold. Before I thought it was cold when delivered because of the driver taking long, but nope - now I realise it’s because the food comes out the restaurant cold! I’ve eaten both there and had it delivered and on every single occasion (without fail) every single food item is cold. Probably the worst McDonald’s I’ve been to (that’s a feat!).

Very disappointing experience at this McDonald’s. The kitchen staff were preparing food without wearing gloves, which is concerning from a hygiene standpoint. On top of that, the staff seemed busy with their own conversations and paid little attention to customers. We had to wait at least 25–30 minutes just to be served. Poor service and not a well-managed store.

When we orders the machine didn’t print the receipt and their was no table service.There were beer cans all around us and trash everywhere.There were no Bathrooms and service was horrible I had to wait 10 minutes for two ketchups.Advertisement misleading. We ordered eat in and they didn’t give us a tray and hesitated to provide us our bill .Never go to this place and make sure the atmosphere is well kept
Not bad , food was ok and service was good . Toilets were rank with pee on the floors

I recently visited the McDonald’s in Acton and was left quite disappointed. While ordering was quick and straightforward, the wait to collect my food was unreasonably long for a fast-food restaurant.

When my order was finally ready, the fries were clearly not fresh. They had been sitting out for too long, which made them soggy and cold. This took away from the overall enjoyment of the meal.

I also noticed that some members of staff were not paying close attention to hygiene, which is concerning in a food environment. It would be good to see more focus on cleanliness and food quality.

Mcdonald's Acton High Street

I was horrified at what I saw and amazed that this branch could pass a cleanliness inspection.

Almost all the tables has left over rubbish from previous customers.

The toilets, I wouldn't have used if my kids weren't bursting.

I have taken pictures, so you can see for yourself and not think I'm just an upset customer.
I brought it to the attention of the management. But it took about 30 minutes for action to be taken.

When I left, about an hour after I entered, many of issues still remained, including the overflowing urinal, with no sign put on or beside it.

Popped in today with limited time to grab a bite to eat, so decided to just order a cheeseburger as usually at McDonald's you can get one of these quickly. I ordered at the machine and waited for 10 minutes while just about everyone else got their orders, including some people with larger orders who ordered after me. I asked a member of staff if they could just quickly serve me my cheeseburger as I really had to go. At that point he searched around for one, and there wasn't one ready so he asked the kitchen to start making one, so it would have been another 5-10 minutes probably, so I had to leave with nothing having already paid, as I had a meeting to attend. I don't recommend going here if you want any sort of fast food, the staff are all in slow motion unfortunately.

Run by a bunch of utterly incompetent and lazy individuals. Food was sitting around going cold because they couldn't bag it up quickly enough. Our order was just left behind one of the machines and ignored. Took about 30 mins to get a single burger.
Avoid.

We stopped at McDonald’s Westfield after the Disney Princess event and it was just what we needed! The kids were still buzzing with excitement, so a quick and easy meal was perfect. Service was smooth, staff were polite, and the food came out quickly. A nice way to end a magical day! 🍔🍟✨

*0/5 stars - A Letdown!*

I recently tried the Chicken Big Mac at McDonald's, and honestly, it was a disappointment. The patty tasted more like processed chicken than actual chicken breast. The "special sauce" was just mayo with some added sugar, and the lettuce was wilted. The bun was soggy, and the cheese was bland. For a premium burger, I expected better quality ingredients and flavor. Unfortunately, the Chicken Big Mac didn't hit the spot. Maybe I just caught a bad batch? Either way, I won't be ordering this again anytime soon.

This happened on Saturday. I ordered 2 large meal deals and only received 2 ketchups. When I kindly asked for a couple more, the staff member was extremely rude and dismissive. She wasn’t wearing a name badge, and when I asked for her name, she refused to give it. Her colleagues also backed her up instead of helping. Completely unprofessional. I’ve attached her photo below so management can identify and address this properly. Very disappointing experience.

An average experience but on the slightly disappointing side. The McDonald's was extremely busy and hard to get access to a touch-screen to order. I didn't know how or where to order at the counter and the toilets were hard to find.

The staff were friendly but it wasn't the best customer service and I was quickly given my order and no departure greeting. I couldn't find the condiment bar to get salt and ketchup.

The toilets were clean, once I'd managed to find them.

The food was cold and the burger was made incorrectly and also missing ingredients, the fries weren't full up and were warm. The Big Mac burger was not made correctly either and the staff swapped it but did not offer an apology for the incorrect items.

The dining area had anti-social behaviour and people loitering which reduced the amount of available seating. I would visit again but preferably at a quieter time. I would recommend it to friends and family as in all fairness, the staff resolved our issues with the food and mistakes happen.

While eating in, we requested tap water. Multiple staff and the manager said they don’t provide tap water and claimed this was policy. Only after repeated requests was a small amount reluctantly provided. Given this appears to be a consistent practice, I have raised the issue with McDonald’s UK head office and the local council to investigate compliance with licensing terms. Disappointing service and attitude.

if only there was a negative review on Google Maps, it would really help to justify the current situation with the customer service I received at this branch of McDonald's.

Yesterday I had travelled all the way from from my house to the ASDA Park Royal McDonald's which takes around 30 minutes on an average to buy a walking distance only to to redeem my discount coupon since I'm a loyal customer of different Mcdonald branch elsewhere.

The person to take my order served me my orders for a takeaway. When I reached home, I could received only a filet fish burger without the patty. You know how ridiculous that sounds?? Have a look at the images. When i called them back and explained them about the situation, the person who took my order that goes by the name of Niti Shah told me that she was sorry and you can come next day to collect the entire meal you ordered on us.

I did go today and there was a new manager. He looked with disinterest and utterly disrespectful when I told him about it. Took the receipt and threw it in the bin. Then when I seen the new receipt, it only had one filet fish burger and nothing else on it. He again gave me a disgusted look and said its not my lookout since no one told me about it. He told someone from the staff to just order fries and drink. The burger came with the fries but I was made to wait like an idiot for 10 mins before I just went away with whatever respect I had after this incident. I don't want to sound racist or offend anyone but the manager who I had to deal with was a tall, dark with specs... I couldn't even ask for his name since he just disappeared and didn't seem interested whatsoever.

Begs the question: was it my fault? No. Was I dealt correctly with this situation? Absolutely not. Do McDonald's really care about their loyal customers? No way... cuz apparently their customer care number which i tried calling multiple times isn't correct. Utterly disappointed and treated so pathetic, it's quite unapologetic and rude!

Very bad experience here and they are providing worst service ,no cleaner,no table service and don’t ask even about food. One crew member Manali so rude and arrogant seriously not even a smile on her face and we faced worst issues with staff and internet today because our network was weak so we asked for WiFi and they denied it we r not providing WiFi. So it not nice u r saying this to customers. It’s better to eat out on another place

just came to home with my takeaway order that did there and i can see they didn’t put my 2 apple pie that i ordered just filet-o-fish meals. This is not first time last time i ordered double fish burgers they put filet-o-fish instead . 😡 now I cant go back to them i living far to them. There serves 👎👎👎👎

25.05.2024. My order on the receipt.  The rest of the photos show what I actually got.  There are no words in the dictionary of cultured people to describe amateurism, lack of professionalism, incompetence, or dilettantism. Is this a new patent for improving financial results? Madhouse.
I will add that this is not the first time.

Ordered my food for the children at 9.45 kept getting changing times for preparation and delivery estimates...I wasn't able to cancel the order myself but then it was cancelled just before midnight by them. No explanation or apology Nothing how rude are they. 11/07/25

OK-ish food, lots my salad was outside of the burger and underneath it, which is not great. Fries could have been better.

Extremally fast service, order was ready in a minute. Clean enough, I don't expect much in such a busy and small location when people move all the time. Clean toilets.

I came to this McDonald in Knightsbridge, I do come here often when I’m in the area, however tonight it was just shambles , I ordered mine and my friends food on the kiosk, my order was with no pickles, so I’m aware I have to wait a little longer.

I ordered at 21:03, I sat down to eat my food at 21:31. Worst part was after waiting just around half an hour for my food, I go upstairs and I see there’s only smudge of sauce on the bun!

I go back to the counter, and the guy who served me I believe he was the manager, asked me how much sauce I want, I said just one shot , I receive my burger back and it’s literally smothered in sauce, than I question it, and he tells me to spread it.

I said it’s not my job.

Took them nearly half an hour to make an order for me and my friend. I understand this is a very busy location, however the restaurant was practically empty and not busy at all.

The guy who served me did seem quite angry as I think the staff working in the kitchen let him down

came to eat at the McDonald’s in Knightsbridge and was honestly shocked by the state of the place. There is no cleaner on site—I’ve been sitting here for over half an hour, and not a single person has come to clean. Tables are covered in old food, trays are left everywhere, and the floors are filthy. It’s absolutely disgusting. This is supposed to be a place where people can sit and enjoy a meal, but the lack of cleanliness makes that impossible. Really disappointed, and I won’t be coming back.

It's a Maccys so scores are based on that. Lots of staff standing around with, it seems, just the one person cooking. We ordered two big mac meals at 6pm and waited 10mins for them to cook them fresh. On the upside it was a great Bigmac!

We placed an order today to be delivered and one more time both happy meals were missing their chicken nuggets... not funny anymore!!😡. Again I tried to contact them without success. What is the point of having a contact number if no-one is able to pick up the phone 🤔. I noticed that when occasionally we give a tips we get a good service but when we don't we know the score. This is the last time I was ordering with them. I miss the one we had on kings road... Much better service .

It’s seems they might have an issue with understaffed/people calling sick/slow service. Staff seems demoralised and it’s visible since the have fully open kitchen.

I don’t usually eat brekkie menu but I had a taste for muffin. Waiting time was very long, usually I get quick service from this chain but waited 10 mins for muffin and hash brown and place wasn’t busy.

Bit dirty too, not terribly but it’s visible.

Food was suprisingly good, muffin with egg and bacon was tasty, hash brown was served very hot.

Busy branch, and the team at the Bayswater are super helpful too. They saved me from a sugar crash  (hypoglycaemia so could go into coma!!) due to my invisible disabilities! Team members Ayo, Mehadad & Chaytanya, and the very helpful manager over the weekend helped and also explained how the feedback form works. The lower sugar content of my special limited edition drink was good my disability and wellbeing, and a great as well! Well done team! Would be 5 stars for the atmosphere but I struggle with busy environments, so giving 4 here, but it’s my situation. Might be different for others for others.
Great place to go if you’re lactose intolerant, you only get a slither of cheese
.this a two (britisj) no too friendli. Don't like Messi. But good good.

Visited this branch today, we are tourists so yes we don’t know what to expect. But this is what we got > slow service for 2 muffins and pancakes. The prep person not wearing any gloves handling the creation of our food was kind of disturbing, I understand with through washing of hands it’s fine but still it doesn’t look good from a customers POV. Next the pancakes were so dry and hard, the cutlery broke trying to cut it, it was like cardboard.

I like this branch, spacious and bright. Nit that many people when I visited and tables next to the window were dirty. Haven't seen any cleaners around.

Food was ok, meets Mc standards but that's pretty much it. As with any other branch now you have to ask for ketchup.

Quick service, no smile thou.

A vegetarian's dream in Europe! Finally got to try the Veggie Delight burger—why don't we have this in the USA yet? The menu is packed with great options like veggie wraps, plant-based burgers, and salads. So impressed!

I’m really disappointed today😞
I placed an order (£9.47) for takeaway:
– two chicken rolls 🫔🫔
– fries🍟
– a hot chocolate☕
– and a fruit muffin🧁(£2.20)
I was in a rush to catch the bus and didn’t check the order at the time. Unfortunately, the fruit muffin was missing. I was planning to eat it on the bus, so this was really disappointing
😭😭😭

Food itself is good, and is prepared quickly.

Open 24 hours, and the seats will be available throughout the night so you can comfortably sit down.

Staff unfortunately wasn't enthusiastic, and wouldn't really say much or reply to a thank you.

Was great one night. Went again, got our food and went home a few blocks away only to realize we were missing an item. I immediately returned with the receipt and the employee basically accused me of lying. Why would I lie about one item out of many in our order and waste my time coming back? Really disappointed to be treated that way. We got our food in the end but I was absolutely embarrassed and felt like I was treated unfairly.

First time in this branch and I liked it quite a lot. First branch with such a character, maybe a little ran down but that's part of the experience. Sitting area upstairs with view on main street.

Good customer service, clean too, although it wasn't super busy.

Food was ok, it's typical Mc, so don't expect much but they are consisted .It could have been a bit hotter but I'm not complaining. Seems they have regular customers from what I notice.

Joseph, the manager here, is genuinely one of the kindest and most impressive people I’ve ever encountered at any McDonald’s in the UK. His warm smile and friendly way of greeting every customer truly stand out and make a real difference. He is incredibly helpful, patient, and understanding, and it’s clear he takes great pride in his work. The restaurant itself is spotless and exceptionally well run even in the rush hour, easily one of the best McDonald’s in the country. What’s remarkable is that he probably doesn’t even realise how noticeable his positivity is; not once did I see him without a smile. Outstanding customer service and a pleasure to experience.

I visited the McDonald’s on Baker Street last week and was very concerned by what I saw. The staff were preparing burgers with their bare hands, without wearing gloves. This is not only unhygienic but also raises serious food safety concerns.

I understand that mistakes happen, but proper hygiene standards are crucial, especially in a fast-food environment where customers expect safe and clean food preparation. I hope management addresses this issue promptly to ensure the health and safety of all customers.

This is one of the dirtiest restaurants I have ever been in. Visited on a Saturday afternoon and while there were plenty of customers ordering, it wasn't exactly packed.

Food was ready in good time so no long wait which was something, however as you can see from the photos it seems that either the staff here don't know how to clean, or don't care! The bins were completely overflowing and literally every table was just covered in rubbish. If this is how "clean" the customer facing areas are, I'm a bit scared to think what it is like in the kitchen!

Avoid if possible. This is by far the worst McDonald’s I’ve visited. We came in as a family of five and found it very difficult to get a table, it’s clearly more of a takeaway location than a dine-in one.

When we finally got a table and ordered via table service, the staff member who brought our food didn’t greet us or acknowledge us, just dropped the tray and walked away. Parts of our order were missing, my child’s nuggets from her Happy Meal and my husband’s wrap. When I went to the counter to resolve it, the staff member implied I was lying, saying, “If we’d missed it, it would still be behind the counter and its not.” I had to insist he come and check our tray. The whole situation was handled very poorly.

The toilets were also in unacceptable condition, only two of three cubicles were usable, they were dirty, sanitary bins were overflowing,if you sat down you would touch it, and the smell was awful. We ended up using toilets elsewhere as I couldn’t let my child use them.

Overall, a very disappointing experience. I wouldn’t return to this location.

Pretty clean branch, quick service but I use the app so my order gets prepared when I'm close.

First time eating just chicken in Mc, it's ok, serves the purpose but nothing else. Lots of batter, quite crunchy but barely any taste. Recommend sauce.

Visited when it wasn’t busy and service was slow.

Store however was clean and the lady was constantly mopping and cleaning tables which was good to see it. Food was ok and coffee was nice as usual.

Great American Restaurant
Your usual staple American burger chain. This one is actually really well located as close to the underground exit, and is at the beginning of the Notting Hill area.
The food was okay and the staff courteous.

UNBELIEVABLE. This is the second time in less than a week that an order has been delivered with items missing and the food which actually turned up was cold. Not lukewarm...completely stone cold. DO NOT ORDER FROM THIS BRANCH. Absolutely appalling.

Their phone number doesn't work. I ordered using Just Eat Happy Meal amd other things at 9 pm and at 10 pm it estimated delivery for 23. There is no way to contact neither of them no way to cancel the order. I am not going to have the meal with a child at midnight!

Terrible experience at McDonalds Bond Street. The queue was extremely long and nobody was managing it properly. There is no restroom available which makes it very inconvenient, especially considering how busy the place is. The service was very slow and orders took a long time to be delivered. The place was not clean at all messy. On top of that, none of the orders were showing on the display screen, which caused even more confusion and frustration. Overall it was a totally horrible experience and one of the worst McDonalds visits I have had in London..

Disclaimer: I only write one star reviews here. If it was a five star review, I wouldn't be writing it because I'd encourage more business and more business normally dilutes customer service. I like my hidden gems to stay hidden gems.

My brother constantly checks my Google Maps reviews (call them art if you will) because he's my biggest fan, and so I felt inclined to write this review to make his year (and we're only in January).

So anyway, on to the famed "Golden Arches". First off, I just want to say that I've never actually been to this "restaurant" but this is more so a critique of the entire chain. Do a quick YouTube search and see how "durable" a typical burger from this joint is. They're basically bomb proof. Like I could literally hide behind one of their burgers and be safe during World War 3. If people from Pompeii had hidden behind these burgers when Vesuvius erupted, they wouldn't have been flame grilled (Burger King pun intended). Seriously, search "McDonald's Bryan Johnson" on YouTube and you'll see that your burger basically comes with a lifetime warranty because of whatever they put into it.

Make smart food choices and in the words of Drake "Thank me later".

🦅 Make America Great Again 🦅

The service was extremely appauling and the price for a big mac meal is £8.40 a total RIP OFF!. The dining facility in this restaurant branch was a useless little dog box! This restaurant company deserves to lose customers...

I walked in from the sideway and it was open but after, I noticed the other shutter was (mostly) down. There were plenty of staff but no customers and I was told "we're closed for technical reasons". I asked different staff twice, what technical reasons and they just repeated the same thing. The only thing said in addition was we willl be opening in about half an hour. When I return 40 minutes later of course, it still wasn't open.

This McDonald’s near Bond Street Station was a terrible experience. There were no self-order display screens, no restroom available, and the space for seating was extremely limited. You can’t even use the McDonald’s app properly to collect rewards or bonuses. On top of that, the place was very dirty and the staff were rude and unprofessional. I definitely do not recommend this location🤮👎🏻👎🏻

Stopped by the McDonald’s in Paddington for a quick bite — pretty much what you’d expect from a standard McDonald’s. The service was quick, and the food came out hot, which was good. The restaurant was reasonably clean and managed well, especially given how busy the area can get.

The only downside was that the burger looked a bit roughly made, as if it had been put together in a rush. Taste-wise it was fine, just not the neatest presentation. Overall, it did the job for a quick meal, but nothing particularly special.

This is a very busy McDonald’s with lots of kiosks. Self order and just wait, that easy.

The food was as expected and filled me up with that consistent same delicious McDonald’s taste that I get in the United States. Cheeseburger, fries and chicken strips were deliciously filling.

I’d definitely come back here again to try their other menu items.

It's to go only place, no siting inside. Kinda dystopian look if you ask me, only kiosks to order your food from. Somebody said kitchen is based in the basement and they use conveyor belt to get meals up. Even more dystopian.

I also used dystopian method for my order. You don't have to check in anymore, app would recognise you are close to a store, sends signal over and inform them they can start prepping your order. Dystopian.

My food was suprisingly good, chips were just ok, never been a fan of chips from Mc, but burger was pretty good. Lots of onions inside so it had that extra crunchiness.

I wouldn't say service was polite, cos it wasn't but those guys get paid minimum wage or close to it, and serve thousands of customers per day. That can kill any soul.

Honestly, Bharat is an outstanding employee and a true example of professionalism. He greeted me with a smile, listened carefully, and went above and beyond to help without any hesitation. His attitude was respectful, efficient, and genuinely caring, making the whole experience smooth and enjoyable. Employees like Bharat truly represent the brand in the best way. Huge thanks and appreciation — well deserved! 👏🔥

Exactly what you expect from a fast food restaurant. Food was hot and fresh and the service quick. When you are in a rush and want something to eat this is a great choice. Right near the GWR station, good thing with the mobile order, even much easier than before to place your order, and they always call your name, how cool is that!
Definitely recommend

This has got to be one of the best McDonald’s I have visited in the UK in the past several years. Such a spontaneous feeling when you see the restaurant clean and the staff smiling and friendly. Service was superfast but with no bargain on quality. And unlike other McDonald’s where no trays are served, this branch serves trays and tissues as well (with a smile) to your table. Thank you for making my day!

Quick, Convenient & Busy Location

McDonald’s on Edgware Road is a great spot if you want fast, reliable food in a busy central area. The service is usually quick, and the staff are helpful even during rush hours. The place stays busy because of the location, but mobile ordering and self-service kiosks make the process easier.

The restaurant is clean, seating is decent, and it’s open late, which is perfect if you’re out and need a quick meal. Sometimes it gets crowded, especially on weekends and evenings, but overall it’s a solid and convenient McDonald’s.

Good for a quick bite when you’re around Edgware Road!

This must be one of cleanest Mc I've seen. Ok, it wasn't super busy but it wasn't empty either, decent amount of customers and they managed to keep it clean. I can tell they have dedicated people doing only this.

Service was kinda cold, food was ok, usual Mc standard, I like to eat in their branches from time to time, more like a guilty pleasure than anything else.
Good place to have a quick warm meal they prepared it well however be prepare for remember of any missing items....

McDonald’s is really heading downhill. It’s the worst feeling when you get home with your Mcds ready to eat after a long wait and an even longer day and you find your fillet o fish is missing. If this was the first time this happened I could understand but Edgware Road McDonalds is consistent in messing up your order. And who is going to walk all the back to the restaurant with or without the receipt claiming a burger was missing? No one because the staff are going to look at you like you’re crazy and just want a free burger

Religious Discrimination:

I ordered through the app for delivery from this branch, and they completely messed up my order. I specifically requested a vegetarian burger, but they sent a beef burger instead. As a Hindu, this is deeply offensive and goes against my religious beliefs — it’s unacceptable.
To make things worse, several items from the order were missing. When I called the store, nobody seemed interested in resolving the issue — not even the manager. The lack of responsibility and care from both the staff and management is disgraceful.
The problem clearly starts with the manager — if they show no accountability, it’s no surprise the staff behave the same way. How can a manager refuse to come to the phone after such a serious mistake?
Be very careful ordering from this branch — their service and attitude are appalling. No apology and effort was made from anyone at this branches. You lot are awful. Will never order from this branch again…
Shame on you manager, you have proved that you don’t care at all. Believe in Karma…..
Manager you are a disgrace.
What worse can be the manager at this branch as you are getting so many negative reviews. You should be fired straightaway. Businesses goes down because of people like you. Shame on you.

For every one negative review, manager cooks up three positive reviews in the kitchen.

11/1000

Manav, Harshit , Arun & all the other staff are so kind, hardworking & helpful here!

I wish them all an amazing prosperous 2026 ahead! ❤️

Kindest felt regards!

Dimitrius DaraArjun HariGovind Singh Cheema 😊

🤣Update a couple of days later:
Today, I guess they didn’t need positive reviews, because the self-service machine (as usual) didn’t print my receipt. The table service took quite a while, and the guy who brought my food clearly wasn’t in a great mood - he didn’t even reply to my polite “thank you” when he delivered it.
With my Med latte, I got two milk sachets and two sugar packets, but nothing to stir it with (luckily, I ordered a salad instead of fries, so I used the salad knife, maybe that was the plan 😆).

The group of 2 who came in shortly after me have been waiting MUCH longer than I did. And they still haven’t been served their food(small update: they was waiting abut 25-27mins for their order).

Meanwhile, the guy in the white shirt is just walking around the place with his hands in his pockets 😄
---------------------------------------------------------
The staff member at this place asked me to leave a review, and I’m happy to do so. This time, my food was brought to my table very quickly, the table was surprisingly clean, and the staff member was friendly, smiling, and polite.
I genuinely hope this becomes the standard here, not just something that happens occasionally when the Google rating needs a boost.

Either way, it’s a very positive experience, especially because these days you often see prices going up while standards don’t, sometimes even drop.

Thank you.

Very busy branch.

Cudos to the team, my order gone missing, they found it asap. Messed up some sauces but considering what’s happening there i can’t blame them.

Those guys work like a robots there, don’t think I would last there long.

Food was ok, Mac standards met. Quite clean as well with so many people around.

Nikil is 10/10 worker.
I was waiting for my order less than a minute. Very nice and fast stuff.
Thank you ❤️

Macdonalds Warren Street Review

Standing Room only

It's Difficult to fault the technical application of fast food here here.

The layout is interesting. No seating in side at street level. But a good amount of seating outside and  downstairs.

The quality of the food is good. Temperature always seems to be a challenge for mcdonald's

Table service was available, but didn't use it this some reason.
Also, our food arrived surprisingly quickly.

The staff were so  focused on ticking all the right boxes, they often forgot to smile.

It's not very busy. So it's not to difficult to provide a good service, even with only one person serving

This Mcdonalds does everything it says on the tin. So nothing to complain. about.

Quick bite before the nearby theatre. The line for the toilets was insane they should definitely institute a code on the receipt as everyone from the street was coming in to use the facilities and paying customers spent an eon queuing. Food was quite and tasty no complaints

The chips were cold and I ordered a double fillet of fish but they only put one fillet in, so I had to take it back. Plus, the WiFi was broken and the men’s toilets blocked. The retract felt like it needed a good clean.

Was there around 11, and upstairs were closed. That meant very few seats. They have priority seats right next to the entrance which is good. Food was ok - got an extra dip which I did not order, but can't complain about that! Music was a bit loud.

Farzana served us, but asked for a review immediately, which I personally found a bit strange. She also came up to us immediately after the meal to ask about reviews again. Felt watched - and a bit unconfortable. She cleaned up after us which was great i guess.

Bit "too much" service for my liking, would consider going to another Mcdonalds in the future.

Branch needs to be closed.

Been to this maccies numerous times across multiple years and finally decide to rate them down for the following reasons:

1. Always give half filled chips - you order large you get medium, order medium you get small, happens more often than not - 3/5 times on average

2. Missing items in order multiple occasions. Can’t even do the simple thing.

3. Last time I checked, Big Mac should have 2 patties in there?? (See picture)

They are lucky that this is a perfect spot around UCL and UCLH and Warren St station.

In the morning I ordered porridge. I received a cold, incomprehensible mass. I asked to replace it with a quality dish. I waited another 15 minutes because they forgot about me, only to receive warm porridge in a dirty glass and with even worse honey. It feels like my dish was thrown away somewhere. I didn't eat it. It was too disgusting.

Extremally busy branch, specially in this time of the year. I'd say avoid if you can or visit during odd hours. But again, I'd avoid this area as much as possible, it's big tourist trap now and very annoying place to be if you live in London.

They try to keep the place tidy and I'd say they do pretty good job considering how many people go through this branch. It's not ideal but I wouldn't expect that. Quick service, people work there as like a robots, I feel for them.

Food was ok, probably the best chicken sandwich I've had in any Mc; still kinda carboard food but better. Good and hot chips, plus for that.

I was thrilled to discover that there was a McDonald's located just ten minutes away from my destination. I had been eager to try the Grinch menu, which had been quite tempting. Upon entering this particular branch, I felt a surge of excitement, curious to experience the hype surrounding it. However, my enthusiasm was dampened by the sluggish service. The lack of organization made it difficult for me to remain patient. I was unsure who was serving and could not decipher the system they were employing. Consequently, I opted to find something closer to the venue I was heading to
Just on the verge to 5 star. They are good but not as others. Run out of  things. They must know how to keep up with busy street like rhis one ia.

Very chaotic busy especially on weekend. Very difficult to get seat to eat. But you get served quickly anyway as you mostly order by screen and not too long wait to order or collect. Great food as always though. Well done to staff for being very organised 👏

Very busy branch but they have VO system so people can hear their numbers and employees don't have to shout. All branches should have it. Quick service.

Food was ok, I only had chicken nuggets, so my expectation wasn't high. Food has served it purpose.

Quite clean for such a busy and big store, compliments to stuff.

Why is McDonalds so popular?
The FRIES. All the way back from Selfridges to the Savoy you could see the McDonalds packed and in the windows you could see all ages snarfing down the fries with ketchup or without. One cute seen I saw a child look at his parent since he had the last fry and what did Dad do- he shared it and both had big smiles and Daddy gave a hug. By the way for seniors like me and you have to watch your salt NaCl intake. Just ask and they will fry you a fresh batch without salt. But see that’s what makes McDonalds McDonalds They are the epitome of customer service. Why else do they stick out-PRICING. Taking a family out for the day can be quite costly and we all want a meal out.  Voila McDonalds has your back. To me personally I don’t understand the appeal of the McNugget but my grandchildren have eaten enough that I am surprised they haven’t laid eggs. I am still a Big Mac fan. The combination of patties, cheese, onions, lettuce and special sauce just can’t be beat.  Also have you ever noticed Coca Cola tastes better there. It’s because they have worked the calibration of soda to syrup to a science. In America it’s one of the few places Coke tastes like it’s supposed to be. If it doesn’t, tell them and they will fix it. Why spend all that money for colored fizzy water.

People are everywhere filling the streets and sidewalks. McDonalds here is nice size with plenty of order machines.

This is my go-to spot for breakfast wherever I travel. The outlet always has plenty of seating, making it easy and fuss-free. I ordered the McMuffin with egg breakfast, opting for the set that comes with an Americano and hashbrown. While the hashbrown could be a bit more flavorful, everything else was consistent with what I enjoy at other locations worldwide. Reliable, convenient, and familiar—this is a breakfast option that rarely disappoints.

I had a very upsetting experience at this McDonald’s today, and I feel genuinely disrespected by the way the counter manager spoke to me.

I ordered an ice cream and a Filet-O-Fish separately. I received my ice cream first and stood to the side to eat it while waiting for the second item. The manager kept staring at me and then suddenly asked for my number. After I told him, he said, ‘This is your food. Why didn’t you come get it? We called you five times.’

His tone was extremely rude and completely unnecessary. I wasn’t in a rush, and that’s the whole reason I ordered separately. There was absolutely no need to speak to me like that. The interaction made me feel disrespected and genuinely affected my mental well-being.

No customer should be talked to this way. This level of attitude from a manager is completely unacceptable.

This can’t be worse. In early October I ordered the burger to go in Charing Cross, and saw this film inside the burger. Even though I reported this unpleasant experience and ask for the clarification, there HAS NOT BEEN any response to my report. I don’t understand why there are many people queueing this branch to get the burgers with films inside.

Very big branch, I'd recommend sitting upstairs next to a window and have a look at TCR tube station. Although table over there was a dirty, and it wasn't very busy. Overall clean branch.

Super fast service, even with small smile, which you don't see in Mc; my fries were hot, but had to ask for ketchup. Think that's a policy now in Mc.

Food was ok, Mc standards, never expected to get some incredible experience from them.

Because of the location I'd expect this place can be extremally busy in rush hours.
Went for dinner, quite a big restaurant in Oxford street with seating in multiple floors, food served was fresh and hot also quick enough for Saturday evening rush. Washroom is tiny and dirty

One of the best Maccas I have been to - actual food looks just like it does on the menu images! Friendly and helpful staff, great location and nice atmosphere. No freely available BBQ sauce, however the chilli sauce made up for it!

A very busy store which is frequent with shoppers on Oxford Street. The service area is busy, small and hectic whilst the seating areas upstairs and downstairs are quieter and more relaxing.

The food and drink are good, hot and fresh.

Service by the staff could be slightly better but as the store is busy this could be part of the reason. However a thank you or parting pleasantry from the staff would be very welcome.
The seating area needs some improvements  but the food was great. I really enjoyed it.

Despite the lunch rush on 17 Dec 2025 and the screen displaying order status not working, the team got food to people quickly and politely. My veggie burger was ready in less than 5 min, looked amazing and was really good. I have been hete before and the service is always quick and good.

It's Mc, I don't expect to get superb food there but something hot and comforting.

Imho chicken sandwich has to go from the menu, it tastes like paper, and it's not only this branch. Very hot fries, I liked it.

Congrats to entire team, this is one busy branch, and they had so many people waiting for food when I visited, and managed to stay calm and get queue unpacked in few minutes. I feel for you, I don't think I would last a day in place like this.

The location is downstairs from the station. Head towards M&S and take the escalator down, you’ll find it there. It took me a while to figure it out, so I hope this helps anyone in same situation. The service was quick, and they have self-ordering machines. It’s a smaller McDonald’s, not a large one

The service at this restaurant was extremely disappointing. The staff were rude and unprofessional, showing little interest in helping customers. Simple requests were met with attitude, and we felt ignored for most of our visit. There was no apology or effort to improve the situation, which completely ruined the dining experience. Good food means nothing when the service is this poor. I would not recommend this place based on the way customers are treated.

Just placed an online order from this establishment and have even reported it to uber eats. I bought 2 muffins and pancake with 1 extra sausage and this is what they sent me. Feed your workers mate this is disgusting. Who ever made my order should be ashamed. Never ordering or eating from here. The Tropicana they gave me is  expired 2 months ago

Came here today, haven’t been here in years and it’s changed a lot. Food was ok I actually got two slices of cheese in my quarter pounder lol however when me and my family tried to find a table, they were all dirty and needed cleaning. There were two staff members laughing around they ran off into the staff area. I then saw who looked like a senior member of staff and she was assisting the ambulance staff as a customer had collapsed outside the toilet area. She insisted that it was her job and she apologised wiped it down and later walked past to insure I was happy. Wasn’t happy that the place was such a mess and staff wasn’t acting on it. I will go there again soon as I’ve just started working in the area. I hope the new chicken range of burgers are nice!!

The truth is that this service is working very badly. I ordered breakfast in the morning and I had to wait like 10 minutes. The people who work here are very slow and then the The truth is that even my hunger has gone away, it's going very slowly, how disgusting.

"Where do I even begin?

From the moment I walked in, it was a disaster. The so-called "food" was an insult—cold, stale chips that tasted like they were fried yesterday, paired with a sad, cupboard-dry burger. And while I’m standing there with a clear four-person order, the staff had the audacity to argue about ketchup portions. Seriously?

The coffee? Ice cold. Unacceptable for a place right beside Victoria Station, in the heart of the city. You’d expect basic competence, but no.

Then came the disability toilet fiasco. I asked for the key, only to be met with pointless arguing before finally being told it was "under renovation." Why wasn’t there a sign? A simple notice would’ve saved everyone the hassle. Lazy and inconsiderate.

And let’s talk about the disgusting state of the men’s toilet—some guy was literally having a body wash in there. I alerted the staff, and their response? "Okay, we heard you." No action, no urgency. Pathetic.

The only redeeming moment was when two staff members handled another situation professionally—credit where it’s due. But that doesn’t excuse the rest of this shambolic experience.

I keep telling myself, "Never again." But in some areas, they’re the only option—and they know it. That’s why standards don’t improve. Avoid unless you enjoy disappointment."

Went to this place to grab some food on the way back home. When opened my big mac omg it looked ridiculous,nothing like it. To get my money back I need to travel all the way back to restaurant. Never again. Please avoid this place, it's a joke.

I ordered from a voucher, which is not a usual order and they forgot some items, when I came back the next day to ask about it they redid my entire order to say sorry. The worker and managers I spoke to about it were all so polite about the whole thing. Clearly a good group of people working here 😊

Some people here really need to remember that it’s okay to make mistakes, it’s more important how the restaurant responds to a mistake
I had my dinner tonight and it was amazing. The guy who took my order, Shakil was very humble, he told me he just started but he looked very professional. Don't hesitate to visit there.

Was ok, located very near to kings cross station so thought id go, ordered mc crispy large meal. Burger was good, but some of the fries were cold and on the tray like theyd just thrown them on, box didnt seem full either. Ordered a sprite and was given a coke, not sure how they messed that up. Also no seating downstairs.

This location tends to be quite busy. 🚶‍♂️The security personnel appeared to be stressed, which may affect the overall experience. 😬 It is advisable to adjust expectations accordingly. 🤷‍♀️ The service was adequate for a takeaway order. 🍟

Esther, was absolutely amazing. After a really stressful day her service and attention to detail really meant the world. After losing my train ticket she had personally kept it in hopes it would return to its owner, I have never been so grateful.

All in all it was an amazing experience and a friendly in counter with a member of hospitality (Esther) who went above and beyond.

Thank you very much

Too busy for own good.
I made a quick pitstop on the evening (Sunday evening).
Queue took 10 minutes.
Finding a seat was closed to impossible.
It was noisy and definitely not relaxing place to eat.

Veru busy and big spot. and no wonder, considering where is it based. Lots of space downstairs to seat and it was also clean event it was full of customers. Some old lady was working there as a cleaner, she was so polite and thanking people who give her rubbish, it made me sad.

Food was good, I got much bigger portion of chips than expected, burger was ok too, standard for MC, and I even got a bit of crunch from gherkin.

We came today with friends and ordered over £30 worth of food. Unfortunately, one friend, who has several allergies, only ordered a drink and dessert and brought her own salad for the main course. The restaurant refused to serve us because one of us had McDonald's food and another type of food. This restaurant is best avoided; the staff are not at all friendly. The place looks clean, but as soon as you touch anything, it leaves fingerprints.

This is in a very busy location however at lunch time on Saturday we found seating easily, ordered via the app and had food promptly. All the customisations on our burgers were adhered too which is rare!

Ideal location for a McDonalds as it is near the theatres. We couldn't find anywhere to have a hot drink prior to going to the theatre so we opted for McDonald's.  It turned out to be a good choice. Ample seating, clean and tidy restaurant, friendly staff, clean toilets. The hot chocolate was nice as was the festive apple pie.

This McDonald’s was absolutely packed with people looking for somewhere to sit and eat. But, lo and behold, the downstairs seating area was closed, resulting in people having to stand and eat. Staff seemed unfazed.

Furthermore, the kitchen got our order wrong. Initially, no drink was served and staff had to be prompted before it was supplied. Upon starting to eat, we realised the extra ingredients we’d paid for were not present on the burgers. We were not the only patrons to experience this; while waiting for our order to be prepared, we witnessed two other patrons complain about incorrect orders at the pick-up counter.

Steer clear of this McDonalds branch if you value your money and a pleasant eating experience. Genuinely the worst McDonald’s I’ve ever had the displeasure of experiencing.

Tried the new World Menu Heist. The Japanese Black Pepper & Garlic chicken bites were tasty and the dipping sauce was great. The Sakura Sprite, however, was quite plain. The restaurant was very busy, so the queue was long. Also, the staff forgot to include my sauces initially but provided them when I asked.

Food was average, my burger wasn't the greatest i've had from Mc, lots of salad, meat didn't have much flavour, I would say weaker from what I usually get, which is weird cos this brand is quite consistent.

Quick service and they were helping one customer with order's mistake, and being polite about it.

I like sitting area downstairs. A little ran down, so that adds character and surprisingly very clean.

The staff at this Mcdonald’s were incredibly heroic. We were in a group in there after midnight when another customer got incredibly violent, threatening and chasing people, spraying alcohol solution into the workers eyes, fighting, tearing apart apart the shop etc. The staff tried their hardest to protect everyone and stop anyone getting hurt. They reacted calmly trying to defend themselves and others and de-escalate the situation despite how stressed and scared they must have been. They reacted by prioritising other people’s safety above their own, and I’m so grateful for them in that situation. I wish I could mention the worker by name, who eventually managed to pin and stop the man prior to police even arriving. Thank you guys so much for everything, we all appreciate you so much!

Had an Arches Burger meal. What a burger. Tastes a whole tier above the Big Mac or Big Tasty or Quarter Pounder. Sofisticated. The meal was ready quickly and put together nicely. The place is clean and staff atentive. Music is nice enough people whistle along it.

Well, it's McDonald's. Restaurant is closed upstairs, so seating is in the basement, and it felt a tad dull/ gloomy. The McMuffins were a little different from back home, the 'buns' were small, and the patties bigger.. but taste wise it's ok/ standard.
As for the Bacon & cheese flat bread, it was great value for £1.99. Unlike back home, condiments are chargeable, but not a surprise.

First of all there were items missing - the ketchup and promotional grinch socks we selected. The coke must've been knocked in the bag resulting in the food soaked. The driver upon arrival had the bag placed on the wet pavement. He just said dunno, raise a complaint. The nuggets were soaked so they had to be thrown away. £30 wasted and no dinner.

Ordered 2 Mayo Chicken at 241 City Road with extra lettuce 🥬—got none. Staff casually said “we don’t have lettuce.” No refund, no solution, just wasted food in the bin. Terrible service and zero accountability. 👎

I am genuinely appalled by the service at McDonald’s Islington.

My friend and I placed a Deliveroo order at 12:00am. After THREE HOURS of waiting — at 3am — we were still unable to cancel the order. Not wanting a driver to turn up while we were asleep, we walked to the restaurant in our pyjamas, when we should have been in bed, just to collect food we had already paid for.

When we arrived, our order was already made and sitting on the side with dozens of other bags, clearly left there for ages. No temperature control. No urgency. No surprise that McDonald’s deliveries always arrive cold and stale — I saw exactly why. We had ordered McFlurries, which by that point were obviously melted and ruined.

The hygiene was also concerning — staff handling food without gloves — but the worst part was the manager’s behaviour.

When we politely asked for our order and showed:
• the order number
• the order confirmation
• the pin code on our phone

the manager flat-out refused to give us the food. No explanation beyond “the driver has to collect it.” We even offered a perfectly reasonable solution: take a photo of our pin code and give it to the driver when they arrive. He refused again.

Instead, he suggested we buy another meal and pay again, while our original order sat there going to waste.

That is beyond unacceptable.

In the end, we never received our order at all. No food. No resolution. Just wasted time, wasted money, and appalling customer service.

This experience left me angry, frustrated, and exhausted at 3am — all for food we had already paid for and could literally see sitting there.

I will not be returning, and I strongly advise others to avoid ordering delivery from this location.
Convenient pit stop! ⛽️🍔 Great location next to the petrol pump, with ample parking. Food was decent, classic McDonald's taste. Perfect spot for a quick bite on the go.

This restaurant is a bad service people they dont do their jobs right. If they can't do there work right then they need to get rid of them and get people that can do the job. when ordering food they dont listen to you but they are quick to take your money. It comes to a point where they just get customers angry for no reason if they dont their job right then there wouldn't been a problem but sometime the workers makes problem like these to get customers upset and angry. It is not nice. Even when the customer spends the last money they had to feed people and then they dont get the order wrong and then they want to take more money of you to get the order right.that is not nice thats bad people. And also there is always missing item in the bag they quick to take your money but dont give your food.

Visited this McDonald’s at 25/27 Shaftesbury Ave with my family during a London trip. My 8-year-old urgently needed the toilet. There was a sign saying the toilets were closed, which I understand can happen. I asked a staff member named Hong C for clarification. She did not stop, did not look at me, and walked away saying “no toilet” while continuing upstairs.

The issue was not the toilet being closed. It was the complete lack of basic courtesy. No explanation, no acknowledgement, no attempt to speak to a parent with a child in need. We left feeling embarrassed and unwelcome. This is not acceptable customer service, especially in a busy central London location that serves families.

I normally don't bother leaving bad reviews but people like this who have no empathy keeps my blood boil.

Did the job!
Surprisingly quick service even though it was packed.

Couldn't use the mcdonald app here though sadly...

Today, October 2nd, I entered this branch at 8pm and waited for 20 minutes for a simple coffee. There were only three people in that branch during rush hour and it's strange why this branch is so understaffed.

Terrible service. Manager and workers were not accommodating for strollers. They do not even have elevator to have patrons sit whom have small children. Do not recommend if you are traveling with kids and want to sit down and eat your food. Other sit down areas are far away from McDonald's and are high tops. This location needs to do better. 0/10 rating to how the situation was handled and lack of customer service.

Probably the fastest service I've ever experienced. Was in and out in about 2 minutes with a full meal and the staff worker who handled my order was very nice. Would recommend for a quick bite in Piccadilly!

Nice place to stop for breakfast. Not busy on my visit. Clean and tidy, well maintained. There were seats on my visit at 7.30am. Food and coffee was good, served quickly too. As with most McDonald's restaurants, the printer failed on the touchscreen with my order. This is extremely common at McDonald's as the staff are busy serving customers. The whole ordering process is delayed by this and should be addressed.

It was complete chaos inside this McDonald's. The self-order machines weren’t working properly — they didn’t even print receipts, so nobody knew which order belonged to whom. It was really disorganized, and I had to wait almost half an hour just to get an ice cream. That’s definitely not what I expect from McDonald’s, especially for something so simple. There were too many people running around, and no clear structure or coordination. Honestly, it was a very disappointing experience.

I went today for my lunch it was totally disappointed and disaster. The table were not cleaned and there was a lot of mess on the floor as well and another thing is they missed my burger and i went there again . It was my break time so they ruined my resting time as well . This branch is always busy so they have to put more staff on busy days especially in London. Very disappointed but food was good

Bigshout out to a lady who shouts order numbers. If she does it whole day, this is a killer job for a soul. And throat.

Very fast service for such a busy place, they have a good crew there.

Food was ok, it’s Mac. Fries arrived a bit cold and we know what it means in Mc, not that great. Burger was ok, kept up with standards.

Clean too and from what I gather lots of seats downstairs and upstairs.

Kinda dystopian vibe as many places in London now.

I recently visited McDonald’s and ordered a chicken wrap, but was disappointed to receive a bacon chicken wrap instead. As someone who doesn’t eat bacon, this was really frustrating. It’s important that orders are prepared correctly, especially when dietary preferences or restrictions are involved. I hope the staff can be a bit more careful moving forward. Mistakes happen, but attention to detail goes a long way.

I’ve always been a fan of McDonald’s. I don’t eat there very often but when I do it always hits the spot. You always know exactly what you’re getting. Unfortunately that doesn’t seem to be the case with this establishment. The chips were stale and tasteless. They had clearly either not been cooked correctly or had been sitting around for ages before being served. The cheeseburger wasn’t great either.

Extremely disappointing. For the first time in my life, I didn’t finish a McDonald’s meal.

Unbelievable this is medium size fries...Even I told the staff right away when I open the bag. Come on... I bet it's smaller than original small fires. Never ever go back again.. Btw, all the pos won't print out the receipt, I don't even have a chance to complain to customer service.

One of the friedliest stuff in all Mc's in London. I was greeted with smile, and I didn't look fake.

No sitting so taken a spot next to window, bit dirty though. Burger was above average for this chain, and they didn't save on ketchup. Very hot chips, always a welcomed addition.

Overall the best Mc I've visited so far. Shame there is no seats, and only few outside but I understand the limitations.

One of the staff member (the one who is gay(respectfuly))was really rude to me while taking the order. I placed the order for medium meal but then asked for large meal price. He said i have already pressed medium next time say large very rudely and loudly infront of other people which was really insulting. There could be a nice way of saying it! really disappointed!

Went yesterday ordered a Mac chicken my parents got two Big Mac and medium fries walked back home like 5 minutes reached I got a stomach pain and went to the toilet couldn’t sleep all night then I vommited my entire body out even though I saw the bad reviews I still ignored pls don’t do my mistake unless you want to feel sick or even what happened to me💔😭🤢🤢🤮🥴
I am from Madrid and I have traveled half the world and I have never seen a Mc Donalds more spacious, so clean and well organized. Congratulations, impressive work. I will be back

On 2 Big Macs , one was made with selection- no onions and pickles but in real it was with. The order was made through McDonalds kiosk, first, by the entrance- could not accept application code and no receipt. I could not check and proof that I ordered Apple pie, which was missing. Thanks to very kind, helpful lady , being around, perfectly doing her job.  The mistake was corrected by her in minutes. (Got the missing Apple pie)
PS. I could not spot her name while ahead worked between us eating 🙂

lovely mcdonald’s, very spacious and very clean inside, heaps of tables staff and quick and polite, food was very good tastes the same as australia, mcdonald’s is mcdonald’s end of the day, however there is a very serious issue. no straw with drink😡 other wise very good experience, the fact that the sauce comes in a little tub instead of a packet is incredible 😊😊😊

The manager Mohcen is absolutely fantastic! I was going here during my lunch breaks in December. When the app and the self-service kiosks were down he really helped me use my offers exactly how I wanted and with no hesitation. I also ordered a lot for my team on a busy day (about 40 burgers plus more) and he and his team had them out promptly, organised and great quality! Cannot praise them enough :)

My go-to lunch spot. Come here regularly for a quick lunch and it's a really busy branch right by St Paul's Cathedral, especially during the lunch time rush so I try to go after 2pm, otherwise it's a long wait for your food. The tables aren't always clean but it's understandable and the staff are very busy. There's also a lot of seating, but I prefer not to sit on the lower section as the WiFi never works.

My favourite McDonald’s in a while world!
Fast, clean, friendly.
Amazing customer experience!
Hard working staff and good location.

Within a ten minute walk are three other McDonald's. The more recent reviews are presumably for one of them, probably Cambridge Circus.

I’d give this McDonald’s in London a solid 4 stars. The place was busy but moved fast, food came out hot, and everything tasted exactly how you expect, which is sometimes all you want. Staff were efficient, not overly chatty, but polite enough to keep things smooth. A bit more seating space would make it better, yet overall it did the job well.

Take out only. Very expensive rent I’m sure so they have no seating. Very disappointing but then most people here getting on trains so we understand.

Food was good and served hot, exactly what you’d expect from McDonald’s. There’s only takeaway available at this location, and the delivery/takeaway time was a bit longer than usual due to how crowded it was. Still, a satisfying experience overall.

I order takeaway from this McDonald’s often and everything is always spot on. The food was hot, fresh, and prepared exactly as requested. Fries were perfectly salted and still crispy by the time I got where I was going. The Big Mac tasted just like it should classic, juicy and satisfying. Service was super quick, and the staff at the counter were efficient. However, since it’s a face paced resturant and there is no dining in, sometimes the staff rush too much and don’t take the time to give you basic necessities such as tissue or sauce. Before you can even ask they have dropped your food and left.

Ordered the soy garlic nuggets, got given normal nuggets after a long wait…
Can see the soy garlic tab pushed down next to the beige nugs

McDonald's in London bridge.
This is one of the good McDonald's. Food always cooked well and chips not to soggy, like some McDonald's.
Always stop here and grab a cheeky meal when waiting on train.

They have self-service machines.

Very bad service. I am waiting for food for half an hour though food already ready.We asked for food but they did misbehave with me,they are also rude to me. We sat down and were quickly harassed by a staff member to write a 5 star review on the mcdonald’s app. She took my phone forcefully and started writing a review. She continued to harass our table to write reviews and this was extremely uncomfortable and unpleasant of an experience, this has never happened in another mcdonald’s to me before and was really stressful.

Ordered McDonald’s from Commercial Road to our hotel via Deliveroo late at night. Big mistake. Huge. The restaurant is roughly 5 minutes away, yet the food arrived at 4:46am, over an hour after ordering. By the time it showed up, we’d aged emotionally and spiritually.
When we finally took the food upstairs, we discovered the bag was soaking wet. Not “a little damp.” I mean biblical flood levels. Turns out the Fanta Orange had been placed upside down in the bag (bold choice), exploded everywhere, and marinated the entire order. It also soaked through onto the hotel’s white bedding, making it look like someone had absolutely lost control of their bladder. Explaining that to hotel staff should be fun. Loved that for us.
The chips were beyond saving — completely inedible, like they’d been dredged from a puddle and then insulted. The Big Mac was somehow both cold and soggy, with the texture of wet cardboard. I took one bite and genuinely thought I’d eaten packaging. Still not convinced I didn’t. I took one bite and immediately regretted every life decision that led me there.
The “sharing” chicken nuggets were the only survivors, and even that’s generous. They tasted like oranges. Not chicken — oranges. A flavour profile I wasn’t emotionally prepared for.
The Coke? Pure syrup. Thick, sweet, and tasted like it was made from the leftover dregs no one else wanted. Honestly impressive how bad it was.
Everything was stone cold, soaking, and borderline traumatic. One of the worst takeaway experiences I’ve ever had. If you’re hungry late at night, I’d recommend literally anything else — including sleep, fasting, or chewing the hotel pillows (they were drier).
Would not order again. Ever.

I went to McDonald's to try their new Philly Cheese menu item, but unfortunately, it didn't quite hit the spot for me. The flavors were okay, but it just didn't live up to the hype. Maybe I was expecting something more authentic to the classic Philly cheesesteak vibe, but this just felt like another burger with some cheese sauce. Definitely wouldn't go out of my way for it again. Maybe give it a shot and see what you think, but for me, it was just meh.

One thing that most customers don't pay attention to is the amount of money they pay compared to the amount of service or the food quality they receive.

I paid less than eight pounds for two.  That is less than four pounds per person.  Considering this amount, I was very satisfied with the food I ate.  I enjoyed the environment in which I sat.  The fact that I didn't wash my dishes, I didn't pay for energy, and I didn't even clear my table myself, the amount of four pounds is so great!

It seems to me that even though they only pay four pounds for a meal, they expect the service of a luxury Italian restaurant in the city center.  While even the most luxurious restaurants in the city cannot be perfect in providing services.

Anyway, my suggestion for someone who doesn't have enough time to cook and is looking for a place to sit and eat after a long shopping and most importantly doesn't want to pay a lot for it would definitely be McDonald's.

Hello,

About 20 minutes ago, I ordered a Cheesy McCrispy menu for takeaway and asked for a couple of sauces for my fries. I simply asked for ketchup and barbecue, but she kept saying things like “you need to pay for this or that” while continuing to chat with her colleagues, barely acknowledging me.

Unfortunately, when I got home, I realized that my order was completely wrong. There’s really just one thing that needs to be done right — preparing the correct order. I was hungry and tired, but when I tried to eat what I received, I could barely take two bites because the taste was completely off.

Sadly, I often experience issues with this branch. When ordering through Just Eat, the food usually arrives cold, and even when I pay extra for sauces, they’re often missing. Drinks are always spilled. When visiting the store, I encounter a different set of problems like this.

After being a local worker in the area I visited the nearest McDonald and had a highly disappointing experience at the Archway location due to poor service quality and lack of professionalism. Despite more than seven employees being on shift, the staff showed little urgency in fulfilling orders. I placed and paid for my order at 12:31 AM, yet I did not receive my food until 12:57 AM—an unacceptable delay for a fast-food establishment.

When I finally received my order, the food was cold and my sauce was missing despite it being clearly listed on my receipt. To make matters worse, my hot beverage was also cold and had been left unattended while a staff member handled cash from other customers before returning to add cream—raising serious concerns about hygiene standards.

Despite informing the staff that I needed to return to work, they ignored my request and prioritised serving customers who had ordered after me. Meanwhile, multiple employees were gathered in the kitchen laughing and joking, rather than focusing on the growing queue of hungry customers.

This experience was completely unacceptable and it is a disgrace that such poor service and hygiene practices are allowed to continue. Management needs to take immediate action to improve staff training, efficiency, and overall customer care. I would not recommend this location to anyone expecting a professional and timely service.

If the option was available to put below one stars, this is exactly what I would rate this location

Had a very unpleasant experience while purchasing food from MC Donald's Holloway road. One of their managers was very rude after him making a mistake on my order, this something very simple to correct.

I would have understood his spontaneous departure from myself if he was either young and possibly inexperienced, or if he was a crew member and ad gone to get a manager.

If you care at all about food hygiene, safety, or basic human decency, avoid this location (and service) at all costs.

⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️

I’ve always had a great experience at McDonald’s! The food is consistently tasty, served hot and fresh, and the staff are usually friendly and quick to take and prepare orders. Macdonalds Westfield  Review

Better than usual

i order food from uber eats and i receive my drink open lid and less than half cola inside. I spent 10£ and the service wasn’t good.Im not gonna order again.

Visited with my partner since we were doing some shopping in the area. Despite going in at busy hours we still managed to get a table. Terrible service always. So unorganised. Food is always cold. Wraps are full of sauce too much . Attitude if people working there is that they cannot be bothered. Delivery drivers always get looked after first. No management of place everyone just wanders around with no specific role.

It is a disgrace that this restaurant "McDonald's® - Wood Green" sent a ridiculous amount of salad via home delivery service when the order was for a Large Quarter Pounder™ with cheese. Two chicken burgers ,I ordered a six-piece chicken nuggets meal and extra large chips. I was going to take it and leave. After my order was prepared, I was given a package. When I checked, I noticed that there were no large chips and I told the cashier.

If you’re looking for slow service and for your order to come out slow no matter how quiet the restaurant is come to this McDonalds. I ordered online my food come cold and my drink was open and messed up everything the driver had planned everything properly as I saw it when picking up my food a £50 order was thrown on the bin. Fris was so cold that you couldn't enjoy same as the food.

I went to McDonald's on Tottenham today, and there was a football match and it was very full. But I was really impressed with how the stay helped 4 different elderly and disabled people that were seated around me, helping them get their food, making sure they were OK even though it was so busy.

During my recent vacation, I stayed close to this location. I found myself preferring the London McDonald's menu over the Canadian one. I enjoyed a chicken box with 12 nuggets, 6 tenders, and 4 dips, along with a large fries. Overall, the food brought me comfort and satisfaction.

I just made an order for my children and as we get home, we realise there is no 2large fries in our order. I’ve called several times but no response. I ordered a happy meal for my nephew and a hamburger save your menu, they put two hamburgers in a paper bag and gave me two bottles of water but I didn't have my fries or the toy, and the happy meal wasn't in its normal box When I saw boxes (there was no stock problem). Spend some happy hours reviewing a film made by Tottenham residents. Enjoy a singing session with friends.
