Below average as McDonald's here is always dirty,  bins overflowing. Lots of youths, teens loiter inside without buying food. Staff say or do nothing. Orders usually incorrect.  Staff belligerent

SHIFT MANAGER PRIYA. SO RUDE. Absolutely appalling experience. The shift manager who served us was rude, condescending, and openly disrespectful from the moment we ordered. Her attitude was hostile and dismissive, as if customers were an inconvenience rather than the reason she has a job.

When we raised a concern about her poor attitude, she rolled her eyes at us and responded with unnecessary sarcasm. At one point, when I tried to diffuse the situation by saying, “You must be tired,” she snapped back, “No, I’m not,” followed by, “You’ve got served, haven’t you? What’s your problem?” This is completely unacceptable behavior for anyone in customer service—especially someone in a managerial position.

I won’t be returning to this location, and I strongly suggest management retrains their staff on basic respect and customer service.

We raised this concern with management and requested that duties be adjusted to avoid further issues, but no action was taken. We hope management takes rider concerns seriously and improves staff professionalism.

Ordered through the drive way, half my order was forgotten. They claim they're 24/7. Went there on 20th December, around 4AM. Door was closed already won't let in any new customers.

The food is nice and tasty. Only problem I have is that there are accessible toilets downstairs that I do not believe are just for the staff as it also says baby change. And upstairs in those toilets are no baby change. When I asked a staff member to use the toilet downstairs, they said to go upstairs. And the reason I asked you to downstairs toilets, because of convenience I have issues which make it harder for me to walk up the stairs. Now I'm sure people have used those toilets like, for example, if you're on a wheelchair, as this McDonald's doesn't actually have a lift. So I feel like that was unfair for them to say. The sanitary towel bins are in a state and need a proper wipe down and clean. The food is nice though and the tables and everything are usually clean

Ordered delivery. Shocked to see carton of large fries less than half full. Just Eat point blank dismissed my complaint so I will never use them again. Who could honestly think that was an acceptable portion at this branch? The rest of my order was ok. Very disappointed with this E17 branch.

Customers service is good and they always come to offer help . My son and I love walthamstow branch because of Naga and there is also a nice guy that serves us anytime we sit upstairs on Saturdays.  Naga is a brilliant worker, we really like her service and she is always neat too

Just had a fab time at McDonald's with the little ones! 🍟👦

I went for the Wrap, it was tasty and filling 👍.
Great value for money too. Service was spot on, as always. Would recommend for a quick and easy family meal ☺️we will be back as we everytime opt for Mc fries and meals!

For a fast-food restaurant McDonald’s offers a surprisingly wide menu. From breakfast sandwiches to chicken meals and desserts there’s something for almost everyone. While the food quality can vary by location most visits are smooth with quick service and clean dining areas.

Wow what canni say about this superb cafe , siting just off the m20 on the a20 , using great local produce the sausages were fantastic, the food perfect .good cappuccino and ample parking , wheel chair access and good local history as to why the place is named the airport cafe is due to the local lympne airfield once home to the road flying corps from 1912 and saved the 1st world war and the second .staff were awesome and super clean environment. I thoroughly enjoyed my food and a little local history. The cafe has a truck stop attached and local car meets and motorists to include motorcyclists attend frequently and enjoy the quirky atmosphere this cafe supplies .

The food was quite fast and quite hot. You could almost call it fast food tbh.  It really reminded me of every other McDonalds I've been to in my life. I've been to countless McDonalds venues and they've been remarkably similar, well, this one didn’t disappoint.  There are numerous ways the team here could have spoiled the experience and they did not.  It was a very clean venue, and not too busy.

I always check my order when getting a takeaway as they almost nearly always forget to put part of the order I've come to expect it. But these clowns took it to a new level they actually forgot to put the burgers in the bun so I got home to a salad bun , I could understand if it was busy but it had more staff than customers probably because people realise they are eating chemicals how else do you explain those fries and can someone explain what that egg is made of in their breakfast that has never even seen a chicken.

If I could give zero stars I would. Have ordered via Uber eats several times and every time the food turns up stone cold and stale chips, despite being less than 10 minutes away. Won't bother again, waste of money
The food is convenient and budget-friendly, but quality can vary by location. When it’s fresh it’s really satisfying; when it’s not, it’s just okay.

Ordered food of just eat. Ordered 3 double cheese burgers on the buy 1 get 1 free deal. Only 3 came! Received coke instead of a strawberry milkshake. The oasis was so watered down it was undrinkable. T
He fries were half filled.

This was fine dining at its best. The food was so delicious. They must have gourmet chefs. Took the family for a meal out and this was sublime. The quality and taste was another level. The staff were great. This is a must place to take the family out for a great meal

I usually don’t expect fine dining at McDonald’s, but this visit really annoyed me. I ordered a Quarter Pounder meal and had to wait nearly 20 minutes not because it was busy, but because the staff seemed disorganised and distracted.

When I finally got my food, the burger was lukewarm, the cheese wasn’t melted, and the fries were soggy and way too salty.

The tables weren’t very clean also the tables were sticky, trash bins were overflowing, and there were napkins and straw wrappers on the floor.

I understand fast food isn’t gourmet, but basic cleanliness, fresh food, and decent service should still be expected. Not planning on returning to this location anytime soon.
Bought a double cheeseburger after seeing the picture, looked really good. Reality not as seen on menu.

On walking in to mcdonald's you could see from looking around the place need a good clean it was dirty ! Wasn't holding much hope for the food wasn't  any better ! When you have staff on the mobile phones on the unit serving food is disgusting think you need retaining! Chips was cold and tasted like been sat there most the night ! Mcdonald's need to send there staff on customer training as there was one blonde haired girl that thought it was funny to talk about me to two members of staff each one making it blatantly obvious they where taking about me so let's talk about how you need to learn some customer service skills cause it was rude and unprofessional behaviour ! Six missing items from the order this morning. Quite obvious when the delivery came that the bag wasn't heavy enough. Ordered because of a busy day. Customer service chat on the app not working properly either. I had to cook my own hashbrowns for my kids.

Placed an order with amendments to the Big Mac meals both were ignored completely. Missed out 4 burgers from the order too. What's the point in it specifically being a delivery kitchen if it's worse than ordering from a regular McDs

Don't bother getting delivery from this place food is cold half my order missing and a refund does not solve the fact that I now have to go to the shop as 2 of my children have no food. Terrible. Avoid at all costs. Just go and pick the food up yourself. Food took an hour and a half to arrive. When it did, it was stone cold and the drinks had spilt everywhere. Also the driver was rude when I wanted to check the order was correct. Never again

Chips are always cold. Often food is missing., always forget sauce.

Currently sat in the newly refurbished branch and it is shocking. Waited 20 minutes for food to come out cold and missing items. No staff in sight and absolutely freezing in here with no heating on! Disgusting staff attitude! I went to order a simple happy meal but the voice machine didn’t work so after 5 cars had passed me I went round to order at the desk, everything was fine, I get to the bay and they passed me my food then a girl came and snatched it out of my hands saying it’s not mine and to wait in the bay…. Absolutely atrocious, staff very incompetent and consistently miss items from orders or get orders wrong altogether, waiting ages to be serve as the staff prioritise online order (uber eats and just eat) over walk in customers! Always missing the ice, meat always burnt, unfortunately after so many complaints I am still not happy with the way they deal with all these situation, the biggest problem is that they advertise the new meat as being better and in my case 9 out of ten was burnt and missing the salt and pepper.

Click and collect order - when I got home we noticed all the fries of the meals were burnt! More like crisps than just snap not a chip. Worst maccys i’ve been to. Always getting my order wrong or they hand it to me flipping cold and stale they can’t operate properly. I never write reviews, but the past two times i ordered from here on Just Eat the first time i they gave me a completely different order after i paid an extra 10 pounds on my order. When ordering through uber eats dont expect the sauce youve paid for or straws for your drinks and they never put them in the bag. Every time we've ordered this happens.

No way to complaint in the app.

Extremely disappointing experience. The staff showed a complete lack of understanding about food intolerances — I dread to think how someone with allergies would have been treated. The team were rude and unhelpful, and the overall service was careless.

I certainly won’t be returning. A real shame, as it could have been so easily avoided with a bit of care and attention.

Just had a uber eat arrive !all food was cold and took over 45 mins to arrive ! When I told driver he just laughed - I couldn't eat my food as cold and not appetising went in the bin ! Asked fr extra onion on my burger didnt get any at all looked like it had been thrown around the restaurant tbh wouldn't feed to my dogs ! Fries medium more like a small Fast food is fast food not 45mins to an hour ! They don't even wear gloves ive noticed in the solihull branch - disgusting now I've no dinner to eat thanks

Ordered a 20-piece Chicken McNuggets meal from McDonald’s via Deliveroo and I couldn’t believe what I found when I opened the box — one of the nuggets had already been bitten into. Not broken or damaged during delivery — actually bitten. Totally unacceptable.

The box looked sealed from the outside, so I don’t even know where this happened — at the restaurant or during delivery — but either way, this is a massive hygiene and food safety issue.

It’s honestly disgusting and made me question what’s going on behind the scenes. I’ve attached photos as proof — this isn’t just a missing item, it’s tampered food.

Really disappointed and shocked. This needs to be looked into properly. Not okay at all.

Disappointed with Portion Sizes and Service

My recent visit to the Solihull branch left a lot to be desired. First, the self-order machines weren’t printing receipts - a basic but important function, especially if there are issues with an order.

I ordered a medium meal, but the fries looked more like a small portion. The carton was barely filled, and it felt like I got the last scraps from the bottom of the fryer. Not what you'd expect when paying for a medium.

To top it off, I paid an extra 80p for a milkshake, expecting the full 400ml as advertised. Instead, the cup was noticeably underfilled - around 2cm below the line it should have reached.

This isn’t about nitpicking - it’s about getting what you pay for. Please don’t shortchange your customers. I didn’t partially pay, so I shouldn’t receive a partially measured meal.

To make matters worse, there were no tissues available and I had to walk back to the counter to ask for some.

That said, credit where it’s due: the staff were polite and served quickly - 5 stars there. Just a shame the food quality and setup didn’t match the standard of the team.

Honestly an awful experience. We came in at half 9pm and the place was absolutely empty. We ordered through the screen and walked up to wait. The wait wasn't that long but when our drinks came out, we had to ask if we can get the ice because the cups were just warm coke. The lad behind the counter said "oh yeah if you want" which is crazy because of course I want the ice. Our burgers came not long after and as soon as we opened we realised they didnt even have cheese on them. We went back up to ask if we could have the cheese and the lad again said "oh we dont have any". We hadn't even touched the food at this point so we asked if we could get it refunded. The manager came down saying they havent looked properly and handed us 2 slices of cheese which felt like it was straight out the freezer. The burgers were awful, every bite more regrettable than the last one. The chips, inedible. One was rock hard and crumbled right before our eyes. I really wish we just got a refund. For something so automated, how do you mess it up so bad???

I was the only person waiting for food in the restaurant on a Monday evening. It took absolutely ages to get just a cheese burger & a drink. The staff were arguing, swearing at eachother & generally messing around. When I eventually got my food the manager (I think? She was wearing a red tie) shouted my number. I took my food and said thank you. She turned her back on me and continued arguing with her colleagues. There was no thank you or any acknowledgement. Also the food was cold so I have no idea what was going on all that time I waited.

Went here at 7pm on a Thursday. Was told chicken selects werent ready and to wait. Waited 20mins in the car park till I gave up and went inside, to find my order sat on the side behind 3 other orders waiting for them to be brought out to other drivers. No attempts being made to keep the food warm. Tried to use app to complain and virtual assistant was non-responsive. Shocking service. Chaos in the car park with cars waiting blocking people in to the spaces. Avoid if you can.

Only : cars in front at the drive in but still had to wait for my order to be taken. No one behind but still has to re tell them my order (probably the last one she took!) then the food was cold, not luke warm but cold!
Had a good service here, food was great, can't complain. Loaded the burgers 🍔 with extra sauce and got spoilt with 4 gurkins! Polite staff, speedy service and fab food...

Literally the worst McDonald’s there is. Don’t bother ordering from here. They ALWAYS get your order wrong whether you have it delivered or collect it. Ordered a large family meal at 10pm this evening, they didn’t even bother sending it and didn’t even bother cancelling it before they closed at 11pm. Tried calling for 15 minutes straight before they closed only for them to ignore all the calls! What a waste of time.

Great place for night viewing of the sky. Had to leave early though because a large group of teens turned up letting off really loud and dangerous fireworks, spoiled the evening.
Nice walks during the day, but watch out for the dog poo!! Not great for kids and toddlers.

Popped in for some food but have to mention how awesome Harpreet was. She was very attentive keeping the restaurant tidy, very smiley and friendly. She was great at interacting with some children that came in, setting them up with colouring sheets and crayons. Just wanted to make sure she got some recognition for doing a fab job.

Twice in a row my Mcdelivery has been messed up, First time no food just drinks delivered had to drive down to sort out to be offered a Donut for the inconvenience. Second time drinks missing got in touch online with support to be told they would refund and as you guessed hollow words no refund still after 2 weeks. It's not the delivery drivers fault but the incompetent staff at this branch which I will never use again

Ordered via the drive through. The fries were hot, which was good. The Philly burger was moderately warm and I felt that it was way overpriced for what it was. Seemed like a big Mac with some Philly cheese sauce on it. It definitely wasn't worth it. The staff were pleasant enough.

THIS ISNT A REVIEW; THIS IS A VICTIM IMPACT STATEMENT

A haunting glimpse into the collapse of modern society.

​I didn't order McDonald’s; I accidentally initiated a ritual to summon the spirit of Pure Incompetence. I am currently sitting in my kitchen, weeping over a cold nugget and wondering if the staff at Hagley Road actually exist, or if the building is just a sentient warehouse designed to harvest human hope.

​The delivery took so long that my birth certificate has likely expired. I didn't get a meal; I got a time capsule. By the time the bag arrived, I had successfully navigated two mid-life crises and learned how to knit. I’m fairly certain the driver delivered the food via a slow-moving tectonic plate.

​The milkshakes were missing. Again. At this point, I am convinced the Hagley Road "Milkshake Machine" is actually a religious relic that must never be touched by mortal hands. I called the store 15 times. Fifteen. The phone just rings out into a cold, dark void. I suspect the staff are currently using the telephone as a spatula, or perhaps they’ve collectively decided that sound is an intrusive concept they no longer wish to participate in.

​The "Food" (A Forensic Tragedy)
​The Fries: These weren't cooked. They were waterboarded. They have the structural integrity of a damp prayer and the flavor of a rainy Tuesday in 1994.
​The Burgers: It looks like the chef tried to assemble it using only his forehead. It’s not a sandwich; it’s a cry for help. It’s a flat, tragic disc of disappointment that has clearly lost its will to live.
​The Overall Aesthetic: "Crime Scene."

​Conclusion
​This branch couldn't coordinate a blinking contest in a mirror. They are absolute, gold-plated, artisanal muppets. If you gave these people a box of matches and a gallon of petrol, they’d somehow manage to freeze to death.
​I am currently staring at a lukewarm Big Mac that looks like it’s been through a car wash, drinking a glass of my own bitter tears because the fabled "Shake" remains a myth. If you want to pay £25 to be psychologically dismantled by a teenager who doesn't know what a bag is, look no further.

​Hagley Road McDonald’s: Abandon all hope, all ye who enter the drive-thru.

I don't understand why this restaurant is so often negligent with online orders. On August 11th, my order was missing nine McNuggets. Today, August 13th, my online order was short of one Chicken McNuggets burger. Why do such mistakes keep happening? I'm very disappointed.

I go to this McDonald’s a lot of the time as it’s my local one.
Service is great, in forms of drive-thru staff and takeaway.
However, sometimes the staff can mess up orders, in fact one time I had gotten someone else’s order. Also the inside may be a bit dirty but still good nonetheless.
Overall a good McDonald’s to go to.

Was great when it opened but has gone massively downhill. Five out of my last six orders have had items missing. This is using both drive through and delivery. There were mistakes before but this is an insult. It seems like a scam that there's always items missing. Are the workers needing to steal food to feed themselves? or is there a bonus for extra stock they've saved by shortchanging customers on their orders? Either that or the staff are completely incompetent. I won't be using this restaurant again.

Made an order for delivery. Used some of my points i have gained. I got charged for a small order even though I had 4 items free by using the points.
When I did finally receive my order all items in the were COLD, not even luke warm actually COLD.
So disappointing as I only live about 1 mile from store, so it must have left the store cold or luke warm. What a waste of money! I would be really p'd  off. Better quality control needed urgently

This 5 star review is Dedicated to Amy. She was very friendly with me and my friends and was very joyful and even pleased to see us. Thanks Amy keep up the great service to McDonald’s you absolute legend!!!!

Visited this branch at around 3am after a late shift because it’s advertised as 24 hours. I walked there specifically, and when I arrived the lights were on, it looked clean, and even one of the self-order screens was still lit up and ready to go. It genuinely looked open.

However, the doors were completely locked. I tried the handles, rang the bell — nothing. No sign, no explanation, and no indication that “24 hours” actually means drive-thru only.

While I was there, I saw at least four other people also trying to get in, all assuming the same thing — that 24 hours meant the restaurant itself was open. Clearly it’s not just me being confused.

Since I was on foot, I couldn’t use the drive-thru, so I ended up walking home hungry. Really disappointing and misleading. If the restaurant isn’t open to walk-in customers overnight, it should be clearly advertised on the website, Google listing, and signage.

Paulita , Staff was very kind , very helpful in getting me the BEST SERVICE from the point of ordering , to serving giving everything what I needed in my table.

The staff display an alarming degree of arrogance, engaging with drivers in a demeaning and dismissive manner, as though they were subordinates rather than essential partners in the service chain. Furthermore, the manner in which the staff communicate is overtly insulting and unnecessarily hostile, creating a toxic and demoralizing environment for delivery personnel. This conduct undermines operational efficiency and damages the reputation of the establishment.
Equally concerning are the recurring customer complaints regarding the negligent packaging of food. Orders are reportedly handled with carelessness, compromising quality, presentation, and hygiene. Overall, this branch exemplifies poor leadership, inadequate service standards, and a deeply troubling workplace culture that urgently requires corrective intervention.

The next driver accepted got the order it got delivered at 9am and when I placed the order around 8:15, when I looked at my order I checked all the food it was freezing cold by the time it got here and one of the McMuffins were customised (no egg & no cheese + added sausage) my double bacon and egg McMuffin low and behold had egg on it.. Tried calling the store 3 times no answer but I used to work at McDonald’s myself and I know how the phones always get ignored and even cut off when it keeps ringing, can’t even get a refund or anything through the app. It’s ridiculous and won’t be ordering again

Ordered food through Mcdelivery app after a long day out. My 5 year olds happy meal was missing and also 9 chicken nuggets. Getting through to McDonald’s customer service is atrocious, I’m never having a delivery again from McDonald’s. I phoned the store and was told I either had to come to store or get a refund through the app which is impossible! THEN SHE CUT ME OFF!! Food was cold and horrible anyway.

Terrible experience yet again. They keep reheating fries instead of making them fresh, and it’s happened to me multiple times now. On my last visit, the fries were stale and clearly reheated twice. What makes it worse is the manager was present and did nothing – in fact, I think he even told the staff member to do night shift after this. That says a lot about the management here. Very unprofessional and disappointing.

Ordered a Big Mac meal for grand daughter got to her house to discover NO MEAT in Big Mac ( Meal straight in the bin) telephoned branch no response complained to McDonald’s customer services absolute shambles. Offer of a meal voucher for having a Big Mac meal without the meat. Trip out to buy an alternative meal and a waste of fuel and time. Have not purchased from McDonald’s since.

I thought I would review your restaurant today Thursday 18th December. I was delighted too see the restaurant clean and Tidy.  Car park well litter picked. In store I found all tables clean on arrival. Well done 5*****

Bit of a nostalgic one as this was the first McDonald's I ever went to as a child, so was nice to show my kids.

Ordered through the app, found a seat upstairs, food was bought to my table pretty quick. No problems with my order.

Place was fairly clean, to be honest after all these years I surprised it's still there, as the surrounding area has gone through quite a decline.

The Town has been part of my life for years, but never really used this restaurant at all.
I have to say having used table service today i was pleasantly surprised by the service received! The young man with tattoos up his arms is a credit to your business. Hope you know who you are!!! I don't think I've ever encountered such a happy person at work in macdonalds in my life. What a pleasure to hear him, courteous, helpful, friendly and chatting to absolutely everyone who came in. He was particularly nice to a bunch of ladies on the next table who found out they had free icecream! Lol just a nice touch when you have good staff regardless of whether or not your in a deprived area..
The food was banging and very hot, fresh, good.

This is in a good location. I used to go here everyday to get my latte fix. Love the £1.99 deal and l always get the Fillet-O-Fish and fries. There is a disabled toilet to the right of the counter and more toilets upstairs.  There is also more seating upstairs and this is because there is VERY limited seating on the ground floor. This is mostly always clean and the staff are nice too. This is not a drive through and is a High Street branch. I would recommend this branch as l haven't had any bad experiences here in the preceding 22 years. I went here with my family and we a nice time considering we got there an hour before closing time (now 19:00). We got 5 Fillet-O-Fish burgers with fries, 2 vegetable deluxe burgers with fries, a portion of halloumi fries, 3 vanilla milkshakes, a toffee latte, regular latte and 2 double chocolate cookies. The seating upstairs was closed. It was busy considering not much else was open. I have to say that Bal & Leon provided EXCEPTIONAL SERVICE 👏 👌. Leon replaced the milkshakes as the syrup used for the milkshakes was virtually empty in the machine. Michelle was nice too. The staff here today were very good. Thanks.

Burger was absolutely disgusting just sitting in a huge pile of grease I've never had this from mcdonald's before the bottom bun was soaking from it. The restaurant got to busy to complain so I lobbed it in the bin. For some reason there's a security guard just standing on the stairs doing nothing ? Cold fries but at least the coke was decent. Just look at the state of this box

Omg if its true about this places closing deffo need it . Popped in other week for brekfast all you could smell no soon as you walked in was the toilets 🤢🤢🤢. Ordered food and left didnt hang around to sort the order out . Gone down hill over the years compared to what it was like back in the 90s .

Honestly…delicious burger didn’t even have to ask for extra sauce as it looks like chef was feeling generous and squirted all over the thing. If you look closely you can jusssst about see the meat. 😍😍😍!!!

Although waiting abit longer than usual for my meal, expected as there was a lot of people in there Melissa I believe her name was stayed composed and was a very friendly face to speak to. Only had a brief interaction but I wanted to let her know she’s doing amazing and to keep smiling. 😊

⭐☆☆☆☆

Cold food, poor service, and inconsistent standards

I’ve given McDonald’s more than enough chances, but the level of service and food quality continues to disappoint. Time and time again, I’ve received cold fries — not lukewarm, but stone cold — which completely defeats the point of ordering them fresh.

On my most recent visit, I was served a double cheeseburger that was so overcooked it was practically inedible — hard as a brick and clearly left sitting around far too long. It genuinely felt like quality control was non-existent.

What makes matters worse is the inconsistent attitude of the staff. Depending on who’s working and what shift it is, the service ranges from just about acceptable to downright indifferent. There’s no reliability or sense of customer care, which makes it incredibly frustrating.

I expect a basic standard from a global brand like McDonald’s, but sadly, this branch consistently fails to deliver. I won’t be returning unless there’s a serious improvement in both the food and the customer experience.

Iv left review on trip advisor but can’t help but leave one here also , I’m so shocked as sat behind a member off staff (female manager) enjoying my brew and burger , for her to call a male member of staff over and listen to her tell him how terrible he is and how she regretted giving him a position , he was clearly looking and feeling uncomfortable as was I , I couldn’t help my self but defend him and pull her on the fact that she chose to give him a hard time in front of paying customers the dinning area was empty she could of called him to a quite area but instead chose to rudely tell him off in front of paying customers , not only him but rudely told a poor girl to wait and continued to talk down to the male member of staff not only in front of me and my partner but in front of another staff member , when I pulled her and I rudely corrected her , the response was it’s fine he’s my brother in law NO love it’s not fine , in my honest a true opinion she likes the sound of her own voice looks down a lower staff members needs to be either demoted or sent on a genuine corse to correct her management strategy…

I can’t fault the brews and burgers here but as for her very disappointed speak to those as u wish to be spoken too I genuinely enjoyed embarrassing her as she genuinely needed to feel embarrassed as that poor member of staff must of felt .. shocking management skill I can guarantee that the staff there must feel uncomfortably with how she presents her self to those

The new grinch meal…
Who decided to create such a menu? First off, the grinch lemonade was not even a lemonade but rather it was overly sour, full of sugar and a nasty aftertaste of apple. Secondly, the seasoning is probably the worst out of the whole grinch menu. Who on earth makes pickle seasoning and places it on fries? IT DIDNT TASTE GOOD. There was a way to make this taste better and you somehow failed?! Actually disgusting I’m so disappointed since the seasoning packets are new to the UK yet you still failed to make it taste good. Thirdly, the menu itself cost £10 for this atrocity. If this is a menu for limited time and costs more, I expect more quality and quantity to be served to engage more customers yet you failed to make it taste good, look good and did not even provide the ‘odd socks’ that has the main spotlight of this menu. I am seriously disappointed that I did not get the socks nor that the food that I was so excited to try tasted disgusting in every way. Please review your new menus before you sell them.

So I tried out the new grinch Christmas menu and had the grumble pie and the mischief mclfurry. The mclfurry was made with crushed smarties( plenty of them) and topped with a nice strawberry sauce. I'm not sure what the grumble pie was all about, I couldn't tell what the ingredients were just from tasting them. For me there was too many unknown flavours in the pie. Made me 🤢 at one point.

Kalim, Muzamil, Nouman, Angad, Samantha, Raj, Tim, Anisa, Minal, Raj Hothi, Fatima, Sam, Mady & all the other staff were all so lovely & polite today!! Really helped us all out especially with Simon & Frankie ❤️🙏🏼

I wish you all a lovely blessed new year!!

Kindest felt regards,

Dimitri Dara Singh Cheema :)

I placed an order via the McDonald’s app this afternoon and paid for delivery. Instead of the fajita wrap I expected, I received just two small pieces of dry chicken inside a tortilla wrap (see attached photo and receipt).

This is completely unacceptable and very disappointing. To make matters worse, when I attempted to contact the store directly, the telephone number listed on their website did not work.

I paid for a proper meal and delivery service, not this. I would like this to be investigated and resolved as soon as possible.

Nice place and the food is good but There is no comfy chair like other places they are hard seating except stool which is in the kids zone.

They do though have tablets for the kids which is useful but might take away from a family convo.

It's service was quick and the food was decent

Very busy today due to a break in but at least the manager was courteous enough to explain the rush. Everyone looked exhausted. The food was nice and hot though and staff were still pleasant during the hectic morning fiasco!

I have given only 4 stars as the gents toilet door is still open when locked, giving no privacy.

The food and the service were good and there is plenty of parking even for a large van.

There is an outside seating area, but it is very  small.
Ordered a plain double cheeseburger meal through drive through and opened this! burger looks disgusting and fries were hard and not fresh have been offered a replacement meal.

Hat-Trick :-(
I asked at the drive through if they had any special burgers on and he said the hat trick. When asked what it was he replied a burger with three pieces of meat in it! Well fair play for the honesty, I present three pieces of burger meat costing £5.99 (on it's own) and assure you, it tastes just as it looks.

Food (Big Mac) was cold, fries were partially edible, but the worst part of the experience were staffs attitudes.

One staff member, who was the designated table and tray area cleaner, spent the whole time talking to an older (early 60's) man at the back of the restaurant,by us, ironically about how kids these days have no work ethic 🤦And when my partner asked her for some BBQ sauce she told my partner that you have to get your own from the dispenser. Now, whilst the dispenser is an option - it is not the only one! I know this because of every other McDonald experience ever, and the fact that when I went to the counter they gave me some without hesitating.

However, when I informed the server at the counter that the other worker is reluctant to help customers because she is too busy chatting, I was initially stared at blankly by several members of staff before the server thanked me for letting her know. Depsite me informing her of this, the table cleaner must not of been spoken with as she continued her 30-40 minute conversation.

Also, shortly prior to me going to the counter to get my BBQ sauce and complain, I noticed the table cleaner trying to stare out my partner when walking past her.

So, all-in-all it was a bad experience and it isn't the first. I am slow to learn, but without a doubt I won't be using this McDonalds ever again, which is a shame because I have been a customer there, in one capacity or another, since they opened up about 30 years ago. But hey, it is what it is.

There have been numerous times on drive through that parts of my meal have been missing. Sometimes the food isn't as fresh and hot as it should be, also you can have quite a long wait on drive thru at peak times. It is definitely one of the busier stores, however, this at times means quality is compromised.
We ordered breakfast and after waiting for ages we received cold and burnt food. Really disappointed

I paid for 2 large milkshakes. 1 inch of milkshakes missing from each shake. The drinks cups stank like men’s aftershave. Where are these cups being stored? These cups should not have aftershave on.

Then the woman who took our order took 2 minutes to come to the drive thru and had an attitude problem.

Then when we got home someone had taken off the Monopoly label on one of the cups! This franchise is scam and I won’t be going back.

Done a drive thur. The food was cold order 2 big macs meals and happy meal with 2 ice creams  bun was like rubber.
The cost £22.70 what a rip off.
The manager was too busy and other staff wasn't even at counter to take money so I drive next window manager said she can't take my money so I at to drive round again and wait as there was another car infront, all because the staff wasn't there in 1st place to take my money.
I would definitely NOT RECCOMMED THIS PLACE UNLESS U LIKE BAD SERVICE AND COLD FOOD.

Worst McDonald’s I have ever had! Such a shame as this is my local McDonald’s. Chips were freezing and so was the burger I got too. Cheese bites was basically frozen still too? Service was awful too. I tried to call the number to complain  and even the number doesn’t ring? I’d highly recommend staying away from this place! 🤢 this isn’t the first time having a freezing cold McDonald’s from this place. Worst thing was I was the only person there too so it wasn’t even justifiably busy. The manager needs to step up!🤦🏻‍♂️🤢

Mine and my partner’s experience here has always been fine, but i just wanted to warn other people about this. My partner got a parking ticket from last weekend for apparently us sitting in the car park for 8 hours🤨 now who would sit in a car park for 8 hours?! That’s ridiculous and disgusting how he’s had to pay a £60 parking ticket for no reason. Just be careful everyone

I paid £5.98 for 2 chocolate milkshakes and over 1.5 inches was missing from each milkshake. I should have paid less money for missing drink.

This franchisee is scamming people and giving less for what they paid. All for profit, disgusting. I won’t go back here.

This branch is Clean and tidy as all the time.
Electronic displays with the whole menu, chairs for toddlers, tablets and drowning placed in a recreational area for kids and plenty of spaces.
Friendly and pleasant members of staff.
Order they are made pretty quickly.
No issues at all :)
author_name: The Red Fox District - Cycling Workshop

review_text:
9.30pm visit not been before so parked up by the window to find out its delivery drivers etc only and had to go inside the main entrance was the other side if the carpark so had to walk the length of the carpark to then back on myslef to walk Indie the the asda the same distance.

So annoying when you have preorerd on the app, they should consider allowing pre orders to collect from window too. All I did was walk 10 minutes to collect and walk back.

Chips where not fully cooked and not salted nor where full protaion sizes.

Nuggets where hot at least
Service was good, but as you can see from the photos, the second Cheese look like it was thrown at the burger. You could have also picked off the burnt bits.

I was served out of date apple pies, and my frappes were half empty. Extremely disappointing, especially when you are looking forward to it.

McDonald’s CS only offered a free medium meal, not a refund or replacement voucher, even though I could have been extremely ill from the pies.

It is surprising how relaxed Customer Support was, in regards to the out of date food. I should report this to Birmingham City Council Environmental Health.

I feel a lot more could have been to compensate and to ensure it does not happen again.

After New Year’s Eve celebrations we decided to get some food on the way home. We arrived at Bescot’s drive thru and were told that the systems were being updated so to return at 2am. We had no issue with this and parked up as it was only 10 minutes.

Eventually we saw cars seeming to be having orders taken so we headed back to the drive thru ourselves. Ordered a chicken nugget meal, and a Big Mac meal.

The fries were disgustingly cold, so during the update they clearly had just left them on the side waiting for orders to come back through. Completely inedible.
The burger and nuggets were also cold but we managed to stomach them as we were quite hungry.

We felt sorry for the staff having to ask people politely to park up especially as they were working on New Years, however all cares for them were thrown out the window when we were given cold food. I understand it would have been such a waste to throw god knows how much food away due to a system update, however that’s not my problem. I waited 10 minutes to be served due to it, and was greeted with a semi edible meal. Great way to start the New Year.

Train your staff better as clearly they think mediocre food is the way to go. Can’t handle it when it’s too busy, can’t handle it when they physically can’t take orders. Seems all they can handle is food that’s been sitting there for hours.

This is the only MacDonalds that rarely serves food at the drive Thru, what's the point of pre ordering requests if your told on my experience 100% to park up. Today I've been asked to park up for the 50th time and I'm in the overflow car park as there is no space in the main car park. Not to mention food gets cold so quick as it's already getting cold when waiting for staff to work out your car and the time it takes. Improve your service by dealing with orders that are preorder, serve them at the driveway like most McDonald's.

There was a period when order accuracy noticeably improved, which was appreciated. Unfortunately, it has since declined again and now requires constant checking, making the experience more of a hassle. When returning to politely ask for corrections to mistakes, the staff can come across as passive-aggressive and visibly displeased. I hope this improves in the future, as it used to be one of my favourite places, but it currently ranks quite low based on recent experiences.

Well I don't know if I generally go about reviewing McDonald's lol but I am today because this one leaves my regular place in the dust! All my food was fresh and hot. I didn't even have my condiments! Loads of lettuce in my burger and fries were well cooked and salted. Ty!

Do you have vermin or is it the people working there🤔 bite already taken out of my burger and its a lil small to be human. I've posted on the main twitter, contacted uber and will be contacting environmental health. It needs shutting down

I tried to contact the restaurant before posting this, but no one answered the phone.
The basics of any food business are simple:

Get the order right
Keep hair and bodily contamination out of food
Cook the food properly

Unfortunately, this place failed on all three.
I ordered two double sausage and egg McMuffins, but both arrived as plain egg McMuffins. Even the receipt didn’t match what was delivered. One of the eggs was badly burnt on one side – darker than the hash browns.
The single sausage and egg McMuffin at least had a sausage patty, but it also had a long, dark, curly hair inside the food. That alone is completely unacceptable and disgusting.
I paid over £20 for this breakfast, yet the most basic standards weren’t met. I genuinely felt sick afterwards and have zero confidence in this outlet’s hygiene.
I will never eat here again. This place seriously needs a food hygiene inspection.
0/5 stars – avoid at all costs.

the food is like any other mc donald’s however the service is poor, i was videoing my mate as he was making the group laugh and the manger came over and told me of for filming i said oaky no problem however then when they called my order out i got my food thrown down carelessly without a word, the continued to watch my mates get a thank you. not just this but the manager was rude to the delivery drivers to after all of this i was ready to eat my food but then found out i had a flat coke and it tasted like pure sparkling water and food was missing items

The staff are moody and rude 24/7 I don’t think they’ve smiled once and I can see them make looks to eachother and make people feel uncomfortable the only happy and helpful staff are the people that do table service shoutout to Jackie who’s always been lovley!! . The food takes ages some of it made me throw up not once but twice chips arnt the best one time I was given a hashbrown instead of my chips and it was the afternoon.. they are constantly moaning and shouting at each other and are disgustingly rude to the delivery drivers overall I only go there as it’s somewhere to sit.

Vicky and the other shorter lady who tidies the restaurant are very friendly and say 'hello' when they see you, fetch things for you and they go out of their way to make sure your dining experience is great there.  They are fabulous and a real credit to this McDonald's.  The other staff are good, too.  Food comes out quickly.

The negatives are the front door, when open, lets in lots of cold air.  The gangs of teenagers hanging around are very intimidating.   The presence of the doorman doesn't fill me with feelings of safety when I go.  He ignored a group of female teenagers who were swearing in the presence of some young children on a table next to them.  I thought that was a disgrace.  I hate teenagers.  I hope the parents complained.

The self-service checkouts haven't been working for a while now and that's unacceptable, as I now can't get a receipt to leave an online review.

Update 2 months later:
Ordered through the app, but some reason came from here.. Still the same tricks from this aweful franchise.. Large fries came half empty, missing both yhe burgers I ordered.. Mcdonalds Uber policy thinks its OK to refund £2 and charge me £10 for fries, drink and a cheese side. All seems like a scam to make more money of folk. Wouldn't eat in here again if tou paid me. Lost a McDonalds customer for life.

Food from this restaurant always turns up wrong. Missing items and unread order notes. Staff can't read an order to save their lives. Most unorganised Mcdonalds I've ever been to.

McDonald’s Broadheath, Altrincham Skibidi Brainrot Review 🍔🚽🌀
Walked in and bro. Absolute skibidi chaos. Weekend energy is off the charts. Shoppers everywhere. Kids in full football kit core. Mums pushing prams like they’re in Mario Kart. Queues moving fast but loud. The place is big. The lines are long. But they move quicker than an admin abuse session in full flow. Respect for that.
Food. Half decent, not gonna lie. But the fries 😐.
How you gonna be that busy and still serve fries that are giving “I’ve been sitting here since the Stone Age” vibes. Not fully crispy. Not fully skibidi. Mild L.
Finding a seat was like a side quest on nightmare mode. Weekend. Good luck soldier. Other days though. Much calmer. Much more NPC friendly.
Now here’s where the Ohio energy crept in 🪰.
Couple of fruit fly looking mandem floating in and out while I’m eating. Not skibidi. Not sigma. Kinda killed the vibe for a second.
Upsides though.
Baby changing facilities. W.
Toilets clean. Mega W.
Music on. Nice. But fam. Turn it down just a notch. My brain’s already rotting enough.
Overall.
Busy. Chaotic. Functional. Fries need a reboot. Flies need evicting. Weekends are unhinged. But midweek. This place slaps.
Rating. 6.8/10.
Could’ve been 8 if the fries weren’t acting like background NPCs 🍟💀

Went through the Drive through, was given a Big Mac with no sauce and some burger patties that were dry and black. Clearly been kept under the heat too long. Went inside to ask for a new one, Lady was very polite and informed the line cook, saw the guy go to change the sauce gun and Judging by his reaction and the way he was carrying himself with a complete lack of urgency (despite it being reasonably busy) i think he deliberately sent out my food with no sauce cause he couldn't be arsed to refill the gun, and then to spite me he absolutely drenched the new burger in sauce and i still got some anaemic dry burnt burger patties. Avoid completely after 11pm

The quality of the burger is bad. I had to get this burger for my kid thou a driveway service and ended up opening it after sometime for a break and was truly disappointed. It looked like someone took a bite from the bottom side of the bun.

This is, without a shadow of a doubt, the best Mcdonalds in the Manchester area. Due to the nature of my work on the road i have sampled many different Mcdonalds establishments. This is the only Mcdonalds where they give you correct portion sizes and always have friendly staff on the window.  There can be a bit of a wait if the queue is full but I have very very rarely left this Mcdonalds dissatisfied with the food or service.
Keep up the good work you are the final saving grace for the Mcdonalds industry in the region.

2025 been again ordered large meal to be handed a medium complained the fries was removed from my order and put in another bag I was handed a bag and was told it's large fries and was given a large drink after leaving it's a small fries in a large box. Just been through the drive through (29th November 2023) ordered the Big and cheesy with bacon and the share box of the cheese bites drove about 2 mile's from McDonald's the fries tasted like they had been double or triple fried and was cold, the cheese bites was the same.... The Big Cheesy had no bacon wasn't given my recipe. Normally good but had stone cold food from an Uber eats delivery and wasn't offered to have the food replaced and wasn't given a refund after complaining to the restaurant the fries wasn't a full portion I tried one of the fries they tasted under cooked and cold the burger was cold sadly this ended up in the bin as it wasn't fit for consumption.

Usually have my McFlurry customised as requested. Today this blonde lady in her 40s charged me way more for a small portion and not customised as requested. When I raised my concerns that it wasn’t how I wanted she said I had to pay more. I already paid for a big portion and then some extra. I asked for other things to be taken away if you’re concerned about the cost. Why not tell someone before they pay if you can’t deliver what they’re asking for.
Never had problems before at this or any other mac. I myself offered to pay more just to have it how I like it. I have to throw most of it away now. Appalling customer service and waste of money. The lack of effort and understanding of customer’s needs was really bad. I work in a customer facing role and it’s really not that difficult. I’m pregnant and won’t be fighting over that but I thought I could have a nice moment whilst everything else is really challenging right now but I guess not today

Hadn’t had a McDonald’s for ages. They’re definitely worse than they used to be, burger dry and tasteless, fries lukewarm not pleasant. And only good thing was the coffee which was actually quite nice. Couldn’t eat all the burger just not worth it and expensive.
I feel so unhealthy after that.

DO NOT HAVE ANY breakfast in this McDonald the meat is overcooked which could cause cancer been to the staff they said it’s fine and done thy extremely rudely they didn’t even said anything or change anything to the meal I am not happy

8th December 2025 - ordered from them through just eat. It’s never right, but this time takes the cake. Stone cold as usual when delivered but they didn’t even pack the right order at all. It actually wasn’t the drivers fault and I know this because the bag was not the correct bag they did not package it in the usual deliver bags with receipt attached. They basically they a bunch of random bits in and did half a job because it was a late order close to finish time. Forgot my drinks entirely, gave a tiny bag of cheese melts when I ordered a share box. The only thing they got right was a Big Mac with no cheese and it looked like it was a “build your own” thrown in the burger box. Such lazy and incompetent staff plague this restaurant they ruin it for the few that try. This is the last time I’m ordering food from this McDonald’s. Will be going altrincham from now on, like I had to when this order was wrong. Been a customer for decades and I’m just sick of the regular incompetence of just making a quick and basic food order. Typically the same experience when I drive there myself.

Near perfect experience today. The restaurant was busy but clean and well managed(Toilets also). Good atmosphere in the afternoon with families, solo diners and an assortment of people.

I got a happy meal which I usually lunch on and a Large Big Arch meal for later. The cheeseburger, fries, melon and sprite thing were delicious, eating happy meals for decades and this was absolutely perfect. I normally order 2 happy meals and it doesn’t seem to matter whether I select a toy or book but I have found this happens up and down the country. Today the happy meal had the right toy.

I heated the Big Arch and fries later and shared it with a housemate and we both liked it, I love everything about it especially the cheese and onion flavours, the patties and sauce are to die for.

Never really had a bad meal from this restaurant since it opened but they were especially on form today, I enjoyed the happy meal and Big Arch so much I thought I would hop on and give 5*

With Thanks

Chips are cold like they have been out all day. Ask for a rap without salad they couldn’t even follow simple instructions the mcchicken sandwich the chicken is stale wouldn’t recommend this McDonald’s

Don’t delivery through the mcdelivery app, if possible it’s even worse than uber. What gives uber the edge is you can get a refund fund for all the things they forget and it’s really difficult through mcdelivery.  I have never had a complete order from this store, they are an absolute joke!!

This is some weird back alley McDonalds, its not Altrincham road

Burger I got was saturated in fat as if they had dipped it in the fryer and its impossible to complain

Quick, delicious, and consistently satisfying!
This spot never disappoints. The handheld sandwiches were hot and flavorful, the golden potato crisps were perfectly seasoned, and the creamy shakes hit the spot. Service was fast and friendly, making it a great choice for a quick bite without sacrificing taste. Whether you're craving classic comfort eats or just need a fast and tasty meal, this place delivers every time. Can't wait to return!
WARNING!!!! Cannot even get a simple order right for one person. Food is always cold. No straw. No napkins. No dips.

Very disappointing experience at McDonald’s – Merseyside Services

I had a very poor experience at this McDonald’s on 23/11/2025 in the morning. I ordered food for my children through the McDonald’s app before arriving. When I went through the drive-through, the staff told me they could not find my order at all.

I then parked nearby and went inside to the counter/reception. I showed them my order receipt from the app, but they told me they couldn’t do anything because the food items were “finished” and that I would have to request a refund myself through the app. The way this was explained was very rude and unhelpful.

My children were very upset because they were waiting for their favourite food and we were treated like it wasn’t their problem. I spoke to both a male and a female staff member who were not wearing name badges. When I asked for their names, they responded very rudely.

This was a very bad customer service experience. I expected better from a big franchise like McDonald’s. I really hope the management team looks into this and trains staff to be more polite and customer-friendly.
the staff(India lady) was so rude, (dont.want.to.explain the details),the medium fries just like small one. toilet is poor.

Lovely and clean, food was well presented and hot. Cleanest McDonald's I've ever been in.  This was down Asher, who never stopped cleaning and keeping the place spotless. Big shout out to her. I hope her hard work is appreciated.

I write this cause I stopped here for something quick to it on the to Withenshaw hospital with my daughter who was in a low mood and swollen eye. ********Serving cold food is a common complaint for McDonald's delivery orders and is considered a potential food safety issue. Food businesses are legally required to ensure hot food is delivered in a way that prevents it from becoming unsafe or unfit to eat.
***********Food Safety Implications
Food that needs to be kept hot must be packed in an insulated bag during transit to maintain temperature. If hot food is allowed to cool over a long period and stays in the temperature "danger zone" (the temperature range where bacteria multiply rapidly, generally between 4°C and 63°C in the UK), bacteria may begin to grow, making it potentially unsafe. Only in this case the delivery driver cannot be blamed !!!!!

McDonald’s at St Ann’s Square is open 24/7, making it somewhere you can go for a light bite even during unsociable hours. I’ve visited this particular location a lot during the past couple of years, and I’ve had many good and bad experiences.

Food is usually OK, although I have had issues here and there with the temperature of the food. On two occasions, I’ve ordered my burger without the bun, and they’ve actually served it without the meat patty. I did find it funny that they’d laid everything out in the box nicely and forgotten the patty though, and this issue was quickly rectified when bringing it to the staff member’s attention.

During less busy periods, when they can actually get the food hot and have enough time to take care, it can actually be pretty good here, and I’ve had burgers which I’ve genuinely enjoyed more than burgers I’ve paid a lot more for. But there are generally day-to-day inconsistencies with the standards and how the burgers are prepared.

The staff seem friendly enough and they do try to keep the restaurant area clean, although it can get really busy during peak times so the waiting area can feel crowded. Luckily, they have recently re-introduced table service, which makes things a little easier.

A recurring problem which I’ve noticed for some time now and which never appears to be fixed are the malfunctioning touch-screen kiosks — they always seem to be out of paper and unable to print receipts, or there are issues scanning my voucher codes from completing the survey. So I usually like ordering at the counter or via the McDonald’s App at this one.

Overall, for a McDonald’s this is above average for sure, but work could be done on improving consistency and the touch-screen kiosks.

I am writing to formally raise a complaint regarding a very concerning incident that took place at Manchester Fort McDonald’s approximately two days ago.

My receipt number was 006. I visited Manchester Fort McDonald’s in the early evening, between approximately 5:00 and 6:00 pm, accompanied by my young child. I ordered a Double Fish Meal for my child.

When the order was served:

the burger was cold, and

it had a strong, unpleasant odour which was completely unacceptable.

I immediately asked to speak to the manager. The manager took the burger back and stated that it would be prepared again. I clearly explained that if the replacement was in the same condition, I would not accept it.

After approximately five to six minutes, the replacement burger was brought back. However, upon checking it again, it had the same unpleasant smell. I therefore informed the manager that the fish stock appeared to be old and asked for it to be taken back.

The manager responded that, in their view, the food was fine, and then advised me to take a refund.

At this point, I would like to highlight that:

a significant amount of time had already passed,

my child had already started drinking the drink, and

had also started eating the chips.

In these circumstances, I believe a full refund should have been provided, as:

the main issue was the burger, which was not fit to be consumed, and

the delay and situation were not caused by us.

Despite this, I was only refunded £3.50, whereas I had paid approximately £7 or £8 (I do not recall the exact amount).

Out of disappointment, I placed the refunded amount directly into the charity donation box located at the counter.

I would also like to clarify that:

the chips were cold, but I did not raise a complaint about this,

my main concern was the burger, which was intended for my child.

I am thankful that I checked the burger myself before my child consumed it, as the situation could otherwise have resulted in a risk to my child’s health.

I am submitting this complaint regarding the service standards and handling of this matter at Manchester Fort McDonald’s, so that appropriate steps can be taken and other families do not experience a similar situation in the future.

I would appreciate this matter being reviewed seriously and look forward to your response.

Poor Review for McDonald’s, Warrington Street, Ashton-under-Lyne

Very disappointed with the poor customer service from the manager, Chloe. We were served burnt food, and when I raised a complaint, she was unhelpful and refused to offer a refund or replacement at the time. Later, during the investigation, she falsely claimed she had offered a refund — which is untrue. She also stated the machine was broken that day, but if that was the case, the food should not have been served in the first place. Her attitude and customer service skills were shocking and completely unacceptable.
If you want a latte don't bother. It's strong and does not contain milk. Also the toilets were disgusting. In desperate need of a clean.  No toilet roll and no lock on the Male cubicle doors

The one star is for the food, which was actually quite pleasant. What wasn't pleasant was the absolutely bizarre interaction we had with a member of staff, while I was doing the mcdonalds survey you find on the receipt. She came up behind me and asked to reverse every answer I had given on my survery and stood behind me and instructed me on what to put for every question, being very rude in the process and scoffing at what I did for a job. Being autistic, I was very upset by this and was starting to panic, so my friend tried to step in and politely tell her that we were capable of finishing the survery by ourselves, she refused to leave and demanded to see us finish the survey with her answers, and when directly told she needed to leave us alone, reacted very aggressively and saying "I guess you'll do whatever you want then!" multiple times, before ultimately leaving us alone where we were able to make our escape. This was an extremely uncomfortable interaction and little would this staff member know, I'm agoraphobic and this was my first time in such a place with a friend in almost 3 years. It took a lot of bravery on my part to even be there in the first place, only to be harassed by a staff member for no good reason at all. Very good way to ensure you'll never get "highly satisfied" on your survey🤷‍♂️

Food was expected and no complaints there. Staff were also pleasant. The male toilets were disgusting and excrement was clearly visible under the toilet bowl. The restaurant area was very untidy and unclean, so decided to take food away.

The service was extremely poor, and I had a very bad experience. I ordered a large portion of chips for takeaway, but what I received was much smaller than expected. When I asked them to add more, they insisted it was already a large portion.
Had a fairly long wait for my order but there were a lot of people. Overall it was clean except for the till, it was very sticky and unpleasant

A new low for McDonald’s Walkden.
Cheese on a chicken sandwich they are useless.  They need to close it down it’s that bad.  I had to wait 10min for this. The kids that work there are rude no idea how to talk to people.

Waited 15 minutes for a simple cup of tea, and I was the only customer in line. No apology, no “thanks for waiting,” just silence. Basic courtesy and time management are part of good service—this location needs to retrain staff on customer care and speed. Please fix the delays and acknowledge customers’ time.

Went via drive through. Had to wait for food which was OK. But I asked for dips twice, once when ordering then again when at window on collection, to be told yeah they're in. Got 3 big mac meals and 1 grinch mac meal. Got home no dips no socks and cost for a packet of powder the difference is totally not worth it

Ordered from uber eats, when food arrived bags were sealed, opened bags to find half my food missing rang the store and the lad that answered the phone told my partner i had to deal with the uber eats app but how can half the food be missing from the delivery driver if bags were sealed surely that's an store issue with staff not quality checking. Pictures added for what i ordered and what i actually received.

Every time I order from here, food in my order is missing, or wrong, or unsanitary (once I ordered 2 apples pies and, cheese bites but then I just got a sugar ring doughnut. Also found a thick crusty eyelash in my friend’s mcflurry, ordered a mcflurry not to be in sight). All the staff here including the manager are extremely rude and have no respect or manners. Kids running around the place screaming. I gave 2 stars for the food because I felt generous, I like McDonald’s but not this one. Worst run McDonald’s I’ve ever known, very disappointed.

Worst McDonald’s , ordered sprite and taste like it hadn’t been changed for a year. I also looked in my bag and saw there was missing napkins and straws I told the worker and she just stared at me. The order took 30 minutes just for a chicken burger meal. Disappointed, need more staff that knows what to do . Another reason is teenage boys were having a food fight and worker decided not to do anything.

I have been to some not so great McDonalds before but this one  in my opinion is the out and out winner.

I got told to park on a Big Mac Meal and a couple of Chicken mayo’s some 25 minutes later I managed to get some cold food.

Barely warm, the milkshake was a mystery flavour and the 7UP was a cloudy tasteless fizzy liquid.

Come on guys you’re the market leaders in fast food but 25 minutes is pushing it especially when your quality is terrible.

Avoid this one guys.

Probably the worst McDonalds in the country. Avoid like your mother in law answering the door in a nighty

After vowing to never go to this branch again due to consistent missed items, cold food, trash burgers and always being told to park up, I reluctantly found myself back here. App payment was made at 17.25 and we left the car park at 17.58. What a complete and utter shambles.

If they ask you to park up, just ask for a refund and go to a different branch, trust me, it will be quicker. 22 cars all receiving food passed through the drive through before we got the first part of our order.

and then, 6, yes SIX times a man left and came back to our car, every time going back to get another item he had forgot off the order, every time I mentioned individually the items he was missing.

I just don't understand with the millions McDonalds corporate spend on ordering systems how it's possible to miss items in the first place.

I'm convinced at this point that the franchise here is ran by someone who will spend absolutely nothing but the bare minimum in all areas with staffing and training being the worst hit. We were all 18 once at our first job, I get it, but some of the staff here seem to be unable to think with their own brains. Maybe that's how they are trained, who knows. They are nice young people, I just wish they could get the training and support they desperately need to deal with the most basic of customer service.

Then there's the missing items, Dips? no chance, we spent near £40 on family meals tonight and the first time he came to our car, the guy turns up with 1 bag, maybe he thought we had half the order already? either way, there was clearly no communication at all between the runner and the other staff.

I flat out refuse to use Deliveroo now, because the frequency of missing items, but we were in the car park, come on!

I'm halfway to thinking it's a conspiracy arranged by the franchise owner to leave at least 1 item out per order, adding up to a large amount of food by the end of a busy day I'm sure.

I do wish McDonalds corporate would do spot-checks on franchises and revoke licences for under performing branches, it's the organisation and management of branches like this that give McDonalds a terrible name.

I'll be very happy if this branch ever closes down, then I wont ever forget and find myself driving into the car park again.

Basically, unless it's absolutely your only option, go to the Salford or Eccles branch.

Thought I would treat the family to a McDonald's meal.
It turns out £39 spent means your fast food is extra fast and you receive it cold.
McDonald's doesn't care about custom, just your money!

Ordered a Fish Filet burger and it was poorly made. There was barely any cheese on it and more than half was missing. Staff were running around but despite this, the order wasn’t made correctly. Not the standard you expect.!!!!!!!!!!!!

The girl at the drive through doesn’t really want to be there. She acted like I forced her to be at that window to hand out the food. I asked her napkins, sugars, stirrers and the dips I ordered, and clearly she didn’t like any of it.

It's just your bog standard McDonald's,  but get stuck in the drive-thru at any particular time, ( not just peak time), and for some reason its as though they just ' glitch'. There will only be about 4 cars and you can be sat there 15 mins. And yes, by then you are aware that you should have simply ' pulled up'... but its too late by then.

Anyway, it's not the worst maccys you're gonna get.

I took a basic breakfast made with pancakes, and they seemed to me like they were defrosted and warmed for the meal, or like they were cooked from the day before. Maybe next time I would try something with eggs, or something that could be freshly prepared.
However, it was clean and tidy up, so no concerns about the atmosphere and nice hard-workers.

Waited about 15 mins and people who order after received theirs to get a burnt burger? And there was a poor woman who had to bring her drink back and forth because it bloody lukewarm service has dropped insanely it needs sorting because 15 mins for a burnt burger is a jokee!

Avoid this shop!!!!! Nothing is done correctly here. In our order, a Big mac, double filet-o-fish, 3 iced drinks, ShakerFried should be included. Instead, single filet-o-fish, and cokes are delivered and no Big Mac!

Very hard to rate this place. If it's dinner service I'd give it 4 stars most of the time. Quickish service (most of the time), mostly friendly staff. But breakfast service is hands down the worst I have ever seen at any restaurant/fast food place all over the UK. A 15-25 min wait time for 3 cars to get a cup of coffee is abysmal. This happens so regularly it isn't just a "One time thing". I don't know if they don't have enough staff on in the mornings or they recruit the D team for breakfast but this is by far the worst fast food place for the morning.

I visited the McDonald’s -Jardine Way, Chadderton, Oldham OL9 9QL drive-through around 10:00 p.m. and ordered a Chicken Sharebox., Cheese burger meal,quarter pound Meal and Mc FlurryUnfortunately, the food had a bitter, burnt taste, and the colour of the Chicken Selects and nuggets looked darker than usual. I’ve always enjoyed meals from this branch before, but this time the quality was disappointing. Please look into your fryer maintenance and oil-change schedule

this place is absolutely shocking there’s genuinely no words for the experience i’ve just had

children made positions and threw it at me then an old man stared at me wholst violentky guzzling down milk pakets and  the old man poured milk and mayo on my chicken seletcs, then a strage man called andy heslop began doing backflips

I'm in Radcliffe, so was able to finally get my hands on the new McPlant burg (no locations near where I live are currently doing it). Inspired by my dad : I ordered an extra and doubled up! Orderd mcdonalds half of the order didn't turn up when I rung they wouldn't take responsibility as it was their mistake put the phone down on me the drinks weren't even full one of the sprite was pure water bad customer service they need to learn manners never buy again from them only 1 star only because I would give them none

But then, something caught my eye- a charming McDonald’s with an adorable and inviting exterior nestled in what seemed like the outskirts of Leeds. It was the kind of place that instantly sparked curiosity.
As soon as I reached Leeds, I couldn’t get it out of my mind. With just a few keystrokes, I uncovered its location on Google: Wide Lane, a suburban gem quite far from the bustling city center. Realizing it wasn’t within walking distance, I marked it on my mental map as a place to visit when time allowed.
Fast forward three weeks, and that curiosity finally got the better of me. Today, I hopped on a bus from the city center, ready to explore. The ride itself built up the anticipation, winding through streets that gradually grew quieter as the city faded into the background.
When I arrived, I was utterly mesmerized. The surroundings of this McDonald’s were serene, almost picturesque, and the building itself seemed to blend modernity with warmth. Stepping inside, I was relieved to find it wasn’t bustling with crowds. The atmosphere was just as I preferred - peaceful and inviting.
The staff added to the charm. Clara and Wendy, in particular, were attentive and friendly, making me feel welcome from the moment I walked in. There was something else that caught my attention - air chargers neatly placed on the tables! I had never seen such a thoughtful touch at any McDonald’s before. It was a small but significant detail that showcased how modern and customer-focused this location was.
As I soaked in the ambiance, my thoughts drifted back to a few years ago when I worked at a KFC in my hometown of Dalat, Vietnam. The memories of the camaraderie among the staff, and the satisfaction of serving customers all came rushing back or even the decorations/designs outside the house. This McDonald’s reminded me of those days, not in the hustle and bustle, but in its unique ability to create a welcoming space for everyone.
I left feeling grateful for the experience. This visit was more than just a trip to McDonald’s - it was a reminder of how even the simplest moments can evoke powerful emotions and cherished memories. If given the chance, I would absolutely return to this little suburban treasure. Thank you, Wide Lane McDonald’s, for giving me such lovely moments to carry with me.

A Descent into Fast-Food Disappointment

From the moment I walked in, something felt off. The air hung thick with the smell of... well, let's just say it wasn't the enticing aroma of freshly baked bread. The ordering process was a chaotic dance of touchscreens and mumbled requests, and the staff seemed as enthusiastic as a sloth on a Monday morning.

I ordered a Big Mac meal, a classic, and a test of any McDonald's. What arrived was a culinary tragedy. The burger itself was a sad, misshapen thing. The patties were dry, the sauce was applied with a heavy hand, and the whole thing tasted like it had been sitting under a heat lamp since the dawn of time. The fries were cold, limp, and tasted of stale oil. The drink? Flat. Completely and utterly flat.

The seating area was a testament to neglect. Tables were sticky, the floor was littered with discarded wrappers, and the overall ambiance was one of general disrepair. It wasn't exactly a place you'd want to linger.

The Verdict

McDonald's, in this particular instance, failed on almost every level. The food was subpar, the service was lackluster, and the environment was uninviting. It's a shame because, in theory, a quick, affordable meal should be a simple pleasure. This was anything but.

A Morning of Industrial Elegance at The Golden Arches
Rock Retail Park, Birkenhead
Nestled within the brutalist charm of the Rock Retail Park, the Birkenhead outpost of this world-renowned franchise offers a masterclass in high-efficiency morning gastronomy. Upon arrival, one is immediately struck by the immaculate upkeep of the establishment; the surfaces glisten with a level of sanitation that would put a Michelin-starred laboratory to shame.
The service staff, orchestrating the morning rush with the precision of a pit crew, are remarkably attentive. Their hospitality is not merely transactional, but genuinely warm—a rare find in the bustling heart of the Wirral.
The Tasting Menu: Breakfast Provisions
The Main Course: The Double Sausage & Egg McMuffin
The centerpiece of the meal was a layered construction of savory textures. The "Double" refers to two heritage-style pork patties, seasoned with a proprietary blend of botanicals and herbs. These were expertly seared to a uniform golden-brown, providing a robust base for the protein.
Resting atop the pork was a freeform egg, steam-poached within a circular mold to achieve a height that defies standard culinary physics. The dish was crowned with a slice of processed cheddar—melted to a precise viscosity—and encased in a toasted, cornmeal-dusted English muffin. While the flavor profile was familiar rather than revolutionary, the execution was remarkably consistent.
The Beverage: Coconut Infused Caffè Latte
To accompany the meal, I opted for the seasonal Coconut Latte. The espresso base provided a necessary bitterness to cut through the richness of the sausage, while the coconut notes added a tropical, aromatic layer. It was a bold, creamy departure from the traditional breakfast beverages of the region.
The Verdict
| Category | Rating | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Ambiance | ★★★★☆ | Spotlessly clean, high-energy atmosphere. |
| Service | ★★★★★ | Staff are exceptionally helpful and efficient. |
| Gastronomy | ★★★☆☆ | Satisfying, reliable, and expertly assembled. |
While some may call it a "quick bite," the Birkenhead Rock Retail Park location elevates the experience through superior cleanliness and a dedicated service team. It is, quite simply, the definitive way to break one's fast before a day of shopping.
