If anyone ever goes into this Subway the one thing you'll remember is Kirsty the manager, she's the best.

The state of your sub at the moment seems to depend on which member of staff you get. The dark haired girl today was literally throwing the fillings at the sub and by the time I'd got home, the whole thing was falling apart and by half way through it had literally disintegrated.  Really not impressed with staff at the moment. The older guy...well he's not a teenager anyway...makes a good one as does the blonde haired lady. I would probably go to Maccies if anyone else is making it.

Came in not long ago and was greeted with a rude manner by a young girl serving. Tried to crack jokes or even a smile and nothing. Dont know if ill be returning to this branch. But the other girl on head was lovely.

Very disappointed, while the staff were nice this new system is awful, I was there an hour ago, paid in cash and my order was order 65. The sandwich is totally wrong, it’s on the wrong bread, it has cucumber on it which I am intolerant too! Lettuce which I deselected, no jalapeños and no sauce. I didn’t even think to check before I got home because you expect it to be right

The drink was right, I took that from the fridge myself.

I cannot eat the sandwich, what am I to do with it but throw it away?

Great subway with really pleasant staff. Happy to help and nice and friendly. The car park is really easy to get too and the food was fresh and tasty. The store was clean and tidy. Highly recommended and will be returning 😊

The new Subway TLC from eccles
(tastes like chicken) I was mildly scared on my first bite. The texture felt just like chicken 6 scroll to bottom for pictures for a closer look It tastes like chicken too, it reminded me of roast chicken I ate as a child. I had it with plant based mozzarella slices, peppers, red onion, sweetcorn & garlic aioli I really enjoyed it after I got over my initial reaction of "oh god is this actually meat, has there been a mistake, best read the receipt 9 more times to check" The cookie was good too, it's sweet but not so sweet that it feels sickly sweet. It has a nice chewy texture, is that what all their cookies are like? I've never had a cookie from there before now Well done Subway. I also gave some to my partner (who isn't vegan) and he was impressed and said it was like chicken too

The staff were happy, kind and respectful but the manager was moody and had no respect to the customers she wrapped up our sandwiches super tight and gave my children the hard stare. When she gave me my packet of crisps she tossed them to me with no respect. Is not okay to do that to their customers I AM NOT GOING BACK AGAIN!!!
It's like dog food. First and last time ordering from this disgusting place.Not as I asked and paid them £15

Just came in at around 8pm to order two platter for tomorrow's and the Subway artist at the counter Named Jat was absolutely inconsiderate, I said Hi to him and he just drop his eye down and continue to prep the football, he was close to me and definitely heard me, I stood there observing him till he get to the till and then he slowly raised his eye as to check if I am still here, when i asked him why he did not reply to my Greeting he started to say he did, i showed him that despite having my headphone (came from the gym) my ears was not covered and could heard him. He is rude and do not uphold the basic of Subway. For someone who own few from the South of England I would strongly recommend that you remind him of his role to acknowledge the customer within acceptable time frame, as he just made you loose a substantial sale.

I’ve been coming here for years. More for the convenience. But it’s by far the worst subway. How they can call it a toasted sandwich is beyond me. It’s more a lukewarm/cold. And has been for years.  Terrible.
The subs that they do are scrumptious I had a sausage and bacon sub I would highly recommend

New system is rubbish. Placing an order via the machines takes too long (they keep freezing), staff end up having to place the orders for people anyway, making orders takes too long now because of this system. Was so much faster and easier before the machines! Just don’t have enough time now on my lunch break to visit this subway anymore.
Upon receiving our order my partner was half way through eating his and there was a HAIR in the sandwich 🥪 it's certainly not any of ours and maybe they need to practice food hygiene. NEVER AGAIN

Went in this morning to order some food and I was greeted by a member of staff who was very rude and very abruptly with their service, will not be going back and please avoid at all costs.
The other member of staff was very polite as even he picked up 🆙 n the bad customer service experience

Worst experience I had yesterday. I come to this subway occasionally, never had any problems issue with anyone. But on this faithful day, the stars were not aligned with me. I came in to order my usual breakfast which is 6 inch Italian Hearty bread with 2 pouched eggs. A young lady was attending to me. She asked what salads do you want in your sandwich? I answered onions, sweet corn, lettuce and green peppers. Straight away she said “you are only allowed 3 choices of salad for breakfast” Now let’s give some context to this issue. I have never been told that ever before and I have been going there for years occasionally for breakfast. I simply told her that’s a bit outrageous as I have never been told that salads were limited for breakfast. She still adamantly insisted that the salads were limited to 3 for breakfast. So in order not to continue with the back and forth, I offered to pay extra just to have my red peppers included in my sandwich.. she declined and said “who even adds lettuce and green peppers in their sandwich ” ..I was shocked when I heard that come out of her mouth. I felt insulted and that was so rude of her to say that to a customer, I was so annoyed. The were other staff in the building controlling the other store, they came to ask what the issue was? I explained to them and the first thing I heard was you have to listen to her. I felt that they didn’t come to listen and resolve but to escalate the issue. So basically they asked me to leave the premises because I was furiated  with their behaviour. This is shared incompetence within all the staff present yesterday as they saw and heard everything that happened yesterday..My Question here is where is the employees discretion in this situation to use common sense? I am now aware of the breakfast rules about only 3 salads. But was that information relayed properly? I would say No. I am always against the slogan “ Customer is always right” because I am aware that some customers abuse power and feed off entitlement. But in this case I just wished the employee used “Common sense “ and discretion to de escalate the issue.. even when the customer offered to pay extra. What difference would it make adding 2 or 3 slices of red pepper. I felt really disrespected and that really changed my mood for the day . BAD BAD  EXPERIENCE! The worst is no parking! I have managed to endure that for a long time but yesterday was the last straw for me. Both female staffs handled the situation unprofessionally. I WOULD AVOID THIS PLACE AT ALL COST!
We ordered 2 x footlongs tonight..Big cheese steak submelt and Garlic cheese steak...Had half of each. Absolutely gorgeous! Definitely have this more often. Guy who served was lovely.

Disgusting people.  Food never arrived. Driver just sat in car outside my home, which i never knew about.  Told me after an hour to come  and collect,  which i had already paid for delivery and wasn't able to get there. Refused to give me the drivers phone number, refused to give a refund. out of pocket by £18

Came here because the west one retail park subway outlet is closed.
I have had subway in 3 different countries across different locations,  this is the first one that puts a cap on the amount of salad for "cost reasons ". The 2 women who are the senior staff were extremely rude. Safe to say I am never coming back again
The store advertises a “12 cookies for £4.99” deal but refused to sell 6 cookies of the same flavour, with no notice of any limit. This caused a very upsetting experience for my children.

Truly horrible service. The person packing my order downright refused to add any vegetable to my sandwich and had to ask their colleague who acted like rhey were doing me a favor  I had to rewrap my sandwich and prop it next to my body hile I headed home
Came here during a lunch break on a Salford uni study day back in May, and it was badly understaffed. Two staff members absolutely overworked. Queue moved at snail's pace and the Sub was awful.

Came in around 5pm on the 28th May 2025. The two members of staff running the front were incredibly friendly and attentive despite both seemingly multi-tasking other responsibilities. Possibly the cleanest Subway I have seen, food quality was better than other locations and staff were lovely - I hope this gets picked up on and recognised properly by those higher up.
Visited a minute ago… well served by Kirsty…. A gemstone 💫 (5:28pm 14/10/25)

I recently visited this Subway location and had a very disappointing experience. My order was prepared incorrectly, and when I politely asked for it to be remade, the staff member did remake it but was extremely rude and disrespectful throughout the interaction. The unprofessional behavior made the entire visit uncomfortable.

I’ve attached a photo showing how the order was supposed to look when remade. I hope management takes this seriously and improves customer service moving forward.
Just ordered food from Deliveroo. They send me an empty sub and asked me to request fefund through Deliveroo. We are waiting for food last hour and now we need to order from different place.

Attended this Subway store on Monday and was served by Luke the new worker. He was friendly and professional even when it was busy. The food was excellent quality and the store itself was spotless. No faults from me, Luke is an asset to your team, I will return again.

Just ordered delivery as got home late snd hadn't eaten yet. Ordered a 6 inch meatball with tomato snd Cucumber for me snd whatever my partner Ordered. Only for it to arrive and the staff decided to put red onion and red peppers on which I didn't order as I don't like.  Disgusting absolutely a waste of money! Won't be ordering again 😡

I have never visited this site before until a couple of weeks ago.
I went there quite late  but everything was in stock , which is not a case for some subways. On entry a dark skin male staff member ( perhaps a manager or supervisor) has greeted me and told me I will be served shorty.
Staff member , young white male who served me was really nice and polite. I was offered for my footlong to be wrapped into 2 separate halfs due to different salad items, was offered bag and given napkins which is not often a case anymore but which should be a standard, without asking for it. Additionally what was really positively surprise for me , when one customer noticed I had a small scratch on my leg to which staff member immediately offered me a plaster. My first visit and I definitely recommend this store . Thank you to both staff members having a great customer service and actually caring for their customers. I wish this was my local branch.

I had a delivery with Deliveroo and the bread was completely stale. Not acceptable for subway at all. The person preparing this would have been completely aware the the bread was stale and below the quality standard that they should be serving. Very very disappointing!!!
The never adhere to the opening times they publish, frequently closing hours before theyre supposed to. And now they lock the doors so customers cant come in, while claiming to be open.

Served by zahra which was the really attentive and nice while taking my order
Made me feel welcomed by providing such q great customer service

Staff not wearing gloves
Give you a kids portion  of salad for a salad box 2 slices of tomato and 2 slices of cucumber half a handful of lettuce  and about 5 pieces of onion
Never again will I come to this subway
Never seen anything like it
The guy working in the morning  wednesday 10december,don’t know how to behave with the customer .doing argument without any reason

Very poor and slow service. The staff member who served us didn't even look up when serving customers and just mumbled to take the orders. After creating one of our sandwiches he closed the salad bar lids and walked off leaving us standing there for 5 minutes whilst one of our sandwiches was going cold with no communication as to what was going on or how long he'd be.
Sandwich was okay though.

The first time that I come here and I find the girl so impolite, she ask me which bread do I want, I say either Italian or Herb Italian one that is white, she said that all are white, NOT AT ALL, it’s clear this woman is not a Sandwich Lover as I am, when you love sandwiches a key part is the bread, it needs to be taste and in the condition you want, in my case I like the most white bread possible from the range they have, and NOT all breads are white as she says, when she open the tray it’s clear there are some difference in the level of more white more brown, whenever I have go to bakeries they never have a problem to check for me which bread is the most white, I ask her to pick the one second from the right, fifth layer, she said all are the same, and then picked one randomly, the one I told her was the one I wanted, one that was much whiter.
Only for this I was about to say to her leave it, I will find other places. Then she leave the chicken tikka all concentrated in the middle instead of spreading it, there is a great difference in a sandwich where the ingredients are all in one side to when they are well spread. The name of this woman is Lydia.
I won’t go back to this place when this woman is here.

Absolutely rude staff not helpful at all The Steak and cheese comes out as bread and salad no meat, and so cold
I asked for a meal but they charged me £3.90 extra.not ever coming back again
Been there twice now and both seperate times they gave me stale bread. They should sort it out. But other than that the sandwich was gud
The busiest shop in the food court and easy to see why. Quick friendly service and plenty of good staff. Loads of seating.

I have dyslexia which the staff weren’t aware off I was already haven’t the worst day of my life and just wanted a sandwhich. I asked for an order he told me to order from the machine I asked can I not order from a person he said nope.  There was self service but I wanted to order from a person because I struggle with the screens as they aren’t very dyslexia friendly. Not the staffs fault but unless there was no till which they most certainly had a till,ans in the evenings I ordered from a man and He was able to help me also just generally wanted to order from a person. There was a till I’ve been to other subways where they have been nothing but friendly and supportive. Do not visit here if you struggle with technology definitely not dyslexia friendly environment they do not let you order from a person. ❤️‍🩹I think the sandwhiches are very nice and the place is very clean but ultimately human interaction is so so important. And we must value this over machines. Also the team that are on in the evening are lovely so no hate on them as I’ve ordered from here before ! Please do better to be more inclusive. Especially when I was very gentle and polite in my ask that I’d like to order from a person. The girls also working did nothing wrong but it was the dude who was currently behind the till. Who at first I thought was going to be really kindly. Please put signs up that say “you can’t order from a person” so that others also know :) !

The BEST subway I’ve been to or had. Sandwich came out and was presented so nice, SO NEAT, Like exactly how it would come out of the subway poster which i didn’t even know was possible, filling was stacked so well, nothing was falling out while eating. This particular subway was very clean, no greasy floor and the lady who i always see working is amazing at what she does!
My sandwich was missing American-style cheese, lettuce, cucumbers, red peppers, red onions, jalapeños, Garlic & Herb Sauce X-Spicy Southwest Sauce

I have deleted my 5-star review from a few years ago, and replaced it with a still-generous 1-star, following the ludicrous imposition of the screen ordering system.

Staff look on in idle amusement as bewildered customers struggle with the byzantine complexity of the screens, grimly searching for the once-simple regular order. Having settled for what their digital masters will allow, the sandwich is made with zero input from customers - the element of personalisation and customisation that was the entire point of subway is now removed.

We're told the march of technology is unstoppable, but if it's gonna look anything like this we face a grim future indeed.
The lady who served me wasn’t friendly at all, but the sandwich is still TOP

Great and efficient service!
The guy at the checkout was on top of it.I showed him the QR code for my app offer, and he spotted it right away on his screen and applied the discount for me. No fuss, no confusion—just really good service.

I am writing to raise a complaint
regarding the unacceptable service my daughter and I received at your Subway location.

Upon entering the store, the door slammed into my daughter, and rather than offering assistance or expressing concern, two male staff members began laughing at her in full view of other customers. This was both humiliating and completely inappropriate.

When we attempted to order a toasted cheese and onion baguette, a request we have made many times at other Subway branches, one of the staff members, whose name badge read “Kristen,” dismissively stated, “We don’t do that.” When I explained that we have ordered this exact item many times before, he acted as though he didn’t understand, forcing me to explain in detail how it’s usually made. His attitude throughout was mocking and unhelpful.

I then asked for a cappuccino, to which he responded, “We only do black coffee,” again while laughing with his colleague. This behavior was incredibly unprofessional and made us feel belittled and unwelcome.

When we finally received the baguette, it was of extremely poor quality, with barely any cheese and only a minimal amount of onion. As shown in the attached photo, the sandwich was clearly made with little care or effort.

When I mentioned that I would be reporting this, the staff member even pointed to his name badge in a defiant manner, as if to mock the seriousness of the complaint.

This experience was not only disappointing in terms of food quality but also distressing due to the rude and inappropriate conduct from your staff. I hope this matter is taken seriously, and I look forward to hearing how you intend to address this.
This occurred on the 04/10/2025 at 17:15 hours

So helpful with supporting us whilst being diagnosed coeliac. Great options and ensure complete care with catering to our needs safely and kindly. Maria and Claire were amazing and enjoyed our experience. So nice to feel comfortable to eat at a restaurant without worries

Unfortunately NOT Halal. Just had to get a tuna sub. Service was okay, just ordered and got my order I guess, place looked clean. They play music quite loud if that’s what you like. Right in city centre.

I visited Subway to get something to eat, after I had donated blood. Whilst ordering my food, I started to feel lightheaded and subsequently fainted in the queue. Maria the manager, helped me back to my feet, made sure I sat down and took my time to eat and drink. The whole team were fantastic, Maria particularly and I thanked them for looking after me so well. They are a credit to your company and I hope the relevant managers will see this.

It’s a fairly large store with two entrances, washrooms available, and kiosks for ordering.
Our order,a 6-inch and a footlong, took about 20 minutes to prepare. The server helped a couple of people who arrived after us, seemingly acquaintances, before working on ours. In the end, he made a mistake with one of our sandwiches, so it had to be remade, which took even longer.

I go to this Subway rather frequently and despite ordering the same thing everytime I get charged different prices. One time I paid £8+ for a veggie delight and they refused to give me a reciept (fairly certain they overcharged me). Since they put the ordering screens in, they have become more stingy with the ingredients and have on numerous occasions made my order wrong.

Honestly the worst subway sandwich ever. They just throw it together it is awful. I love subway but this chain really lets it down. The sauce all down side instead of on the sandwich. They forgot to add a meat that I paid for and I only have ham on one half of my sandwich. Every time I go it’s honestly such a let down !!
Havrath is a great guy, he is very fast to make the food and always looking after you ! In generell this is the best subway in mcr
This is by far the best subway l,ve ever been to.during lunchtime rush they are so efficiently and the sandwich are always consistently good

Subway on Portland Street is in a prime location within Manchester city centre, a short walk away from Piccadilly and Oxford Road, right by the Novotel. The interior is large and has a good amount of tables; it has the classic Subway look, so it could do with a refit in the future to follow the newer store look.

The opening hours are pretty good; they’re generally open well into the evening, and in 2025, they were open on Christmas Day, which is when I visited. I ordered my usual Jacket Potato with tuna and cheese. The sandwich artist was friendly and changed his gloves for me to reduce cross-contamination after letting him know I was gluten-free. Mohi delivered excellent service from start to finish.

Food quality was good, everything tasted fresh and was made with care

It’s pretty good value for money here too - the prices seem to be lower than other Subway locations in the city centre, with my jacket potato costing £5.59 on this occasion, in the Arndale, I’d pay at least £6.19.

I look forward to returning to this Subway location in the future.

Decent outlet. The staff can be a little rash and careless and add items you had asked not to add. Good part is the long opening hours which makes it really convenient. Read at the store that it remains open for take away even post 11:30. Location is also great - right next to the bus stop
Mohi (name on badge) was really helpful and recommended a sauce that I’ve not had before with my bmt. Great food and service and very clean environment.
Amazing how much they welcomed me love them wow thank you so much guy honest the best place ever thank you so much service could go quicker

After arriving I was greeted to a smiling customer assistant, a warming experience and excellent customer engagement. I can’t stress how much I understand what retail workers go through, I witnessed multiple angry customers pass through whilst I sat close-by. Its a shame that some people see these people providing an excellent service as lesser than themselves.

A great experience, thank you

Never had any issues before, but they've installed ordering screens instead of ordering it in person. Would fully recommend avoiding, the sandwich they made me had loads of salad items missing and the salad they did put on was pathetic. I didn't dine in, so I paid £10 for a sandwich with hardly anything on it.

I Never usually put reviews, but good work has to be appreciated.

I Was served by a colleague named Umair. One of the finest and sweetest employee ever in subway. We went in 5 minutes before closing and he was still patient with us and happily helped us throughout our order process.

Definitely deserves all the praise and appreciation. Great Job!

Don't come if you're in a rush. The new self service tills are so slow it'll take at least twice as long to use as talking to a person. There was only one person behind the counter doing god knows what for the first 10 minutes, because it took us 16 whole minutes from when we ordered to get our sandwich, and he didn't give us our sides until we asked for them. To top it all off, our Veggie Delite sandwich featured what is clearly a piece of chicken (see photo), so if you have any dietary restrictions, I wouldn't trust them for cross-contamination if I were you!
Cross contamination happening with veg food being fried in the same oil as meats. Didn't enjoy the taste of chicken on my hash browns. Won't be ordering such things from Subway or this place anymore!

Came in on Sunday evening, 14/09/25, two of the most incompetent staff I’ve ever seen in my life working. Why on earth they have the self service tills now I’ll never know. Completely useless. Waited 25 minutes for a sandwich and it was still wrong. Please don’t come here.

Went to Subway Manchester Fort today at around 10:30am. Bought a footlong with half chicken tikka and half rotisserie style chicken.

After eating half of my rotisserie style, I thought I’d save the other half for later. As I went to pack my sandwich up, I seen that there was a hair on the sweetcorn on my sandwich.

I went to complain and ask for half a refund and the person on the till (never seen her before) said she can’t give me a refund because I’ve already ate half. I told her that im asking for a half a refund because I ate half a subway. She said she will make a replacement but having hair in my subway put me off, so I declined that offer.

She also did not apologise about the hair being in my sandwich, and first tried denying that there was hair on it.

It’s a shame because I’m a regular customer and told the lady on the till in private so other customers wouldn’t hear me say there was a hair on my sandwich.

Not coming here again. Spent money for a footlong subway and ended up only having half.

Great staff

Clean place

What you expect from a subway

Seating area is decent sized

Keep up the good work

We got a couple of sandwiches and two coffees cost was 13 pound 50ish for the meals, coffee was around a two-star pretty below Average Sandwiches was a four Star and was quite nice and fresh. I would go back again for a sandwich but I wouldn't go back there for coffee. The weight was pretty long but it is fresh food service so if you want fresh you have to wait so I'm not complaining about that. The staff was pleasant enough and the atmosphere was quiet and the price is quite standard for the area.

You can’t really go wrong with a subway sandwich.

Get to pick all the ingredients and meats you want, relatively fresh and packed full of ingredients.

The queue is relatively quick and fast when ordering.
Always great service and friendly staff here. It's the only subway I like going to in the area, it's always busy so you know they're doing something right.

This is a very good Subway location and one of my favourites in town right now, in the Arndale Food Court. The gluten-free selection is growing, including bread and jacket potatoes, with staff being trained on dealing with allergens and willing to wipe everything down as needed to reduce cross-contamination.

Service is fast and friendly, and my go-to order right now — a jacket potato with butter, salt, pepper, cheese, tuna mayo, sweetcorn, and red onion — is made to a good standard.

It can be busy sometimes, and I do feel cautious coming here during peak times, but the sandwich artists usually try their best.

Pricing is reasonable and in line with any other Subway, with my potato costing £6.19. If planning to use the Subway Rewards app, I’d say to have it ready ahead of time, as it can sometimes have issues when in the food court..

Today we decided to take Subway for the first time in Arndale, which is located near Aldi. We have already eaten in this chain and know that there is a menu for £ 3.99, which includes a snack and a drink with a sandwich. Throughout the service we asked what is included and what is not included in this sandwich to get the menu for £ 3.99. We asked the staff what we can include and agreed on all the ingredients, because there are no self-service screens there. But at the checkout we overpaid twice, because our order was not correct, although we agreed on each ingredient with the staff. It was the worst experience and I do not recommend anyone to eat here, because you will be deceived and you will overpay. We are hungry, because this food was not enough. Not satisfied, because for this money it was possible to eat in another restaurant. This was our last visit, we will never return, and we will also tell all our friends and acquaintances about this terrible experience.

Not very good for vegetarian and people who done want meat. I ordered Tuna and the lady used who served ham and chicken Etc. smothered my bread with it. The salad person also didn't care.
Not for everyone.

Pretty poor experience at this Subway in Arndale. As you can see from the images, the lad that made it squeezed it tight, and added about 3 pieces of red pepper, few pieces of onion and 3 jalapeños! I know the cost of living is an issue, but I didnt know we were rationing salads! I'd avoid this place.

The staff was quite rude, and the drinks tasted more like water than anything else. As for the baguette, it was really disappointing — it felt like eating plain bread with some sauce. You could hardly taste the chicken or the salad inside.
A total scam place. You bay almost 10 pounds you get a bread with a trace of chicken and vegetables in it. Stay away from this place and go and buy yourself proper food. Never visit again.

Before ordering, I strongly recommend peeking through the kitchen door—you might lose your appetite and save yourself the trouble. If food poisoning is on your bucket list, congratulations, you’ve found the perfect Subway!

The dirtiest food establishment I have ever come across. I had remind the lady to change her gloves as she was touching everything around her. One of the ladies has very long hair she did have a hat on however her ponytail was so long when she turned to take the sandwiches out the oven her hair was touching the sandwiches on the line🤢. Just save yourself and go to the Deansgate branch not worth any illness you will most probably get.
I’m a regular customer I come for the good food and I enjoy it and the amazing customer service by Priya fantastic approach I will highly recommend this place
The staff were very bad, I ordered fries, this amount! My last visit to this restaurant, my advice to anyone do not come to this restaurant

Got served by a lad who took the time to give taste tests of the sauces to my mate before he ordered. Absolute legend. Impeccable choice of tunes. Perfect breeze from outside on a crisp early-autumn evening. 10/10 would recommend. Most welcoming liminal space on this side of the pacific north-west.
Vasantha was brilliant at crafting my Sub. I absolutely enjoy everybite. Friendly and helpful. This Subway just got 5 star and the credit goes to thier amaizng sandwich artist Vasantha!

Come here often but my last visit was particularly amazing. Aman served me and he was all about customer service. You just know he's passionate about what he does. He knew his stuff and I'll definitely be coming back.

Using his hands for all the ingredients disgusting and the portions were tiny because he didn’t use the scoop. Also took it out half way through toasting because he was clearly in a rush even though im the only one there. A disgrace to the brand.

Just ordered Veggie Breakwich via Uber Eats and they have added Sausage and Becon in it.
I am Hindu by religion and a vegetarian. Tried calling them, but no response.

Avoid, these people can't even read simple instructions.

I don’t usually do reviews as I am an hospitality manager myself, however this was so unfair on the one member of staff who was actually doing his job (amazingly).
Awful customer service (not from the one person working, he was incredible and deserves a raise!!)

Wait times are wait times, they can’t be helped. I’m paying £10 for a sandwich ( £9.19 to be exact), I don’t expect staff to be stood around laughing at the one team member doing his job, it’s completely unfair on him.

I had a breakfast sub delivered and it was freezing 🥶  I went to the shop to see if they would change it and was told because I selected not toasted on the bread they couldn't help me???? The egg bacon  hash brown were all freezing and the sausages wasn't even cooked     i only asked them to change it  and basically chased out the shop for complaining  it is disgusting

Ordered delivery
Got a chicken and bacon sandwich they didn’t even toast it sent it freezing cold
Hash browns came freezing cold
Cheesy bites came freezing cold
Haven’t bothered to heat anything up in the slightest will not be eating or going back here again

Been coming here for years, its always been our favourite branch. Lately however it has turned into the local youth club!
I draw the line at having to dine with tweenagers shouting, Vaping, swearing, punching and kicking holes out of each other!
gave me wrong order and didnt even care

We were served by the lovely Shazzie/Shayze? tonight. He was patient and kind and exceptional service. The store closes at 6 and we got there at 5:45. This poor guy only gets paid until 6 but is still expected to serve until 6. SUBWAY MANAGEMENT THIS IS NOT ACEPTABLE! He was so lovely and kind and is a very valuable employee. Well done Shazzie!! I hope you are recognised for your hard work!!

Subway has always been a convenient option for a quick meal, and I have visited various branches many times over the years. The biggest strength of Subway is the ability to customise your sandwich exactly the way you like it, with a good range of bread, fillings, vegetables, and sauces. The food is generally fresh, and the portion sizes are fair for the price. For someone looking for a lighter, healthier alternative to typical fast food, it still remains a solid choice.

That being said, my experience with Subway has been mixed, which is why I feel three stars is the right rating. The consistency between different outlets can vary greatly—sometimes the vegetables are crisp and the bread fresh, but other times the bread feels dry or the ingredients are not as appealing. Service also depends on who is working; some staff are friendly and efficient, while others seem rushed and uninterested, which affects the overall experience.

The atmosphere is another area that could use improvement. Many branches feel basic and not always the cleanest, which takes away from the enjoyment of eating in.

Overall, Subway is reliable for a quick bite but does not always deliver the same standard every time, leaving room for improvement.

Would you like me to make this feedback general for all Subway outlets worldwide or specific to a particular branch?

Horrible customer service, really made me feel very anxious, I’m autistic and I felt very unwelcome. Everything I said was wrong, the person behind the counter wasn’t impressed that I didn’t know what to have. I tried to make conversation about it being cooler inside the shop than in my non air conditioned van, to which she they replied “no it’s not, we’ve got all these ovens and fridges throwing out hot air”, then when I decided what to have she asked me what salad. I picked my choice and asked for double onions and pickles, I was told “no, it’s a set amount and you can’t have anymore” so ve been coming to subway for years and I’ve always asked for the same and never been told I can’t have double. However it’s not just that she said no, it’s that she was abrupt and she didn’t stop going on about it and even when I told her I was feeling anxious she carried on, only stopping when I said I’m autistic. I told her “I’m feeling anxious and emotional and I need to leave, I really felt like she was trying to humiliate me in front of my colleague. She then said “sorry I didn’t know” which annoys me because as far as I’m aware I don’t legally like have to have it tattooed on my forehead nor do I have to announce it!

It’s not even like they were busy, there was only me and my colleague in there, no other cars in the car park. I told her ‘please just let me pay and I’ll leave and I won’t come back” I really felt like I was in the way and not worthy of her kindness.

Wow what a Subway, one of my favourite places to be in the world. Best sandwiches anyone could ask for. Megan and Erin made my sandwich ready in minutes while being so very extremely busy.

Subject: Complaint about Subway Service and Staff Behavior

Dear, Subway Authority/Customer Service

I am writing to express my dissatisfaction with the service I recently experienced on the subway. Not only was the service itself problematic, but I also encountered very unprofessional behavior from staff.
The staff member I interacted with did not respect proper language and instead shouted at me in a rude and unhelpful manner. This behavior was completely unacceptable and made the situation much more stressful. As a passenger, I expect clear communication and respectful treatment, not shouting or disrespectful language.

Some concerning cross contamination. Went for the first time and was shocked to see bits of meat (including pork) and tuna in the salad bar. Very dangerous for people who have allergies and unfair to anyone that doesn’t want their lettuce infused with ham…There were breadcrumbs everywhere, a sticky counter at the till and it’s just generally disgusting.

A little mess in the midst of a lunch rush might be justified, but this was about 15 minutes before closing and there were no other customers, so why was it so messy?

The pre filled sub bag (from too good to go) was leaking with sauce. We showed this to the lady who just shoved another bag down on the counter and rushed to the back before we got chance to say anything. The baked potato had been packed upside down and the only edible things were the cookies. Thankfully we were refunded by too good to go because that bag was not worth £5.50.

If you have allergies (or standards…) DO NOT EAT HERE.

Fantastic staff !! My daughter has a seizure while waiting for her subway while on a shopping trip with her friend. Thee staff couldn’t do enough to help her at a very busy time. When her mom went to thank the manger he said that someone had complained and said she should have been moved out of the way. So the manager was fuming and refused to serve her. I’d just like to thank the staff and everyone for understanding on the 22nd December around 12. The staff are a credit to subway

There was a man and woman working today, it was extremely slow and the lady wasn’t very helpful and didn’t exactly make our food the best, although, the man who served us today was absolutely amazing!! He was so so lovely and gave the best service - making up for the poor experience prior to the till, I wish I caught his name but we ate at around 2 - lovely lovely guy and we appreciate everything you did to make our experience better!!!

This is the worst Subway I've ever been to. They skimp on the fillings way more than other Subways. Plus, they don't even seem to know their own menu – they'll charge you for stuff they don't even put in your sandwich.

I went there to have a coffee while studying for an hour, but I found the environment so dirty that I decided to leave before my coffee was even finished...

I ordered a Pumpkin Spice Caramel Macchiato for £6, expecting at least something resembling the drink you advertise on your menu boards. Instead, what I received was nothing more than an overly sweet mixture with an artificial spiced taste, completely lacking the balance and richness of coffee. It was far from what you show in the picture – a misleading joke, honestly.

If you are unable to prepare a beverage that even comes close to the product you promote, you simply should not advertise it at all. Customers should not be deceived into spending money on something that feels like a mockery of your own marketing.

On top of that, the environment of the store itself was extremely unpleasant. The tables were sticky, dirty, and clearly not cleaned, making it uncomfortable even to sit down and drink. For a company of Starbucks’ reputation, this level of neglect is completely unacceptable.

I feel not only disappointed but also a sense of regret and even guilt for having chosen to spend my money and time in your store. I trusted the brand and walked away feeling cheated.

I'd popped in for coffee and pastries just after 8am today and things seemed disorganised as they were running a bit behind.

I went back at lunch time to pick up a coffee for my husband and the 2 same baristas were there plus one other. Two are making drinks, and the one who made my morning coffee was collecting dirty crockery, 2 items at a time.

There are about 6 people waiting for their order and then the crockery collector looks up and sees another 4 of us wanting to place an order. Our orders are taken efficiently, but then one of the basritas disappears and the other goes to talk to the maintenance man half way through a customer's order. The third started topping up the already full tray of clean mugs...The second barista came back and got on with wiping the sink down...

The man next to me watched in disbelief as customers were being ignored and called over "is no one making coffee now?". He is blanked. I call over "Can someone make coffee please?" The barista who wasn't doing anything now she's finished counting mugs snaps at me "There are things GOING ON HERE!".

WOW said the man next to me. I replied to the employee "you should have let us know when you took our payment", and she snapped back "YOU can just WAIT!"

GASP from the man at my side... A coffee was slapped on the counter - "Do you think its yours? She's not making eye contact at all now" he said as another coffee was slid in his general direction.

Luckily they were for us and so we could finally leave and shake off that unpleasant atmosphere.

Absolutely disgusting I'm a typ1 diabetic! I always ordered iced green tee with sugar free syrup. Today I received a syrup with sugar which I checked with my meter. You guys could have killed me with this shit now I'm running more high. Thank you for the worst service ever

Peter taking orders randomly instead of the ones who ordered and queue. Hes rude, unprofessional and i can see even stuff is tired of him

I am a very old and loyal Starbucks customer, with over 20,000 stars on the app, and I usually experience friendly and professional service. Unfortunately, my recent visit was very disappointing due to the behavior of a staff member called Anastasiia.

I understand she may be new, but her attitude was completely opposite to Starbucks’ customer service standards. I asked politely if I could use my Blue Light Card discount and showed it to her on the app, which was fine. However, while I was on my phone, she looked at me in a way that made me feel uncomfortable, as if I was taking the discount from her own money.

When she noticed me taking a screenshot of my own phone, she said I was not allowed to screenshot the card or use a picture. I found this very inappropriate. What I do on my personal phone is not her concern, and it felt like unnecessary interference rather than customer service. Staff should understand their boundaries and focus on serving customers respectfully.

This experience really disappointed me, especially as a loyal customer who normally enjoys Starbucks. I am happy to share my customer ID with Starbucks customer service if needed. Please advise and train your staff at this branch to treat customers with respect and professionalism.

Dear Starbucks Team colliers wood,

I’m writing as a loyal and long-time customer who has always appreciated the Starbucks experience. However, I recently visited one of your stores and felt compelled to share some honest feedback regarding the current state of operations.

The store was in a very poor condition — there were cups and rubbish scattered everywhere, and the top seating area was closed off before 6 PM, even though the store stays open until 8 PM. This created a very unwelcoming and neglected atmosphere.

Additionally, several staff members were having loud personal conversations, including speaking in their own language behind the counter. While I respect and value diversity, this, combined with the complaining and lack of professionalism, made the environment feel more like a staff break room than a welcoming space for customers. It became difficult to relax and enjoy my coffee.

As someone with over 20 years of experience in the food and beverage industry, I fully understand the challenges that come with the job — especially under pressure or with lower wages. But it's crucial to maintain a professional atmosphere and prioritise the customer experience. When the team is disengaged, it reflects poorly on the brand.

I genuinely believe this store has potential. With stronger leadership, more support for staff well-being, and better focus on customer service, it could easily perform at a much higher level. Happy, well-managed staff not only improve day-to-day operations but also drive better KPIs and overall business growth.

Please take this feedback as coming from a friendly and loyal customer who cares about the brand. I hope these issues can be addressed to ensure that every store lives up to the high standards Starbucks is known for.

Thank you for your time,
Adlene

I come to this Starbucks almost every morning before work and the team has always been friendly. I’ve never had an issue with this branch until today. I ordered a toffee nut latte and waited around 3-4 minutes, as it was understandably busy. When my drink was called out, it was announced as a vanilla latte. I kindly let the barista know that I had ordered a toffee nut latte. She then asked me to go back to the checkout, and I genuinely thought she was going to correct the order or refund the difference - I was already prepared to say “it’s okay, these things happen”..

Instead she told me I had to pay extra because the toffee nut latte costs more. The extra 1 pound itself is not the problem. What upset me was the impression I was given, as if I had deliberately ordered a cheaper vanilla latte and then pretended it was a mistake so I could get the more expensive drink. That is absolutely not what happened and it felt very uncomfortable to be treated that way.

I asked why I was being charged extra for something that wasn’t my fault and what made it worse was her reaction. She smirked, looked at her coworkers and laughed. This made me feel as though she genuinely believed I was trying to get away with something. It was embarrassing and disappointing.

I kindly ask that the team review the camera footage from when I placed my order, as it clearly shows me asking for a toffee nut latte. Being made to feel like I was acting dishonestly was unfair and very upsetting.

I visit this store frequently and I truly believe the situation could have been handled with kindness. Not asking me to pay an additional £1 for a mistake that wasn’t mine - especially after waiting for the wrong drink and being put on the spot -would have been the very least gesture of good service.
The service is really great, my son loves to have babychjno here. I’d like to thanks Amy and Zeina last time they helped me with coffee beans, and did a really tasty coffee

I am writing to express my disappointment with the experience I had at your Hammersmith King Street, London location earlier today. I have always enjoyed visiting Starbucks for its pleasant environment and friendly service, but my recent visit was far from satisfactory.

First and foremost, the cleanliness of the toilet area was extremely poor. When I entered, I was appalled to see rubbish scattered all over the floor and an overflowing bin. Even more concerning were the numerous tissues with blood on them and a used tampon left beside the toilet. This is not only unhygienic but also poses a serious health risk to customers.

Additionally, the service I received was not up to the standard I have come to expect from Starbucks. The staff were not as friendly or welcoming as usual, and orders were taken without noting customers' names. This led to a mix-up between my order and that of another gentleman, causing unnecessary confusion and delay.

Overall, my experience at the Hammersmith King Street location was very disappointing. I hope you will address these issues promptly to ensure better service and hygiene standards for all customers in the future.

Absolutely Poor service, not reach Starbucks standard in my point of view . I have order a grande latte I have got coffee just a quarter to full . Quite upset !!! Lovely 🥰 place good 😊 people, perfect 👌 coffee caramel Frappuccino 😜❤️

Thank you for making my day on 13th Feb, your little drawings really made my week!

The best Starbucks in UK.. nothing yo do with the venue. But the people that work in there are so lovely - you should reward them i wish  i would new the names. Everyone works so hard. Congratulations as its a busy place

Very nice Starbucks, I saw a review on here about someone being sacked whilst being pregnant which is unfortunate but the Starbucks branch itself is pretty - however, it doesn’t seem like they treat their own staff well, I overheard a member of staff telling someone who appeared to be her manager that her knees are killing her and the manager didn’t really say much.

​Whether you’re a local professional or a tourist visiting the nearby Tower of London, this Starbucks at Tower Place is arguably one of the most stunning locations in the city. I’ve visited many branches, but this one stands out for its unique blend of modern design and historic views.
​The Ambiance: A View Like No Other
​The highlight here is the upstairs mezzanine level. It offers a panoramic view of the Tower of London and the Thames, creating a striking contrast between the ultra-modern interior and the ancient stone of the fortress. It’s the perfect spot to sit with a laptop or a book; the natural light and the sight of The Shard in the distance make it feel far more premium than your average high-street coffee shop.
​The Menu: Seasonal Favorites
​I recently tried the Tiramisu Cream Iced Latte, and it was exceptional—rich mascarpone-flavored foam with a perfect dusting of mocha powder. If you’re looking for something lighter, the Melon Pearl Refresha is a great "pro tip" for tea lovers; the fruity pearls add a fun, bubble-tea-style texture that’s very refreshing on a warmer day.
​Service & Perks
​The staff here are remarkably efficient despite the heavy foot traffic from commuters. Pro Tip: If you’re staying in-store, take advantage of the free refills on brewed coffee. Also, don’t forget to bring a reusable cup to get 25p off your drink—it adds up!
​Overall, for the views alone, this is a must-visit "Bucks" in the heart of London.

Had a really good experience here.
ALINA she was very nice and friendly. She is efficient, attentive, and really makes me feel welcome. Thank you for providing truly excellent service:)🤩

I would say I have been a loyal customer as I have been buying coffee from Starbucks every single day. Recent developments have make me frustrated and disappointed. Why I’m so disappointed? In my area so many special Starbucks shops have been closing down where you had comfortable seating, cozy light and brilliant friendly staff. Brompton Road shop, Holland Park shop and Chelsea shop in Kings Road are closed now. There you could park with your car shortly and crab a coffee. For e.g in Brompton Road you can’t imagine how many cars with police officer, traffic warden, fire fighters and NHS driver dropped by to get a coffee every single day. Even to make a walk nearby was possible. With their new shops and concept, I can find in my area from 5 now only 2 with no washroom facilities, located nearby bus stops, super bright light, small, loud, banging doors or nearby hospitals ? (Who wants to go there if you are not sick except it’s very good  for the nhs workers) I feel that an essential part of what a coffee shop suppose to do has vanished. Therefore my favourite coffee shop now has become coffee Nero. In their shops I’m finding all those things I miss in Starbucks unfortunately. People are invited to seat comfortable, quietly in the morning to read a newspaper and welcome to start the day in peace. The quality of coffee is the same except I miss the pistachio one.

Good:
- during off-peak hours, it's not so busy and the place is filled with a scatter of people typing away at their laptops. There's the occasional loud child, but they don't stay for long
- refurbished: there's more sockets and more tables and chairs to sit down and work in

Bad:
- during peak hours, it is packed with loud children and parents talking away (as with any other café)
- rude and dismissive staff: a lot of the time, the starbucks workers aren't proficient in English, so it's hard to understand what they're saying (and vice versa) which makes them angry at us (this doesn't always happen). Also, the staff aren't great at explaining the policy they have to follow which often irritates me since they just say that they have to charge me more money instead of why
- loud staff: today's staff are currently shouting at each other from across the counter which makes it loudenough for the entire café to hear. ATMOSPHERE RUINED.
- expensive drinks: not always great value for money (i don't recommend the green tea latte - there are blobs of green tea powder at the bottom which are often bitter)

Rozi, we asked her 2 iced American coffee with two sizes of white mocha sauce, the little one without water and the middle with water, so I made them 3 times wrongly, but this is not my actual problem, my problem is that I saw her pouring espresso As it is in Standard, the drinks are done in the right way, and after more than 20 seconds, I wanted to pour it into my cup, and I was a supervaser before in Starbucks, and I know how long it is to actually put the espresso on the drink before the taste changes. Izabela is the Bomb! Thank you for an amazing service!

Visited Starbucks at Battersea Power Station today and was served by Naveen — absolutely amazing service! 🙌 He was super helpful, patient, and friendly. I tried brewed coffee for the very first time, and he made the experience really easy and enjoyable. Thank you, Naveen, for the great recommendation and making my visit special! ☕

This is a busy Starbucks location at Battersea Power Station, which opened as part of its transformation back in 2022. I loved the art-deco-inspired décor, although it could have been more practical, with people crowding around the same areas, and I did struggle to get a table — which shouldn’t have been such a struggle given the size of the café.

I went for an Iced Gingerbread Latte. Prices were in line with other London Starbucks, costing slightly more than elsewhere in the UK. My drink quality was OK, but I’ve had better at Starbucks — the espresso here tasted slightly burnt, and my drink lacked sweetness.

The baristas were busy, so service felt unfriendly and impersonal. Even the water jug was by the condiments bar, so it was a get-your-own kind of deal.

Plenty of other places for coffee in Battersea, and I feel, other than looking pretty, they need to do more to be worth a revisit.

Very disappointing experience at the Starbucks in Battersea Power Station.
The staff member who served me (Masum) was rude and unprofessional and spoke to me in an embarrassing way in front of other customers. When I asked for help or to speak to a manager, he claimed he was the manager but showed no customer service or leadership skills.

He also didn’t seem to know how to use the Starbucks system or apply Rewards offers and made no effort to help. This behaviour is unacceptable, especially for such a prominent location. I expect much better from Starbucks.

So I ordered a drink and a croissant while my drink was good and fast, I just want to remind them about the croissant. The female employee was telling to the male one, who took my order at the till. The staff are kind, quick and polite.

I want to express my outrage at the way they treated me today regarding the restroom in the establishment. For two months now, I have been buying coffee and breakfast from here every morning. It has struck me that both restrooms are permanently closed under the pretext that they are not working! 99% of the time it is like that. But at the same time, I have personally witnessed several cases in which "close" customers receive a key for them from the staff. This morning I politely asked for the key, but was told that the restrooms are not working!!! AGAIN!!! And since I doubt this information already, I asked if they could open it to make sure that it is so. The lady I spoke to said that she could not, because the key was with the manager Peter and he is down in the basement. I asked her to call him, she went and never came back. Neither she, nor the said Peter. I don't know about you, but for me this is extremely shameful and embarrassing on their part. The Law clearly states that restaurants that offer seating and food for consumption on site MUST have a working toilet for their customers?!? In this case - is it permissible to work after this important condition is not met? At the moment, all kinds of viruses and bacterial infections are circulating, how come they allow themselves to be a breeding ground for such??? In the case of an open toilet (1 out of 15), it is very, very dirty. I don't see any reason why, when 8 people work per shift, why doesn't the toilet clean itself every hour? They are lazy or don't care about our health. You can always see staff loitering, and even the tables are dirty. There were 4 baristas of Asian origin who were very nice and polite, in stark contrast to the other employees who clearly didn't care that they were lying to your face. It became very unpleasant and unfortunate for that.
really nice people , shout out to that indian and filo guy who served me today !!!! 🫶🏽🫶🏽

I was waiting at the door with another person watching the staff scurry around getting ready etc. At 6 opening time,  nobody came to the door. 3 minutes after 6 a woman came near the door ignored us and proceeded to lift supplies near the door. A few minutes later a tall man comes to open the door, he looked away while we came in. No apology or acknowledgement of my thanks. Unfortunately no matter how good service is,  staff can defeat all the good work of others. The coffee was good, the place was clean mostly but not entirely looking at the stand where sugar is. I was just not impressed, maybe it was just not a good morning for them.
I was shocked to see multiple flies inside both pastry cases, walking all over the pastries. Staff did not seem to notice or care. Completely unacceptable from a food establishment.

The staff are so friendly (think her name is Anna). The cafe is also clean apart from the toilets ( which I dont intend to use). The pastries are also the same like other Starbucks but would be nice if they had a more variety like i have come across in central london but thats ok. The coffee is very nice and the staff do meet the expectations. Not all staff know how to do a coffee, i have been to Starbucks that I was served watery coffee for example but here it is not the case. The prices are also normal. The seats in the sofa are often taken which is annoying but apart from that nothing to be said. There is space outside when there is nice weather. There is a square outside so nice views from outside.
Best staff especially Mike who goes out his way to make you feel welcome.  10 stars 🌟

Good coffee, clean, fair priced. Loads of seating and air conditioned nicely. On weekends there’s plenty free parking in the area. Across the board it’s a standard Starbucks. Good range of cold coffees and drinks too. If your looking for a bit of a cold sweet treat and you’ve never tried their ‘frappachina’ probably thats how it’s spelt.

I am very upset with the attitude of the short girl(Chiara). That's second time my coffee was messed up by her. She apears to be very arrogant and rude and clearly don't know how to make a simple Americano. That's the coffee made by her. I am awaiting explanation from Starbucks. Is this how the coffee should look like!!!???

I was served by a short young lady with braces, unfortunately I didn’t get her name but she was horrible. She made my drink incorrectly despite it being labelled on the cup. I told her politely and she wasn’t apologetic at all, she just grabbed the cup and remade the drink then slammed it on the counter which was very rude and embarrassing. I’ve recently moved to the area and this was my first experience at this Starbucks, I haven’t returned since, I now go to the one on Clapham high street as the staff are much friendlier and more welcoming.
Perfectly  competent service and Frappuccino.  Can't say I experienced the poor service some have mentioned.

This shop purposely denies accepting Starbucks gift card as payment method. They only accept cash or card. I had been here several times within 2 months and they claimed the machine is out of order every time. 2 months!!!! Isn’t ridiculous for Starbucks not helping this shop to fix the problem for 2 months? Maybe the manager here just wishes to increase cash sales or to keep loyal customers away from this shop. Anyway, if I encounter this issue next time, I will help them to report this issue to head office.
Remarks: all other shops I visited within the UK accepted gift card as payment method. Only rejected by this shop.
The atmosphere was nice and the coffee was delicious.
Shoutout to Roshan and Sky for giving me a much needed paper bag😄

In a hidden corner of London Victoria near to platform 17 I found about the only relatively quiet place to sit and kill an hour before my train. No queue, clean and tidy throughout, well stocked fridge and very friendly service. It’s the simple things that matter and when asking for a spoon, the customer service barrister wrapped it in a spoon to hand it to me, not required but very thoughtful - simple things.

I recently visited this Starbucks location and was extremely disappointed with the service at the counter. Two staff members (who I believe were named Mnih and his colleague) were completely inattentive to customers. They ignored me while chatting casually.

To make matters worse, they allowed another customer who arrived later to cut in line and place orders ahead of me. This was not only unfair but also reflects poorly on the store’s management and professionalism.

As a regular Starbucks customer, I expect efficiency, respect, and basic courtesy. Unfortunately, this experience was frustrating and left me feeling undervalued.

I bought a surprise bag through the Too Good to Go app. The staff were kind and asked me about allergies before quickly handing over the bag. It contained 3 sandwiches, cinnamon bread, and was delicious. It was great value for money and very tasty.

I’m not sure I’ve got the names spelled correctly, but Sagar and Besty were helped me find exactly the coffee I was looking for. They were incredibly helpful, kind, and professional. They honestly made my day. Highly recommend!

I never leave a review anywhere but i absolutely loved this starbucks. The team was so amazing and kind.  There was this young girl called zoey and she was an absolute sweetheart and was so patient with me and was so happy to serve the cutsomers!! She brightned up my day!!!
Overall a nice Starbucks store, plenty of seating and the staff are friendly. No bathroom though.

I got a surprise bag through the TooGoodToGo app with four lovely pastries for just £3.99. They were tasty.

In other shops, I sometimes get pastries worth less than I paid. But at Starbucks, the staff carefully checked the pastries' prices and packed them, which was very considerate. I was impressed.
Excellent customer service and a very nice environment. The staff were friendly and helpful, and the place felt welcoming. Highly recommended!

This branch needs some better management. At 1pm yesterday I watched them sell an Italian tourist two Chocolatey Mousse Lattes for her kids even though they were out of ingredients. When she tried to query it - her english wasn't the best - the short white female barista first told her quite rudely the drinks were right (they weren't) then that the chocolate was mixed inside (it wasn't). The tourist gave up (I don't blame her). It's a busy shop for sure but there's no excuse to treat any customers like that.
Rude, bad mannered, ignorant barista lacking basic communication skills.

Rude service, said gosh alright then when the woman was being rude and then she practically threw my drink onto the table, it wasn’t even mixed, she took no time or care into making the drink and i was the only one in there.

I have a fowl sour taste in my mouth as the milk had definitely gone off, would never recommend coming here !!!

Hana and Agata really made my day and put a smile on my face with their brilliant service! I was having a bad day and how attentive they were and knowledgeable about the menu really turned my day around and made me want to treat myself and my family after shopping in Sainsbury’s. Thank you both!
I oreder Venti size of Christmas Blend Eggnog Latte but it was came out like normal latte drink. A guy who made it he is like pratical. I never had this happened before

The staff spent 6 minutes chatting up, just to make three drinks, then number three came and another couple of minutes till the Asian guy chatted the new woman up.
And they wonder why there are so many less Starbucks in the city

Large, spacious and well-run. You may not be a fan of multi-nationals like Starbucks but it’s far better than the Sainsbury’s cafe before that they tried to reinvent several times.

Like other examples of this partnership, it feels calm and the Starbucks experience you expect despite being between checkouts and a trolley rank.

It’ll be hard to resist grabbing a coffee to alleviate the slog of grocery shopping and might help to turn Sainsburys into more of a destination.

As long as the friendly quality of staff is maintained it’s hard to see how this won’t be a success. I just wish they still served a cheap bowl of chips, but you can’t have both it seems.
The coffee made with sincerity is delicious as expected. It's savory and well-roasted. The staff is very friendly. I come here every week. The staff's smiles make me feel good.😊

Speedy service and kind staff! The abuse the workers and manager have to deal with from problematic members of public is very upsetting. The manager is always nice to customers and has turned this branch from a sad lifeless place to a much nicer better environment that I keep coming back to. Staff are better trained, coffee quality is much better and the atmosphere is truly great. The great manager always deals with conflict in a professional, customer centred way, and remains calm even when the customer isn’t in the right! Kudos to him and the team 😀

Convenient Starbucks in the shopping centre. The line can be long sometimes but soon moves quick enough. Saturday mornings can be quiet with plenty of seating (see video). I’ve come did some work here travelling between meetings which worked well.

I accidentally bought coffee beans instead of ground coffee, and it annoyed me for quite awhile and decided to try my luck at startbucks. But the staff were so kind and helped me grind them on the spot! It really made my day. I’ll definitely be going to Starbucks more often!

The Starbucks at Surrey Quays Shopping Centre, located at 13-15 Surrey Quays, Unit B, London SE16 7LL, is a popular spot among locals and shoppers. Due to its high foot traffic, finding available seating can be challenging, especially during peak hours. The store’s limited size contributes to this issue, suggesting that an expansion could enhance customer comfort.

While Starbucks is renowned for its coffee quality, some patrons have expressed concerns about the food offerings at this location. Specifically, the value for money regarding food items, such as the thinness of cake slices relative to their price, has been a point of critique. This indicates potential room for improvement in the food menu to meet customer expectations.

In summary, while the Surrey Quays Starbucks maintains the brand’s standard for quality beverages, addressing issues related to seating capacity and food value could significantly enhance the overall customer experience.
It’s a decent space and is busy most of the times

A solid Starbucks branch. The space is surprisingly huge, so it’s much easier to find a seat here than in other locations. The staff are lovely—very polite and efficient with orders. A reliable spot for a coffee break.

Life is hard in service based business where the customer is always right. But you know what? Show up as a customer who knows about customer service and enjoy the banter with the staff (especially when you're from our of town). We're here to make everyone's day a little brighter right?

So thank you to the lovely team who gave me A++++ service when I only ordered a coffee and oatmeal. The cafe was spacious, welcoming and bright and I was able to journal in peace.

Set me up for the day

Hello viewers!
Yesterday, London experienced heavy downpours. Although the weather did not put me off venturing out, taking the 185 Victoria to Dulwich Sainsbury's.
Where I collected my 'Too Good to Go' surprise pastry bag from STARBUCKS. Take a look at what I saved from going to waste!!! For as little as £2.59.

STARBUCKS was clean, welcoming, and cosy with a serene ambience. I was happily greeted by a staff member who readily acknowledged the 'reserve' on the 'Too Good to Go' app. I explained I was early because of the rain. While I waited, she took care, filling a logo paper bag with 'surprise' goodies 😊
I ordered   A acoffe and they served peaple after me and when I asked a women called  me rude , it is disgusting and unprofessional
Darren just saved our Macmillan Coffee Morning! What a fantastic team at this branch, a BIG THANK YOU to Darren and his team for their help you are both amazing 👏

Clean café with friendly staff and good coffee. Plenty of seating inside and out, and handy that there are toilets on site. Easy to order on the app too. A bit on the expensive side, but a nice place overall.
A really great experience i got served by sanket i had a really great experience. I'll give a cappuccino with hazel nut.And it arrived in very quick time

Went to have a coffee, waited round 3- 5 mins , infront of the bar , there was a guy making coffees ,also a girl stocking cups . No eye contact no farewell, waited there patiently , by the time she was ready to take an order another costumer walked infront of me . I told the girl staff I’m waiting for quite while well before , she rudely said yeah but the tills are here so !?  Just wanted to make a point how  and tell her how I am to know where the tills are and how orders are made here, as there there is any sign 🪧 or at least you can talk to people when you see them waiting around . Her answer was I am the manager and can refuse you to have coffee here if you speak to me like this 🤣after that answer got from her I just walked away and said to her actually even if it is free I’m not going to have one with the attitude you are showing and specifically when you are telling me you the manager. Wow the Starbucks really know how to train their staff / managers . Never again
Great starbucks! Nice drinks, food and atmosphere. However noticed on multiple occasions table and chairs get dirty and are not wiped often.

Majority of the times my orders arrived spilled everywhere and on the occasion when the drinks are not spilled, it’s not done properly.

The audacity to send out a peach ice tea looking like they just used the last drop of syrup, throw in together with ice and water and sent out to customers who paid £5.30 on deliveroo for it.

Seriously, when will the management actually step in and get their team trained properly to do the drinks with correct SOP?

Absolutely ridiculous

I often stop by the Starbucks in Greenwich on my way to work, and their pistachio & white chocolate loaf cake is my absolute favorite. So soft and super yummy, I've bought it several times already. Really love it!

Pretty sizeable Starbucks with 2 floors and outside seating. Situated at the edge of a retail shopping park. I've been here plenty of times and it's a bit hit and miss whether you will get a great coffee or a coffee where you only taste warm milk. Staff seem friendly, I can't help but feel sorry for them as it always seems a bit understaffed when it gets busy. It's popular with families with little kids/babies. Upstairs is my favourite spot and it's very relaxing and the chairs are comfy with floor to ceiling glass to view outside. There is no lift to get on the 1st floor.
If you are out shopping and need a break why not give it a whirl.

Quite possible the worst Starbucks I’ve ever been to (and I’ve been to a lot). It’s so bad that I travel 5 miles to the next closest Starbucks in Belvedere to avoid this one. Why? (1) the staff. There is a lady who is loud, rude and obnoxious. She looks at you as if you’ve crawled off her shoe. She screams your name and slurs her words, you ask her to repeat the name and she won’t do it. You ask her to add milk to a burnt oatmeal, she won’t do it…you get the message here. She clearly doesn’t want to be serving customers (2) the place is filthy. The counter areas where they work are covered in dirt, boxes and beans. The toilets are disgusting. We went one day at 9am and it was filthy. Clearly nobody cleans it as we were the first to use it that day. (3) coffee and food quality is awful. Coffees are cold. Separately, I ordered an oatmeal and it was burnt (see image). How do you burn oatmeal? Asked for it to be made again and they refused. Asked them kindly to add milk and they refused - “that’s how it comes”. I had to get a refund. I have never asked for a refund at Starbucks…it’s really a last resort.

If you’re the manager at this location, I would recommend employing a cleaner, firing the rude lady who serves coffees (local lass) and then firing yourself for running the world’s worst Starbucks.

First visit to this branch. Impressed with cleanliness and brightness. Very busy during time of visit. But staff managed well and friendly. My daughter feels the food/sandwiches fridge was over filled and un-tidy.

This is my fave local Starbucks however the staff can be very rude, most of the time they’re lovely and I never have a problem however the customer service I received today was appalling, the drinks and food was nice as usual but it wouldn’t have killed the staff that served me to be polite.

The place has amazing location.
The service is not the friendliest.
It is very busy specially weekend.
I understand it can be frustrating for the staff as there is no time to take even a little rest but smiling doesn’t cost anything, being grumpy is not something that working in customer service is acceptable.
I think our server messed our order a bit.
I realised when we been already away so I didn’t come back to fix it up.
Prices are ridiculously high tho for the festive drinks.
I ordered this toffee but latte and I asked for small one which was 6£!!!!
I think I was charged and given the middle size and my boyfriend who wanted medium sized Acai strawberry drink for small one instead.
We pains more than 12£ for 2 drinks!
This is a lot.
Fantastic place with great coffee and pleasant atmosphere. Big shout out to manager and staff, they were all awesome. Pumpkin Spice Latte was exactly what I needed for 7 a.m.!

I would like to share my sincere appreciation for Tara at the Starbucks Greenwich branch. She consistently delivers an exceptional level of service and professionalism every time I visit. From the moment customers walk in, Tara greets everyone with a warm smile and a friendly attitude, creating a welcoming and positive atmosphere.

What truly stands out about Tara is her professionalism, attention to detail, and genuine care for customers. She always ensures that orders are prepared accurately and promptly, while maintaining a calm and cheerful demeanour even during busy hours. It’s evident that she takes great pride in her work and values every customer’s experience.

Her ability to connect with people and make each visit pleasant reflects true dedication to customer service. Staff members like Tara make a real difference — she embodies the Starbucks values perfectly.

I truly appreciate her consistent efforts and would like to thank her for making every visit to Starbucks Greenwich such a pleasant experience.
Starbucks café is quite close to the Greenwhich pier. You can buy a take-away coffe. I liked nice and polite staff.

The flat white was subpar. It had none of that subtle sweetness you expect from steamed milk. Even the iced coffee was bland. You'd literally find better coffee from a fully automatic machine at a convenience store. Okay for a quick break, but not worth staying long.

Takeaway coffee this morning after a long exhausting shift. Execellent customer service from Swami (who served me the best Caramel latte with coconut milk - I've ever had!!!) And Sneha who wrote a very warm message on my cup. Both brightened up my day and both deserve a pay rise ☺️ much appreciated to both!! Satisfied customer
The girl and guy who make the baristas are awesome, I love them and love the coffee they make ❤️❤️
Service served by a lovely and friendly gentlemen swami, who recommended the pistachio hot chocolate and trust me I don’t regret it best Pistachio hot beverage I’ve had!

Avoid this poor excuse for a Starbucks like the plague (the amount of flies in this place, you'd be forgiven for thinking you were IN an actual plague)! This is the absolute worst Starbucks I have ever visited.

Full of dirty tables and about a dozen flies crawling all over the till area; this place is filthy. The floors aren't being swept/mopped regularly (sticky) and surfaces are not being wiped down.

Staff are unfriendly and seem more interested in their phones than delivering a basic level of customer service. If you need a straw, don't be surprised if the moody staff members huffily toss it on the dirty countertop for you. Some staff were not in the correct uniform and most weren't wearing name badges, which is unusual for a Starbucks. One member of staff was an exception to this; he was really good from what I observed.

My mocha was made incorrectly, gross and I had to throw it away, because there was no way I was going to complain to staff, especially after reading a "polite notice" to customers about "not abusing" staff.

I hated writing this review but these points need to be highlighted and immediately addressed.

This is the result of an Uber eats delivery. Due to bad and CARELESS packaging, everything in my order is ruined.
