A hot chocolate that wasn’t even hot after waiting 50 mins for the order to arrive, a coffee that didn’t even have what I asked for in it, 2 cookies RUINED and a half edible brownie. Absolutely disgusted, and do you think Uber eats will reply? Nope. Absolute joke of a service.

Been to other starbucks around London but this shop has made the best drinks (funny how same drinks but different place different taste) so cheers to baristas from this place! Especially Korean ones, they’re friendly and noticed me after couple visits.
I tried too good to go app and the various items I got were decent.
Really busy place during weekend

Very small and cramped Starbucks location. There’s considerably less space for customers in this outlet than most Starbucks, and there’s no restroom either. Their air conditioning seems to be broken as well, so there’s two giant fan units that make the interior feel a bit like a wind tunnel. The staff are prompt and friendly as usual, no issue there, but corporate seriously needs to consider this location for remodeling.

The Italian lady Betta and not too sure about the other one with long hair are the worst.
One can tell she wasn’t happy and stressed and her body langue was the worse.
The other one had been brave to say finally I didn’t order any extra with my coffee.
Worst costumer service.

Baristas/workers always greet you with big smile. Nice place.
They are located next to famous `blue door of notting hill`.

Unfortunately, this was the most disappointing Starbucks experiences my wife and I have had. From the moment we walked in, the atmosphere felt cold and unwelcoming. The short and big italian woman wearing a black apron who served us was noticeably unfriendly and seemed irritated by our presence, which made the entire experience uncomfortable. The lack of basic courtesy created such a negative vibe that we ended up not even drinking what we ordered. We left feeling unwelcome and disappointed. Shame that we experienced this in Portobello Road.

This actually has to be the best cappuccino I have tasted and I am not a fan of overtly sweet coffee. This cup was frothy to perfection, brilliant temperature. Really nice staff on shift.
Thank you to Ideama. The place was clean, being inside westfield, warm and pleasant

Aaron and Abdul made my whole Starbucks experience. The care when helping me decide my flavour preferences and the service was genuinely 5 star. Zumra also gave the sweetest smile to us as customers. I thank this team so much for a lovely Starbucks experience!

Quite embarrassing to see that none of the staff feel bothered enough to maintain a level of cleanliness at this place. I see at least 7 staff in the counter area and not a single one of them seem interested at all in stepping out of their comfort zone to at least clear n clean the tables. Shambolic at best.
Unfortunately, my drink was filled mostly with ice, so there was very little drink inside. Disappointing for the price.

Absolutely unacceptable hygiene at this location. The floor was dirty and full of trash, tables had leftover food and drinks, and I saw mosquitos on the food in the display case. I’ve posted a video to show the conditions. This place needs serious attention from management.
Professional and fast service. Very good atmosphere. Very clean place. I recommend it.

I ordered a Lavender Latte (£6.10) via the app and went to the store to pick it up. The app showed that my order was ready, yet when I arrived, the staff were busy making hot drinks for other customers. I waited patiently for them to finish, but when I finally asked about my order, I was told that the Lavender Latte was unavailable. Shockingly, I was informed that they could not process a refund and instead asked if I wanted to choose another drink.

I agreed and selected a Matcha Latte (large, £5.10). The staff initially assumed the two drinks were the same price, but I pointed out the £1 difference. Again, I was told that their system could not refund the difference. This is completely unacceptable and absurd for any service-oriented business.

After waiting over 20 minutes in total, I finally received my Matcha Latte, which turned out to be mostly foam. I only realized this after I left the store. I had to return and ask if they could remake

This experience was frustrating, disappointing, and a complete waste of my time. I sincerely hope you will review your service processes and train your staff to handle such situations more professionally.

Can be a very long wait in the morning to get a coffee, even when only semi-busy, longer than other Starbucks branches for some reason... Could do with some blinds on the windows. Apart from that, just a Starbucks.

Ridiculous branch.
I had bought an iced venti caramel macchiatto coffee in the morning and because I have been in meetings all morning I didn't manage to drink it so at around 3 pm when I finoshed my meetings my coffee was melted and warm and still not drunk. I know that Stsrbucks are all about changing your coffee if you need to, hence why the high price, so I went back and asked very kindly if there is any chance they would redo it because I didn't manage to drink it and it was still full. The girl said ok but then her manager came in spoke to her in front of me in a foreign language so I don't understand what they say and said no we can't. She then changes her mind and said ok but "only for this time". Then she prepared the "new coffee" which I thought was dine in nanoseconds  I was wondering how she prepared such a drink so quickly. She then gave me the coffee and reminded me again that "they don't do that and it is only for me". Then I had a sip. And it was the same coffee. She gave me back thr same coffee with the melted ice cubes inside and the diluted milk and syrop and she had just added ice cubes inside to make it look new.
Of course the coffee was disgusting with all the melted water in it and plus new ice cubes inside so I threw it away.
Not only her manners were very unkind but she lied to me too. And all of this for what? To save a scoop of coffee? Is it made out of  gold? It wouldn't costed them 10 p and U was charged £5.5 for that coffee. She has no idea what service or manners is and this revolted me.

The staff are great, but the place is a furnace. The huge window traps all the sunlight. It needs some kind of film on the windows, or blinds - and some AC. I've been twice and it has been unbearably hot.

⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️
Outstanding Experience at Starbucks Marylebone Station!

I had an absolutely fantastic experience at this Starbucks location. From the moment I walked in, the customer service was brilliant—friendly, attentive, and genuinely welcoming. I was served by Forhad, who deserves special recognition for his professionalism and warm demeanor. He made sure I felt acknowledged and taken care of, exactly the way great customer service should be delivered.

I ordered a cappuccino, and I have to say—it was the best I’ve ever had to date. Perfectly balanced, rich, and incredibly smooth. Alongside the coffee, I tried the Cheese Bread, Almond Croissant, and a Tuna Panini—all of which were fresh, flavorful, and exceeded expectations.

Whether you’re stopping by for a quick coffee or something more substantial, this location delivers on every front. Highly recommend, and kudos again to Forhad for making my visit so memorable!

The staff are customer oriented. They are friendly and very polite. This is a nice stop in the morning after alight from train in Chiltern Railways. I am delighted to have a cup of hot chocolate to warmth during this winter time. Good job for the staff for starting their shift very early. They deserve an extra credit for it.

I ordered a large coffee, hoping to enjoy it before a long journey. Instead, I got a cup of watery liquid with a burnt taste — more like a puddle than a proper coffee. Really disappointing, especially when you expect a good drink to start your trip. Very misable experience

I don’t know how you can mess up a Starbucks coffee, but this was it. Three coffees, all barely drinkable. Americano tasted charred, hazelnut latte tasted like expired medicine. Normal latte was bearable, but definitely not as good as usual in other Starbucks.

Disappointed, and unlikely to come back.

Food’s okay and service was decent, although the manager should really learn the names of his own staff…

Rudest person behind the till on the 21 August, at 16:30 on shift. I asked two simple questions, matcha on the menu and if they take the points app. Rude, shouldn’t be in a customer facing role. Place is dirty and two of them stood behind the counter on their phone.

I would like to commend Jayati for her excellent service during my visit to Starbucks. She was very friendly, attentive, and welcoming, which made the experience truly pleasant. Jayati took the time to assist us with a warm smile and ensured everything was handled smoothly. Her positive attitude and professionalism really stood out and made our visit memorable. Thank you, Jayati, for such wonderful service.

I asked for quad espresso 3 white chocolate mocha sauce, light ice, shaken and pouring cream.

2nd item iced matcha green tea latte semi skimmed 4 vanilla syrup cream cold foam whipped cream.

The staff gave me half cup of espresso item. They don't even know what I ordered and they don't even know how to do it. The worst experience which I ever had in my life with Starbucks. I won't prefer this place anymore.

The woman was very nice and wished me a happy birthday, but the man working wouldn’t allow me to redeem a birthday treat because my app was the US version. I live in the UK now, and have never been stopped from using my Starbucks app to pay at UK stores. So it really sucks to find out the app now doesn’t work on the most inconvenient day. Especially when the birthday policy is exactly the same.

That doesn’t make any sense to me from a customer service perspective. Even if the app technically isn’t working to scan for a birthday treat, it shouldn’t stop you from giving someone who spends so much on coffee at Starbucks throughout the year a very reasonable drink on their birthday (I ordered a vanilla latte).

He told me I could have a coffee if I paid for it. I just left so I wouldn’t cry. Honestly ruined my day.

This coffee shop made my day! Nothing makes your day brighter than a little "thank you :)" written on your cup by a cashier. And on top of that, my latte was just the way I like it. Quick service with a smile to make your day.

Dennis is always very kind and remembers me every time I come in! He is always very personable. In general, it’s very very infrequent that i’ve ever had an issue with my drink here, and the one time I did- they apologised and gave me a free voucher to make it right. Love coming here before University, always a nice start to the day!
The employee Minar was really very kind and welcoming. He did not rush us on the contrary to take his time to advise us.

Small shop with limited seating and no toilet. The store was clean and bright. Good customer service, friendly staff and fast service.
The coffee is always good especially the white chocolate mocha.
Clean and bright Starbucks, but limited seating and no public restroom. The staff are friendly and prompt, however. This location gets substantially less busy than the one around the street as well.
Nice place to sit and watch the world go by. Coffee is good. The lack of toilet facilities is not ideal though…
Can’t thank the team here enough for always starting my morning right and putting a smile on my face. Thank you !!!!

I visited Starbucks this morning and was surprised to see that Ahmed, the staff member on duty, was working completely alone — taking orders, preparing food, and making all the drinks. Despite the pressure, he was incredibly efficient and polite. However, for such a large company, it’s unacceptable to have just one person running the entire store. It’s not only unfair on staff but also unsafe. Starbucks should ensure there is at least one additional team member scheduled to support operations.
Well done Ahmed and thank you for your service.

Went here at around 11.15am on Saturday and was served by the most lovely lady at the cash register, really friendly, bubbly and very upbeat. Some of the best service I have had, an asset to the Starbucks team.

⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️

I absolutely love this Starbucks in Maida Vale! The atmosphere is always warm and welcoming, and the team goes above and beyond to make every visit a great experience. A special thank you to the manager Lucio for running the store so smoothly and always greeting customers with such kindness. Ester, Etya, Enzo, and Magda are wonderful as well – friendly, attentive, and always serving with a smile. They truly make this Starbucks feel like a community spot, not just a coffee shop. Highly recommend!

My school is very close to this Starbucks so I go here 1-2 weeks and it is amazing. Especially ash. Very kind worker. Recently, there was this new worker who was very nice and in the end, he gave me a free cup which I have today!

This is one of my favorite places to work in the area. The quality of coffee is on par with what you'd expect from Starbucks, with a variety of drink options available. However, I must admit that I'm not a big fan of their food offerings in general.

The space is quite large and comfortable, but there have been occasions where the printer has been offline and they have missed online orders. However, the staff are quick to serve once they are made aware that an order is taking longer than expected. Overall, it's a great spot to work, study, or relax with a good cup of coffee.

Can't fault their service. A group of us wanted coffees, so ordered and got them, then our 2 year old decided she had to have one too. One off the group asked for a small cup, which they gave us, and poured a little bit of hot chocolate into it for her. The guys serving saw this happening, and poured a new hot chocolate in a small cup specially for her (cooled it with extra cream) and gave it foc.

Shortly after the 2 year old dropped the cup,I went back to buy a replacement, but they replaced it foc again.

Marked 5 star, only 2 for atmosphere as nothing can make a tube station have good atmosphere, but otherwise great.
The coffee was so hot you burn your hand even with the cup holder. The coffee was also not good at all. I would not go back at all.

The best Starbucks in KB!!

Lovely and welcoming staffs, always smiling and ask me how I am doing. Thank you so much for making my days brighter.

I had my first experience at Starbucks Coffee in London, and it was amazing! The food was delicious, fresh, and full of flavor. The coffee was rich and perfectly brewed, just as expected from Starbucks.

A special shoutout to Sadman Ahmed Sunny for his excellent service! He was incredibly friendly, attentive, and made my visit even more enjoyable. His warm attitude and professionalism truly stood out.

Friendly staff. Exceptional service. Especially from Namu. She always goes out of her way to make someone's day, from the smallest things.

Service like this is what brings customers back.

You are amazing guys!

this is my regular starbucks now and one of my favourite branches, everytime i’ve come in the staff have all been really sweet and accommodating.

Today’s experience at this store really pleased me however and caused me to write this review - Min served me and she was really sweet and helped me with my order. Special thanks to her. Thank you Min💗
Reliable Starbucks brew... Friendly staff here and a convenient location on Baker Street. Quiet little area downstairs and a sunny terrace outside... Great place to relax with a coffee
Great 👍🏽 staff excellent customer service 100% perfect
Service from  Lily, Hansol and Joshua was amazing! Super friendly will definitely be back just cause of their politeness!

I went to this store on September 24th at 4pm tired from the hospital. Starbucks coffees always give me energy. The very nice and kind behaviour of this lady who was in charge there made me forget all the tiredness and discomfort and realized how well she treats her customers. Unfortunately, I forgot their names.  I will definitely go to the store again and recommend it to everyone. Everything was great.
Tasty drinks, great service, nice place with lovely atmosphere. Nishat is very friendly and always smiling.

Every time I ask for less ice — expecting more milk to make up the difference — I end up with too much foam instead. Most of the staff don’t adjust the milk levels, which is frustrating. The only exception is one Asian male staff member who consistently fills the drink properly. I really appreciate his consistency and attention to detail.
It’s disappointing that the rest of the team can't follow such a simple request.
The manager called Dylan is so sweet. He has improved the customer service a lot since joining
Such a nice and welcoming Starbucks, one of my favorite in London. Really chill vibe and friendly service. I always stop by when I’m in the area.
Very good service from the young barrista (I think he is called Dec). Very attentive, patient and helpful!

Stopped here for a quick coffee after shopping nearby. Cake was excellent as was their coffee. Don't think the server understood my request for a takeaway cup but eat in as was dispensed a ceramic cup..No real concern just personal preference. Otherwise faultless experience today

Great place to sit and have an evening coffee.
Across the road from Harrods . Walking distance from Knightsbridge tube station.
Amazing view from the upstairs seating area. Great service and friendly vibes. Come with your friends or family or just by your self to have a coffee. Bring your tumbler for a discount as well. Enjoy your coffee ☕

Excellent service and great coffee! This is one of my favourite Starbucks locations in London. I grab my latte from here almost every day, and it’s always a great experience. The shop has a lovely atmosphere, and the service is impressively quick, especially considering it’s on one of the busiest streets in the city.
The staff are consistently friendly and helpful. I always receive outstanding customer service from Ashley — she’s kind, polite, professional, and always goes the extra mile. I’d happily recommend visiting this Starbucks to anyone!

Excellent location.. opposite of Harrods
Excellent & fast service.

Definitely my favourite Starbucks in London. Simply because of the plenty of seating options even when it’s busy. It’s fairly spacious and service is quick. Great ambience so it’s perfect for doing some work, also having lovely views of Knightsbridge

Starbucks at 25a Kensington High Street, London W8 5NP, operates primarily as a takeaway location, offering limited seating options with only two stools facing the wall. Despite its compact size, the store is known for its friendly and efficient staff, providing excellent customer service. The coffee maintains the high-quality standards expected from Starbucks, delivering rich and flavorful beverages. However, the food offerings have received mixed reviews, with some customers noting that the quality does not always meet expectations. The store operates from 6:30 AM to 7:00 PM on weekdays, 7:00 AM to 7:00 PM on Saturdays, and 7:00 AM to 7:00 PM on Sundays, catering to early morning and evening visitors.  ￼

Needed a treat after a long stroll and stopped at this Starbucks. Glad they’re open with strictly enforced safety measures and the app-ordering system is in place. This was the most delicious white mocha frap I’ve EVER had. Totally satisfied my craving and did so safely. Not a long wait at all.

Service is not friendly. The Barista has some clear attitude problems towards customers, which made the coffee less enjoyable. When asked if they do Puppuccino, the barista said Starbucks never do Puppuccino - which is clearly untrue. Such a shame, it is so close to the park. In addition, the store seems always messy and untidy - hence, I have it one star. No good experience ever with this Starbucks store.
Ordered chicken salad and a hot chocolate was dam, tasty, and the staff is also good

Worst Starbucks I’ve ever visited. Asked for a cinnamon dolce latte and was told they don’t have that. I pointed out that it’s on their menu   and the barista asked their colleague what it was but couldn’t pronounce “cinnamon dolce”. The other employee said no so I just got something else. The drinks are poorly made without proper proportions. I’ve previously been a barista at a location in another country and would be appalled to have a customer a drink like the one I received. A pink drink was so sparse on coconut milk that you could see through it. On another occasion I asked for a decaf latte and specified clearly that it was decaf. I was given a caffeinated latte and pointed out to the barista that it wasn’t decaf and unfortunately I can’t have caffeinated drinks due to a medical issue. He remade the drink and I confirmed with him that it was decaf which he replied yes. It was not in fact decaf as I was up all night with medical issues because I had caffeine. Employees are rude. Drinks are sub par and lazily made. Save yourself the hassle and walk to another location nearby.

Dear Starbucks Management,

I would like to share feedback regarding my visit today to one of your branches.

First, I would like to note that the initial staff member at the counter was very welcoming and professional. The greeting and order-taking were excellent and truly reflected Starbucks’ service standards.

However, the issue occurred with the barista who prepared the coffee. The drink quality was very poor and the taste was unacceptable. When I politely requested a replacement, the barista (an Indian staff member who prepared the drink) refused to remake it and only made a minimal adjustment, which did not resolve the issue.

Unfortunately, the barista’s attitude during the interaction was unprofessional and dismissive, which negatively affected my experience.

I would appreciate it if this matter could be reviewed, focusing on drink quality standards and customer service training for the specific staff member involved.

Thank you for your time and attention.

This was one of the worst customer service experiences I’ve had. Eugene(asian Chinese lady)and URVIL were rude, arrogant, and their behaviour felt racist. I was made to feel humiliated and unwelcome.
This kind of treatment is unacceptable and should not be tolerated. Management needs to address how staff interact with customers.

Nice outlet.
A bit dark and a bit dark.
I think it needs refurbishment like the new ones around the world.
Coffee was great as usual and the service was fast.
Location is superb and well placed.
The outlet is clean and all in place.
Employees are cheerful and was of a great help.
Thank you
Good but feel the taste of the coffee here didnt like it, not same other brancehes

Really bad experience. I asked for double espresso and hot water on the side and a cake, but the staff refused my request saying that their policy doesn’t allow them to do it, while I ordered the same thing in baker street branch and other branches.

I am buying things, not just asking for hot water without getting anything. Double espresso is very strong, and I would like to have some hot water to make my stomach feel better, what’s wrong with that? I think Starbucks really needs to improve on this.

Also, the staff who refused me, her attitude was really bad!

I just ordered some juice shots and they were enough to get me some energy. They were out of matcha but that’s men everywhere.

I’d definitely come back here again to try their other menu items.
They didn’t allow me to use the toilets after I bought a coffee because they had already “closed them” at 7:00 pm — even though the store closes at 7:30 pm
A friendly store where staff take their time to help customers.

I’ve lived in this area for over 10 years and can hands down say this store has one of the worst staff I’ve ever came across. As it’s super close to me I tend to pop in here and there as it’s convenient but never a pleasure to go. The staff are useless, constantly chatting and ignoring customers when it’s not even busy. The guys that work there needs a real course in customer service.

Very friendly Staffel even in The early morning hours. The Pancakes i ordered tasted fresh and were delicious. The Tea was also hot and very good. I can recommend this Starbucks in Paddington. IT IS also The one which ist opened The earliest in this area. IT Is Not The biggest one but There are varioua Options top Situation down For awhile.
I had the best first matcha latte of the year! Thank you so much Heeji for the delicious drink 💖
Lady serving today was very friendly and service was excellent!
The lady working provided great customer service🙌
I don't often see people adding a Happy little smile to my coffee 🙂
Order medium latte and lemon loaf for having in. They gave us coffee and cake with disposable spoons and knives. Tea cup was without plate. Few table were dirty. Need lots of improvement

Popped in for a pumpkin spice latter to drink in. Greeted with a smile and not a long queue which was a surprise. Drink made quickly and tasted great. Didn’t get a seat on ground floor so had to go downstairs which is huge, has good art and also the toilets.
I think they can fit more seating upstairs and should dot some tables about. There is some seating outside.

The service really depends on the staff
when the manager or some Koreans were there quite decent though I've got to say the last time was just terrible... served by Grace, a little Chinese girl and Araf? Airf? idk small guy
Grace, the lovely lady at the til was trying her best to sort things out though...the rest just played with the drinks messing around, on their phones with a bunch of drinks to make and my lovely Caramel macchiato turned out to be a wet latte.
NO ONE can mess with MY LOVELY CARAMEL MACCHIATO
I just wonder who hired those amazing people
especially Araf, who made my drink, the worst barista ever skill-wise, attitude-wise
Take it. you deserve it.

Popped in again this morning and this time was served by George and Haley, friendliest service and most efficient and prompt turnaround I've ever experienced at a Starbucks, my 2 drinks were completed by the time I made it across to the collection bay, can't give enough props to George for the service

I popped by early on a Saturday morning, got served immediately by a gentleman who looked very tired, he popped the order through, made one larger than it needed to be and had to ask me again whether one was almond or both were almond, obviously half asleep but it was laughed off. The coffees were served up very quickly as they were quiet, the place was clean and well stocked. Standard Starbucks experience really.
Good service, polite staff but the place was quite dirty. I would recommend only for take away.

The restaurant itself was clean and conveniently located. The drinks and food were also reasonable quality. Unfortunately, despite seeming well intentioned, the barista taking orders seemed to struggle to remember what we wanted correctly, requiring a long time to understand what we wanted and making multiple mistakes...

Friendly staff and great drinks! The store is located inside the station, up the escalators on the first floor. It's slightly hard to find but easy once you know.

I ordered on the app and my drink was made quickly. It's a small store, so sometimes seats are limited but there were some available. There are plug sockets at every seat (no USB though). The Wi-Fi worked and was fast.
Friendly staff and nice location. Right next to the Hilton.

Friendly staff Even in the Late Hours of the day. The frappuciono with strawberry taste was Great. The small Starbucks inside of paddington Station ist located on the First floor via escelator. There is also room to ist down and watch Whats going in downstairs at the sainsbury local store. There is also a small Counter with Souvenir mugs and a Counter with fresh Sandwiches.

Very friendly staff, helping a tired American (me) order an iced coffee and I needed to ask what to call the milk I normally order in US.  And no problem at all when I asked for an iced water as well while picking up drink at end of counter.   Not a lot of seating in the store but this is in a train station so probably don’t expect people to stay extended periods of time.

Ordered an Iced Toffee Nut Matcha and a Short Americano. The server, June, used an unwashed blender and got rude when I asked her to clean it. She took longer on purpose, and when I asked for a refund, she dismissively said “yea okay.” Another staff member Sue tried to help, but June said, “She wants a refund anyway, so let it go.” Very poor hygiene and attitude. The staff also looked overworked and tired
Liam is amazing! He remembered my order and always greets me with a smile! He’s amazing

Had the  best caramel Frappuccino ever. I asked the girl named Aliya for some extra caramel as I love caramel and to my suprise she just overloaded it with caramel. Usually they don’t it at starbucks but this girl just topped it with caramel dripping, just the way I like it.
Thank you Aliya for giving me the best coffee ever. God bless
Most slow service ever , i have been a barista my self at Starbucks but these people set a new definition for slow service 👍🏼

Waste of money
More ice than drink

The space is clean and cozy and the music is not too loud. The servers, Susana and Jaga, went above and beyond in ensuring that my order was right, that all allergens were accounted for, and even volunteered to heat up our sandwiches and toasties. I had the pea and falafel wrap for the first time and it was very good, a perfect balance between light and filling. The pistachio scroll is also delicious, and though I didn’t order it this time, the polar bear-shaped cake pops look very cute.

One of the worst experiences I’ve ever had when it comes to service imagine ordering a double espresso, where the branch is literally empty and there is and it’s not a busy time and you wait for 8 minutes!!!!!! The women with Hijab is just horrible ( if you don’t wanna work then just leave) even though I’m Muslim and I’m telling you that just for you to know that it’s not a race issue. Maybe because I’m from an Arab country she was giving us an attitude and she didn’t wanna be helpful enough to give me my order in two minutes just like any other branch because it’s just an espresso ☕️

One of the worst experiences we’ve ever had. I know Starbucks products very well and I’m a regular customer. Unfortunately, I walked into this store for the first time and immediately noticed the barista looking totally annoyed and unmotivated this afternoon. After ordering, I received a muffin that was unbelievably dry (who knows how many days it had been sitting there), and the coffee experience was even worse. My iced caramel macchiato was basically comparable to dirty water.

I will never return to this store, terrible customer service and terrible products.
No toilette available. For the rest usual Starbucks. Decent outside terrace but just few seats inside. Typical rush hours place 😊
The slowest starbucks in the world over 30 minutes to get a drink! The staff is fast, they are just 2 people. They just need more staff

Beautiful Staff and Good Management.I start with the first bartender that i've met,from Italy miss Vivianna after with the Manager Mohhamed ,the Bartender Chery,and the rest of the other shifts i've known.Thank you guys for your professional's behaviour.
Great Starbucks location that’s easy to get to.

I had a wonderful experience at Starbucks in shippers bush Green Despite being in a rush, the girl making the coffee was impressively quick and had a genuinely nice attitude. She managed to make my order swiftly, even though I was running late. Additionally, I must commend the staff for their consistency in providing excellent service, especially considering the challenging parking situation in the area. Every visit, they have been on point with their service. I highly recommend this branch for anyone looking for great coffee and exceptional customer service!.
It is what you’ll expect from a Starbucks coffee chain . You can’t go wrong !

Clean and tidy little Starbucks. Less seating than it had previously and it now feels pretty clinical. Toilets were clean and there was no queue when I arrived. I popped in out of the rain for a quick drink and to check some emails.
Great location with nice views out the front window. Your own choice of about twenty different styles of seating, plenty of plugs, free wifi. Classic Starbucks coffee.

there coffee is so gooddddd i love it had everything and staff are supper atentive... sometimes they sometimes give you freebies- go there guys its awsume.. i love it !!!!! lol best times i have hafd there, but one bad thing wasthe loos stink of farts and there is noooooooo ,looo paperer"!! but oherwise great guys

It's a Starbucks. This one was clean, the staff was friendly and polite, and it opened early (which not many other places in the neighborhood do). However, it's a Starbucks, do don't expect anything groundbreaking.
Well! Better than other Starbucks here, great location and atmosphere. Servings are great as others
Exceptional staff, very friendly and after 3 days they had already remembered my order!

One of the dirtiest Starbucks I’ve ever seen. The staff were nice and speedy, but wow if there isn’t trash on the floor. The seating is also often taken by long term sitters; don’t expect to find a spot. The queue to order can also get long, but staff prioritize mobile orders, so I would recommend that route.

Really enjoyed the coffee, I wish I could give a positive opinion but... the toilet was a real disgrace and spoiled my visit. No idea who looks after it (looks like no one does); it was below standard. Such a lovely place but shows how one “detail” can ruin everything; please improve!

Service is fast - coffee is good. Music is light jazz / lounge. The sidewalls of the counters could do with a wipe down. This Starbucks is also on the Main Street so there is lots of traffic in and out. Free internet here too

I walked a long way to arrive to this Starbucks branch avoiding other coffee shops brands as the others don’t offer stevia like a sweetener. Well, Starbucks is the only one which offers it and this Starbucks branch didn’t have
Always enjoy Starbucks. This one is well located on Kensington High Street. The washroom wasn't in a decent state, though.
Much nicer Starbucks than a number I have visited recently.  Fairly small, but clean, bright and airy. Staff very helpful and drinks nice and hot. Plus it's quiet, so that's a bonus

A bit quieter than most branches of Starbucks, but it has a good atmosphere. Chilled, relaxed and you don't feel under any pressure to be actively drinking.

A very laid back place.
A little bit of quiet in London Town, and great coffee
Great location! In centre, but hidden a bit, so it is nice quiet place. Ideal for work or studying :) great coffee too.
Always consistent. This location did not disappoint. Friendly staff and great coffee.

Big, good place for work, 2 floors, 2 restrooms. Nice place to spend the afternoon on any project. Service is fast and promptly. Could be a little more clean, that's the only reason I didn't give 5. They close around 8pm, so it's a good place to get yourself into work. Free WiFi.

I go to this Starbucks every week as it's a great place to work and the staff are generaly friendly and remember you by face. You'll see loads of patrons with laptops and books there. It's got room for improvement however - the music is far too loud ( given 4 years of feedback to them about this and it's still not reduced.Patrons mostly wear their headphones), the lighting downstairs is a bit too dim for working (they have recently increased the brightness a little), they have moved 50% of the downstairs seating and tables upstairs bizarrely, as downstairs is bigger. It's far too crowded upstairs now. The toilets are frequently out of service/closed, the paint is peeling from rising damp downstairs. There are far too few plug points so there's usually a battle among patrons for the three plug points. Yet I go there, as it has a good atmosphere and the staff don't bother you while you work.
Nicely located Starbucks with large windows. Good place to have a coffee while you are waiting for your laundry.
Very quiet and cozy Starbucks. Prices are Starbuck's normal. Downstairs too dark.
Just in from NY and this is the perfect place to recharge as it's lovely and mellow, with room to work and catch up!

Very disappointed that they no longer have egg nogg latte available. It's Christmas eve, the staff even say it's the most popular drink yet they have already run out! So much for the next 12 days of Christmas without the number 1 drink on offer. 😡

I feel very disappointed, I just ask for a little bit of hot water to warming up milk for my new born baby, for some how staff in this branch refuse my request so the only way the get some hot water was paying around £ 2.70 for a cup of tea with the tea bag on the side :,(

Terrible service, rude and abusive staff, incorrectly priced products and very unapologetic. It's a Franchise store not official Starbucks and this reflects in the poor quality of product and service.

Very kindly service and emoloyers. But,  , unfotunatelly, many  customers  are not polite.

food and glasses, dirty paper an spoons everywhere.
I m very , very sorry for  you guys!!!!!!!

I always order short white mocha but at this place they gave me a cup smaller than the short without informing they ran out of short cups and then they gave me a wrong order so i had to return twice to the cashier to get my preferred coffee was very annoying

⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️
Great stop at this Starbucks in London on Christmas Day! We grabbed a delicious Cappuccino, a creamy Papiccino, a fresh muffin, and some chocolate to take home – everything was spot on and hit the spot after our city walk. The service was quick and friendly, even on a holiday, and the cozy vibe made it perfect for a quick break.

Highly recommend if you're nearby – solid quality and a nice treat in the heart of London!

An average Starbucks. It is 2 story with many seats. They seem to be understaffed b/c it takes a little while for them to make drinks and food. The music is a little off mood to the style of the shop, but overall mediocre.

This branch of Starbucks looked very attractive from the outside - modern, chic and relaxed. Unfortunately, the experience was not up to the usual standards.

First, I was directed to order on the kiosk, which was not as quick and easy as just asking the staff to for my very straightforward drink. There were plenty of staff who could have taken my order. What I thought would be the saving grace was that I could type my name into the screen and so for once have my name called out correctly by the server. Alas, my name was pronounced even worse than usual.

I selected to have my hot drink in store so was expecting to get a mug, but it was still served in the takeaway cup. The staff apologised.

The WiFi wasn't working, which is primarily what I had come to Starbucks to use.

The plug socket also wasn't working.

One of the toilets was out of order so there was a queue for the other ones.

Despite quite a number of staff, the upstairs tables were dirty with lots of previous customers' empty cups, trays and food wrappers left for a long time. I didn't see anyone clean up.

Starbucks - please do better - at this central London flagship location, your values need to be expressed here more than anywhere.

I love starbucks its my constant shop but I'm at this store right now & there's this guy mumim & I asked for hot water at I am feeling sick & i already got a coffee & some egg bites. Since it's cold & I was feeling uneasy I asked for some water & he was so rude & abrupt said "I won't give u hot water if u want a hot drink I can give u that(I avery rude and abrupt tone)" I asked why not ? He continued "ITS A STARBUCKS POLICY"
Kindly fix ur staffs attitude please cause if it continues so this store will loose customers as there's other stores around d corner.
It was early morning of the boxing day,glad I found this open for a quick latte.

Came to this starbucks with my friends and had a very unpleasant experience. My drink tasted a bit ogg and i went up to speak to the person on till. I asked her to remake it politely and she said no no no you drank from it already. I explained to her that yes i did habe 2 sips but how would i know if its bad without trying it. When she kept refusing i asked her to call her supervisor and at first she denied then said she was on break. However, the other barista thankfully stepped in and offered to. The initial barista ( who had a nametag with the name jessica) told her not to call the supervisor but the barista still did. The supervisor ended up resolving the situation and said she will talk to jessica. I didnt feel that was enough and am extremely disappointed at the service. Ive been a loyal customer for over a decade and rarely experience experience like this. I will probably be back soon and hope the attitude is resolved.

One of the best Starbucks locations in London! The staff is incredibly kind and helpful, and they were very friendly when taking my order. The space is clean and comfortable, and I felt really relaxed and happy working with my laptop. The new fresh baking products were amazing too! Both the quality of the drinks and the service were excellent. I’ll definitely be back and highly recommend it!

I had a very unpleasant experience at this Starbucks location. A staff member, who had taken off his uniform and was sitting at the terrace like a customer, was speaking loudly to his phone for a long time – which was already quite inappropriate.

But what shocked me most was what happened later: I approached the toilet and he told me it was closed. When I calmly tried to verify it, he physically touched me, poking my shoulder to stop me from checking.

This kind of unprofessional and invasive behavior is completely unacceptable – especially coming from someone representing the store.

It made me feel extremely uncomfortable, and I believe this should be taken seriously by management.

I hope Starbucks reviews how their staff behave both inside and outside their official role.

I have been a customer for many years, and this was the first time I had a problem. I went with my fiancé and we ordered 2 sandwiches and a juice. In addition to the place being empty, it took almost 20 minutes for them to deliver my order. There were two women and a man serving. The two women were completely unprepared, they were drinking juice, talking, laughing and throwing paper at each other. Is this really normal to do in front of a customer?! My sandwiches arrived completely cold, and they simply forgot to make the juice, I had to wait longer for the juice to be prepared. What a disappointment!

I absolutely love this Starbucks location! The staff here is always so friendly and welcoming, making every visit feel like a treat. The atmosphere is cozy and inviting, with plenty of seating whether you're looking to relax with a book or get some work done.

The coffee is consistently excellent – my go-to order is the Caramel Macchiato, and it's always perfectly made. They take the time to customize drinks just the way I like, and I can always rely on them for a great pick-me-up. Plus, the pastries are fresh and delicious – the lemon loaf is a personal favorite!

I also appreciate how clean and well-maintained the store is, and the baristas are quick and efficient even when it's busy. It's clear that the team takes pride in their work, and it shows in every interaction.

It’s a refreshingly quiet place if you need an escape from the busy Regent street. You don’t go for Starbucs for novelty, rather, you seek solace in the known. The drinks met the expextations in this sense. I would, however, advise the manager to instruct the baristas not to make sour faces if a customer kindly declines to give their name. This disease is the only thing that spoils my enjoyment since I’ve been a customer for 30 years. Funnily, a group of 6 guys ordered about 8 drinks before us and they all had their drinks under the same name as my husbands, who I was visiting with. Imagine the mess if we gave the same name again😅. I love SB but you guys need to grow up!

Coffee shop was busy but finding a table hard as blocked by laptop users not eating and drinking. Only accessible toilet was closed as apparently out of order but seems fine to staff to use. Seems that this shouldn’t be labelled an accessible location as no toilets available unless you can climb a huge flight of stairs.

On the positive excellent coffee and good service otherwise.

I didn't really want a Starbucks, but was desperate for a latte and couldn't find many coffee shops in the immediate area.
The raspberry and white chocolate cake was really good, and there was plenty of seating, which we needed at the time as well.

All round pretty standard. I don't really expect high quality from Starbucks but the averageness was truly obvious the time I went. Busy at a lunch time. The coffee was not great but thankfully not burnt. My overpriced match roll was verging on stail. Wouldn't be rushing back for anything outstanding. Great coffee is an essential especially for a high priced shop but I think Id rather drink my office instant coffee next time.

People were kind and nice place
This cafe can contain a lot of ppl, so there’s pros and cons. Everytime I visit here I struggles with the long que of toilet. Bur the other things r all perfect.
I recommend this place to travelers and workers.

Staff are fantastic. They had a promotion on with free cups. Not only did they flag this to us, but were very patient whilst I registered.

Thank you Amy!

Alsi fairly spacious downstairs.
Very nice and professional staff! I got this lovely message, but I didn’t notice it when I grabbed my drink. A big thank you to whoever did this. It really made my day!

Friendly barista but a very rude supervisor who has no skills to engage with customers.
Apparently, there is a regulation that forbids Starbucks staff from adding honey into a cup for their customers.
I have been a Starbucks customer for many years to remember, and I have never heard this excuse.
AND the store was empty, it’s not like they were rushed off their feet.
Very disappointing.
The team were operating as if they had 8 arms. Very busy store and my mobile order had slipped through. To make up for it they gave me a voucher. Very thankful to them!
Nice, convenient new Starbucks in the area. It looks quite modern inside. The staff are helpful and fast, which helps when there are long queues in the morning. There's extra seating downstairs

Best coffee shop in this street, quick service.
Staff polite and attentive.

Starbucks in Soho is a cozy spot, especially during autumn when they offer delicious pumpkin-flavored drinks. The seasonal flavors add a festive touch, and the atmosphere is perfect for relaxing or catching up with friends.

Asked that I want the have in for my drink. Still got the drink in take away cup.

Barista couldn’t keep his tongue in his mouth while making drink.Fully understand that could be a condition and people need to make living.However,  It’s the food and drink environment , may be cover it with face mask.

This Starbucks in Soho is a solid spot for coffee but has room for improvement. I ordered a hot latte but got an iced one instead; it was still good, so I kept it. The location is prime, though it was very busy, and the staff seemed a bit stressed. Despite this, they stayed polite and efficient. Seating was clean but crowded. A reliable choice for coffee, but expect a lively, fast-paced atmosphere. 4 stars!
This Starbucks is sometimes busy, sometimes very chill. However, the toilet is often in use and tends to be a bit dirty.

⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️ Great coffee stop with handy location just a bit busy! ☕️🫖🧋🎂🧁🥮

I recently visited Starbucks Coffee, Waterloo Station at 12 A Mepham Street while passing through Waterloo Station, and overall it was a solid experience.
It’s super convenient if you need a familiar coffee before or after a train journey, and the location inside the station makes it hard to beat when you’re on the go.
That said, it does get very busy, as you’d expect from a station branch, and that means queues and limited seating at peak times. I noticed a few tables weren’t cleared as quickly as I’d hoped, and finding a place to sit can be a bit of a challenge if the station is crowded.

Overall a reliable Starbucks with good coffee and a really handy location perfect for commuters or traveller ☕️

⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️ Absolutely Outstanding Experience at Starbucks – Waterloo Station

One of the finest Starbucks locations I’ve ever visited. The staff here are incredibly welcoming, quick, and professional, even during the busiest rush hours. The store is always clean, the service is fast, and the coffee tastes consistently perfect every single time.

Their team really knows how to handle customers with a smile, and the atmosphere makes it a great spot to relax while travelling or waiting for a train. I genuinely appreciate the quality, the warmth, and the attention to detail they bring.

Highly recommended for anyone passing through Waterloo Station — easily worth a 5-star rating!

The worst Starbucks — and it’s even at a major station.The opening hours are different from what’s listed on Google, and I’ve been waiting here since 6:15.Even though the sign says 6:30, the doors were still closed until 6:40.
I couldn’t wait any longer and had to leave after that.

The staff members were friendly and welcoming, which made the visit really enjoyable. My drink came out quickly and tasted great, and the overall atmosphere was relaxed and comfortable. A great spot for a quick coffee break!

Had a wonderful experience at this branch like no other. Perfect place to settle down for a cuppa whether you're or returning from a long journey. The ambience, staff and of course the coffee makes the so much better

As a traveller from the UAE, this Starbucks was my first stop after arriving in London — just a short walk from my hotel near County Hall. After a 7-hour flight and 4 more hours at the airport, I still had time to kill before hotel check-in (the usual 3 PM rule), so I ended up spending a couple of hours here.
I had an iced latte and a warm apple crumble dessert with cinnamon (I forgot the exact name), which was comforting and tasty. The dessert was definitely the highlight. Unfortunately, the iced coffee itself was quite weak — mostly ice and water, lacking flavour. I was also hoping for a few more savory breakfast options, but the selection was limited.
That said, the outdoor seating was a real plus. It gave me a place to sit and relax after the long journey, and the location — right near the London Eye and South Bank — made for a pleasant atmosphere to pass the time.
It wasn’t a bad experience, but with better coffee and a bit more variety in breakfast, it could have been much better — especially for weary travellers like me looking for a warm welcome to the city.

Starbucks serves high-quality coffee and beverages.
The flavors are rich and satisfying.
Customization makes every order unique.
The ambience is peaceful and inviting.
Cleanliness is well maintained.
Service is professional and efficient.
The café is perfect for casual meetings.
A great spot for coffee breaks.

Served by Giocano. He accidentally gave my drink to the wrong family. When I asked him about my missing drink it led to almost an argument when I had waited for 15 minutes for a tall caramel Frappuccino. He tried to deny that I even made the order?! Giocano was very rude and found the moment to be funny as he snickered at me for being upset.
This was at 15:30pm 24th Jan. I will be filing a complaint form too.
Good in and out keep moving Starbucks. Positive is right behind the Marriott and London Eye so easy access. They have lots of travel mugs. Transactional stop

The staff insisted on making the drink their own way even though I clearly explained how I wanted it. When I said I wouldn’t take the drink, the barista threw it in the sink with obvious attitude, splashing it all over the counter. This kind of behavior shows a serious need for proper staff training — especially in customer service and anger management.

Unfortunately, this isn’t the first time I’ve experienced similar behavior in multiple Starbucks stores across London. Very disappointing and far from the standards expected from Starbucks.

Beyond the already excessive prices for this chain, we were at least expecting a reasonably decent level of service. Absolutely not.
The waitress was extremely unpleasant — I can’t tell whether that’s just her attitude or if it was because we were foreigners. I asked to use the toilets, as a key was required to open the door, and I clearly explained in English that the door was locked. She replied that there might be someone inside.
There were two of us waiting: an elderly lady and myself, six months pregnant. I asked again five minutes later and received the exact same answer. After ten minutes, I asked another member of staff, who opened the door straight away. There was no one inside.
The toilet paper was jammed and unusable, the toilets were dirty, and overall this is by far the worst Starbucks I’ve ever experienced in terms of service. Not to mention that this unnecessary wait completely disrupted our sightseeing schedule.
Avoid at all costs!

⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️

I love my visits to Starbucks! The atmosphere is always cozy and inviting, perfect for a quick coffee run or a study session. The staff is friendly and knowledgeable, helping me choose the perfect drink every time. I recently tried their decaf latte , and it was delicious—smooth and perfectly balanced. Plus, I appreciate their commitment to sustainability and quality ingredients. Highly recommend stopping by for a pick-me-up! ☕️✨

We went to see Christmas lights. It was very cold outside, this was our saviour. Nice warm inside, loved their Christmas menu, quick service, very friendly staff, tasty food. We are anyway fan of Starbucks coffee and food 😄

if i could give this place 0 stars i would, i work in a starbucks back at home and you would never get service like that there. just got something from the fridge and i put it down and said hi and just got blanked she didnt even tell me the price just continued to laugh and talk bad about people with her friend i think staff need more training on customer service even said thank you and still got blanked not a word was said to me, i hope this improves when this review is read from a supervisor or manager.

I was disappointed with my experience involving Simon regarding the student discount. At the time of purchase, my student profile did not have a photo, and as a result, he refused to apply the student discount and required me to pay full price. I uploaded a photo to my profile the following day, however this raised concerns for me, as not all customers may wish to have a profile picture in order to receive a valid student discount. I felt this situation could have been handled with more flexibility and understanding !

SIMON owes me a coffee !

This Starbucks, located right next to UCL Hospital, is incredibly convenient for anyone visiting or working at the hospital. It's a small spot but has a relaxed, chilled vibe that's perfect for grabbing a quick coffee break. The service is fast, and the staff is friendly, which is always a plus when you're in a hurry. Great place to unwind for a few minutes or pick up a drink to go! Highly recommend if you're in the area.
Small Starbucks branch ideal for a quick order to go.  Supervisor/Team leader is very polite along with the other staff.

I don't know if the temperature at this place was set to such a low degree on purpose, but it made my friends leave the place after few minutes and go to enjoy their coffee somewhere else. I stayed for a bit longer because I wanted to charge my phone, but it was not worthy for me to get sick, so I left the café shortly after my friends.
The coffee is good, though, as in any Starbucks place.

First visit to a Starbucks! I normally use the other place. But happily surprised and had a decent mocha and hot chocolate here! Not the cheapest but still a nice cup of coffee.
Staff pleasant and friendly.

For the past few months they seem to have an ad-hoc approach to opening times. Door sign and internet says 06:30 but never is. Just opening sometime between just before 7:00 and 7:30. Used to like this store to grab a coffee just before going into the office. Will have to grab one at another store now.
Conveniently located across from the British museum. Visited at around 0900 on a Tuesday, and the place wasn't very busy, no wait. Americano was hot and tasty. Warms the soul. Great customer service.

Downstairs was closed off for no reason while there were people actually sitting there. Toilets were downstairs so subsequently we weren't allowed to use the restrooms. We were forced to stay upstairs where there were like 7 seats and 3 tables. When we took one of the outdoor tables for the inside they weren't too happy with it either. Friendliness wasn't great either. My thoughts is there wasn't a manager present, it wasn't particularly busy and they were too lazy to go downstairs all the time.

I just wanted to share how kind the staff was during my visit. I had several items in my hands and one of the baristas offered to help carry them to my table. It was such a simple gesture, but it really made my day. Friendly, attentive service like this is why I keep coming back. Thank you!

SUBJECT: Serious Security Concern at Starbucks Great Russell Street

Dear Starbucks UK,

I am writing to express my deep concern regarding a theft incident that occurred at your Great Russell Street location on Sunday, 4 January 2026, at approximately 4 PM.

After visiting the British Museum, I stopped by this Starbucks for coffee. Unfortunately, my backpack was stolen inside the coffeehouse without my knowledge. When I reported this to the staff, I was informed that similar incidents have happened before and that the CCTV system could not be relied upon.

This is extremely disappointing and alarming. The coffeehouse is situated directly in front of one of the country’s biggest tourist attractions, making it a prime spot for visitors. It appears that thieves see this location as an ideal place to steal, given the lack of security measures. Furthermore, a fellow customer mentioned that the same individual seemed to be eyeing her belongings as well.

I sincerely hope Starbucks UK will take immediate action to improve security at this branch—whether through functional CCTV, staff vigilance, or other preventive measures. Tourists and customers should feel safe when visiting your stores. Hopefully, this review can raise awareness among other visitors as well.
A standard Starbucks branch right across from the British Museum where you can relax after visiting the museum. It is usually crowded inside. There are also seating areas on the lower floor.

The sweetest thing anyone's ever done for me . So sweet of the barista, made me feel special. Thank you he is special too and so kind to make another person feel special . I have kept the cup as a reminder also all the staff are always so sweet, smiley and helpful

Food and drinks were good, atmosphere was nice but the woman who served my drink was quite rude. I don’t know her name but she has dark hair and is tall, maybe late 30’s or in her 40s, has what sounds like a foreign accent. I asked her for sleeves for the cups and I never got a response just got a weird look and them put down at me and then I asked her nicely for a third one for the third drink and she looked at me weird again and said “ it’s not extra hot” to which I replied “ Oh I know, I just prefer a sleeve, it’s easier to hold” and she replied “ well that’s a waste” and pretty much flung the sleeve at me. I work at Starbucks and I know we are only meant to put sleeves on the cups if they are extra hot, a tea or an Americano but if the customer asks we’re are aloud to give them. Personally, I don’t waste them, I keep the sleeves to use for other drinks until I can’t use them anymore. This woman acted as if I just asked her the world.

I still giving 5 stars even I’m not happy with one woman at the counter today. She should have not be at the front if she could not control her emotions. Other 2 staffs today still remain Great service but her…
The most watery flat white I’ve ever had! Looked like americano with a little bit of milky foam, bitter and tasteless!

When I clearly slammed the donut, the seller ordered another cake, but not a donut. When you want to scan the application, you will be refused .When it was time to pick up the order, they simply handed me two drinks, seeing that my hands were full. When I asked for a coaster, the staff unanimously ignored me. Then, when I asked to put the drinks in the coaster, the employee knocked over my drink and tried to shove it at me so that I could take it. I asked for a redo, since I paid for a normal drink and not for a half-empty glass. And they gave me a second drink with a nutcase. People like that should be fired and given jobs to normal people.

For someone that comes every day to this branch, it’s quite disappointing to have someone like the lady that served me today to treat with such disrespect, I work next door, and of course I get my coffee here, and when I came back to say that my coffee was not fully filled for a Venti size, another lady (G) jumped in to say “so what? you asked for a macchiato and that’s what you got”. I’ve never been unfriendly, specially given the fact that I work in retail, it’s very disappointing to see and hear this, as I feel like people like her don’t represent the whole team, and it’s not about making another coffee “just this once”, like she said, it’s about feeling welcomed in a place. The first server was amazing, very understanding and amazing, the one with the black apron, names starts with a G, shame on you.

On the smallish side for a Starbucks, buts it’s super clean and the staff are friendly, efficient and know what they’re doing overall.  They didn’t blink at my girlfriend’s frankly terrifying list of specifics on how she likes her Matcha, which was a relief and in stark contrast to other sites!  Maybe it’s a franchise thing!

Best flat white in central London, by Mintu!
Lovely, super friendly team!
Special shoutout to Mingying/Mingjing (I didn't catch her name properly) was fantastic. She had very good service, going above and beyond for me, highly reccomend!

Very nice branch of Starbucks. Good space as most the one near Oxford street is tiny and small and no real seating area is available. Also this branch has restrooms which include the  special needs one. Staff are nice as usual in Starbucks. Would come again for sure
Thanks
Great service at the Waterloo East branch. Gingerbread Latte was nicely made, and the baristas were super friendly and personable. Yacoub and Jahan brightened up my afternoon.
The staff here are amazing and always very friendly and serve you with a smile (I’m often served by Jan)! Good coffee and friendly service is always a good start to my day!

The renovation looks good, much more spacious downstairs. Staff are very efficient, the store is clean and busy. The issue I faced was I purchased the vegan breakfast wrap (which needs to be heated up) and I wasn’t offered for it to be heated. Tried to eat it cold, seems impossible without it sticking to everything including the packaging. Didn’t taste great and for the £4… it wasn’t worth it.
Minufa and Jan are absolutely lovely. They are two very happy people who create a brilliant atmosphere for the customer and have lots of positive energy!
This is one of the best Starbucks I have be to everyone is so nice and kind and happy . It a great way for me to start my day

Lines Lines Lines.

There is a line to get in line, there is a line to go to the restroom, there is a line to pick up your order, there was even a line to get out the door.

Other than that the drinks were pretty good, and they had very interesting food options!

I ordered the mango pearl boba drink, and my boyfriend ordered the tiramisu latte. Both were very refreshing and delicious.
I happened to like the Starbucks in the UK better than the US!
Not bad. Very small coffee shop but in an ideal location next to the station. Service is pretty slow. Drinks were like usual, nothing special and barely any whipped cream.

I’m very happy and satisfied with the service at Starbucks here in London.
The welcome is warm, the staff is pro and the drinks are always excellent.
A very good experience every time I visit!

I was refused a glass of water while placing an order and was told it’s due to “new rules” — something I’ve never encountered before. My husband, who is diabetic, was shocked by this lack of basic courtesy, so we decided to cancel our order and leave. We won’t be returning, and I wouldn’t recommend this place based on how we were treated.

"Absolutely appalling customer service. The staff have zero manners or basic courtesy - no 'please,' no 'thank you,' nothing resembling professional behavior.
The person serving literally just dumped the coffee down and walked off without a word. Not even eye contact, let alone any acknowledgment that they're serving a paying customer. It's like they're doing you some massive favor by taking your money.
Basic politeness costs nothing, but apparently that's too much to ask for here. When you can't even get a simple 'thank you' or show the most basic respect to customers, you've got serious problems with your service standards.
There are plenty of other places where the staff actually understand that customer service means treating people with a bit of decency. Save yourself the aggravation and go somewhere that values your business."

Just another starbucks but I love the seating near the staircase.
Tried the vampire frap which was surprisingly nice.
The choc and strawberry flavours went well together

I had a bit of a rough morning, but the Starbucks team completely lifted my spirits. I ordered through the app and they included a thoughtful little message with my order—it was such a small gesture, but it made a huge difference. Moments like this really show how much they care behind the scenes. The coffee was great as always, and that personal touch truly made my day! 💖💖💖
They struggle to make a coffee despite there being no rush. I ordered a coffee Frappuccino and seemed to have got milk mixed with caramel. Spacious environment to work in downstairs or upstairs.
Lovely place, cozy and clean. I love quiet atmosphere downstair that I was able to study and work. Will be back here xx

Update: when you sit downstairs just ask what time they close it. As when they do, upstairs no space so you have to go out and look for somewhere else. Maybe put opening hours to your downstairs as I as a customer look at opening hours and decide my visit based on that. Online says 7pm and it doesn't specify that downstairs closes earlier. I'm waiting for a friend for 5pm so I planned to stay there but literally got kicked out as no other place to sit.

I've used this Starbucks once a week for over 2 years now - when going to office. It's always great service and queue moves pretty fast in the morning. However, in the past 3 weeks, I've bought the same drink and every time was not made well. Today, it was not even full cup and tastes like it's missing some ingredients. Might need to look for a new coffee place to grab my office drink.
Great customer service. Had white hot chocolate  with coconut milk. Made perfect. Clean area. But place of a tumbler for £34 should be decorative shelf than a display. 😳 Crazy 😀

So as usual I had to have a shot of eggnog latte and  Nabil brewed the best! Brewed with lots of commitment and passion! It’s the best. I recommend you stop by when in the area and let Nabil treat you to a perfect eggnog latte. I totally appreciate my gift! It’s yummy! Thanks Nabil!
The espresso is despicably awful, especially if you drink it black. As for the other coffee types, I wouldn’t say they are the worst, but they are certainly not the best either.

I love going back to Starbucks as I already know what to expect. Also, more importantly, because they're the only one who have the option of almond milk. That alone will make me come back over and over. Hope it will always be there.

I’ve been to this Starbucks many times before but they’ve started to not accept cash from now on. It’s a disappointment, I won’t be visiting this particular branch anymore. Cash payments are legal and vital to the society.

Unfortunately, we ordered tea - with milk, to drink in cafe. You basically get an amount of milk based on whatever the staff feel like pouring in on top of the tea bag in hot water - far too much in this case.  Despite leaving the teabag in the mug for the duration, the tea remained overly milky.  If one is paying well over £3 for a mug of tea, providing some separate milk to add yourself, after the tea has brewed in water for a few minutes, is not really too much to ask.  Could do better.

I went to the Starbucks in Covent Garden today and had a very unpleasant experience. I was standing in a long queue to order my coffee and politely asked one of the staff members if I could use the toilet while waiting. Instead of helping, she told me that if I needed a toilet, I should go outside and use the public toilets in Covent Garden.

Her attitude was rude and completely unnecessary. I was genuinely shocked. Because of this treatment, I changed my mind, cancelled my order, and went next door to Red Farm restaurant where the staff were polite and I was able to use the toilet without any issue.

Very disappointing customer service. I won’t be returning to this branch.

Been here a few times in the last couple of years. Never had any issues. Drinks have always been done well even when its busy. The layout downstairs is nice and tucked away, which is amazing when you need a break from the madness of London. Staff are nice and sometimes do art on the drinks cup, which is always lovely! Highly recommend and hope to return soon!
Always very busy but good customer service.

Barista doesn't know what he was doing! Or maybe he's too tired of serving customers.
Gave us the wrong order, he made another one and still gave us wrong order! No apologies recieved not even changing our order!
I am lacto intolerance and gave me a semi skinned while my order repeated 3x to him that it was an oat latte and oat cappuccino.
Time of serving 18:30 16th November at Starbucks Covent Garden.
It was a very busy Starbucks, but the staff were working hard to keep things moving. Despite the rush, they stayed focused and did their best to serve everyone efficiently.
Please change the opening and closing hours to the appropriate times, as it is not 24 hours anymore as they barricade both doors at 12am

I had an online meeting and wanted somewhere to sit. So I purchased a coffee, and then tried the free WiFi. It didn’t connect; tried it many times and was unsuccessful. I then tried hotspot, though the coverage was poor. I’d not recommend the place if you want to do some work or have a catch up.

Have tried this place for coffee couple of times at morning, afternoon & evening. No doubt staff is friendly n chatty. Even chat with their colleagues n ignore the customers.
Most of times, queues are long enough to make u feel that one train is here.
Coffee is not what u can get from other Starbucks. It’s more weak. Tried tea but it’s more watery.
Super cool location inside station building. So convenient to have coffee or tea before get train. They know it’s station, people come n go. Not permanent customers. So sometimes they charge u wrong. Please keep an eye on them. Hope they’ll improve their tea or coffee as they have improved customer service in last few months.

New member of staff on till and as I had a voucher she had to call a senior person over. That person was attentive and professional. On collecting my order the gentleman was a little more interested in smiling with young lady customers than to fully complete my order. I had to wander a little as the straws were not indicated. Finally, the tables were a little messy. I appreciate that it is a busy location, but that should be watched. Overall a nice site, suited for busy trade.

A typical Starbucks coffee shop.

It is not too big, but spacious enough. It offers service inside, but also has tables outside on the perimeter of the cafe.

The restaurant is located in a quiet area and offers a beautiful view.

They have a wide variety of tasty products - of which I have tasted. The coffee is ok, and the teas are from the bag.

The staff is kind and nice.

The place seems to be well maintained, but the tables are not really clean.

One thing puzzled me: why does the air conditioner blow cold air when it's winter outside and it's already cold.

We visited this starbuck and order a few drinks and enjoyed hanging out in the cool relaxed atmosphere. Great drinks and views. Excellent service by baristas- Manager Sarah and  Barista Rebecca. Definitely recommend a visit here.
Flys on the food and no toilet paper in the only restroom.
Extremely delicious Cookies and Creame Frappuccino! Loved it!

Not the best Starbucks around. It's small and the AC was blasting, there wasn't a lot of food choice. They needed to change my drink and though I know it's Starbucks policy so the lady did it, there was no apology, no pleasantness, no "of course, that's fine", just took my drink and changed it...it makes you feel bad for asking

The seating area is quite nice. One of the servers initially got our order wrong, but quickly apologized and fixed things right up. The WiFi worked well. The tables were kept clean. Good selection of pastries available even in the evening.
Starbucks is always in my list of favourite choices. Nice staff, nice drinks, clean restroom and nice decorations.

This was my first time having tea at a Starbucks in London. The staff were kind and accommodating. I asked if they had any special drinks unique to London, which was a tricky question, and they couldn't pinpoint one.
However, they offered to make a matcha latte with frothed milk for me, which was delightful.
Caramel Frappuccino was great! And the service was wonderful!  Thanks to Pesh for great service and good communication!
The best Starbucks I’ve been to! Great service, great vibes and a great attention to detail. They never fail to remember my drink and always greet me with a lovely smile every morning.

TodK and Anna Maria were absolutely wonderful.
I was visiting from out of town and stopped by this Starbucks multiple times a day for four days. By my third visit, TodK already remembered my matcha order - such a thoughtful touch.
Both TodK and Anna Maria were incredibly friendly, positive, and attentive throughout all my visits. Truly great customer service.

I was in the hospital yesterday and I was very tired and it was very cold. I went here. The staff were super friendly and the overall atmosphere of the café is great. Thank you Anna Maria and  Aditi . Their good behaviour made me forget the cold and tiredness.

When did it get to the stage that a 500ml bottle of still water became almost as expensive as a coffee? £2.35 what the hell!! What's really great is the prices are hidden away so as not to scare you away before you hand it 9ver at the till.

I am a Thai student who studied in London for 1 year. I drink Starbucks coffee at the Euston tower branch almost every day. The baristas here are friendly and provide very good service. Thank you very much again and again from Ally. ☕️
Refused to accept unidays student discount, asked if only this store was told that Starbucks only takes blue light discount (NHS) even though I had the app and the app had the discount on it

Very lovely food and service, our food was delicious and very quickly prepared. The staff was so very very nice, a blessing, we had to get a product for a family member at home, and they so kindly packaged it for to-go, and gave us a nice box and bag free of charge for it. They filled our personal water containers with hot water for free, and were very accommodating to our situation.

Starbucks – Brunswick Centre/London:

A large, well-lit branch with tons of natural light. Cheerful staff and the usual array of STARBUCKS goodies. Seating and tables, WI-FI all available. Need to shut the doors during winter…

For various reasons, over the last four years I've become a regular at Starbucks in the Brunswick Centre near Russell Square, and invariable turn up with my eldest son Tom, and my bike, which is a pleasant fifteen kilometres ride from my flat, but only five minutes  walk for Tom who lives in Seymour Place.
There's nothing that exceptional about the coffee but I enjoy seeing the welcoming staff, and friendly atmosphere, and you can sit inside or outside in comfort and the special bonus is that there's never any traffic noise.

During the freezing cold day they are keeping the store door wide open and they run air-conditioning, They dont want you to spend cosy time thats why .do not visit completely Avoid , if you are book worm or want to chill out by your self or with a friend than this place is not for you.
There are plenty cafe.a in.Brunswick centre.

Nice, friendly, attentive staff who proccessed our order efficiently. Me and my sister brought overnight oats my personal favourite. Toliet facilites for customers (ask a member of staff to unlock it for you, they have a button to open the door from behind the register) kitted out with accessibility friendly equipment, furthermore it's a large Toliet ideal for wheelchair users. Beware 1 ply toilet paper you've been warned, quadruple up when using it pro tip, your welcome. 😉🤣
Very clean, calm and relaxed environment. ❤️

Wouldn't normally leave a review for a chain as most people already know of Starbucks lol, but the people working in this one are so friendly and they always write something nice on my coffee cup every morning!

A small place but very vibrant, live and in central london where you wont miss anything.  Great coffee and they do lots of other stuff. Very busy place but that is what they want. Enjoyed their coffee and warm cookies. Cleanliness need little attention.

This is my favourite Starbucks in London. Mostly because of the people who work there. They are consistently lovely to me - even when suuuper busy as this place can be. I really appreciate their friendliness and being recognised by name (and dog). This is deffo a place to grab a beverage as it is small, but you can sit outside and soak up the colourful Carnaby St atmosphere.

Place was clean and the workers were nice. However, I asked for a pumpkin spice latte- that every other Starbucks carries- and they had no pulling spice, I then ask for a drink advertised on the boards and they said they didn’t have either of them. Really let down, worst Starbucks I’ve been to in a long time.
