Clean, spacious Starbucks with ample seating and restrooms. It gets busy in the mornings and at lunch, so definitely mobile order if you don’t plan on staying.
Standard Starbucks with a great location near St. Paul’s Cathedral. Very close the tube station. Friendly team, clean, and efficient with service. Good overall Starbucks.

Very similar to most other Starbucks. It's usp is that there are chairs and tables ro watch the world go by.

A great place to take in the city...assuming you're a tourist!

Service...exactly what you expect from Starbucks.

Starbucks festive drinks are back! Praline cookie hot chocolate with vegan whipped cream topping is a must try - it is rich in flavour, and tasted way better that any other hot chocolate I have tasted. The drink itself is definitely worth the price. The place also has a huge selection of pastries and cakes - which all look fresh and delicious! This is definitely my favourite Starbucks in the entire city of London - great location, clean and cosy atmosphere. The service is always quick - The staff are also helpful and friendly. In the past few months that I have been going here, I've never had bad experience. Thank you!

Amazing service from Mao and Ashra! They were so welcoming and made the experience really enjoyable for our family. Their child-friendly service stood out, which is quite rare these days. Truly appreciated and highly recommended!

Coffee was good compared to most Starbucks. Unfortunately, the opening hours were not clear as hand edited on the sign. Closing time appears to have recently changed to 7pm from 7.30pm. So they have closed half an hour sooner than I was expecting and I am now drinking my coffee on the street. The staff were not apologetic at all, just smirking at me.

This place is ran like a street vend joint in the 3rd world. The toilets are an absolute joke, and gives you an overall look into how clean this establishment actually is.

The language skills of the staff is poor, it's incredibly hard to order when met with a thick accent that is barely intelligible.

Management, you need to put more attention on these things it's incredibly bad.

Standard Starbucks branch. Generally good service from baristas as long as you know what you want and prepare to pay promptly. I will say there is one older member of staff who seems to not enjoy serving me and seems to take time to avoid greeting me and looking from one corner of the eye as if I am stupid (could be due to my ethnicity and I am presumably a rich uni student which is not the case). This has happened a few times and this person is all smiles and friendly with anyone who isn't the same ethnicity as me.
As someone on the spectrum I get anxious making a lot of eye contact when meeting people. I assumed on my most recent visit that this person was having technology issues with the POS system so another younger barista with more politeness took my order.

The stools by the window are a great place to watch the world go by but the arms made it too right to fit my hips in. I’m a small fat person with pretty unremarkable hips but I found them very uncomfortable. I didn’t actually buy anything because I don’t have any interest in supporting Starbucks’ awful political history, especially at the moment.

One of the most horrendous Starbucks, which is something this place cannot afford to be in this location given the quantity of Starbucks within the area (Goodge Street one is better by a mile). I ordered a Mango Dragon Fruit refresher, and for some reason they gave me dragon coconut. Even though I said Mango specifically.

To make matters even worse, they added lemonade to the drink which clearly tells me the person behind the till is far too inexperienced. They then proceeded to not only give me the wrong drink size, but the label didn't even have my drink on it meaning they messed up someone else's order. Genuinely how incompetent does this store have to be to not mess up one person's order but two? Oh and to no one's surprise, my drink tasted disgusting.

Never in my life will I ever order from this place when there are better quality Starbucks that are like 2 minutes walking distance to this one. Shame on this place.
Incredibly care-less attitude of staff. The rudeness of staff also astounds me. Bathroom was closed to paying customers. Dirty tables and floors. Do better! Poor management and mars the brand!

This Starbucks is decent, but very tight, there's very minimal room for personal space between tables. Like, imagine actually feeling the body temperature of the person sitting at the table behind you, ugh.

The other Starbucks in the area is even smaller, so maybe look at another area if you're looking for an inexpensive cafe to remote work in.

Tired after your visit to amazing Westminster Abbey ? Go and recharge your battery in this Starbucks ! A small one where seated tables might come as a miracle ! Chairs are uncomfortable, probably to make sure guests are not staying in for too long !
Efficient service and the staff were super polite. The barista who did my coffee did a lovely job and I did not have to wait long at all considering it was so busy! Thank you
Friendly staff. The place got busy during lunch but is nice to relax/ warm-up / cool-down after a day of sightseeing around Westminster
Was okey, but there is no toilet so you should not expect setting with comfort here like any other cafe , staff are good and efficient, I think a bit pricy but this is London
Had a very good experience when I was served by a barista named Deeya. She made me the nicest classic hot chocolate and her smile and kindness was much appreciated. Will be coming back here again.
Toilet like this at 17:50. Disgusting. Also the coffe master of the store passing the vacuum. For sure they don't wanna brush at closing time
Batista had no interest in serving us. No milk in 2/3 drinks, didn't add the correct syrup, served in take away cups after asking if we were staying in. Only small things but wouldn't rush back

I ordered a coffee and was bitterly disappointed. Bitter being the operative word. The coffee is bitter to taste and the cup is half foam. I would have let that go, but the operator who served me was very rude, sharp and abrupt. Not sure if your having a bad day but that's not my fault. Being a little bit more cheerful wouldn't go astray. I won't be returning.
Quick coffee stops, laptop work, and light meals with a view of the Thames or Tower Bridge

I very rarely use city cafes because I use a private automatic coffee machine from a renowned brand at home... but this place left fantastic memories for my family.  We came here by accident from the street for 1 hour, as a haven, a waiting room before the LEGO Festival in a nearby hotel.  The man with a blue ponytail serving us is a true friend of man 😊 - he has a fantastic approach to customers, he talks to everyone very nicely, the coffee he served us was very tasty.  Additionally, he gave us delicious cupcakes for free - my daughter was delighted when she received two 😄 for her baby cafe 👌 A wonderful, clean, nice and intimate place to rest and take a break from everyday life.  Thank you and we wish you many satisfied customers of this coffeehouse👍

Been a few times.  Service is ok but not friendly.  Visited last week and the girl who served me, I think she was new, was pleasant but made a mistake with my order (not a big deal at all, she just misheard me) and her colleague who had to remake my drink was rude and condescending to her.  Very unnecessary and unpleasant to watch.
I ordered a VENTI size iced latte with oat milk. But the coffee wasn't filled to the top; it was only about half full. And my cup is VENTI size starbucks cup. I have attached photos for reference.
0 generosity. Placed an order for myself and wanted a paper cup for my NHS colleague so I could split the drink. Told blankly no I can’t allow this. Service was appalling. Lesson learnt.
Good selection of the usual Starbucks range of food and drinks. Always busy.

I am writing to share feedback regarding my recent visit.
On January 20th, in the afternoon (around 4pm), I experienced unsatisfactory service from a male cashier on duty.

During my order, I was not asked whether I wanted whipped cream on my drink. After receiving the coffee, I asked the barista if it could be added. The cashier then stated that he had already asked me and that I had declined, which did not reflect my experience.

I chose not to pursue the matter further and left with the drink as it was. However, I found the interaction unprofessional and below the level of service I would expect.

I hope this feedback can be used to improve customer service in the future.

I recently got the best TooGoodToGo bag I've ever had from Starbucks. The staff were incredibly friendly, and the atmosphere inside was cozy and clean.

There were seats available outside for a pleasant dining experience, and the place was not crowded at all.
Highly recommend!

This cafe was so much better then the coffee shops around the area. The employees were kind, patient, helpful and very welcoming. The coffee hot as it supposed to be and hits the spot when you crave a good coffee on a cold day. Absolutely must try!!!
Lovely place, quiet, lovely surroundings. I was very happy reading and writing. I will come back once again from Madrid. Pleasant staff. Delicious Chai Latte.

Really unwelcoming staff. Went in here today with the intentions of getting a cinnamon bun and Frappuccino.

As soon as I went I noticed that the main woman working there had a bit of a scowl on her face. She did serve another customer before me but I stood there for a good 5 minutes.

After she had served the other customer I stood there waiting to see if she would serve me. She did not and she avoided eye contact.

I left wondering what I had did to be treated with such disdain. She really gave me the impression that she disliked me or had a prejudice against me.

There was also a young barista who stood with her back to me the whole time. She also did not approach me.

I wouldn’t go into this Starbucks again. It is not appropriate to treat customers as if they are annoyance when they are coming in to buy food out of your establishment.

It’s such a pity as this Starbucks is in a lovely location.

Your typical Starbucks offers a variety of hot and cold drinks. The chill cabinet was well-stocked. The staff were friendly, and the place was clean. It is a relatively small Starbucks with about eight seating areas and no toilets.
This small store is always very clean and the staff are polite and friendly. It's a great hub for commuters who want to collect their coffee on the way into the office.

I ordered a venti mocha latte, a very popular drink at Starbucks. I have been a 21 year long Starbucks customer. What I got was a venti matcha latte. I asked them to fix the correct drink. I got a grande mocha. I asked again for them to fix the correct drink. The venti mocha I got was the WORST coffee I have ever tasted. The barista was highly unqualified to run the barista station and was rude to boot! I eventually got the idea the person was purposefully mistaking the drink so he could give the mismade drinks to someone else.
Went at the end of staffs shift with Ali and Athira working. Such a cozy and peaceful place with great service and manners from both staff. would strongly recommend this place. 👏🏼👍🏼

I placed an order yesterday for a Venti drink.  Served me a Grande.  When I questioned it they looked pretty pleased that the receipt said Grande. This was annoying as my child also validated that I did indeed ask for a Vente.  Okay, I thought, your loss for the cheaper beverage.  I went back again the next day ordered my Venti drink with no ice.  Guess what, it included Ice!! Took my drink but I will definitely not be back.  It's a bad day when you can't order just one drink without a problem.

Outstanding service that genuinely considers local preferences by tailoring recommendations and ensuring personalized experiences. Attentive assistance is provided within a welcoming environment where staff are friendly and attentive to individual needs. This setting is especially ideal for weekends when the streets are less congested, making it easier to enjoy a relaxed visit.

It's a Starbucks so you know what you're getting. Expensive coffee but it is what it is. Given four stars as it has to be viewed within the context of comparable chains and it compares relatively well. If you're in this part of London and need a coffee and somewhere to meet or work for a bit, it's worth a visit..
Excellent service, listening to personal preferences. Diligent service in a good ambient. Great for the weekend when there aren't as many people in the street.
Racist, the manager was literally starring at me and judging me by my color and then i asked them to add cream and they didn't do it on purpose
They have 2 toilets and both closed, out of service, no toilet for customers...horrible. The coffee was so hot, you can taste the burned milk from overheating.
This seasonal drink Tiramisu is outstanding. A bit pricey but taste so good, look like a real tiramisu

Terrible experience store was disgusting, both on the shop floor and behind the bar.  Milk left out but no drinks being made. A staff member came with a cloth wiped up the blood then wiped the table down with the same cloth and then wiped the main serving counter aswel. Utterly disgusting 🤮
Very relaxed atmosphere, service was quick and the coffee was great! Love the little message to you. Thank you Renato! He was by himself behind the counter for our whole experience.

Popped in the other day. Food and coffee was really expensive as expected, considering the portion size. The restroom requires a special code to unlock which was quite inconvenient. I guess it is for safety.
Did not open on time at 6.30...4 people walked away as staff member waived us away...not good enough as this type of customer service is not forgotton

I work nearby and after lunch always go for a Caramel Macchiato and never had a problem before until today. It's a rainy day, I go inside, order my coffee and go for a paper napkin to dry my glasses when I was about to turn myself back to cashier the lady behind counter is shouting to me saying: "don't forget that I have to charge!". Honestly, very impolite and rude. If was going towards the exit I could even understand but no, I hand to her my own Starbucks cup so it's not like I was going to leave without paying. She might need some more training on how to deal with customers. My rating today is only because of this unnecessary and bad experience today cause so far service has always been good.

Very basic Starbucks. No nice chair or corner to sip your drink. No toilet either.
Luckily they are not franchise and accept vitality vouchers.
Nice staff and good service. No toilet but there is outdoor seating. A small Starbucks with approx seating for 15-20 max inside.

great place! the staff is always friendly and they provide great customer service!
The quality of the coffee is excellent and a nice kick to start your day!
amazing atmosphere
Small but I still manage to get a seat by the window every time. They have power/plug sockets by the table so great for all us mobile workers.

There are plenty of alternatives to this cafe that would appreciate the business. Customer service at this location is lacking terribly. Two registers, two clerks, only serving one customer at a time. No names taken for the drinks so no idea who’s coffee is who’s. ‘Coffee at the bar!’ Customers are forced to delegate. Everyone reaches for it. Situation at register ordering... only utilizing one register, for unknown reason. Staff looks at me raises her eyebrows and gestures to me to come forward, blank stare, then other staff gestures at me that another customer was paying, suggested to me I was cutting queue and pointed at the customer at the other register, ... he’s here... I say, oh you called me forward.... she appeared ready for order, other customer at other register... eh what’s going on? No hello, no thank you, no common courtesy.

Quick service, large seating area downstairs but no one is Starbucks ever seems to be able to get you name right on the cup. Today I am “Aus”
One downside, clean your public toilets. Yes it’s busy, so even more reason to make sure they are up to standard.
Nice decorated seating areas with comfortable chairs. The very downside is the toilet where I don't know when the last time it was cleaned, really did not need to smell that.
Another amazing Starbucks store. The downstairs seating area is just perfect. Everything you could want from a store.

I go there very often and service, coffee, seating and location is great. I however need to deduct 1 star as you get literally thrown out in the cold when they're closing the downstairs area. Upstairs isn't much space and mostly all seats are taken, so when you hear "sorry, but we're closing the downstairs area in 5min", then this means you'll be standing outside in 5min.
Great coffee shop!! Super kind staff, the baristas are wonderful, , happy and helpful!! Hey nice location! I love this Starbucks, it is the best in all of Southampton!!

Ordered a "grande" Hot Chocolate cup just 5 minutes ago, they gave me an half-empty "grande" Hot Chocolate cup. Seems legit.

And by the way, it's Marco, not Mashko.. it's not even close!

Generally had good, simple service. Order, pay, and wait for your order to be called. Today however, don't think the manager is present. Ordered and paid but never got the call. The girl today set the cup on the counter and went right back to chit chatting with coworkers. Found out later it was my order and no apologies...

I understand it happens sometimes and I was just the unlucky one today but please be more careful in the future.

I never go to Starbucks as I don't drink coffee. But I was working near by and someone bought me a got chocolate.  I was amazed at how tasty it was. Most enjoyable and not too sugary which is normally the problem.
A typical Starbucks with friendly staff and plenty of seating available. There is also free wifi here which makes it a good place to work. They also accept payment via their app or Apple Pay here.

After a great nights sleep at the Strand Palace On a bright Saturday morning in February I took a walk down the Strand & the smell of fresh ground coffee pulled me here. A big friendly smile greeted me followed by a perfectly produced and presented coffee, there are a few seats outside one of which was empty, so I sat down & watched London folk go about their business while I enjoyed a perfect coffee. Do pop in and enjoy for yourself, it’s a Flag ship store

Good cup of coffee as one can expect at Starbucks. Fast service, kind staff.

Toilet (just one toilet overall!) wasn't clean at all, improve the toiletcleaning and easy 5-star review!!

Over the past 3 or 4 months until now (June 2016), I've got coffee here  3 times -- each time my drink was not correct. I hate to leave bad reviews but after another customer told me they regularly get his order incorrect it seems a bad review may be required. (Note: Today, I saw my drink being made incorrectly but was able to only partially correct the drink.)  Personally, I will choose a different starbucks or stop at Sacred Coffee.

Staff on Monday morning are rude. Some blond haired woman literally shouted at me as I was the wrong side of the queue, yet I was the first person standing there. Service is beyond slow, if you even get served. Do yourself a favor cross the road and go to Pret, same coffee and lovely nice staff

Typical Starbucks where tourists are the major customers. Despite that staff tend to serve fairly fast for a Starbucks and also clear tables.
Does mean sometimes but or miss that there anywhere to sit. There are tables outside which seem just too tall for the chairs.
Only one toilet and can get bin customers using it.

Forever my favourite Starbucks! Every single member of staff are so nice, friendly and chatty. I've always felt more than welcome here. They keep the café very clean and make the drinks so perfectly compared to other Starbucks. The café is really nice chilled design and pretty central Manchester as its on Oxford road, good for students and workers.

This place is real cozy and comfortable. Seating up front is good for people watching and the seating in the back is more for people just wanting to relax and chat or tend to their mobile devices.

Staff was awesome and patient. Come here before your day in London.

Woman called 'Di***' working here ruined a perfectly productive afternoon, stacking plastic crates as loudly and carelessly as humanly possible (literally booming through the cafe making me jump every time) during a delivery. My ears are still ringing. (See pic, those things). Huge metal things I understand make a noise but she was literally throwing the crates on top of each other.

Update - 15 mins later and the door is still propped open (it's bloody freezing outside and god-onky-knows-why the Aircon is still turned on) and she's still smashing and throwing plastic crates around.

Update 2 -
Thank god another staff member has just taken over what this woman was doing, he proves that it is possible to do that job without crashing warehouse sounds filling the whole shop.
The great Seattle’s coffee at London. As always the great coffee shop.
Very cozy, friendly staff. Great place to have a coffee or quick something to eat.

I like this branch of Starbucks. It has a lot of squidgy, comfortable seats and it is lovely and warm inside on cold days. The baristas make great drinks and are friendly and efficient. I would like to be able to visit later, but sadly it closes quite early.

Back from a meeting so took the cut through via Villiers Street.
In urgent need of a strong coffee so my only immediate option was this Starbucks.
Really nice place and great service, nice hot latte and a small (calorie mad) piece of cake.
Was walking a few miles so thought I could justify it.
Great coffee shop and service was really good.

This is a reasonable branch, small amount of seating but it's tucked down a street behind the train station so not huge inside. The busy atmosphere means that it's often messy and not very comfortable for a pleasant visit but fine if you're taking out.
Seating and toilets need looking at, but the service has mostly always been superb. They get your drinks out fast and they take your order in a good way. (friendly manner) I like it!

Tucked underneath Charing Cross, it is easy to miss this branch of Starbucks. The food is of the usual good standard, and the service is particularly friendly: I always felt very welcome. Choose your table wisely, for the toilet at the back is popular with tourists looking for relief: a queue of people with crossed legs usually forms, sadly. Otherwise, there are plenty of tables, and plenty of places you can settle with your laptop and tap away in peace. Oddly, the WiFi was occasionally slow here; can I blame tourists again?

Lovely Asian guy with a mullet was super friendly, had amazing vibes, smiley, bubbly, polite. Also had a lovely female colleague with dark brown rues and hair who was so on the ball in taking orders and giving such an efficient service. Kudos to the staff who weren't as talkative / didn't deal w me but helped in making the service quicker. Turned a stressful morning into a very pleasant one and I managed to get breakfast and get to work on time 🥰💗

After being second in the line. I watch the clearly racist male European manager who wore glasses serve the non coloured people who walked in after me. Then when I spoke up he gave me the wrong order continuing to waste my time as I waited for my correct order no apology just an insincere “enjoy”. I do not even trust my drink. He needs the boot!

Monday 29th September 2025 at 8:40am

Really impressed of the fast service. Baristas are really nice and welcoming as well. The iced caramel macchiato I ordered was one of the best so far I had compared to other stores. The place is  clean and neat as well. It also has a great location with a nearby bus stop and it is just in front of the Borough Station.
Staff really friendly as you would expect from starbucks. Great coffee as usual. One toilet which is a shame as everyone is queuing up for it. Can get very busy .
Quick service , nice ambiance , good value of money if u are a coffee lover

Very modern Starbucks that doesn’t attract quite as many tourists as the nearby Vigo Street location, but still is absolutely packed all weekend long with no place to sit unless you come in the early morning. They also typically only have four baristas working, so drinks come out slow. Mobile order well in advance of arrival.
Staff are friendly and always smiling. The PSL was back and I had to try and then back and had it hot and a cheeky muffin.

Really nice quiet and professional Starbucks. Busy yet not overcrowded and a completely stress free zone. Lovely staff serving and tasty drinks (as usual). No feeling of being rushed out the door like in some other stores (mainly by customers usually and not the staff)

The service is quick and coffee is nice. However, I don't know why but Cold brew tastes different in this particular branch. Maybe it is just me. Also as it is known for some branches, they only have one toilet for everyone.

I have been in Starbucks literally dozens of times, all over London with my dog, a well trained and clean Labrador who will stand quietly by my side and wait...

In this store, this was the first time I was told I had to leave because she was with me. I was told by the manager that it was city wide policy that dogs are not allowed in Starbucks. This is strange as I’ve never been told that was the case, in several years of visiting them. I then went down the road to another and was told that this is incorrect. Never has the staff in that particular store been told that dogs are forbidden. He suggested the manager was lying.

Whatever the policy, it needs to be clear and put on the doors. And the manager was particularly rude about it anyway.
A cafe that forbids dogs is ridiculous in my opinion. They claim it’s for hygiene reasons - but if I had not showered for a month and just walked across a muddy field they would let me in. A dog who’s just had a bath with clean paws is not allowed. Tell me the difference in hygiene between a guide dog and a pet dog?

Sort it out Starbucks, and get your policies straight.

"If you go in that place, make sure you ask for Augie! /
She's nice, she smiles, she'll let you bring your doggie! /
She doesn't make a difference if you drive Rolls or trucks /
Just go and have a coffee with Augie at Starbucks! "

Alexander King*
Good service, coffee spectacular and danish was delicious.

Asked for four coffees (regular) and got huge buckets of coffee with three shots at 4.80 pounds each.  These aren’t regular size at all.  Should have been explained to my elderly relative before charging him.

It’s a nice Starbucks HOWEVER, they stopped accepting cash. Not all Starbucks’ are cashless but this store particularly chose to be cashless. I won’t be able to buy from here anymore as I prefer using cash.
Had a great experience at this branch last Thursday. Plenty of seats and great staff. A special shout out to Anne who went over and beyond ❤️🫶🏽 The cherry blonde was lovely

Excellent service at Starbucks London Bridge station. Special thanks to Femin and Aan for their outstanding customer service. They were very friendly, polite, and professional, even during busy hours. The coffee was perfectly made, and their behaviour towards customers was truly commendable. One of the best Starbucks experiences I’ve had. Highly recommended!

I visited this store today
It was great experience, friendly staff always serves coffees with smiles. Quick service and clean place ,
Best place to have break

Starbucks always delivers an outstanding coffee experience. From the moment you walk in, the rich aroma of freshly brewed coffee and the friendly smiles of the baristas make you feel welcome.

I ordered a Caramel Macchiato, and it was perfection — smooth, flavorful, and perfectly balanced. The drinks are always made with care, and the quality is consistently top-notch no matter when you visit.

The café itself is clean, comfortable, and inviting — ideal for working, studying, or just relaxing. The background music and cozy atmosphere make it the perfect spot to unwind.

Kudos to the Starbucks team for their excellent service and attention to detail. Every visit feels like a treat!, Super staff they treated super manager sridar given us best coffee

Starbucks never disappoints! The moment you walk in, you’re greeted with the comforting aroma of freshly brewed coffee and the warm smiles of the baristas. The service is always fast, friendly, and consistent — even during the busiest hours.

I ordered my usual Caramel Macchiato, and it was absolutely perfect — smooth, rich, and just the right balance of sweetness and espresso. The drinks always taste fresh, and the quality is consistent every single time.

The store is clean, cozy, and has a great atmosphere whether you’re catching up with friends, studying, or just relaxing with a good book. The music adds a nice touch, and there’s always a welcoming energy that makes you want to stay a little longer.

A big thumbs up to the Starbucks team for maintaining such high standards! This place truly knows how to make every cup feel special
Great store with very good ambiance and friendly staff. Freshly baked pastries are amazing, must try.
The staff was so kind. They got our names all wrong😂
If u order anything else on top of your drink Coffey whatever, wait time is 15min. First time I ignored this way serving, second time well… but today I pis…..

The worker there was terribly rude and lied about the bathroom being out of order. Immediately turned to his coworker to talk bad about us as if we didn’t understand. Even though we ordered and wanted to stay, they treated us with the most utter disrespect.

Your female staff member, K--, seemed unhappy with her job. Her tone while taking and confirming our orders was so rude, there was no smile, and she was visibly frowning the entire time. Seriously? The location is great, but how can anyone feel encouraged to come back or recommend the place to others if the staff behaves like this? I didn’t expect this kind of poor customer service in the heart of London!

A really nice stop! Very well placed within the City, the staff are friendly and helpful. The store had a full range of food and treats and I was able to plug in and get some work done while I had my breakfast and a coffee.

The store is clean and tidy with an accessible toilet.

Starbucks at Shad Thames is a pleasant and convenient spot for a coffee break. The location is great, with a cosy atmosphere and plenty of seating. Staff are friendly, service is quick, and the drinks are consistently well made. A nice place to relax, work, or enjoy a coffee while exploring the are

I gave this star bucks a 5* purely on its reasonable quiet location on the shard Thames along from Tower Bridge. Has a lovely little court yard and water feature out the back , with surrounding shops.
Clean and tidy with available toilets (also well kept and clean, well was when I visited, albeit had to que).
Nice for a quiet pit stop (if not overly busy) and to relax..

Standard but well located Starbuck
The best thing about this Starbuck is the location and that it has toilets. The product were as expected (high quality for high price). But really nice place for the terrasse in summer.
The staff at this store are amazing, really nice seating with AC or you can sit outside. So nice to see Rakib from south bank store. Chilled place to sit and drink some coffee.

Beautiful Starbucks in a fantastic location, I sat in there today for a meeting, I bought a coffee of course. This is a really nice location I can’t begin to imagine the rent but this branch looks like a flagship. Amazing location with great views outside into the courtyard and amazing space inside.

Can’t say the seating was comfortable I think they went for style over comfort, but I can’t lie the interior looks fantastic. The service was as normal, functional and professional. The staff were very eager to clear the tables which is nice to see but a little rushed but that’s not really a complaint.

The most important thing about this review is the beautiful interior and the ideal location, it’s literally a hop skip and jump to tower bridge and the Thames river. I can imagine in the summer that place will be packed with tourist. Let me know if you agree with me, enjoy the images.

A lovely lady named Isu served me, and was very sweet and made my experience a great one rather than just another day picking up coffee.
PS, the coffee was great as usual❤️
Thank you for the coffee and the great atmosphere Isu.

The Babka is just dry flavourless dough with barely any chocolate.
The staff and service is excellent. They always keep the place clean and attend you with kindness.

I'm truly impressed by the outstanding service provided by Jaishree, a delightful staff member at this branch. Her dedication to assisting customers with a warm smile is remarkable. Today, as always, she went above and beyond to ensure my experience was enjoyable. The food and drink were delicious, and her cheerful demeanour made my visit even more pleasant.Thank you for the chocolate too! :)

Absolutely disgusting! There were fruit flies on the muffins and cake slices. Even though they were inside the glass cabinet! There were dead insects on the floor. The place needs a deep cleaning! The tables weren’t clean….. it was awful

Small Starbucks right near the exit of Old Street underground station. There’s typically a morning and lunchtime rush as you would expect, but note they often forget about mobile orders, so ordering in-person can be faster. They also seem to have a high turnover, so inexperienced staff frequently make errors with orders. The food selection is decent with lots of sandwiches, but the stock dwindles later in the day. There is very limited seating in the cramped back of the store and the restroom requires getting a key from the counter.

I come to this Starbucks often and it’s always a great experience. The store is spotless, the lighting is lovely and there’s plenty of comfortable space to relax or get some work done.
The service is consistently 10/10 -everyone is so friendly, helpful and welcoming.
A big special thanks to Ajay, Hoon, Vivian, Muhammad and Mukut!! they always go above and beyond and truly make this location stand out!

Fully recommend!

I had a lovely visit here. Everyone is so friendly and it’s fast and courteous service. This bagel is by far the best things I’ve ever had from Starbucks. I am so sad that they do not have this in the states :(

First stop once stepping off the plane in London and it did not go as I expected from my US frequented locations.

1. The female associate had zero customer service/ hospitality energy and was very dry and condescending.

2. When I requested to have my muffin and cookie warmed, I was met with "We don't do that"

3. Me requesting my Caramel Macchiato made upside down and to line the cup with caramel simply did not happen.

Still plenty more places to visit, but definitely was not expecting this from Starbucks who is focused on gaining back the customer support and trust 😐

Such a lovely staff in this Starbucks, enjoying their greeting every time I'm there.Thank you guys, you are amazing
Came today for order just drink, I'm only on Sundays at this branch and the barista who took my order remembered which milk I need in my coffee, it was such a good experience from her customer service. Not sure I can pronounce her name but it's starting from D....and very long one😊.
Thank you darling, you made my Sunday morning.

I live in Japan and haven’t been to Starbucks very often, so when I was asked for my name while ordering, I didn’t quite understand and simply replied, “Name?”. When I went to collect my drink, the barista called out with a cheerful smile, “Mr No Name!”, and that’s when I realised what they had meant. We both burst out laughing. Their warm sense of humour and kind service made all the difference. Although it was late November and quite cold in London, that moment truly warmed my heart. It’s become one of my fondest memories from the trip.

Very bad experience at this Starbucks. I ordered an iced matcha and from the very beginning I clearly told the girl that I wanted it blended, as that’s how I like it. She still didn’t make it blended and when I politely mentioned it, she refused and said “that’s how it is”, completely ignoring my preference.

I calmly explained that I didn’t want it that way and told her that if she couldn’t make it blended, that was fine and I would prefer a refund. She then agreed, but the service was awful  she made the drink with a very bad attitude and almost threw the drink at me in a rude manner.

Starbucks is known for customising drinks, so I don’t understand this kind of treatment. Very disappointing service. I wouldn’t come back.
Naha & Siyul made the best matcha I’ve ever tasted. She’s so friendly and welcoming, really made me evening. The best barista I’ve ever come across
Enjoyed fantastic time here! Karthik is such a wonderful man who helped me a lot. The atmosphere is warm. Love it so much!
Wonderful staff and great service every time. Everyone is patient and welcoming, and I’ve been a regular for a year with nothing but positive experiences! Thank you!
Fast, friendly and professional service. Thank you Karthik it's greatly appreciated - you certainly brightened up my morning.

Quite large normal Starbucks. Most of the USB plugs don’t work. Could do with a good clean the toilet was filthy. Full of dust all over the pipes toilet paper everywhere sink and mirror dirty. A good clean needed all round. For such a large company very bad I expect better.

This is a nice Starbucks location on Whitechapel Rd. I visited here daily during my recent trip to London, due to it being located right next to my hotel, on both occasions ordering my favourite, Iced Pumpkin Spice Latte

On both occasions, my drinks were very good, the speed of service could have been slightly better, although they can get pretty busy, and I'd rather them take a little longer to make the drinks with effort and care, which appeared to be the case here.

The interior is comfortable, and there's a good amount of seating, and a nice feel around the cafe area.

Prices are in line with other Starbucks locations in London, a little more expensive than the rest of the UK, but that's OK.

It would have been nice to see them remain open a little better, but there are other Starbucks locations nearby, so I can understand them wanting to close some stores early.
It was s first experience in london! Quite spot and very cozy and comfortable !
Picked up some London and England mugs to take home. The 2 baristas were friendly and helpful. This location was clean and organized. Thank you 😊

Absolutely terrible shop. Staff cant do a job right. i order a cheese toastie and get ham? im allergic to that and they cant even bother to pick up there phone. I could have ended up in hospital. Im disgusted on how careless you can be to give a ham sandwich without checking.

I recently visited this Starbucks branch and unfortunately had a disappointing experience with the management team. The attitude displayed by the staff felt unwelcoming and dismissive, and their communication lacked basic courtesy and customer care.

Instead of providing helpful service, the team appeared indifferent and gave the impression that they were enforcing their own rules rather than prioritizing customer experience. This left a very negative impression, which is unexpected from a brand known for hospitality and professionalism.

I hope the management takes this feedback constructively and focuses on improving customer service, staff training, and overall professionalism to ensure a better experience for future customers

Very poor service at this Starbucks. The barista Sohaib and another staff member were extremely rude. I waited over 20 minutes for just one coffee, and when it finally arrived, it was not properly packed in the cup. No apology or explanation was given. Management should take action against such unprofessional and rude staff.

We met Alex who was arranging the merchandise on the shelves. And because of his impeccable service and wonderful attitude we ended up buying a ton of stuff! If only every Starbucks had an employee like him! He truly made our day even more delightful. Thank you for everything Alex!

Went on Sunday afternoon, super busy, the staff there were very helpful, the girl at the cashier counter was very sweet, cheerful and charming. Had to wait for a bit, but the drinks were nice and hot. But the toilet was awful (Men's).

Worst Starbucks experience I’ve ever had. The barista, Syed, provided shockingly poor service. No friendliness, no effort, no basic customer care at all. I ordered a coffee with caramel syrup and somehow received a cup that tasted like disappointment instead. There was no caramel syrup in the coffee whatsoever.

When I returned the drink, instead of remaking it properly, he simply dumped more syrup into the same coffee which had gotten cold by now and handed it back like that fixed anything. It didn’t. The drink still tasted bad…

Starbucks is supposed to be about consistency and customer service, this was the complete opposite. Lazy service, bad attitude, and an even worse drink. If this is the standard, I won’t be coming back.

The clerks here are very friendly, especially when I pass by every morning to buy coffee, I feel like a good day has started. Especially a clerk named Daren, he is very nice and would make me feel good about the atmosphere of this store.

Darren was very kind and polite to me. The coffee was served quickly. I guess this Starbucks had some problems in the past, but now everything is fine. However, this is my first visit. I will come back and see if I was just lucky or if this is just a good Starbucks now.
Most incredible and friendly staff ! Very kind with fantastic service ! Always make the coffee most enjoyable and amazing! Thank you so much 😊

Really good experience at Starbucks Mile End. The team here are polite, professional, and always smiling. Drinks are made well and exactly how you ask for them. One of the better Starbucks branches in East London.Soni, Darren , Hafsa, Romena and Sunny they all are very welcoming and has excellent customer service.
Amazing service and made a great drink. Hafsa provided excellent customer service. Thank you

The Starbucks is clean, organized, and overall a good place. However, there’s one major issue — there are only two employees working, one taking orders and one making drinks. In the mornings, it gets very crowded, and this causes long waiting times. Many customers avoid coming because their drinks take too long to be prepared. Starbucks should really consider adding at least 2–3 more staff members, especially during busy hours.

I’ve never had an issue with this Starbucks before, which is why this was so disappointing. I ordered my usual iced blonde roast Americano (I get this daily), but was given an iced Americano made with the default/dark roast instead. The difference in colour alone was obvious.

I pointed this out to the employee, but was told it was blonde roast when it clearly wasn’t. The drink wasn’t corrected, I couldn’t drink it, and I ended up throwing it away — meaning I missed out on my morning coffee entirely.

This morning, I wanted to go into the store to buy coffee, but found that the employee was playing with his mobile phone in the store. The door was closed and did not welcome the customers. I had no choice but to go to the opposite store to buy it.
Good coffee, as expected - but they don't carry scones at English Starbucks - I found that a bit strange. People were very nice though. Can't wait to visit again someday.

Poped into this starbucks for a quick morning coffee and crossiant.

Servers were happy and friendly and offered to warm up my crossiant. Unfortunalty my coffee was pretty poor and quite badly made.

Located within the London bridge retail section, near the Shard. My favorite place to work from when I'm on the "road" with no office access.
Staff are always friendly and work diligently and work well as a team.
Atmosphere is calm and comfortable considering its in central London. Always nice music playing in the background but not so loud you cannot hold a meeting there.
Coffee had always been well made by staff here, never had to complain about poorly made coffee and I noticed they try to make the beverages better when the taste isn't quite on par for some customers which were being served at the same time.
I have decaf most of the time and this is always well made here.
If a mistake on dine in or rake away they look to resolve that really quickly without question regardless of whose fault it was.
Always looks clean but the bin area could be better as it overfills quickly.
I like the little quotes that Kiera leaves on cups. I went back twice on the same day and its a nice pick me up to go with my coffee.

There are very few things I find pleasurable in this life however my tiramisu iced latte is one I am extremely fond of. I requested such drink, and watched two perfectly good ones go to customers who had ordered before me, yet when mine arrived it was as though it was thrown together by someone who doesn’t have a clue what they’re doing nor do they care. However, I requested that it be altered as it was far too bitter for me, even though I had asked for minus a shot of espresso. I watched them pretend to fiddle with it and handed it back to me as though I am none the wiser.
Truly shocking and disappointing behaviour from careless, untrained and undeserving employees. Walked away and chucked it where it belongs, in the bin.

I went to redeem a TooGoodToGood voucher.

1. First they had doubts that so soon I can use my voucher then I showed them up the time about the -from-to period my voucher is redeemable.

2. Then I got 3 foods from which 2 was the same. Boring ones.

3. They were much nicer with the general customers as I was just a TGTG one. Very sad experience, I wish I didn’t buy a voucher to them.

The 2 items i bought were very nice (ginger shot plus a flapjack type bar) but there was only one person serving (no sign of anybody else) so the service was very slow,  no comfortable chairs just wooden high chairs / stools and no toilets.
I had issue with my mobile order but Ivano was really nice and made my order perfectly. Thank you so much

Bullshit, shitty experience I had here never gonna come back. The girl name Binnie ,
any drink I asked for she said I don’t have it and making faces after that I got so disappointment. I had free drink in my reward she said no I cant to do that although no greeting no smile so rude towards my friends and all the customers as well never gonna be back for sure!

It is nice cozy place for coffee, with plenty of coffees I didn’t know that exists. Nice smiles, kind, happy to see you personnel making that place even better. But why I write this review? I meet there Binni. She bring me coffee before I can order it with words ,, There is your coffee sir”. It was fresh made hot coffee.I was looking bit confused, what the hell she reading ours mind. So if you go there clear your dirty mind and don’t think about your pin 😃. Please don’t challenge her about coffee you think because I visited that cafeteria almost every day.
I visit pretty much once a week.. Staff here are the best I have experienced in Starbucks. Super friendly, super quick - real credit to their organisation.
This woman serving right now at the till is so rude - I am so disappointed. I think her name was Tina she definitely has red hair and this was the last time visiting this outlet.

I’ve been to this Starbucks several times, and I  run into Julia❤️several times.She’s one of the friendliest and patient Starbucks partners I’ve ever met. Every time I see her, I feel a sense of comfort and know I’ll have a great experience.

Service is terrible from the staff is fine the prices have gotten ridiculous. I ordered a double espresso and was charged £3.75. I had to walk out because that's just an insult. I know prices have gone up for everyone but Starbucks service is no better than any rival. It looks like they're just being g greedy. Just close this branch down.

Very dissatisfied and disappointed! After reading the reviews that say this is a fake Starbucks, I cannot disagree! The price of coffee nowadays is ridiculous high! And I wanted to use my Starbucks points. They said they are a Franchise but all Starbucks are Franchised and have points system!!!

Very poor, my last time in this one!! Sorry

Pictures show scanners???

spilt my drink down myself 😂 staff jumped in super quickly and took control of the situation with no stress at all and even replaced my drink! as someone who also works in a coffee shop at a train station (the burgundy enemy) this was incredible service, the whole team on shift is a credit to the store (8:45 - 13/9/25)

Terrible coffee at Starbucks prices. Whilst "making" my coffee I realise it's a bean to cup machine, no barista in this coffee shop. Will never be fooled by this place again.  Use the Nero express opposite.
The staff were incredibly gracious following a slight spillage, remade our drink when they absolutely did not need to. The gentleman in the white shirt was a class act.

It does what it says in the tin - fast and basic Arabica bean coffee. The best Starbucks in the Broadgate Circle area - spacious, clean with large windows that fill the room with natural light. Especially good to get some work done with lots of power plugs and long narrow tables next to the windows. The staff while not overly enthusiastic are friendly, helpful and smiley.
I get my morning coffee here. I’m generally served by Max or Sira who are both cheerful and efficient.
Close to Liverpool station this is a perfect place with lots of seating to base yourself if you are here in the morning before meetings or in between meetings when you come to London.
Nice shop very warm atmosphere. Staff do their job I guess. Not very warm or welcoming they just do their job. A smile and a hello would help. Shop is very clean.
Amazing Starbucks! The drinks taste amazing and the staff are lovely, fun and attentive. Plus, big space for a relaxing break.

It’s a starbucks, but here you che find a good location in central London (Liverpool st station) with a big building with two flors just for customers, and also here you che find a really good quality of the drinks, with professional team and friendly
Great service and friendly staff. However, I waited for maybe five minutes, then realized the cup with the name 'Horan' written on it was mine. Horan is not my name. Far from it.

Three floors of converted Georgian townhouse make for a relaxed, comfortable, and reliably pleasant experience. In my opinion, this is the best Starbucks in London.

Next to the busy hub of Liverpool Street station, it's an instant relief from the crowds, a perfect pause between the station and the next door Spitalfields Market.

The food and drinks are standard, so I won't really discuss them. What makes this branch great is the location and the style. Over the three floors there is a healthy mix of tables, sofas, and comfy armchairs, so that even in an (often extremely busy) lunchtime rush, you can usually find a space by exploring the floors.

My only minor niggle is that the lift breaks down fairly regularly, and when I was visiting last year, I was heavily pregnant and getting up those stairs was a real killer! Obviously, if the lift is down it makes two thirds of the seating area inaccessible to those with wheelchairs, pushchairs, and lots of luggage.

Get there early, grab a good seat (not right next to the air con though, or it will get cold very quickly), and enjoy the atmosphere.

Not much to say about this place, it’s a Starbucks, but the staff were friendly and it’s a decent size. The WiFi was easy to get onto and it’s comfortable there. There’s a huge window so it’s great to chill and do a spot of people watching. Perfect for having a nice drink after a busy day at Petticoat Lane and Old Spitalfields Market!

I come most mornings before work, staff are nice and friendly, service is quite quick and drinks are a good quality. Also lots of seating areas on the upper floors which are kept clean and tidy and have easy access from the stairs or there's a lift.
Great coffee, great service, would have been a 5, but they've stopped the cheese n marmite!

This Starbucks was great. The service was quick and reliable, and the line wasn’t too bad considering the peak hours. As always the wifi was also fantastic too. If the Starbucks is a bit too busy, you might find yourself without a seat because it is rather small.

Nice. Well designed and modem looking.  Carpark is available to use.  Service can be a bit slow if busy.   Is a good size and seating it's ok.   Free WiFi is available.  Which is not a bad speed.  Coffee drinks are good. And good choice of food and cakes to choose from.

Simply the worse.
Ordered on the app:
- Asked for venti = got a grande. (I thought they changed the size as it's long time I didn't use Starbucks.) but no.
- Asked for extra hot steamed = got it not even warm. So when arrived in the office need to drink it in one sip inside of having it for 30min. Difficult with glove to realise when you take the drink.
- Ordered at 16:13 and should be ready within 2/3 min. Got it a 16:25 as they waited for me to arrive in order to make it.
The principal mean of ordering on the app and asking extra hot is to make sure you do not queue and wait and you can go back to your office with still hot drink and not a frappuccino instead.
- Maybe the last but not least: the waitress (young Asian) that give the drink on the counter without mentioning anything, not a smile, not a thank you, not a eye contact... For your own healthy for this waitress: leave your job and find something you like! Yes it's just coffee or chocolate but when you work 17h and you want to have something nice to drink back your office it is nice to see happy people doing their job too and providing a smile never cost anything.
Really I have just moved from moorgate and definitely not ready to spend my money there again.

It's a Starbucks - the McDonald's of the coffee world. However, the staff in this branch are friendly and fast. One thing about Starbucks - You'll get more coffee for your money in Starbucks than an artisan coffee house...

Nothing special on the menu but it is a nice spot with fast wi fi. They did forget to heat my sandwich and I had to get up and tell them that part of my order was missing. They finally realized that someone just forget it in the little oven.

I am sorry but this branch of Starbucks is not good. The coffee is just like other branches but the seating and staff behavior is a bit iffy. I tried to use the bathroom and it was a horrible experience, someone knocked on the door and got the staff as well. They kept knocking the door until I opened. It was very embarrassing.
Sorry guys but you should do better.

This Starbucks might be competing to be the smallest branch.
The staff are not friendly at all and it might to do with the tourist nature of the locstion.
Their filter coffee machine has been broken for the last two weeks.
The seating area outside are not bad though.

Normal Starbucks, it is pretty slow. Considering it’s small it gets busy. They have some seats outside, but make sure you get a table before buying your drink and having nowhere to sit, and maybe double check that the table is clean.

Worst customer service by young girl. I wanted to give no stars but could not review without giving one. Bought 2 frappucinos and she just threw the ingredients. She was reluctant to serve anyone. I do understand it's not the most exciting job but she could have made a bit of an effort to communicate with customers entering the store.

I went there twice. The first visit was great, but today, 3rd June on Sunday, 11AM, I was really disappointed. I ordered an iced Americano and a cinnamon bread, and the cashier did not any answer at all. Fine. But he forgot my order that happened a second ago!!  Then I asked him whether I ordered or not, and he just looked at me for a while and arranged the cups behind bar, so I was a little embarrassed and  called him again, then he got out the bread without any apologies. I had to stand in front of counter a few minutes after Ive already got my coffee!! Also, he did not give my any forks and knives(I have already told him I ate inside), so I had to ask him again. Seriously, what’s your problem? I am not sure that he was annoying because of his work on weekends or personal issues but BE PROFESSIONAL!!! I am so angry and even think it was racial discrimination because I am not a British. Seriously, Starbucks, you have to hire a person more prudently.

Good to grab a cup of coffee if you have a bit of time. If it's rush hour, please note that due to all the offices around and the fact there are not many Starbucks around the area, you may wait a bit, but not too long. I would like to open an exception in my reviews and mention a specific member of staff, Zizou, who brings a smile and good disposition to the "just woke up and did not have my coffee yet" zombies (myself included), whether if it's the 7:00 in the morning chaos or 8:00 Sunday morning slowness.
Delicious eggnog latte, making the most of them before they run out until Christmas 2019

The guy serving on till yesterday managed to complete the transaction without muttering a single word to me. I understand they were busy and looked a little short staffed (he was working on bar and jumped to the till), however it doesn't mean he can just assume to avoid all human interaction altogether.
Great service and Ricardo is very friendly, and always like to put the special ingredients

This may be my favorite Starbucks I've every visited and I've been to many around the world. The location right in the middle of St. Katherine's Dock is perfect for a relaxing and quiet break from the noise of London. The circular building is really unique and the upstairs seating gives a great view of the marina.

Great location within the dock, however, no toilets on site, there are some but you have to leave the building to use them. However, I still come here often and I recommend, lovely upstairs seating section when it’s not busy :)

Update (2019-03-13): This Starbucks has now shut and is no longer in business.

Very small coffee shop but set in a beautiful location. Be aware there are limited facilities, but given the size this is understandable
Apologies for the photo quality, but I took it from the other side of the dock!

Few Starbucks in London have better views. It's small, but out of peak times you should be okay. It's 1300hrs on a rainy Wednesday and there are plenty of tables upstairs where you can do that bit of work for an hour while wishing you owned one of the yachts moored below. Lovely staff as is so often tbe case for Starbucks. Definitely worth a visit. The wifi works just fine too.

Great location right inside the St. Katherina Docks a little bit off the tourists path. Great to enjoy a coffee and a snack in this cozy Starbucks location. Would recommend this locations over any of the other bigger coffee shops in the area.

Basic menu with basic food.
An relaxing venue but not so cozy.
The music and staff was great tho.
And ye ofcourse the white mocha and sandwich was great (even tho the sandwich was only bread and bacon.
Very nice Starbucks coffee with friendly staff, clean store and good selection of food. Nice for a quick visit whilst walking or visiting tower bridge.

Very nice decor and welcoming staff that make your order quickly. However, my caramel macchiato was bitter and tasted burnt. Staff were made aware and a fresh one was made, however it tasted exactly the same. Will definitely not be returning to this store.

The staff was friendly and the place had a nice chill atmosphere but I feel they need more practice in making the more difficult coffees. They failed to make a quality coffee after a few tries when they should have there training down to be qualified to work as a barista.

I am true Starbucks addicted, especially I am in love with the cold foam cappuccino which is just amazing and super tasty. That was my favourite drink this summer on a very affordable price. The taste can be changed depending on if you want sweater to be added or not. Highly recommended!

This is a new update for my favourite coffee goto place - Starbucks Reserve @ The Gardens mall. The other evening my wife and I decided to treat ourselves after shopping to take advantage of a Tuesday promo - spend RM38 in a single receipt with card or mobile app and receive a free glazed donut. Good deal...my grande latte was usual, good quality and choc muffin excellent. However, my wife's grande caramel frap in her tumbler felt short changed. C'mon Starbucks, you are better than this. Just because there is a promo on offer donut (?) cheat on the purchased product. I still give it 4 stars as I hope this was a one off disappointing experience.

I visited a few days ago with my pregnant wife who urgently needed to use their toilet. Angela the supervisor was extremely kind. She took care of my anxious wife showing compassion and sensitivity, reassuring her she could use the the toilet and even stood outside to make sure everything was fine while I grabbed myself a coffee. Also the young male was very nice and made me a great latte. We will definitely come back!

Nicole and Leslie were so kind to me, my phone gas ran out of battery, they gave me their charger and super dooper coffee, that made my day!!!
It was clean and comfortable, very nice atmosphere and kind personnel.
Thank you very much indeed!

The service it's not the fastest, but the product are lovely as any other Starbucks.
The place can get busy, so just me kind with staff and spread positive vibe, they're working as well.
Smile 😊

Excellent customer service when we visited last Wednesday at lunch time. The ladies serving us were competent, friendly and courteous, service was fast and efficient with great tasting beverages. Plus store clean, tables bussed regularly and distancing in place. A truly pleasant experience!

Spacious and a quite place to hang out in between your meetings. The WiFi was not working properly which is a minus, but decent indoor and outdoor seating space makes it the best place for quick catch-up or hangout
It's a Starbucks, it is what it is. Not the best coffee in the world but not the worst either. Quite expensive for what it is and you can do better for less. OK though.
Super friendly and efficient staff, in 2years of going there 2-3 times a week, they've never got my order wrong. Brilliant.
Nice place, new and clean, but no wifi. Not great if you need to work.
Coffee not to usual Starbucks standard and unable to use Starbucks loyalty app (not their fault )

Excellent service and lovely staff,  madga, loretta and yu were lovely in helping me decide which muffin I should buy.  I got the apple toffee one and it did not disappoint! Lovely location just right on front of the bank underground station.  Gorgeous views. Highly recommended

Expected better from Starbucks. The person making the coffee needs to be retraind on how to make a cup of coffee. My vanilla latte was served boiling hot totally spoiling the taste, actually tasted burnt. Waste of £5. Won't be going back there again
Price is skyrocketing single espresso cost £2.5 now a day. Service was prompt and efficient.

I recently took a brisk walk through London's bustling business district and stumbled upon the Starbucks on 87 King William St. Tucked away in a compact space, this Starbucks is quite unique. With no seating and limited bar counter space, it has a charming 'hole-in-the-wall' vibe.

For those familiar with the Starbucks experience, this location delivers the same quality in a pint-sized package. However, it's important to plan ahead. Due to its small size, the café can feel crowded even with just a few groups inside. My recommendation is to use the Starbucks app to place a mobile order before you swing by. This little trick can save you time and help you avoid the hustle in the cramped space.

Although this Starbucks maintains the brand's standards, it's not the ideal spot for settling in with a laptop or having a leisurely chat. Its small scale makes it more suited for a quick grab-and-go.

An interesting fact: this Starbucks is just a stone's throw away from the historic Leaden Hall Market, a location featured in the Harry Potter films. So, while you may not find a cozy corner to lounge in, you can enjoy your coffee in the midst of cinematic history. Overall, it's a convenient stop for a quick caffeine fix, but not quite the place for an extended stay.
I regularly visit Starbucks in general but this one doesn’t have toilet facilities, it should be a legal requirement to operate one if you’re a coffee shop

I often come to this Starbucks and the staff members here are so lovely and welcoming, there are usually 2 ladies who wear the hijab that are so sweet, welcoming and cater to my needs so well!! I believe her name is zoya, she’s super sweet and makes me feel so welcome!! Service is amazing, I will be returning!!

The sweetest team I’ve ever come across. I’ve been coming to this Starbucks every morning for over a year now, and it genuinely brightens my day. A simple “good morning” and “how are you?” from the staff goes such a long way, and they never fail to make me feel welcome.

There are two lovely ladies who usually work together on the early 6am morning shifts  I’m so sorry I’ve forgotten your names but your kindness, smiles, and positive energy never go unnoticed. You make my mornings start on such a positive note, and I really appreciate you both.

Thank you for being such a wonderful team and for making this Starbucks feel like a little morning safe place 🤍

If you’re lost in Canning Town and need a sign from the universe, fear not—it’s probably just the Starbucks logo calling you in. And trust me, answer the call.

First off, the **location** is golden. Right near the station, so perfect for dodging the rain or pretending to be productive while waiting for a train that’s already late.

Inside? **Spotless.** I don’t know who’s in charge of the cleaning here, but give that person a raise and maybe a cookie. Speaking of cookies—**the bakery’s fresh**. I bit into a croissant and swear I heard angels singing in buttery harmony.

The **coffee?** Hot, strong, and comforting—like a hug from someone who really knows how to use an espresso machine. Whether you’re into flat whites, lattes, or just want to order something long and confusing to feel fancy, they’ve got you covered.

Now let’s talk about the **staff**—these guys are the MVPs. Friendly, fast, and somehow still smiling at 7 a.m. I don’t know what kind of coffee they’re drinking, but I want some.

In short, this Starbucks isn’t just another chain—it’s the neighborhood's unofficial living room with better lighting and fewer screaming kids (usually). Come for the caffeine, stay because your phone’s charging.

Five stars and a cinnamon swirl.

I saw nearly twenty flies, and this is not my first time saw that, I have to leave this bad review! Hope boss/ owner/manager could make some changes!!!!! So disgusting
Don’t buy croissant or any cake at Starbucks!

Favourite Starbucks to go to in London. I’ve become a regular now 🤭

The staff is amazing, they’re the friendliest, always ask how one’s day is going, they take care in making one’s drinks (especially because I have a rather complicated order but they don’t complain or grumble about it), and they’re usually quite timely with processing orders.

There is seating upstairs which is clean most of the time and quiet - depending on when you go. But yes overall I recommend!

I visited the Starbucks at Churchill Place around 16:05 pm today  during my break to grab a quick coffee. I was waiting in line in my security uniform when a man in a suit came in after me and was served first by the female barista behind the counter. She decided to ignore me completely and chose to serve him instead, clearly treating him better because of his appearance. This was clear discrimination — I felt humiliated and left the store without ordering. This behaviour is totally unacceptable, and staff need proper training on equality and treating every customer with the same respect.
Vickey (alongside all staff) was lovely and catered towards my requests perfectly!!

Great location inside Churchill Place, modern space design with plenty of space for sitting and working. Right in front of Barclays bank 🏦 so we just ordered our favourite Green tea latte with vanilla syrup while waiting for the banking service.

Canary Wharf, located under the Barclay  Building.
This Outlet is more open plan but we'll laid out,very good service .The quality of the drinks was a cut above other Starbucks i visit .What i do not understand is my go to drink is 20p less at Canary Wharf than it is on the Thanet Way Whitstable. !! How so ?

Service quality has really gone downhill. My drink arrived with spills on the cup, forcing me to clean it up myself. Attempting to sit at the counter, I was promptly informed by staff that it was reserved for delivery only—confusing given the presence of customer seating there. Additionally, a customer left the door open, letting in freezing cold air, and staff were indifferent, leaving me to close it. Tables remain uncleaned for hours, littered with empty cups and trash.

Very nice starbucks, i often frequent. They were a little stingy with the oreo flakes on top of my cookies and cream Frappuccino, so i requested for more. You can’t get away with that when you are serving me. Photos show the before and after of oreo flakes.

Starbucks never disappoints when it comes to coffee! Their lattes are a perfect blend of rich, smooth espresso and creamy milk topped with a velvety foam—ideal for cozy mornings or chilly afternoons. The iced coffee, on the other hand, is a refreshing treat, offering the perfect balance of bold flavor and a cooling kick. Whether you’re after warmth or refreshment, Starbucks delivers every time. The consistency in taste and quality, combined with their welcoming atmosphere, makes every visit worthwhile.

Classic Starbucks, quick service even when busy. Big shout out to Alina who is always so nice taking my order. Excellent service, friendly, thoughtful and a reason to choose this Starbucks over others in Canary Wharf

Had a very poor experience today and will be escalating this up to corporate. Manager Zoe is a poor representation of all Starbucks and should be removed from her managerial role effective immediately. She goes against Starbucks, mission statement and what they stand for. Lacks people skills.

By far the best Starbucks in London.  So many branches have lost their way.  This place retains the original essence of vibe, layout and exceptional service.  Hats off to Stephano and the team.  Thanks for making our day

We tried strawberry acai and tiramisu ice latte. The acai was great but the ice latte was not good at all.
The store is very clean and nice. The staff are very polite and helpful. The entrance is from O2 and they security will check the bags. There is an outside area to seat or for smoking area.
No one needs plastic flowers! Real plants, even evergreens like small ferns and pines, help to reduce carbon and require little maintenance and offer space for insects. Think sustainability! #SDG17

Good coffee but unfortunately bad customer service. The order was messed up and when we asked for missing items - we were given completely something else. Tried to explain that wasn’t what was ordered, but no luck ( I guess it can be blamed on the crowded morning)
Thanks Dipika. Made no effort to help us with our order. My wife has allergy and you couldn't care to help at all. We were able to make proof on claims made by Dipika and she was just unhelpful

It is located near the entrance to the mall. Parking is easy as it is part of the shopping complex. It has two entrances, one from inside the mall and its own entrance next to the outdoor seating. Plenty of seating inside and outside.
Staff are friendly. Coffee quality and service are typical Starbucks fare.

Service was good but the coffee was a major let down. The milk had been over heated and was close to impossible to hold with scalding. A little bit of seating is available, really a pit stop branch rather than a 3rd space.

Asked for an iced cortado and was told by Manuela who took my order that they don't do it! Not even showing willingness to make the customer happy and make it.
All i was looking for was an iced cortado -  with ice inside!  Mandela was adamant they don't do it , not even.bothered to try and make it, as the other Starbucks make it and never issues. Mandela was making such an issue out of it! So I just left!
Will never come again when she works!

Forgetting to collect my drink after ordering, I really can’t thank Stef enough for making another coconut refresha for me even the store has just closed. Soon to leave the country, I will miss the store and thank him againnnnn :)

Very busy ad  slow service and sadly 3time they game me wring coffee . They don't understand dry Macchiato.  Waiting 10 minutes for panini and after they did sorry we left too long in the oven so it's hard and than gave us another 1. 😔 please is not very clean especially the sofa
Had a green tea lemonade. Was nice but they added to much lemon and it was slightly bitter. Lovely service though. Would probably come back but ask for less of the citrus fruit.

I waited 10 minutes for my hazelnut latte, because they said they ran out of syrup. Once I got the cup it doesn't taste at all of hazelnut and the latte was flat.
I wasn't told it had run out, there was no communication or offer of a different flavour.
I'm certain when I asked they just gave me the unfinished coffee without the syrup.

I would avoid, much better coffee shops inside the Excel centre.

Visited custom house branch yesterday the atmosphere and the drink was amazing but more than that the guy maybe his name was Sajan was even more better. He made the best chocolate mousse latte, was soft spoken and gave me the best customer service. Will visit there again

Man lali really made us feel so welcome with his service he gave such greindly and amazing service and made sure our drinks were made with extreme care and love didn't just double check but triple checked his family welcoming atmosphere made us feel so cool this is a literal start inside this starbucks he deserves all the applause he can gather

Had a great service monday first time visiting.

Great service from barrister bruce who really stood out with his customer service.

Served me a pumpkin spice 😋

The place looked clean and presentable and not a bad word to be said..

Thankyou for your service...

(Did not have food)

Disgusting caramel latter so bitter and smelly cookie wanted to throw up but it’s food we need to respect that also their sandwiches were that fresh! I am sorry very disappointed!!! It’s just the name now now quality!

We tried it, and we loved it❤️
I tried the blueberry crumble croissant bun. And it was insanely good. It wasn’t to sweet, and the blueberry jam in the middle was perfect.
We tried also the blueberry muffin, this was also fine, but we were agreed that the crumble croissant bun was more interesting.
Highly recommend to come and try!
Thanks for the kindness and everything😘✨
🫐🥐🧁
I like this Starbucks as it has plenty of seatings arrangements  and very quick service

Exceptional Service from Knowledgeable Baristas!

I recently visited this Starbucks and had an outstanding experience thanks to two employees, Dev and Adarsh. I had some specific preferences and questions about customizing my coffee, and they were incredibly helpful.

Adarsh took the time to listen carefully to my taste requirements (like strength, milk type, and flavor notes) and patiently explained all the customization options available. Dev jumped in to clearly break down the differences between roasts and brewing methods in a way that was easy to understand, without being overwhelming.

Their knowledge, patience, and genuine desire to help me get the perfect drink turned a simple coffee run into a great learning experience. They are a credit to your team. This kind of customer service is what makes me a loyal patron. Thank you, Dev and Adarsh!

The staff are always so lovely and accommodating! I asked for a decaf flat white as I cannot drink coffee due to health issues and the staff members were so sweet about it and ensured that the drink was decaf. Thank you so much to you both! :)

I approached staff to request access to the disabled toilet, as the door required a code. I hold an access key for disabled facilities, but since your toilet also needed a code, I asked the staff for it. They provided a code, but when I attempted to use it, the lock did not work. Only after I had tried to enter the code was I informed that the staff do not actually have access to the disabled toilet.

This occurred on a match day when the store was extremely busy, with over 30 people queueing ahead of me for the main toilets. I have Crohn’s disease and needed to empty my colostomy bag urgently. Being sent back and forth, given incorrect information, and ultimately being unable to use the disabled toilet caused me significant distress, discomfort, and a completely avoidable medical situation.

Disabled access should be reliable, functional, and understood by staff. Today, it was not.

I would appreciate a response explaining how this happened and what steps will be taken to ensure that disabled customers are not put in this position  again

📍 Great spot right next to Wembley Library
I ordered a latte and a cappuccino – both were excellent .
The place is quiet with comfortable seating, perfect for reading or getting some light work done.
Highly recommend it, especially if you enjoy relaxing near a library.

I popped in quickly to check if they still
had the Bearista mug(which they didn’t) but to discover this branch has the nicest staff I have ever talked to in any starbucks! they were kind and understanding, especially after having to tell me the cup sold out in 5 min.

Nina is honestly one of the best parts of this Starbucks. She’s always super friendly and somehow remembers my order every single time — even when it’s busy! She goes out of her way to make everyone feel welcome, and it really shows that she cares about her customers. You can tell she genuinely loves what she does. The place just feels better when Nina’s around!

I stopped by this Starbucks and got a toffe nut latte made by Nina — honestly, it was one of the best coffees I’ve ever had. She was super friendly and kind, and the whole experience just made my day. I’ll definitely be coming back again, and hopefully I’ll get to see Nina next time too.
